huge pho is huge and delicious!!! i like the huge bowls ($3 extra) that you can share with friends, its a good bonding experience! make your own pho is the best
Loud. Crowded. Dirty. Bad Service. Extra charges were given to us. Wouldn't go again, but the games were fun except for when they wouldn't work and stole credits off of our cards.

I love this market they have a large vegan and gluten free section and their produce is 100% organic BUT It isn't always the freshest. I support my local stores over the big chains but sometimes I can't buy certain produce items here due to the over ripening or the rough treatment they have seemed to had in transit. They always do have good sales on vitamins and supplements.

For the price, I was expecting better quality food and variety.  You get what they serve you regardless of any food allergies that you may have.  The menu is the same every Sunday brunch.  The place seems to be good for small celebrations like birthdays and baby showers.  The service was okay but a better explanation of the brunch when I called on the phone would have changed my mind about eating over there and their website is misleading when it comes to the menu for brunch.  The tea was really good but not the coffee.

Had a great dinner here. Really liked the walk from canal street down to the restaurant as well. Loved how relaxed and personable the wait staff are.

We started with the fried dill pickles and aligator sausage.  Both were delicious and both sauces with appetizers were homemade. Ordered a shrimp po boy and macaroni and cheese. Both were outstanding! Also got the category 5 hurricane and it was the best hurricane I had on my whole trip.

Don't forget to grab a box of pralines to go! You'll be too full after all of the good food. Best pralines I have tried.

I'm very displeased with my experience here the girl up front was very rude when I went up to the bar to get some drinks I was not carded when my friends who are african American went up right after me they were carded which they were already carded up front so I don't understand why they were carded twice and I was only once I think that was very racist of them the workers there just make you feel uncomfortable for being there i had such high hopes for this place I am so let down I will not be returning
AMAZING food!  I'm not kidding.  Fun place.  Really glad I found it.

I've tried BBQ from all over the country and this place is definitely a top competitor. To me, it really all comes down to the meat and the sauce. I tried the pulled pork sandwich with red sauce when I visited. The meat was tender and moist but let's talk about the sauce...HANDS DOWN, some of the best BBQ sauce I have tried. It was a perfect mix of sweet, spicy and vinegary-everything in proper proportions so that nothing was overpowering. 
The prices were very reasonable for the amount of food. 
I only had two complaints about the place: I wasn't a fan of the potato salad (and a lot of other people would probably love it, it just wasn't for me) and I would have liked to see some in house brewed sweet tea instead of the soda fountain type stuff.
Great space with fabulous St. Louis themed art. Try the meatball pizza, arancini, and the gelato. We will definitely be back.

The pizza is great, the service is friendly. However, a large pizza here, which costs about 25$, is the size of a medium/small anywhere else. The toppings are good, but they definitely withheld the steak on the lobo pizza.

Portion size of the food is big. I ordered the nachos with beef and cheese special needed a little salt but other than that it was good. service on the other hand we had a new waiter not very attentive friendly but not attentive how to call him twice would come again to try other stuff on the menu and a different waiter

I ate here with one of my coworkers and the burger was really good!
Its has a really cool atmosphere, wish we could have stayed and played the corn hole game!

They have a wide variety of bubble teas & smoothies but I noticed they use powder flavorings. I wish they used fresh fruits and real red bean.  I've never tried their frozen yogurt although a glance at the toppings appear standard as that of a froyo type shop. 
I stepped in at 8:30pm on a summer evening and there was no one at the counter to take my order. A lady finally walked out of the back room after 7 minutes or so. 
Overall, it wasn't the greatest but I can't say it's the worst. If you intent is to go for the bubble teas though, keep driving about 6 miles east toward Taiwanese Tea House in Castleton.
Beautiful decorations, fast service, delicious food. Margaritas are great too. It's a must visit place if you are ever in Tucson.

A coworker recommended this place. I went with my wife for our dinner and we both got some kind of burger. I myself was ok with the food but it was kind of pricey. My wife didn't really like anything on the menu but then again, we just went without looking at the menu, so the restaurant is not to be blamed. The service was great, and so was the setting. Overall a pleasant experience.

Went here for dinner last night for dining under the stars. We got there pretty early, around 5:45-6 and were seated right away. 
We ordered the bruschetta for an app, it was very good. I wish there had been more of it but at $6 I can't complain, I just love tomato and would have gladly eaten the whole plate by myself!....maybe next time. 

For our entrees I had the ravioli quarto formaggio and my fiancé had the fusilli lunghi. I LOVED my dinner, it comes in a delicious cream sauce. The portion was a nice size, I think it was 7 or 8 raviolis and I took 3 home, the sauce is very rich so its not something you would want to eat a huge portion of all at once. My fiancé said his meal was good too. 

Our waitress was very nice, a little slow, but that's to be expected on such a busy night. We also heard her pacify a rather drunk and obnoxious patron sitting near us in a very tactful way. 

My only complaint was that she said she had to take our app and entrée order all at once due to how busy they were. It would have been nice to  have something on the table while we were picking our entrees since we were both very hungry, but she did space them out so at least everything didn't come all at once.
I love this place, friendly staff, the food is good, service was quick whenever I was here and the cooks are pretty funny....can be a bit pricy though.
Great service!  Terrific comfort food!  Lovely, fun setting.

No stars this is the nasties place to eat she is texting while making food... I seen another Waiter smell the drinks to see witch was witch.... Eating food off customer plate.. With the manager right there.... I walk out and called corporate but no like....

Chain restaurants suck.  But when you have two tired children in tow, burgers, fries, free balloons, crayons, and dancing waitstaff are just the ticket.  It saved our afternoon.  The burgers and shakes are actually pretty good.

I'd actually give them 4 or 4.5 stars but considering the pretentious nitwits who have given them 1 star, I figure I need to make up the difference.  

I've been coming to Gullifty's for over 20 years now and I have got to say it's always been one of my favorite spots on the Mainline.  Nice comfort food and overall consistency in both food and service.  If they were located in Philly, they'd get 4 stars.  Considering the options on the Mainline, they get 5.

I've always liked the sandwiches, the appetizers (the beer battered onion rings are amazing, as is the fried cheese), the pizzas (the apple and bacon pizza remains a favorite), the pastas are excellent.  And their cheesecake is to die for -- unlike any restaurant cheesecake, it really tastes homemade.  My daughter likes their ribs, I'm not terribly impressed with the salmon, but the fish and chips is a good one.

All in all, a solid neighborhood selection for from-scratch food.  Order on MLD and beat the crowds.

Sometimes it's the little details that can make or break a good restaurant to be a great one. My 3 Stars easily could have been 5 if for one little detail. Salad Dressing! 
My wife ordered the Chicken Caesar Salad and I had a side salad with Blue Cheese Dressing with my Fantastic Hamburger. The Hamburger was pure perfection. The 1/2 lb patty was juicy, cooked med rare with grilled onions and cheddar cheese. One of the Best Burgers I've eaten. Oh yes the Dressings. 
I asked the server if the dressings were home made in the restaurant. He's was so proud to say yes they were. Little did he know that the dressings he and his kitchen think are so wonderful are actually terribly horrible. The Caesar Dressing was an Oily Based concoction that was so over powered with anchovies and garlic my wife could not stand eating it. I couldn't either. My Blue Cheese Dressing was so over ripe that after one bite I couldn't eat anymore. 
With so many great dressings available to purchase from Sysco or US Foods I'm always so surprised that restaurateurs insist on making their own thinking theirs is better. Let's just say Ken's, Marzetti or Girards dressings are all available from their local foodservice company that come with great consistency and are shelf stable.

The restaurant itself is very nice & relaxing. A block off the main drag of State St also adds to the quaintness. I would definitely go back although I wouldn't order anything that requires a dressing.

I don't want to take anything away from the food but, the service was the star. 

For midlevel restrauant, the service was well above standard.

Madeline was our primary server. She visited frequently, corrected problems before we could even bring them up (missing side), was familiar with the menu, polite, friendly, anticipated our needs, etc.

I enjoyed the Hopstorm IPA with my Spicy Chicken in peanut sauce with soba noodles.
Still fantastic! Had the crossaint sandwich and the quiche is amazing!!! Everytime we go there the pastry to the lunches are fantastic. Coffee is very good as well. A place everyone must try!

Well, 5 star update for this place because flat out, they deserve it.  Even though we ended up having our son, Milo at TMC, the bulk of the labor was spent here (18-19 hours of the 25 hour labor).  95% of that time was spent with Sandra (Sandy), who is an absolute rockstar of a midwife.  We couldn't have done it without her, even at TMC, where she loyally remained with us.  She was just so absolutely wonderful.  
Leading up to the pregnancy, we had bi-monthly, then monthly, then weekly, then even bi-weekly visits here.  All visits were professional and courteous.  More importantly, they were kind and considerate.  We also attended the 5-week, 5 session classes offered to expectant parents.  These were great to have in preparation for the labor, although really, there is nothing that can truly get you ready. Still though, very much worthwhile.  Since the birth of our son, we've taken advantage of some new-parent "meetings" where new parents (and children) can meet to discuss items pertinent to raising their children.  These events are always moderated by someone affiliated with the Birth Center, so there is professional advice and opinion input into the discussion as well.
I really and simply am so happy that we have this place here in town.  Not just during the pregnancy, but afterwards as well.

Bbq heaven. The pork and brisket sandwiches were amazing but the highlights were the banana pudding and loaded fries ...holy cow they were so damn good !!! Must try if you visit nashville or live  in Nashville !!

I am from Saskatchewan and when I came to Alberta I hated shopping around for insurance as customer service in this province seems like a myth.  Millennium is amazing...I reside in Leduc and use millennium even though they are in Sherwood park because they are that good.  Amazing prices, great service and prompt.  Good job peeps.

Was visiting Indy and checking out different Brewery's.  This place was really hard to find it's on the back side of Mass Ave.   total hole in the wall place.     I believe this place just opened and they were still working on it.   The space has character.    You can get a pint and some pretzels and chill out a bit.   I'm a fan of brown ale and theirs was just incredible light for me and didn't feel any of the other beers I tried.    If  you're in town stop by as I'm thinking this place will improve.

I love Red Lobsters and their free delicious biscuits are wonderful. This isn't the busiest location but I love the quite atmosphere. We usually go to the one in Boone crossing in Chesterfield but that one is always so busy. So we try to come to this one because we feel we get more personal attention.

Sunday jazz cruise without the lunch. Heard people say the lunch was good, but I'd wager that lunch in the French Quarter would be better (2 blocks away)! 

There wasn't too much to see but it is nice to slow down the trip a bit and unwind on the only working steamboat in the area (Creole Queen is not a steamboat). Tour guide over the speakers is unobtrusive but does tell some interesting about NOLA and the surrounding communities. 

Go inside and check out the jazz band, and the engine room downstairs. All in all, a good trip for just $25.

I heard about Jones for the longest time as I have seen them featured on a few "Best of Philly" lists so I spent the first day of the New Year at Jones and was not disappointed. I went to try out the brunch (who can do a bad brunch, right?). This place has definitely earned a spot as one of my favorite places for brunch. Honestly, the prices are a on the steep side, not surprising due to the area, and most of their side items SHOULD be included on most the breakfast plates. For example, I think the omelettes should come with a portion of tater totes or a fruit salad, however; you have to order that as an additional side. 

Despite this shortcoming on the brunch menu, the food and drinks are truly great! They have some of the BEST pancakes I have ever tasted! They are large enough to be shared by two people and can be served topped with warm maple syrup, bananas and walnuts. Not to mention they are flurry, buttery and melt in you mouth delicious. They are to DIE for. I ordered a fruit bowl (berries, grapes, apples) the Grasshopper milkshake and I tried the Quiche. The quiche was light and fluffy and came with a side salad. Again, tater totes should be added on the plate. In addition, the quiche should come with an additional slice, its that great. 

Last but not least, the drinks. I tried a White Cranberry Orange Sangria and an adult milkshake. The sangria was so-so. It came in a small wine glass which was a disappointment since it was about $9.00. I had better tasting and sizes elsewhere. On the other hand, the adult milkshake, you guessed it made with alcohol, was creamy delicious made with chocolate  and topped with mint.

Overall, Jones for a Sunday Brunch is delicious! Hopefully, I can try the dinner menu soon!

First time in and I loved it!!! I moved to Santa Barbra to go to UCSB from San Diego and desperately needed a fill. I went into Star Nails and Diana helped me. She was so sweet and gentle and not to mention THE BEST!!! 

My nails look PERFECT!!! She takes pride in her work and in little details. 

I can't wait to come back in a few weeks for another fill!! Thank you Star Nails and thank you Diana for making me feel beautiful.

This place is absolutely amazing! It was my first time at Rescue Spa. I've been to spas and had facials since I was a teen with my mom and this place delivers! The best of the best is Tirzah! I was super lucky and got a facial appointment with her due to a cancelation. I'm pretty picky about facials because I have combo skin with enlarged pores and its always congested with gunk. I love a good extraction! Tirzah was pro-extraction and she was perfect! (i've been to places where they just do a good cleansing and squeeze a little but my face is not clean & I've been to places where they OVER extract and it hurts). Her voice was soothing, she was super friendly and professional, and she gave great advice on how to better manage my concerns. She even gave me a complimentary microdermabrasion session to see how I like it because she recommended it for my skin problems. What an angel! My skin feels super clean, it's super soft, and so dewy it glows! Now I feel like all the products I use will absorb better. They do charge a bit more for her services but she is worth every penny! I immediately made follow-up facials with her for the next 3 months. Love!
LOVE AJ'S, I love getting a cup of coffee and sitting in the patio.

I have been to the Duke a few times now, mostly for Sunday brunch, which is irresistible at $11 for steak, eggs, [chunky!] hash browns, and a beer (or coffee with Baileys).  I've also met up with friends for happy hour, appys et cetera, and I have to say I have been really impressed with the food and with the wait staff.  Both are above average, much better than what you would expect for this neck of the woods (Argyll industrial area). 

The Duke offers your usual pub food, but it does it right.  For example, many, many pubs offer mini-yorkshire puddings, but often they are dried out and overcooked.  The ones they make here (see my picture!) are just like your English granny would make, light, fluffy and delicious, and served with a drizzle of horseradish sauce. Perfect!

Other great appy offerings I've tried are the quesadilla, the halibut bites, and lemon pepper shrimp.  Most appys will run you about $10 for a large portion, and if you come at happy hour on a Friday, the appys are half off - $5 for a meal-sized appy!  You don't often get deals for good pub food like this in Edmonton, certainly not in the downtown.  

If you're looking for a sports pub style place, the Duke offers numerous TVs and a projection screen, so it's a good place to watch The Game. It also offers a dart board, pool tables and VLTs.  

Things I don't care for at the Duke - it gets pretty loud in there, and even louder when the talent for the night shows up.  The last live entertainer wailed out such classics as "Do You Come From the Land Down Under", needless to say we left in short order.  The selection of beer they have on tap is also somewhat limited, slightly better than the average blue-collar pub (i.e. - they have Stella), but I wish they offered some of our fine local Edmonton beer on tap.  I think a pint of Charlie Flint or Aprikat would go great with my Sunday steak and eggs.

The service and food here is WONDERFUL! We came in a party of ten and had to get out in a rush to go. They were extremely nice, packed up our food, and the atmosphere of the restaurant gave us a first great impression of Nashville as it was our first time visiting. We ordered the Tuck, Hot Chicken, Chicken, and Brisket sandwiches and everyone loved theirs. Nothing was oily and everything was reasonably priced. I can't wait to come back again because everything was delicious!!
Great buffet. Even towards the end of the buffet hours the food remains fresh.  Good price.  Good service

I love the bacon cheese burger, but everytime I come here no matter how busy it is or even when it's dead I get crap service with crap attitudes from servers. The food and its location being really close to my house are the only reasons I come back.

Myself and coworkers visit here often during the week and hit up the lunch buffet.  I have also come here solo and gotten the lunch buffet to go for the same price.

The Indian food here is great and the best I have had in Indy.  As usual the items on the buffet are not as spicy but you can ask and the servers will bring you out additional cayenne pepper to spice up your plate.  The chicken tikka masala and the buttered chicken are delicious as well as the lamb shanks.   The vegetable pakora is always fried perfectly.  also go with the garlic naan!  There is plenty of variety of everyone.
This place is great! Nice decor, very clean, professional friendly service and yummy food. Can't ask for anything else. Will definitely be back!
Lost my reservation for two people, not going back. I know they were busy but that's a little ridiculous.

I had a friend visit me on several occasions and this hotel was the best experience she had in Philly. It's best to book in advance as rooms do fill up but it's worth it. The atmosphere is very quiet, very respectful of privacy and personal space, it feels like a haven of calm in the midst of the city. Rooms are very clean and decorated simply but with character, so it doesn't feel like you're staying in a catalogue photo re-enactment. Great staff as well! Especially recommend this place for those in the LGBTQ community. It was also the Lesbian-friendliest place in the gayborhood.

Justin is a great guy who does a nice job with the cut. Prices are great.  It's easy to park.  No appointment is necessary, and you can get in/out quickly. I can't go 5 stars without the straight razor neck shave, but I would definitely recommend Justin's shop.  I've been there twice in the new year and will definitely return.

The pizza is good. Great gluten free options. The staff in charge of answering the phones seem like they are high, and don't care about doing anything involving conflict resolution. There were two instances where my pizzas were not delivered, and no recourse was offered. The second instance they literally said, "O we have your pizzas going to another house. Would you like a refund or...?" Terrible customer service. I hope this changes because I like the food.

I will preface my review by saying that I had no idea this was a Steven Starr restaurant until after I came back home and read the yelp reviews...

That being said, after running into massive lines at both Morning Glory and Sabrina's and desperately in need of food while also wanting to try something new, we stumbled upon Jones and were seated immediately.  Looking around, I was impressed with the 70's inspired retro decor- the shag carpet, the stone pillars, the cozy seating and comfortable booths perfect for lounging... while my boyfriend's immediate reaction was 'it seems like an upscale Denny's...' Giving a quick first glance at the menu and it appeared that might be the case- although I was hoping for better. 

We each ordered a bloody mary (a weekend necessity that so few of our favorite brunch spots offer...) - they were great and the spicyness was done just right.  Our attentive waitress returned to take our orders- I got the heuvos rancheros and my boyfriend the salmon eggs benedict.  

Our food arrived promptly- though the portions were a bit smaller than I had expected for the price we were paying (Is that how it worked in the 70s?).  My huevos rancheros was drenched in shredded cheese- the eggs were ok, though I felt the dish as a whole was a little bit of a mushy mess...usually when I order this dish you can actually see the tortilla shell and they use cooked black beans, though happy to see that they made this without the chorizo ...My boyfriend really enjoyed his salmon eggs benedict - althought thought the english muffin was odd and strangely thin.  We didn't really get the tater tots on the side....is this *really* '70's comfort food' or just plain high school cafeteria cuisine? 

Overall, our meal was a bit overpriced, but got the job done- I would have rated this a two based solely on the food, however, Jones gets a third star for good service, excellent bloody mary's, and cozy decor.   

Will probably be back at some point for the bloody mary.

This place was packed, so we thought it would be really good.  Unfortunately, Busy so thought it'd be good.

The service was really friendly, but as far as attentiveness it wasn't great.  

I had the stuffed peppers with cornbread and mac and cheese.  The cornbread was bland and the mac and cheese was very lack lustre.  It wasn't seasoned and didn't taste that cheesy.  The stuffed peppers were mushy and quite disgusting.  

My friend had a fried seafood platter.  The only thing good on the plate was the fried catfish.  
Service was friendly - but not great.  The okra tasted odd.  

I'd definitely skip over this place if I were you.  I don't know how this place got so many good reviews.
The best Mexican food. The food is fresh and the salsa can't be beat. The service is excellent as well. You will not be disappointed.

Wow. Wow. Wow. I wasn't sure what to expect but I've been looking for a really great sandwich place in Nashville and finally tried 51st. I had the Cuban sandwich and I think it may be the best sandwich I've had in town. When I pulled it out of the bag, I was amazed at how heavy it was. It was packed with meats in the perfect proportions and condiments were just right. The bread was fresh and soft. Kudos, 51st. I'll be back and I'll be bringing people with me.

Had the perogies with sausage, just average .
The hamburger was a frozen patty, no taste, for juice. Sent it back, they took it off the bill. Won't come back here

I first visited the Barnes Jewish cafeteria while waiting for a family member whom was having surgery. I stopped in just before the lunch rush to discover a wide variety of lunch options, ranging from several selections of hot dishes, sandwiches, salads, and more. I ended up going with a Chicken Parmesan entree, which was priced at about $8 for a reasonable portion. Seating is plentiful, however it does get crowded during lunch so be advised, after the lunch rush you might find others using the cafeteria to work while waiting on people at the hospital, in fact I even used it to catch up on some work while waiting. 

Overall the experience was better than I had expected from a hospital cafeteria, decent food, reasonable options, and a fair price makes this a decent option while visiting the hospital.

Came here this morning for the first time . Good Donuts, not the best in the city. Also Way over priced.. I almost had a heart attack when she said 30 dollars for the dozen. I can't speak for the Chicken though.

I am gluten and yeast intolerant, so I was in heaven when I found this place. Not only did they have all my normal foods, but I found flax seed pizza dough and vegan cupcakes that were divine. Everything was laid out easy to find. I am now a regular!

This place always has what I need. I've been to Whole Foods, but they don't provide a lot of options for gluten free diners. I have been to Rollin Oats at least once a week to get items for my kitchen. 

I can get Quinoa pasta which is the best gluten free pasta out there in my book, and they have sales occasionally so you can stock up. They also have vegan cheese since I'm lactose intolerant. This is also relatively well priced. 

Overall I find this store to be the only one in the vicinity that truly suits my needs.

We did a dinner cruise. Unfortunately, we picked the one day of our trip with 2 waves of thunderstorms during our cruise!  the jazz band, and passengers, were literally blown off the decks.  Also, we were expecting cajun buffet, but that was not what was served.  We met a lovely couple from Scotland as we huddled on deck.  We would actually give this another try, but would probably opt for the cruise only and would skip dinner.  The short time we got to enjoy the jazz band on deck before the storm blew in was Fantastic!!

Love this place, love the al pastor tacos, taco Tuesday was so good! $1.50 each taco..awesome deal! My friend got the quesadilla with mushroom and rajas, spicy but really good. The tortillas are amazing, I love this place

As a member of the Southern Arizona Concierge Network, I was dining at this El Charro location this past week. The decor and ambiance was fabulous, the servers were so very attentive, and the food was delicious! Their Sangria is to die for!! I have been at their other locations several times and this store did not disappoint!

My brother took me to Philadelphia Archery and Gun Club for my first gun shooting experience.  He's a member here and has tried many of the shooting ranges around and in the city.  He brought along a 9mm and a rifle, an sks? or something.  I don't know I think it was of the Russian assault variety.  He gave me a lot of information in a short period of time and I mostly retained the stuff I needed to know immediately.  

We arrived and were greeted by some lackluster staff that had me show ID and fill out a form before directing us upstairs.  To be fair it was after 7:30 and they close up around 8:30 or 9.  The upstairs gentleman, Mike, was really nice.  He liked my name and said it reminded him of something from Sherlock Holmes or Hopalong Cassidy and then proceeded to show me his dvd case for Hopalong Cassidy.  It was weird but I liked it.   I don't know what would happen if I had gone here without a member or someone skilled with guns so I can't say much about the staff on that level but nice is nice.  It was $5 for me to shoot since I was with a member, apparently Wednesday is ladies night and ladies shoot free so keep that in mind if you were blessed with girly parts. 

The range is smallish, with about eight or so lanes.  Each is equipped with a target holder and controls for moving your target.  My brother brought zombie targets but the range has targets (for sale I think) if you need them.  According to my brother the ammunition is a little pricey but I don't know much about that.  I don't know that I'd go back and shoot again but it was a cool experience and I can scratch it off of my list.
Fresh food, I love it. If you want a Fancy dinner this is a good option also prices are good, service is ok. The crab cake appetizer its really good
My step-son had a super fun time and it was a great bonding experience for us.

Oh my God, why did I think doing a food crawl of Philly over 4th of July weekend was a good idea? Because it is--I just can't control the weather (....yet) and couldn't escape the 98-degree heat!

Luckily, Johns Water Ice appeared like a hazy oasis in the concrete desert heat. The boyfriend and I stepped up (i.e. collapsed) at the counter, caught our breaths, and ordered some good ol' Philly water ice.

I got chocolate. It was good. Cool and refreshing and not overwhelmingly sweet for my poor, dry, parched mouth. I liked the wateriness. The boy got banana. It was made with honest-to-goodness real bananas! You could taste the realness! You could even see the little black dots that are in the center of bananas. Plus, it wasn't fake shiny yellow! He got half a cup of bananas, half a cup of chocolate and swirled them together to make some good banana chocolate. It was very delish. Yes, I said "delish".
What seemed like a tourist trap actually turned out to be one of the highlights of our trip. Sam and Bruce were exceptional ambassadors and the entire staff made the voyage memorable.

I went here for dinner tonight. I soon as one of the servers saw us he opened the door for us. The inside of the restaurant has a nice modern look to it. There were also beautiful paintings on the wall. For dinner I ordered their chicken Parmesan. It is not on the menu, but they were happy to make it. It was delicious. The chicken had great flavor, and it was nice and moist. The fettuccine that came with it also tasted great with the chicken. I also ordered an Arnold Palmer to go, and they gave me a huge cup of it. I can tell that they care a lot about their customers. If you live in or are visiting Tucson. I would not hesitate to recommend this restaurant.

This is a wonderful spot for lunch! The service is great, and the portions are just right. Our server checked in with everyone, and seemed glad to be there. The hot chicken sandwich was divine!!! I loved the people-watching, too, on that busy corner.

This is a chain, but the quality is pretty good, the flavors were fresh.  My husband's flat bread was good and I enjoyed my chicken picatta.  Our waiter was friendly and attentive.  The reason I'm only giving three stars is the dessert.  We ordered a chocolate mousse cake that came to us frozen.  It's pretty hard to appreciate the delicate texture of mousse when it's frozen, and for the price of the dessert that is unacceptable in my book.

If there was a zero star rating that's how I would rate this location. 
It is the worst ice cream ever. Not sure how the ice cream is mixed but it is NOT creamy. The ice cream is like eating air. Ewwww.
Legit authentic Mexican food.  It's run by a family and they never disappoint.  Give it a try and you'll leave satisfied and full.

If I could give 0 stars I would. This place is TRASH. First of all they kept telling me the wrong total price so I had to keep adding more food to meet the delivery charge until I realized when I got the receipt, they added the tax after the delivery charge so it was at least $10 over the real total!!!! All this so I can satisfy my craving of sweet potato fries and what do I get. BURNT SWEET POTATO FRIES. Why even deliver them to me? Who would eat this? Disgusting. No class, all about the money.

Loved this place. Came for a brunch of sorts - three donuts, coffee and a half chicken for two. The donuts we had were all hot - strawberry lavender, vanilla spice and the honey glazed that came with the chicken. All were fantastic - soooooo good. They are about as big as an Entemans donut and they are indeed light tasting although I'm sure not light in calories. The chicken was also very good - got the dry rub Za'atar which was excellent. Za'atar is dried thyme, oregano, marjoram, or some combination thereof, mixed with toasted sesame seeds, salt and sumac. Great stuff. A couple of the pieces were drier than I'd like and would have been great if there was a dipping sauce. None the less - really liked it although for those who have had Bon Chon this is similar - although I liked the crust on this chicken a lot. Hopefully next time we will get a fancy donut however they were all sold out when we got there at 330. Oh great service and solid music too.

Came here to try the beers and to get some dinner. The food here is amazing!  I would come here even if I didn't like beer!  We got the fish tacos and the burger. The fish tacos were very good and slightly sweet. Very similar to other ones I have had previously but definitely worth getting. The burger is fabulous. The meat was cooked perfectly - very juicy and messy!  I'm glad they didn't ask us how we wanted it cooked because we might have ruined it by getting it done too 'well.'   We got a flight of beer and the Vintage cider. The Vintage cider tastes a lot like apple juice so great if you have a non-beer drinker in your group. Wish I lived nearby so I could come here on a regular basis.

Giving Noboru 2 stars is my way of being generous. 

I went for dinner over the weekend with some friends, and we were very disappointed. Considering the price for dinner, we all had expected a lot more.

At first glance, it appears as though there is a lot of sushi. However, after a closer look, we realized there actually was about 5 different types of sushi all with slight variations. For example, there was a shrimp roll, shrimp tempura, spicy shrimp tempura, shrimp tempura "sandwich", and shrimp tempura with a special sauce. I'm sorry, but that is not a good selection!

After we ate some sushi we decided to try some of their other dishes as well. The sesame chicken was pretty good if it was fresh, if it sat out for more than 15 minutes it would dry out and become hard to chew. Also, the fried rice was really bland and had a awkward texture. 

I know people go to Noboru for the dinner, but not the dessert, but since they had dessert we had to try it. On Noboru's website and Facebook page they advertise chocolate covered strawberries and even crepes! We saw nothing of the sort. There were miniature cookies which gave one of my friends and I a laugh because they were advertised as "homemade", when they clearly came from the nearest Acme. They also offered 3 small pieces of jello in a bowl, some fruit, mini cakes, mini brownies, vanilla soft serve ice cream and tart frozen yogurt (which, as a major fro yo fan, I was very disappointed in).  The only dessert item that my table semi-enjoyed were the brownies. 

Finally, I do have to say their cold veggies were delicious! I read some reviews prior to going, and one reviewer mentioned how great the asparagus was. I'm so glad I read their review and made sure to try the asparagus, because it was by far my favorite item there! My only suggestion would be that, while good cold, it should be served warm. I had one piece that was under a heat lamp and was so warm and delicious!

Overall I would say, avoid Noboru unless you really like shrimp and overpaying for food. It may have been the cleanest buffet I have ever been to, but no matter how clean a place is, if the food is not good then it is not worth it!
Really good burgers and my fiancee really enjoyed the buffalo chicken sandwich, the Buffalo Buddy. French fries nice and crisp. A little pricey, but great atmosphere! Will be back

I thought this was a really fun experience.  The buffet dinner was good.  We had green beans, finger potatoes, creamed spinach, 2 types of pasta, roast beef, gumbo and roast pork loin.  The jazz band was good and touring the boat was fun.

Lanna Thai Cafe has got a good thing going, but there are a few things that prevent this restaurant from really shining.

Before we get into that, though, let's talk about the food.

We ordered six dishes at Lanna, and all were very good.

*Ginger Salmon:  This was the star of the meal.  Perfect salmon, perfect Thai veggies.  Would definitely order again.
*Tom Kha Gai:  Standard, but on the better side, with a definite "kick" to it thanks to diced Thai chilies in the soup.  Includes tomatoes and onions.
*Chicken Larb:  Good, tastes like larb should.
*Red Curry:  More "authentic" than most local restaurants, but not terribly spicy.  It's notable for not being excessively sweet like the curries found in most Thai places.  If you like your curries sweet, you may not like this, but we enjoyed it.
*Beef basil:  The beef was tender and the vegetables were good, but this, like the following dish, was not particularly notable.  Definitely felt like it needed more basil, however.
*Pepper beef:  Good, notable peppery flavour (as it should have), but otherwise standard Thai fare.

The restaurant itself is in a converted house.  This gives it a charming café feel, but also means there's not a lot of seating.  I definitely recommend making reservations; we came on a Thursday evening and the restaurant was full the entire time we were there.

Service was poor, but this is likely because there was only one waiter/cashier in the entire restaurant while we were there--the guy was clearly overworked.  Unfortunately, this means we had to wave him down anytime we wanted anything (and he never once refilled our glasses, though we didn't bother asking).  This is the primary reason I docked a star from the review.  No restaurant with poor service gets 5 stars from me.

Prices are reasonable, if slightly high for Thai food.  Most items on the menu are $10-12 (with most seafood being $13 and up)  However, even though we ordered 4 entrées (plus larb and a soup), we were only given one bowl of rice, and we were charged $6 for additional rice.  Not cool.

So, service was poor and charging so much for extra rice seems a little underhanded to me, but it's probably the best Thai food I've had in Reno and prices aren't terrible, so we're likely to return.

I was so excited for a mac & cheese only store and ended up disappointed. The mac is only ok and they have a weird policy that doesn't all you to order a basic mac & cheese plain. You must order the more expensive "classic creations" from the menu. The portion is a good size but I wouldn't recommend it over all.
Haven't been here in a while, the quality has not changed a bit!  Still delicious food with great friendly service!  Love this place!  Wish there was one in St. Charles County!
Awesome food. Great surroundings.  A must go to apt. Enjoyed a romantic dinner. The Caesar had a divine garlic flavor. Can't wait to come again.

Great selection of wine and very knowledgeable staff especially Charlie who used to be at Bottlestop down the road. The prices are about average for big liquor stores but you will find some gems here that you won't find anywhere else. Strong varietals are Zinfindels, Shiraz and Syrahs and Cabernet blends. Of course these are some of my favorites and other varietals are equally represented. Also has a tasting area which is pretty cool

The atmosphere was very cute, but had over a 90 minute wait.  Not even any space to stand in the bar area, so very uncomfortable.  They should use the NoWait app or accept reservations for parties of 5. I will try to return, hopefully they will figure out a waiting area or how to accept reservations

I saw this location a few weeks ago when I was at the AT&T Store next door and decided to stop in once they opened. Well I was in the area today and saw the "Now Open" sign and decided today would be the day. When I walked in I was immediately greeted by a young lady. She asked if I had been there before and I told her I hadn't. She explained the menu and handed me the order forms to make my own sushi roll or sushi bowl. She also showed me the menu with sushi and the sushi bowls with the combinations they already had. This was the hardest decision of my day! 

I decided on the Korean BBQ Beef bowl on the menu with white rice. The food was fresh and tasted good. Now I do have the confess I don't like carrots so I threw them all out so my sushi bowl was more like BBQ Beef bowl. 

The restaurant its self was nice and clean. There were some tables to dine in and there was seating in the window on stools. I ended up visiting the restrooms and they were clean and smelled good.  

I'll definitely be back soon to try more bowls and rolls!

I have gone to EWC for about half a year now. Overall, they are worth going to. The blue wax they use is unbelievably merciful for Brazillians, let alone anywhere else on your body. The person you get is a bit of a hit or miss. If you find the person you like, make sure you request them from then on. The second girl I worked with did my eyebrow and upper lip. If the wax had not been so forgiving, I'm sure I would have left with skin removed. She waxed the same spot on each side of my lip 4x each. That is an absolute no-no. I would know. My mother is a cosmetologist and that's against protocol. Fortunately my first visit there for my Brazillian was swift and efficient. This was the case from then on when I made sure she was the only person I had appointments with.

The last note is that the girls at the front desk are somewhat unfriendly on the phone. They have a bit of attitude when you inquire about available time slots. A single fluke isn't a big deal, but speaking to them more than once on the phone resulted with a bit of a verbal backlash on my end. 

Good enough thanks to the wax. Keep tabs on the person you trust with wax and it should be fine.
Awesome service, friendly. Cutest little cafe. Delicious food. Totally recommend this to anyone!

Great pizza. There's a wide variety of menu items anywhere from wings to calzones. Save room for zeppoli. Nice to have the option for a glass of wine or a mug of beer. Customer service is good and the restaurant is clean. Will keep going back.

I like this gym. I have never had to wait on a machine while people sit around and talk. even during busy times. i will admit it had been 6 months since i walked in to a gym due to family issues and stuff. i felt like i was starting at the gym as a newbie.

I walked in and they had more tredmills and added extra room for the weight machines. it does not have the huge retail area the other gym i went had but for $30 cheaper than the previous place I deal the best i can. 

for roughly $16 a month, i get access to all the locations in the area.

Fun games. Kid friendly but if u want happy friendly staff u r not going to get it. "Ma'am go around the other side of the wall" even though it ended up at the same place. Just puddle doff and angry. Not fun and vibrant as the games and atmosphere try to vibe.  This should be Disneyland by the game vibe but wait staff hate they work here

Came here for the Char Grilled Oysters and they are absolutely amazing!!!!! 10 times better than Acme!! Blackened chicken was amazing and the chicken gumbo was to die for! Last but not least the Fried Peanut Butter and Strawberry Jelly sandwich left me speechless!!  The bartender Michelle was amazing and made the best drinks ever! Ask her for a Strawberry Hennesy! It'll change your life!!!

I dont give 5*'s... But I believe this place, the Dr's and Staff deserve it. After 10 days of watching my dog wither from 47-32 lbs, we reach a conclusion with marginal cost with the work of Dr Rader. They recognized our objectives and were very responsive and driven to resolve or give us plan to manage our pets needs and ours. Great asset to pet owners. Not a number... As I feared. Well managed, cost effective, attentive and intergrity.  Thanks
Great beer selection. Good peaple at the bar. Would like to see better drink speccials and for them to BRING BACK the NACHOS.

Beautiful. Booked a casita last week Fri-Mon. Accommodations were roomy and clean. Breakfast provided every morning was yummy. However things took a turn Sunday afternoon after my wife and I came back from our outing. Our keys had been deactivated. I went to the front desk and they were confused asking if we wanted to stay an extra night. No, I made my original reservation to check out on MONDAY. 
Come Monday morning around 930am ( check out is at 11am) the electricity went out, which also resulted in my shower going from hot to ice cold. Upon checking out I mentioned that I wanted a receipt ( to make sure I wasnt over charged etc), gentleman stated " Well I cant do that right now since we have a power outage". Mind you no apologies to my wife and I for the inconvenience! Bummer, such a lovely place but with glitches....

We took a family member who came from Boston, MA & we had a fantastic Bartender who was pleasant & on top of all guests at the bar!  Drinks were great, great entertainment & definitely a place we will visit again!!!  Loved the License Plates on the ceiling. And when we do return we will be bringing a License Plate to add to the collection!

Eunasia, Deedra, and Maxwell was incredible. A great refreshing way to start my morning. They gave me good recommended drinks as well as a pretty good go to iced machiatto to try. Parking sucks though. But they make up for this in service. I enjoyed the way they made me feel.I enjoyed the conversation. There are also food I will get that later and tell yah how it went.
Awesome hotel.  If you're looking for something central (close to State Street), this hotel is perfect.  It has charm with a very affordable price.  Highly recommend!

this rating is based on 1 sandwich. i haven't had anything else and cannot profess love for anything more than a glorious sandwich special they had today. it was a ham (either blackforest or virginia) & swiss with thin green apple slices, grilled onions and toasted with copious amounts of butter. it comes with mayo but i requested otherwise. UNBELIEVEABLE. our waitress Morgan was also very patient and accomodating. good stuff!
Great local diner. Had the corned beef hash and eggs. Made perfectly with fast, friendly service. Will be hitting this place often. Open 24 hrs.

I stumbled across Phillips produce while traveling down the road and decided to make a stop as I was running early and it looked very old school. I was surprised to see some major brands like Dole  but I realize sometimes that must be necessary if they want to keep a good inventory. The prices seemed competitive with my home area and the convenience of just hopping out of your car to purchase your produce with out taking a hike through a big box store was very nice. 

If I was from the area Phillips Produce would be a regular stop for me.
Seriously don't waste your time. We have a group of 18 and have been waiting for 1 hour and 33 min. And the place is only 75% full

Fantastic!  This is a must stop for anyone visiting or living in Boise. Terrific wine selection, generous portions, and wonderful service. Traveling through Boise on a family vacation and looking for a nice place for dinner, stumbled across Capitol Cellars on Yelp. The location is well appointed and has a sophisticated atmosphere. I had the Senator's cut of the prime rib with a ginormous Idaho baked potato. My wife had the caprese salad, and the chef prepared an off menu butter pasta with freshly grated parmesan for my daughter.  The service was very attentive, without being overbearing, and the owner stopped by to check on our meal and experience. This is a five star restaurant. If you get the chance, stop by for a visit you'll be very happy you did.

Even though we had stayed at the hotel before, this was our first time at Spinners.  We had heard great things about it from other hotel guests and also my parents and my brother who had been on past trips.

We went for lunch because we wanted to make sure we would get a full rotation during daylight.  I think this place gets really busy just before sunset, but at lunchtime on a weekday it wasn't busy at all.  

The food we had was pretty good.  We got the caprese cocktail for an appetizer.  It was much more than two people could eat when expecting more food, but we enjoyed it.  For my main course I got the Italian panini, it was very good.  I forget what the hubby ordered, but it was some kind of seafood dish and I remember that he liked it.

We really loved the views you can see from up there.  And they have signs on every other pillar naming various cities and their distance from you.  That was fun and interesting.  

I would recommend this restaurant to anyone in the area for a unique experience and one of a kind views.

Absolutely horrible experience. There was a long wait for the food, the server was aloof and uncaring, but the worst part of all was due to some "technical problem," it took nearly an hour to get the check. Management was completely inept and uncaring. The right thing to do in that situation would be to comp the meal, but no. Just a lame apology--no discount, no free dessert or drink, no compensation at all for the huge inconvenience caused by them and them alone. Definitely won't be returning to this location. They obviously weren't ready to open and need a completely new management team who know how to fix mistakes and make things right.

This is the BEST place ever! Super helpful and nice people. The atmosphere really enhances the workout. Not for one second did I feel out of place or embarrassed at my level of fitness. LOVE these people
Been in 3-4 times,  same attitude from different sales clerks, too rushed to be helpful, even if no one else is in the store. Won't go back.
So delicious! They were extra busy when we were visiting with a party of 6 plus a baby and everything was fabulous. Will definitely come back! Great food and attentive staff!

A hidden gem. We come here in the summer to sit on the big comfy rocking chairs looking out over a bucolic setting while we enjoy our delicious ice cream. It is a peaceful way to briing a lazy summer weekend to a close.

So I generally am not particularly critical of restaurants but today this place needs to get this review. My husband and I arrived and were placed on the wait list. No big deal. Some nice person gave us a sample of the deep dish pizza and we quickly changed our minds on what we were going to order. The pizza was no better than Pizza Hut. We were seated and ordered the artichoke dip and a flatbread pizza. The artichoke dip came out. Most of the chips were small pieces. Hardly suitable for dipping. The waitress did not bring us flatware or napkins. She seemed to disappear so we had to flag someone down to get those. The flat bread pizza was something kids could make. On grocery store flatbread and pre fab low cost ingredients.  To me the biggest disappointment was the level of food served for the price. Expected higher quality for the price. The appetizer was sub par at 9 bucks with bits of chips and the grocery store flatbread at 15 bucks was unimpressive. Probably won't go back because there are local non-chain restaurants that are far better.
My wife and I found his place on our last day in New Orleans on the other side bourbon street (Frenchmen area). The service was excellent and the food tasted good.

Philly's only Takoyaki source is great. So as long as you don't mind eating Japanese food cooked by a Japanese man because his wife is American like Jane, then you will love Maru. It's cheap and BYOB. Definitely try their dessert  balls

The food and drinks are very good but for the second time in a row our meals were cold. After the first incidence, I called the restaurant and spoke to the manager.  He told me he had other complaints and the main reason was the removal of their plate warmers. I asked the manager to bring my concerns to the owner - which he said he would.  We tried another dinner and again our plates and meals were cold.  There is no excuse for a restaurant to serve cold food!

We ate here on our last night in New Orleans. The dining room was very clean and our waitress was great--really friendly and attentive! We ordered the crab and artichoke dip as an appetizer, and I had the fried shrimp platter. The portions were very generous and the food was good good good--much better than we expected from a small diner-type establishment. It's also open quite a bit later than most of the other dine-in restaurants in the area. Very glad that we checked it out!

Came in to Whetstone to watch game and grab some dinner.  As always, I want to use a burger with bacon/cheese/fried egg as my yardstick.  They only have one burger on the menu, and it comes with cheese, slaw, pickles and onion.  Bartender seemed more interested in dancing around than waiting on customers, and took a bit of flagging to get her attention.  I ordered the burger, told the bartender to hold slaw/pickles/onion and asked if they could add bacon or fried egg.  The bartender told me that those are "brunch things" and "dont belong on a burger".  I still cant understand how restaurants refuse to figure out a way to overcharge me for an egg, but no bacon??  There should be a law against that.

Anyway, I just ordered the burger with "just the meat and cheese".  As expected, it came out with the slaw/onion/pickles, so I sent it back.  Bartender came back with the correct order a bit later, and it was really underwhelming.  Burger was small, served on a thin squished bun, and just looked pathetic.  It was cooked properly and tasted ok, but in a city with thousands of pubs, it was weak.

Stars off for bad service, no bacon, no egg, and underwhelming meal.  Wont be back.
Great selection! Ordered the happy herd which was more than enough for 5 people. Food was a bit cold but overall great taste! Will definitely be coming back.

The food deserved a higher rating, but it is in a strip mall next to a bar and grill
on Ulmerton Road. Not a place you would expect to spend $125-150 for two people without wine.

Atmosphere              85   A dime a dozen in NY, but A+ for Florida
Service                       85   Rearity in Florida- heads up professional service
Food                           75   On the low end of up scale creative cuisine
Value                          70    Expensive for where and what you get
  
It is a "miles away from Florida" venue. If you don't mind spending the money to experience a big city restaurant, it is great.. Unfortunately it is underappreciated from someone who comes from a big city like myself. Terrific change of pace venue.
Under all conditions, I would recommend it.

Amber Indian is worth the drive to suburban hell--it's by far the best Indian in the metro area! The weekend buffet was beautiful--spicy chaat, pickles, onion pakoras, nav rattan korma et al. Everything was intricately and uniquely spiced; I usually find saag paneer and channa masala somewhat pedestrian, but both dishes made my tongue dance at Amber Indian. It looked like there were a ton of options for meatitarians as well. This buffet rivals my faves on Devon Avenue in Chicago. It's a must-go for anyone who enjoys Indian. 

The only downside was they had two TeeVees playing some really inane-looking Tim Allen movie. Blech.

This place has the worst dining experience. Dining in has been the worst experience ive had. My husband and I came in, was seated immediately but waited nearly 30 minutes to be greeted by our waitress, which was after we flagged down a hostess for one. Once she came, we decided to order drinks, apps and dinner all at once due to the long wait time just for introductions. Well an hour later, we still hadnt received drinks or any of our food. Once our drinks arrived (FINALLY), so did our dinner (minus the appetizer ). The waitress said that our apps were given to another table and they had already started eating it. I honestly received better service at the bar while waiting for my table. I completely urge you to come here after you've eaten someplace else. Games are fun. Wish the gaming area was bigger but no complaints there. But def dont dine here. Take my word. U WILL be disappointed.

Well for living in Philly over a year and never stepping foot in the actual establishment is rare. Ive had some of their food at games and the airport but never actually had experienced the real chickie and petes. I finally got around to the place for the PSU vs. TEMPLE game. 

Definitely a hoppin place for sports. 

My room mate and I probably looked like the fattest little asian girls in the place, we had a serving of crab fries, 2 HUMONGOUS servings of the mussels with white sauce and a dozen wings..... we finished every last bit!  Crab fries are a MUST, and the mussels are pretty dayum good for a sports bar. Other than that I didn't get to try much, the wings were ehhh nothing to rave about. And sweet potato fries are not that great, the sauce they come with are wayyy too sweet.

The beer towers bring back lovely college memories at champs sports bar at penn state...... ahhh that was the life.
This has been the spot my wife and I visit when we are in Phila, BYOB great hookah and awesome Afghan food, perfect combo.

Pork roll burger is great (a tiny bit less salt would make it outstanding).
Duck fries are good but a thicker fry would make me happier.
Not even remotely a fan of chorizo or quack n cheese although my daughter is. 
The fried cauliflower is fantastic and i would go JUST for that. 
Gen tsos wings are just "ok" in my opinion. 
Chicken salad was fresh but sparse which was a little irksome as i love chicken salad.
Tomato soup is divine and I'm not a tomato soup kind of gal, so that's saying a lot.
The blue forager burger was ok, my socks stayed firmly in place. 
There are so many more things to try and i will return to see what makes my toes tap!
The atmosphere is homey, the noise level can be deafening so i look forward to them eventually expanding and with more space, less noise perhaps? The beats are decent, which helps!
I'd like to see a little more owner interaction.. These are local guys who really should try mingling a bit more with their customers, especially since they are getting lots of talk-up from their patrons. Just a thought.
Wait staff has always been efficient and courteous. The place gets high marks for cleanliness!
Food and service was great. I had the pork chop which was off the hook. Place is small but it was enough. I do highly recommend for a higher quality meal in Treasure Island. Ry the carpaccio.

This place is awesome! They have options for anyone with any dietary preference. My wife and I got the veggie burgers and they are super vegan friendly and everything is absolutely delicious. My mother got a turkey burger with cheese and she loved it! This place will definitely be our Sunday go to!

The $10 sampler was good.  Only one out of the five beers I didn't like.  It was the Country Roots Stout, which I am not a fan of stouts.  Others may live it.  I tried the Five Beer Burger and not the best I ever had.  They should not try do hard to come up with a different burger and just stick to the classic burger.  I just felt they were trying to hard to be different and it is not necessary.  Less is better in my opinion.  The BBQ chips were amazing.  Overall, I had a nice experience.  I would come back for the beer but not the food
.

Let's start with the service. NO ONE IS KNOWLEDGEABLE ON PRICES. You have to go to the register to find out how much anything cost. So your stop takes twice as long because you have to ask for every price. Which is probably why none of the staff is very welcoming.  Store is clean and many varieties to choose from. Expensive for they amount on some things so make sure you ask the price first.

Nice outdoor atmosphere, big problem with no way to call them. The website has no contact info, only hours open and closed. We wasted our time going there to find the kitchen was closed more than 2 hours before closing time.

My absolute favorite and only place for sushi. Everything is always fresh and delicious, well prepared and well presented.  Their teriyaki is quiet good and made from real steak, not scraps like you often find. The service is great and we are always greeted by the staff and sushi chefs with geniune enthusiasm and warmth.

I don't get it!  I really don't get it!  I think I do not make enough money to get it!  I mean the food is really good, that's why I'm giving it three stars, but the value for the money isn't there.  I know I know you pay for ambiance and the name and the scene but I am just not at a point in my life where I feel like paying for all that nonsense.

This was an amazing experience. I called over to them after a quick Google search because I was having lower back pain. Carol was wonderful on the phone. Pleasant, helpful, and caring. She got me an appointment and even gave me location tips in the event I was lost since I would be a new patient. The office itself is very clean, professional, and inviting. Now to Dr. Parnagian. This experience was nothing short of wonderful. He took the time to ask questions, examine me, and do some quick evaluations (which were super informative). He then showed me on the model and in the diagrams what the issue was and how it was causing me pain. They why and the how....Not just oh you're nerve is pinched.... How, why, and even showed me real time what the problem effected in other parts of my body. He was personable, professional, and just easy to talk to and listen to. I was so impressed at that point I was happy. Then, he adjusted me. When I tell you how amazing I felt after I can't even explain the difference. It was mind blowing. So happy I chose this practice and this Dr. Honestly- go, you'll see for yourself.
Great lunch spot! Love the salad bar! It's loaded with fresh veggies, an assortment of proteins, roasted tomatoes and roasted cauliflower! Friendly staff and he food never disappoints.

As a Nashville native I am always looking for new place to eat. This morning we decided to give this place a try. Upon getting there we found out the wait would be over an hour. We all decided that we would wait it out because we knew the food would be good, at least that's what we heard.After about 45 minutes we decided we were going to try to get a drink from the bar inside. After ordering a drink at the bar we were told to drink would be brought to us outside. Several minutes later a nice gentleman brought our drinks out  and reassured us that we would be seated as soon as possible.After about an hour the owner came out let us know what was available and what was not available. She was very upfront and very pleasurable to deal with as she said please come on in there are plenty of things great and left it on the menu. 

 I had the Germantown and it was excellent. It has  been a long time since I've enjoyed a such a great meal , yet alone breakfast . The eggs and salmon were delicious as was the fruit and OJ. Brave the wait the food is well worth it .
great brunch i ordered melts chicken sandwich and stuffed French toast. And it stacked me full for sure. Second time for Sabrina's, will come again

I ordered takeout. All three dishes were devoured with much gusto; the pork chops were tender and flavorful, the Chicken Under the Brick was delicious, and I enjoyed the shrimp and crab cakes and roasted vegetables. The portions were just right. I had just enough room left over for the delicious tiramisu. 

I just had a few issues with the take-out; there was only one fork and knife packed for three entrees (the remaining two had to eat with spoons), no napkins, and my drink that I got to go was so full that it spilled on my walk to the car. 

Basically, the food was great; the take-out service, not so much. I'll definitely visit again for a sit-down meal.

First time and last time going to this car wash was very disappointed car was streaky vacuum was really bad did not clean interior dashboard for the $26 price tag I think this is way overpriced not happy at all I would not recommend anyone they're it could have been a bad day but this was an extremely bad first impression
We have been living in this area for ~ 10 years. This is the best pizza. The white pizza is fabulous and the knot rolls are addicting.

First time to NOLA.  Very good soul/creole food.  Good home-cookin.   

Everything was good.  Most memorable was the Gumbo.  The best gumbo hands down I have had in NOLA.

The bbq was tasty! I had ribs, potato salad, mac and cheese, cornbread, and a sweet tea. I really enjoyed the spicy sauce on the table. I wasn't a huge fan of the cornbread. I was expecting it to be more cake like than crumbly. The meals here are affordable. I'd come here again.
No frills taqueria that hand makes their tortillas. It's a great little place with delicious tacos, but my favorite is the enchilada

I have been going to this salon for several years now. I always get an eyebrow wax done as it is very affordable (Only $6!) and they always do a great job. If you don't ask for a specific person then whoever is available will help you, but this doesn't usually bother me as they pretty much always do a good job. It doesn't look like much from the outside, but I don't trust any other salon in the Philadelphia/Upper Darby area to do my eyebrows anymore. FYI they accept cash only but there is an instore atm. I would also try to avoid going on a Saturday because it can get pretty busy and the wait will be longer.

The blue duck is one of my favorite brunch places in the area. You can't ever go wrong with the blue duck fries. Their stocks french toast is very good and they always have something on special that I end up getting. I highly recommend The Blue Duck for brunch

Reviewing the after hours tour, which I went on last night. In a word, EXCELLENT! I was kinda thinking it would be a little cheesy, maybe so-so food, but this was truly entertaining AND educational. The actors do their parts very well (Thomas Jefferson has been at it for 15 years) and the after hours access to Independence Hall is totally worth it. To think, you can experience the place where democracy and independence was debated into the wee hours the beginning of July, 1776. You meet by City Tavern and then go to dinner there. I had the turkey pot pie which was good and creamy but I probably wouldn't order again because the turkey was cubed rather than how it comes off the bone. Tasty though! The bread they serve is really good, from authentic period recipes, but the little sweet potato discs (?) were delicious. I also had the Thomas Jefferson Ave which was good too. The apple and pear cobbler was not a win for me. Others liked it though. Off we went to the state house, aka Independence Hall, where the keeper met us at the door. We got to see Jefferson, Adams, and Franklin debate parts of the Declaration of Independence! The stair banister is original to the building so just imagine who has touched it. Amazing. Just a great night. I thought the price was way high but it's not for this experience. If you're on the fence, just do it.

Arthur, Kymist, Jennifer, and everyone else at Knockouts what an amazing time we had at your Restaurant Sports Bar and Grill. Customer service that you provide is second to none. Very rarely do I visit an establishment and made to feel like I am part of your family. Your Liquid Marijuana Rum and Coniac Cocktail was amazing. Your Chicken Fingers and Fries Great food. And Homemade Ranch just Brought all the food and Drinks together.   
Thank You for a Wonderful time. See You again soon!
Been here twice. Really pleased both times. Chicken and dumplings are on par with the best out there. Mac and cheese are killer too!

we go here about 4-5 times a month~!
my family's new favorite restaurant~
considering that it's a chinese style buffet, i'm very impressed with the sushi, maki, and sashimi selection, and on top of that the price for dinner is only $11.99!!

I have to say, they have recently gotten rid of some of my favorite hot food dishes, such as fried oysters, and baked scallops, which were amazing!! and also their hibachi is not as good as it used to be, but all in all, for what you are paying, i honestly cannot find a better deal!

they seem to be trying to cut costs here and there, but I hope they don't cut too much, because I love coming here..

also surprisingly, compared to all other Chinese buffets, the staff here speak pretty good English, which is hard to find at other restaurants, which is a huge plus!

Overall, it is very clean, and well kept, and I hope they bring back my baked scallops!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

I really enjoyed bringing my son for a master/young master father/son haircut. Julie is very personable and always did a great job.
Unfortunately, when I began to set up a new appointment, the price for our haircuts was $20 more than last month. I called to see if there was a mistake on the website and learned that they increased prices on March 1.
While I really like Julie and highly recommend her, I can't justify paying  $74 for simple haircuts for a man and a 9 year old boy.
(And a 37% price increase seems a bit much. I'm just glad I noticed before I showed up and had the haircut.)
I do wish Julie and the crew the best of luck.

such a cute little coffee shop! Free wi-fi, can order lunch and a tea and get plenty of work done! I LOVE their tomato carrot bisque, if you've never tried it, you're missing out. I'm not a fan of coffee, but they have SO many delicious hot tea options, which many coffee shops are lacking. Definitely check this place out!
Great service and great food! Definitely recommend the hikers Benedict! Great salads for lunch
Really enjoyed the blacked shrimp and fried oysters.  Great service, even in the middle of rush hour.

I've frequented this restaurant plenty of times in the last decade,  business and pleasure (including a delightful birthday dinner once) but recently I was so turned off by the service that I decided it was worth this post and further that they don't deserve my patronage. 

I came with a friend,  physically a little rough around the edges, but great.  Upon arrival we were guided to a table where the contacts from there forward were limited to the initial order and the bill.  My straw never came, there was no beverage refills and my order was very incorrect (how do you confuse fish with shrimp). Directly across from us were 4 white middle aged women dining and they of course had full attention of our young server. It was very disturbing and upon exiting I planned to tell the host but he didn't bother sticking his head out of the diner. Unacceptable.

Stopped by at 6:45am on a Friday morning. Super cozy, hip, and friendly coffee shop with very high quality pours. I thought the pour-over was quite inexpensive and one of the best I've had. Bustling in the morning, this would be a great spot to congregate with friends or just relax and read a book with a great brew. So glad I live close by!

Came here after the auto show and were pretty shocked to see that it wasn't busy at all. Especially for lunch time on Super Bowl Sunday. We came in with our kids and thankfully they placed us away from everyone. The food was great, the kids enjoyed it and our waitress was very good. I would go back again in a heart beat. Also, one serving of fries fed our family of 4. We took home our pizza and fries, since we couldn't finish it all.

Ordered carryout this evening.  Place looked hopping between Xmas and New Years.  Love their bar-b-q- sauce.  This time I just wanted my own order of nachos with pork BBQ.  They use home made potato chips.  Really good!

I feel bad having to do this, but I have to downgrade my review again.

I was there Saturday. It is still a deal for $8 and I still like the food overall. However, the quality of some of the food is slipping. Here are some of the foods that I think they need to improve:

Corn on the cob: Bland taste, butter and salt didn't help
Sweet and Sour chicken: Breading burnt 
Pizza: Horrible
Fries: Burnt
Pot stickers: Bland
Hibachi beef: Do much hard pieces of fat
Sushi: Getting worse, bland, too much rice
Deserts: Just horrible, don't know how else to explain it.
Fruit: Must be old, no taste

In addition, they seem to stop serving my favorite desert, the eggcups. I have been there several times in the last month for takeout and dining for lunch and dinner and they have not been around.

In addition, my biggest complaint is the service. The service has gone way downhill. Usually, they bring you some fortune cookies and a tip tray. After not seeing the server for 15 minutes after we finished eating, I left my smallest tip ever ($2, I usually leave $4 or $5) and walked out.

In addition, I don't believe the staff should eat in the main dining area. My family was in the restaurant business ( I spent several years as a busboy, waiter, cook, etc),  so I don't think I am 'better' than they are. However, when my server is eating lunch two tables over and giggling with friends, should I have to have dirty dishes build up on my table? Should the hostess have to run across the restaurant from the dining area because people are coming in? When you work in a restaurant, you should eat where people can't see you.  A customer should not have to feel that they are imposing on the staff to serve them. That's the way that business works.

I am afraid this place is starting to go downhill as the other Chinese buffets have gone in the area. My wife and I usually go there three or four times a month, and do take out every couple of weeks. We decided to take a long break from this place, and try some other places. I still like the place, so I am hoping they read these reviews. I don't want to have to come back and give a two star review.
Food was awful!!!!! Tarter sauce tasted old, gumbo tasted like air freshener, and even the salad was horrible, payed way too much for food that was uneatable, very bad experience

LOVED this restaurant.  I saw this cute little patio area randomly while I was grabbing bagels next door one morning and made a mental note to stop there the next time I went out.  My date and I went there tonight and were extremely pleased.  The wait staff was wonderful.  They were sweet and polite and very responsive to our needs.  My date had eggplant park and said it was delicious.  I had a grilled chicken Caesar Insalata and it was also great.  The chicken was grilled perfectly.  The desserts were AWESOME!  Since it was our first time at this place, were were given the Dolce Patricia for free and let me tell you, it was incredible!! I highly suggest it.  We also tried the Tiramisu and it was excellent.

Attractive atmosphere, great wait staff and good food..I will return to this restaurant again!

La Creperie Cafe was very cute with tasty food. We had a really sweet waitress who was attentive even though she was the only person working the dining room. The menu had a variety of salads, crepes, quiches, and paella so you can be sure everyone will find something to eat. Be sure to leave some room for dessert! The dessert crepes are definitely big enough to split between two people. Try the Napoleon. You'll thank me.

Remember: This is a BYOB so don't get caught without a good bouteille de vin!

Just plain delicious food! Chinese chicken salad and gluten free Thai pizza was the best ever. Fresh and tasty. A bit spendy for a 12" GF pizza (24$), however worth it because of cravings for normal food! I would recommend this eatery to anyone. Friendly, effecient servers. We will definitely go again.

I'm a frequent gulliftys diner, both before and after the renovation.  I loved it before but it really is an awesome gem now.  The new menu items like the duck and fig flatbread are soooooo good!  

Great beer selection and great dunks overall!

Awesome authentic Indian buffet in Carmel.  The lamb and marsala are particularly tasty.  Pricing is amazing - $$ indeed.  I have been searching for a decent Indian buffet since moving from California, this place is it.

The ACE was a Dream! We staycationed here for Christmas this year and it was just what we hoped it would be. The staff was excellent, the room was cozy and everything from the food (Josephine Estelle and Seaworthy) to the pool was top notch. Chris at the bar really made our evenings. I've always loved staying at the Portland ACE and this was even better! We will be back!
Try the tacos al pastor ($15). A bit expensive, but it's 4 tacos with pico de Gallo, green salsa and beans... very tasty!

Beds are super firm..hard to get comfortable at night. It's a good thing the pillows are soft. Rooms are nice and clean but hot as BLAZES. They don't even air condition the hallways even in August...our air conditioning unit was broken in our room and they let us struggle with having to fix it for 5 days before they finally switched our rooms to one with a fully functioning AC. Breakfast is okay...don't get the scrambled eggs. Idk what those were but they definitely weren't eggs. Just stick to bagels. I really do like the location of this hotel though, very close to a wide variety of shopping and dining options, from casual to very nice. Update- went to stay at a nearby motel 6 and had further annoyances about this place considering the price difference... First off, motel 6 manages to have food and drink vending machines and a microwave...Hampton Inn has a solitary drink vending machine and no microwave. Get it together, Hampton inn, these things are important.
this place is awesome they have good food in restaurants and good gambling. prices are fair on everything and good service.

I may be from Philly, but I'm not sure I can discern between good and bad water ice. Is there even really a difference? Regardless, Johns is tasty, its close to where I live, and its a cool scene (really seems like the whole neighborhood is there some nights). They don't have many flavors, but the no-frill atmosphere is part of the appeal. If you're in the hood on a hot day, worth a stop fo sho.

The owner is very bad in service. She was not only rude but just didn't have any manners to run the business.

The food in general was just OK but I will never come again because of the service and rudeness.
