I go here for the iced coffee. Probably the best in town, great flavor and smooth!! Also their deli has some delicious food and oven fired pizza. Their deserts are also amazing. I wouldn't ever buy groceries here as they are EXTREMELY overpriced and owned by the same company as Albertsons and food city, so I'll go there. The staff is always friendly and they have great catering dishes.

Only ordered there pizza but man is it salty and just plain. Lack of toppings doesn't help but the crust is cardboard. New to area I just want a good pizza but for anyone new to Port Richey area there's no such thing. 
Not good tasting and not good visually.

Nannie Franco's is great for slices and I like their garlic knots.
That's all I have ever eaten there and it's been good. A slice for a dollar, sodas too. 
I gave it four stars because it's inexpensive, the slices are HUGE and it's pretty good quality for the price. It's nice to have a slice place so close to home with a nice outside eating area.
I see a lot of delivery reviews that are negative, but I have never had anyone be rude to me there. Mayeb if I try something else, I'll change my tune, but I am really happy with Nannie Francos.

First and foremost, i am a new baby vegan and have not consumed egg or dairy or meat for almost 4 months now. I know, I know, tell me about it. 

I dined here on 07/29/17 and asked my server about vegan options. She went over the menu with me and I decided on a bowl of the Mediterranean Vegetable Bow tie pasta, sans pesto and cheese. It arrived, it was hot and delicious and filling, I paid and left. 

A couple hours later, I ended up feeling quite sick, and I wondered if it could have been something to do with my food, so I googled the nutritional information and found out that my pasta had egg in the pasta noodles, and dairy in the sauce. My waitress lied to me. 

I know it's not that big of a deal, I'm not allergic or anything, but come on. If you're not sure about the menu, ask. 

If I could give 0 stars I would. Never again BPS.
Best music on Broadway, in my opinion -- you won't hear classic rock covers here!
Previews with audio only?!! Asked management and they said no video on previews . Will turn on video when actual movie starts. WOW

Late meal after flight, steak tips app cooked a perfect medium rare, spinach dip app was average.

2nd meal was lunch today, slow service (not at all busy), but the "Club Shula" was a good sandwich.

3rd meal dinner tonight, very noisy, slow service (not too busy), didn't get the soup I ordered, but a very good steak cooked perfectly.

Oh how I miss Buddy's Grill...I used to go here once or twice a month while in college and it was for one thing and one thing only:

Chicken Sonoma

This is the end-all-be-all of chicken dishes!!  Thinly pounded chicken breast that's lightly breaded and then topped with asparagus and mozzarella and some sort of madeira-like sauce.  OH DELISH.

The coffee is alright, not the best I've had but it's inexpensive and does the trick. Their cookies on the other hand are great! Order one with your coffee. The have an array of sweet baked good, I didn't try them but they looked quite yummy.

The shop is small but nice with a few tables to hang out/work from. The people working there were very nice. The store front is easy to miss since but it's on the corner of an industrial building.

My rating is mostly for the coffee but the cookie/service rating is more like 4 stars. If you're in the area this is a good option.
One star is significantly more than they deserve. Incredibly poor service repeatedly.

Best Asian Chicken Salad in Reno. Yum. The other salads are good too. The Gorgonzola dressing is made in house. It is to die for. You can buy some to take home and we have. Yes. It is that good. 

We like the creative pizzas with unique toppings. Don't come here expecting the same pizza you can get at a chain pizza joint. The food here is fresh, delicious and anything but boring. The service is consistently friendly and attentive.

The drawbacks are minor. I'm not a big fan of the TVs they have somewhat recently installed around the dining area. It detracts from the atmosphere and adds to the noise level. There is a Bully's in the same mall so if I wanted TV and noise I would go there. I wish they would bring back the whimsical murals and remove the big screens.

Over all though, we love this place.

Came here for the first time today, 12/4/2015, for a Brazilian wax after a few painful experiences at International Salon. My overall experience here was great with Valerie! 

I called in an hour prior to make my appointment, and I was pleased to learn that residents of the tri-state receive 50% off their first appointment. The receptionist did warn me that if I arrived more than 9 minutes late for my scheduled appointment, they would have to cancel, so leave yourself some travel and parking time! 

When I arrived for my appointment, I was welcomed and checked in by one of the receptionists. She wasn't sweet or offputting, but I wish she would have given me the salon info packet to read while I waited. I had to fetch one for myself. 

This is where I learned about their specials! There is a student special for brazilian waxes (buy 3 get 1 free for $141). Yes!!! 

I only waited for a couple of minutes until my tech, Valerie, came to welcome me for my appointment. She was very down to earth and sweet. She's been waxing for 6 years! Unlike some techs, she told me how much I needed to undress, so it saved me from that awkward guessing game! She also explained all of the products she used on me and we had a nice conversation. Their salon's purple hard wax was very also very smooth and GENTLE! The temperature was perfect and she was very clean. No double dipping! I was out in less than fifteen minutes. 

When I was done, Valerie instantly picked up one of their salon's products and put it at the checkout counter. I was a little off put as I was put on the spot, but I didn't mind because I was interested in the product anyways. The wax itself was $23 for the first time, but it's normally $47. Despite a few things, it was a great experience! I already booked my next appointment with Valerie!! Hope this helps you! =)
We sat in the bar area which had a nice casual vibe .  Service was good and the ribs were delicious.

Its definitely not my favorite starr restaurant, but it has a few good options for the price point. I go to Jones when I'm nearby, but I dont usually travel into the city to eat there. The food quality used to be much better years ago, but it has really fallen off lately.

If you want to pay for parking ($150/mo for two cars, first-come first serve) and then have your car towed consistently because the people running the company don't know how log resident vehicles with a license plate.... Then, yeah - great place to live.

Crab fries + mussels + crabs + ice cold Yuengling + 300 million TVs and projectors to watch any sports programming available = one of my versions of heaven. 

Get here early though, if it's a big game night it gets packed pretty quick and you'll be waiting an hour for a seat.
This place was absolutely disgusting and sucks that we have to wait here for our bus. The bus was 20 mins late and we couldn't even sit because the whole place was disgusting.

I always hear about Federal Donuts and how you have to get there early. 10am on a Saturday morning, this location was completely empty. 

The doughnuts are nothing special in my opinion. The texture was good but it's just your typical cake doughnut that can be made at home if you had a fryer. They were all the same doughnuts with just a different sugar or glaze over it. I tried four flavors but none of them particularly stood out to me.

The fried chicken wasn't bad. It was moist, but again, nothing to rave about. It comes with a honey doughnut but I didn't get any pickles as stated on the online menu. I had a dry topping and a wet topping. For the dry topping, I chose the coconut curry. It tasted more like regular curry powder dusted over the chicken with no hints of coconut.  And the chicken 

Coming from New York, I think everything was pretty reasonably priced.

Was excited for wings but the service was horrible. Cristal was our server didn't ask if we were okay or if the food was good, playing with her hair, talking to other waitress' in the back. Didn't make any suggestions for the wings or if they came dry or in sauce and they are known for their wings. Terrible service I don't expect the world but being a server it would be nice to check on your tables at least there was a table before us that said the same thing waiting all day for their check and they actually had to take theirs up her to pay it so they could leave. I wouldn't give this one star if I could. Not from Reno and def will not be back.

I don't get the fuss. My etoufee was pretty good, but mac & cheese had little flavor, fried pickles were all batter. My friend loved the collard greens, I thought they were ok. If you want classic southern fried food, there's got to be a better place. Service was lacking too. :(

Went here yesterday for lunch, and had the Duck Fries which was phenomenal and I'm reminded how delicious it was from the first time I had it. I also got the General Tsaos Chicken Taco, which was good but just a little over fried. Nonetheless, blue duck is overall amazing and I love coming here.

Our large group ate on the deck near the water. Another place with good atmosphere.  Our service was good and had live entertainment.  We had the gator bites which tastes like chicken just as it is claimed.  The Cuban was above average but a large sandwich.  It could have used a mustard or dressing to compliment the pressed sandwich.  I used the spicy dipping sauce that came with the gator bites.  The star of the evening was the carribean chicken.  Juicy and full of flavor and complimented with delicious mango salsa.

Sure, it's more expensive than Lowes but what do you expect from a small store in the Rittenhouse Square area, they have to cover their ground rent/mortgage.

I've always found the staff here to be extremely helpful.  When I walk in there and suggest some kind of ridiculous fix I am trying to do, they politely suggest different ways of doing things.  They do it without seeming condescending as well, which is quite a talent (especially considering how stupid my ideas generally are).  I don't think I would have the self control not to call me an idiot.

Lowes and Home Depot will always end up being my stop if I am doing something major but whenever I need little bits and pieces, it's Rittenhouse Hardware every time.
The wings were good. The loaded baked potato fries weren't. The fries were soft and the bacon was bland. I will go back for the wings, but next time I'll try the tots.

I preface this by saying I came in at 9:30pm which is 30 min before the place closed.  I had ordered the Jambalaya for 15.95.  It was very poor and tasted bad.  On the wall was a Zagat sign from 2004.  My guess is they have been slipping for past 12 years.

I think this place is over priced and unless you have a coupon, it's not worth it.

Most times we call for take out and most often put on hold.  We use to try and get delivery, but the delivery time they quote is always outrageous so we now opt just for pick up.  

Last time I ordered a salad, which they put in the bag with other hot food.  I certainly didn't want to pay for an already overpriced side salad that was wilted and warm because it was sitting with the hot food.  

The hot wings were unimpressive, small and not worth the $$$ we paid.

The  bright spot is the pizza.  They have interesting toppings and I do love the BBT.

For the money I think there's better places to go.

Really interesting and tasty pizza, but either they don't have enough help OR they have the wrong person on the phone. We needed delivery as we are sick and we live 10 minutes away. The woman on the phone said, "I'm not sure if I am supposed to take this order", so my husband got irritated and told her he would come and pick the pizza up. She hung up on him. When he called back, he simply placed an order and she took it. We will think twice about ordering here again. I must emphasize that the pizza is good, but nothing is good enough to put up with a grumpy rude order taker. Employees need training!

The food was cold and disgusting.  The waitress was rude. We were supposed to get a glass of wine but we didn't.  The worse experience I ever had.  I would not recommend this tour even if it was free.

This was the worst dining experience of our entire trip to New Orleans.  The place is in a great location and looks fine from the outside, but the food quality is just NOT good.  Maybe it's because we had such great meals everywhere else while we were in Nola, but every single part of our meal was a disappointment.  The service was ok... a bit slow, and not terribly friendly.  I had a PoBoy which was just... lousy, and my wife doesn't even remember what she has (not because we had been drinking; he hadn't.  Just because it was terribly forgettable.)  

There are many other FAR-BETTER places to eat while you're in Nola, so please take your money there instead.  If you are considering Cafe Pontabla, INSTEAD, go down the street to 1109 Decatur and eat at Coop's Place, which is a much better restaurant with great food and great atmosphere!

This will likely become a 5-star review when I have a taste of my 2nd, 3rd or ... well, we can stop counting after that, right?

I tried their Stout (Mastadon Stout brewed for the ISM/Yelp Paleo Party) and loved it. It wasn't as 'cofee' as I expected. Instead, it brought a lot to the party (no pun intended). I could tell they put a lot of thought and heart into the brew. 

I think we have a winner. Bring on the next pint!

Ninki has all the intentions of trying to serve Japanese food.  However, it falls short of that, amongst other things.

While its location is what brought me here, it is not AT ALL what you expect to see when you walk into a Japanese restaurant of any sort.  It looks like a late 80's, early 90's bachelor pad.  Kind of like black laquer threw up on Japan.  No bueno.

The service was less than decent, considering it was a fair 20 minutes between visits from our waitress, who was extremely hyper and difficult to understand because of it.  Needless to say, we finally chose our Hibachi bowls (shrimp for me, steak for JTR), and then waited our "alotted" 20 minutes for the food.  Did I mention we watched a table of four (who ordered before us) get their food 5-10 minutes before us?

The food smelled great.  I was extremely excited at the look of other people's plates, but when ours arrived, my hopes immediately dropped.  The veggies were covered in butter and oil, and my shrimp was sitting very lonely on the other side of the plate by itself.  A small heap of arugala made a nice wall between the veggies and the shrimp.  The steak looked fairly appetizing, but the plate was also pretty sparse and lonely looking.  At least serve this stuff in smaller plates so it doesn't look so pathetic!  JTRs fried rice tasted like a stick of melted butter.  Luckily, I found the silver lining on the menu: steamed rice.

Recap?  I won't be back.  Proverb of the day: A Japanese restaurant's ability to not screw up a bowl of steamed rice is no reason to return.

I went here with some coworkers while visiting Philly for business. It was pretty empty when we first arrived but could have been due to the weather. I had the chicken kabob with samosas. The samosas were pretty decent, had a nice flavor with the cilantro sauce. The chicken kabob came with rice and a side salad with a ranch dressing. I am personally not a fan of ranch but I guess this is the only option for salad dressing. The entree came with bread that was very good. As for the kabob, I would recommend asking for spicy. It gave it an added flavor that really made the chicken taste better without being overly hot. The rice was a bit dry like others have mentioned but they do add a tiny bit of sauce too. The kabob could have had more onions and peppers. I only got two small pieces for each kabob. Overall, if you are hungry and have no other choices give it a try.

Priced us $500 dollars less than they ended up charging and blamed us for the misunderstanding. Horrible communication and service. You can tell they get commissioned for work they bring in because they pointed out every minute flaw in hopes you would let him fix it. Will not return.

They moved out to Catalina and Tanque Verde. Eaten many times- from burger, broiled fish, to steak.
Consistently delicious ribeye! Fish (broiled) is delicious and the beer battered sandwich is great- a lot of food. Sides are good, except side salad is iceberg and tiny. My hubby loves the pineapple coleslaw!
Fair/ good service- in that department they have slipped some.
Prices are good for amount of food and comparable with other steak places. It's good for a family or friends dinner out.
Lunch specials are good.

If I could give this place a negative 10 I would. Im not writing a review just to write a review so ill make this short. The food was completely disgusting. I went with my wife after hearing somewhat good reviews and decided to go. Wow was I surprised.  1) the food is way way way to expensive for the taste. 2) I ordered the short rib that was dry, bland, and honestly uneatable and oh btw it costs $34. 3) Wife ordered the salmon that was under cooked and bland. Way to much salt. 4) the waitress was rude and not knowledgeable about the menu. I am shocked that this place has remained open for this long. Save your money and buy the ingredients from public, watch youtube recipes and make a better meal then what cafe pontes  is offering. I think the chef should consider a career change

I heard really good things about their Mac Mart truck that goes around. So I went to the restaurant when it opened up to try their Mac and Cheese. I got their grilled chicken pesto Mac and Cheese and I was actually pretty disappointed. I don't think I will be coming back here again.

Tasty burgers. I had the NOLA burger topped with 2 fried green tomatoes. First bite burnt the S*#@ out of my mouth though. Literally blistered. They need a disclaimer on that particular one, similar to what you see on McDonald's coffee cups. I mentioned it to the server... no apology, just "oh it must have come right out of the fryer". Gee, ya think so! lol. It was good, and I would go back, but will definitely do a finger test of the temp before diving in.

Really good food. We ordered the appetizer for four which would feed 8. Don't order the large one I'm afraid how big it would be. We then had stuffed crab with greens and Mac and cheese. Food was very good. Service was excellent

You should go to Guo Bin for one main reason: the POTSTICKERS. 

Seriously, Guo Bin's potstickers have set the standard against which I judge all other Chinese restaurant's. Their potstickers are definitely authentic and homemade and their strength can carry the entire restaurant.

Ask for vinegar, not soy sauce, to dip them in (or at least mix vinegar into your soy sauce). Why? It's what us Asians do with potstickers; the acid really brings out their flavor. 

Potstickers aside, I also love the friendly staff and atmosphere. The hostess/owner would ask me why I don't come by more often and the waiter would always try to give me life advice.

(I've moved out of STL 6 months ago. I wonder if they still remember me...)

What a gem of North Redington Beach a great hole in the wall with awesome pricing foods. You must try the Shubee Shack crab cakes and the crab balls, very yummilicious. 

Place your order at the little window and relax on one of the benches while Chef Pat makes your meal fresh and hot!!!
I had the crab balls -nicely crisp on the outside and crabby and soft on the inside. A fresh, delicious beachy experience.

Pros: professional staff, clean building, great atmosphere, I never feel pressured to buy anything. I love the use of essential oils and bottle of water at the end! Prices are fair. 

Cons: while the visits are extremely relaxing, I still have knots in my shoulders when I leave. My chiropractor has recommended that I find a new place to get massages, but it's so hard to find one that makes you feel comfortable! I've made it a point to ask at the beginning of the appointment for focus on my knots, but they're always still there after.

I agree with others who rated Citizens Bank one star. I had a bad experience recently when I tried to close my Dad's account there after he died. Since my name was on the account, I called one of their representatives and she told me what to do to close the account. Unfortunately, she didn't give me all the information I needed to know, and I ended up with over $200 in additional fees owed. A lady called me from their Recovery Department, and even though I explained what happened, in the midst of my grief, she said I should have known about things pertaining to my Dad's account because my name was on it. What made matters worse, my cousin referred me to his branch, and I spoke with the Branch Manager, Tamera James, and she said she'd help me by talking to the appropriate bank representative on my behalf. After our first conversation, she didn't return my phone calls. I resolved this matter today on my own. Avoid Citizens Bank if you can go to another one that is more customer service oriented.
Excellent food, don't miss it. The meat is perfectly cook and the desserts are out of this world. Highly recommend it.

The food is very good. The service is a little lacking. The atmosphere is definitely very homey as it is in an old house. I was not very happy paying $4.00 for three small glasses of ice tea. It is absurd to have to pay for refills on fountain drinks. I am sure they charge for seconds of rice as they only brought me about a cup of white rice with my entree. I feel that I should be told there there is going to be additional charges that are not specified on the menu.
Ill give a 1 star because the beer was cold other than that NADA! Tweekers everywhere rude dealers horrible food and pit bosses that need a real attitude check! Won't return.

Mm yea GC is SUCKING these days. How complicated is it to make lo mein properly if you've been cooking it all day every day for... decades? Lo mein here has become consistently greasy & has a lovely burnt flavor. It's so oily that I leave the last few mouthfuls so I don't have a heart attack on the spot. The food here overall is overpriced and crappy. The one thing I order [shrimp w/broccoli] is really hard to F up, but eating with other folks has shown me how hit or miss GC is. For some reason it always takes 10 light years to get the food when I order in store for sit-down service. That gives me homicidal thoughts that I can't shake for days.

This place isn't even trying anymore. They know they don't have to because an entire neighborhood that's flush with cash thinks GC is the only viable option for Chinese. See all the reviews post 2008? They ain't lyin. Forget MY first review & get your Chinese somewhere else!

This is my first time at a Sprout Chain. This store reminds me of a blend of Trader Joe's and Fresh market. There's a great deli section (something TJ lacks) and ample veggies. Plus prices are far better than Fresh Market and Whole Check... I mean, Whole foods lol.

Avocados were 3/$1, strawberries were $1 and they had lots of organic options. I tried a few of the samples that were being given out and everything was really good. There are not a lot of cash registers so I do see long waits possibly being a problem in the future.

Not impressed with Uber so far, signed up for the first time, had 2 different coupons and the app didn't give me a chance to use either, I'm like everyone else and always wanting to save money, I choose Uber over Lyft to use the coupon to save money, and didn't get to, also was given the driver name and then 2 mins later the driver name switched, not sure if this is normal or not.

I took a chance on this salon because it had a couple of good reviews.  The person I worked with was Jill, who is new.  At first, she was really good as far as haircuts.  However, she has a lot of "issues" that limit her availability and focus.  I was happy with her work up until the last haircut in April 2012.  So, I brought in a picture, let her know what I needed and she immediately got loud and argumentative and made a scene.  It actually was rather shocking to see her reaction, however, somehow it calmed down and she finally cut my hair.  Again, it wasn't what I asked for, so I contacted her the next day and asked for a refund.  She played a lot of games about that and when I contacted the owner Christie, her response was basically, "She'll refund you, don't come in our salon again."  So, if this sounds like the type of "service" you'd like, Ciao is the place for you.  For me, it's Ciao, Ciao!

Came in on a Wednesday for 2-for-1. Not a lot of variety of beer which is why I gave them 4 stars. 

They had a live band which was over by the time we arrived about and hour before closing.

Parking can be difficult if your coming on a busier night. Other than that i will definitely be coming again. Great beers!!!!

Really nice, friendly little spot with amazingly cheap, large breakfasts. And they deliver? Sign me up for more!

Great grits.

I got the homejoy groupon when i was shopping around housecleaning service.  My first experience wasn't great.  The housecleaner forgot to vacuum my livingroom and left some counters dirty.  I emailed Homejoy and they rebooked a cleaning for me immediately with a very good cleaner.  Their customer service is great and their website is easy to use.  I have had 3 other cleanings since my first experience and every single one has been wonderful.  I am very pleased with this service.

I was initially skeptical when this restaurant came in--it replaced a sandwich place that was not stellar but was dependable and it seemed like they were going for some sort of hipster soul food concept which I found suspect. However I would say Wishbone was an improvement. The chicken is really good in all of its forms--chicken and waffle, chicken sandwiches, and just plain chicken. The pies are really good too and I'm a big fan of the mac and cheese. And they have exceptionally fast delivery--averages about 20 min or less rather than the hour or more that seems standard for University City. They dont package everything in neat boxes but just sort of wrap it and put it in a plastic bag so if presentation is important to you, just know that. The portion sizes are probably on the small side but I personally found everything very filling.

For the most part, this is standard fare BBQ. To be completely honest, there's nothing special at all about the actual BBQ. It's not bad, it's not great, it just is. The sides are where Edley's can shine. The mac'n'cheese is usually always on point, and the cornbread and butter is excellent. For those complaining about parking, parking always sucks around 12S. This is not an Edley's specific issue. Decent bar, decent beers, not amazing, but adequate for BBQ. We'll go again, I'm sure, but if you want an outstanding pork sandwich, there are better places.

I am kind of disappointed with this place. I took my pup to petsmart in Smyrna to be groomed and just had a horrible experience, so I wanted to find a new place for her to get bathed. I called her and was told they aren't taking new clients, because of some reason that made no sense. I was asked if I wanted to be added to a several month long waiting list. Why would I. I feel like I was calling a Dr. Office.

We found ourselves in Santa Barbara UCSB graduation weekend, yikes! Anyway we were looking for a place to eat and found Chase on Yelp. So we headed out. A bit of an odd experience. We go there and parked in back which was easy and great. Came in, the host asked us if we wanted a table, we said yes for two, he said just a minute and then never came back. He walked by us several times and we looked at him like have our table. Before he sorted it out, we sat at the bar. The bartender was nice and made a great lemon drop. The menu was tough for me as a vegetarian so I sufficed with salad and a bread. My b/f had chicken piccata which is their specialty and he thought it was just average. It is a cute little place. Maybe the menu will suit you better.
I love Ariana - and always order the same thing. Chalaw kadu. I wish I could have some now and am wondering how I can make my own!

I didn't want to complain publicaly because that's rude. But the service here was terrible. I asked for my burger to be cooked to medium and they served it to me charred, almost burnt.  My burger was to come with a certain sauce and when I asked to switch it, the server told me there would be an up-charge.  I was charged for lettuce and tomato, but the server didn't make mention of that. The server was so busy adding all the extra charges to my bill, he forgot to actually refill waters, bring my margarita and even bring silverware to the table. The server didn't even serve the food (there was a food runner) and the server didn't come to make sure the order was correct, which it wasn't. Note: It was two hours to close, the place was empty, my order was wrong and the service was terrible. 

  I was with four other people. No one particularly enjoyed their food. 

  This resturant won't last. But, if it does, don't visit unless you enjoy disappointment.

Service was terrible, two people walked in after us but were seated before us. By the time we were seated we got to watch everyone else around us order and get their food and then finish it. I mean scrambled eggs does not take 45 mins to make. During this long wait we only saw our waiter 2 times. This place is just not worth the wait.

Ok, so I used to love Smashburger, but now I avoid it.  I went there about three months ago.  The morning after eating there I felt so weak and nauseous.  I had to cancel going into work.  I'm assuming it was food poisoning/ a bad batch of ground meat.  I have felt fine since then.

Really good pizza (really thin) but the rest of the menu is pretty average fair. They make a giant pizza you have to measure your front door to see if it will fit but most people only eat about a slice of it.

This is the walmart  closest to my house so I seem to always go there by default however, I might be finding a new one. The past few times I've been the meat selection hasn't been that great and last night the deli counter! whew! it took us 25 minutes to finish our order. The deli person was super slow had to ask her co worker a question about every single thing we asked for and oh before she even took our order we stood and watched her put away the meats from the other customer verrryyy slowly. It was super aggravating and ill tell you one thing, if we DO go back to this walmart for groceries we are definitely  skipping the lunch meat counter

This restaurant is awful and the prices to match it are dreadful. Don't be fooled, because the restaurant looks beautiful from the outside. Unfortunately the sushi is not up to par with other places such as Mikado and Kobe.

My family and I went over a show at the Walnut (5 minute walk which was awesome) and was recommended by two friends to try it out. Honestly thought the price was a little expensive for what you got, especially my meal of a Turkey Pot Pie which was $19 and that's all you got, no soup or salad or side. Don't get me wrong, everything that we had tasted really good, but for comfort food it wasn't comforting when I got the check. Definitely recommend the Chicken Parm, Mac & Cheese and if you don't mind the price the Turkey or Chicken Pot Pie (depending on what they have). Also I highly recommend getting a booth on the mezzanine over looking the whole restaurant, it was nice to be a little secluded from everyone else and had that sense of VIP. Only problem is that sometimes a server won't be right there when you need one and will have to wait for them to come back upstairs.  For an overall experience and the fact that it was a Stephen Starr restaurant it was good, just thought it could have been a little more for the price I paid.

Yumioca is nestled in the same plaza as LETS Grill, Tropika and Syphay. I've been here one time previously but never realized that they're owned by the same people as Tropika. 

Both times that I've come it's been dead. We were the only ones there on a recent Monday evening. The inside is quite nice and several different areas to lounge around. They have a couple shelves of board games but some aren't in the best shape. Saw connect 4, monopoly, scrabble and jenga (the Asian coloured version). Selection isn't the greatest. 

Only one worker was manning the front. They were out of lychee pieces (huh?) and only had enough tapioca pearls for half a drink. I know it was near the end of the night but that's still pretty ghetto. 

- Vanilla bubble tea ($4.95) - L and Jboy shared a vanilla BBT. Tasted like vanilla ice cream in drink format. A little too sweet for my taste buds. 
- Mango slush ($5.15) - went with mango bear pieces. Slush was pretty good except  several sips had bits of ice that hadn't been crushed enough. 

Decent enough BBT here but I wouldn't be making a special trip out for it.

This is a place you could easily pass right by, without realizing what is tucked away on the side of the road. The studio grounds and chapel are all beautiful, nestled up against a backdrop of the Catalina mountains and all those stately saguaro cacti. You can't take a bad photo here. 
Visiting the little gallery/museum is very interesting. For anyone who has ever lived in Tucson, Ted DeGrazia is a legend so getting to see his work and discover more about his life is very interesting. Everything is very well done and maintained. My favorite part is that beautiful little chapel where you can go and just sit and reflect. Unfortunately, I read that the chapel was recently badly damaged by a fire. Here's hoping they're able to restore this charming little gem of the desert.

At first glance you see a small, log-cabinesque structure as you drive down main street in Imperial. Then it hits you what it is with the large sign in front of it and the cars in the lot. I cannot say enough about this place, it usually has super fresh veggies at a reasonable cost along with other items that you can use with the veggies and fruits! Fresh breads too, along with milk and dairy items! The owners are helpful as well as those who work there as well.

This place had a long line, and we went on a Monday! Worth the wait, though. While there was parking behind the building we had to circle a few times bc it was still so busy!

While I don't have any other hot chicken to compare this to, I was impressed and found everything on my plate to be delicious. The brisket tacos were also very yummy! The sides weren't anything special.

After we ate we walked around this neighborhood, which was really fun.

After years of hype, I've finally tried Federal Donuts! They're good as donuts go--not greasy, kind of cake-y but not dense.

We got one of each flavor and shared them as a group back at home. Without knowing which donut was which, it was honestly hard to tell what flavors they were on the ones dipped in flavored sugars. The flavors weren't terribly strong. I liked the chocolate donut best.

Next visit, I look forward to trying their chicken.
Work was fine but far more expensive than expected. Jeez.

My first visit to this business even though I have passed it for years, and after I got over the $26.99 shock, I was excited to be getting what I expected to be a great wash. My car wasn't very dirty to begin with and the interior was slightly dusty as it was washed a few weeks ago. After I left, I went to the grocery store and when I got out of my car, I looked on the passenger side and was surprised and disappointed at how streaked it was on both doors. Front bumper was sad looking also. I have to say I couldn't believe I had just gotten a car wash, except the interior looked nice (one of the stars). I attempted to call when I got back into the car to explain that I would like to come back another day, but got the answering machine. I'll try and call tomorrow, but only as a courtesy call because I doubt that I even want to go back. The employees were very nice -- thus the other star. Don't waste your money unless you are getting an oil change, then I think it might be a decent price if you don't expect much.

You can't beat the prices at Aldi. You just can't. The normal amount you spend at other stores will get you twice as much here! I always find new items or brands that my family actually prefers over national brands! Their special buys section is awesome because I almost always find something that I need or had on my wish list for some time. 

This store is my usual store even though another one is probably closer to me. I love the staff at this store. They are always friendly and helpful. If you're a regular like I am, they will definitely recognize a familiar and friendly face! The store is always clean and well organized. Sometimes the Special Buys area can get a little out of hand, but that's what happens when you have so much good stuff, you run out of places to put it! 

Aldi is actually a German-owned company so every year they feature German week with goodies actually brought in from Germany! I don't know that much about Germany, but I know they make some good stuff! Their produce section has a really good selection, but just be aware that you won't find everything that other stores carry (such as kale or other exotic items), but very good for the basics. 

Tips for any first timers: bring your own bags (you can also purchase plastic/paper if needed), bring a quarter for cart rental (which you get back), and off you go! No coupons needed! They only take debit cards or cash so make sure any cards you are carrying includes at least one debit!
Worst property I've ever lived at. Lots of rude inconsiderate neighbors. Poor management and extremely overpriced. Do not waste time or money here.

I'm sure this place may be good when they have food but....no ribs, no turkey, brisket is served only until 3...hipster attitude from servers. If I could give zero stars I would. How do you run out of everything at 7 pm on a Saturday?!!!

My mom and I came here for breakfast. I had eggs, toast, bacon and toast she had an omelette. We were both pleased with our meals. We haven't found many other places in New Orleans where we could eat for under $10 each. The coffee wasn't very good and they forgot our water but I would definitely come back.

Fantastic thin crust pizza with great toppings. I recommend the eggplant and ricotta, while friends of mine swoon over the tomato pie (either because it is really fantastic or really garlicky, hard to say!). It really is Italian pizza; I believe the owner is from Naples. My favorite pizza joint in Bucks County.

Loaded up on carbs here last night at their stand at Spruce St. Harbor Park.

Crab fries + cheese sauce = yum. Not really much else to say there, other than you should be warned that they're really hard to stop eating.

We also decided to try their crab-seasoned kettle corn, which was about 5X larger than we had anticipated (if only someone had gotten a picture of my face when she handed me the bag, haha) - that thing is MASSIVE! About the size of a jumbo bag of chips. But they're good! Love that sweet + savory. Just be careful of the staples when you open the bag!

The new management are doing a decent job there. Greens are good and withstood the hurricane very well. Love the final four golf holes with the water of Tampa Bay as a factor and a great view. The clubhouse was nice as well. People that work here were nice. I would play here again!

My boyfriend and I have been coming to this location almost everyday for a year. We order the same thing every time and normally they manage to get it right. In the last two weeks this location has gone drastically down hill! 

Last Sunday, I waited in the drive thru line for what seemed like forever. Once I got up to the speaker I placed my order and was given my Total. When I finally made it to the Window, they asked what I ordered and told me that it looked like my order hadn't been entered. I gave them my order two more times then paid. When I was handed my Medium Iced Coffee (black) and Medium Iced Coffee with Milk and Carmel Swirl, it was very clear one of the drinks was incorrect. When I mentioned this to the girl in the window she proceeded to raise her voice at me and tell me the drink was in fact what I ordered. The Manager finally comes to the window to see what was happening. I calmly told him that I ordered a Medium Iced Coffee with Milk and Carmel Swirl and the drink I received was incorrect. The girl in the window began yelling that I had ordered an Iced Carmel Mocha. The Manager did remake my drink, but neither him or the girl in window apologized for yelling at me and telling me I didn't know what I ordered.

Not ready to give up on this place, I went back this morning to get the same two drinks. Again they handed us a Carmel Mocha, and argued with my boyfriend when he said the drink was incorrect. He tried the drink that their request and when he insisted it was incorrect they continued to say it was in fact what he ordered. We eventually left with the incorrect drink because we were tired of arguing and needed to get to work.

This location needs to get it's act together and learn some Customer Service or it will permanently lose our business.

Dont go here to gamble! Dealers are not friendly or nice and you will never get a drink! They have one cocktail waitress for the whole casino seems like! Awful experience will wait for new casino to open or go to the nugget or downtown!

Waited 20 minutes at the bar just to get a drink and gave up and left. The place was a mad house and clearly don't have their stuff together.  Curious to see if this place makes it because they have serious management issues.

Aloof and unappreciative of this customer- we bought a custom cake (a bit over $300) and they wouldn't let us taste any flavors (they were all "unavailable" ) We got vanilla and chocolate because who is going to take a chance on lavender goat cheese when it isn't available to try?  They didn't give us any of those flavors to try either, but a chocolate cupcake was available for purchase (why thank you!) the vanilla we took a chance on (it's vanilla). They also kept telling me that most everything would be "over our budget". Idk a budget of $300 for a cake for about 25 people doesn't seem cheap ‍-  (granted we aren't Beyoncé's crew!) Also, our event was early December and the manager didn't return my correspondence promptly, and when she did get back to me, her excuse was that they were so busy with thanksgiving pies (I feel valued!) We decided to go here because of reviews, their cupcake wars win, and to support a new(ish) Philly bakery, and, by the time we figured out that it wasn't panning out to be a good experience it was too late to change bakeries. But, if I could do it all over again, I would find a place that appreciated us, our money, time and occasion; hope this helps you do the same- unless you're Beyoncé then you're all good ;)

if I could give this place zero stars, I would. Stay far far away!!

So, one lovely night a couple of weeks ago, I was on my way home from Trader Joe's when my alternator crapped out in the parking lot. Fortuitously Dobbs just happened in the same lot. Feeling pretty lucky about the situation, we pushed the car into a spot at Dobbs. Given a choice, I would have preferred to take the car to the dealer, but I had no opinion whatsoever about Dobbs and so convenience won out. 

A few days (and lots of money) later, I get the call that the alternator has been replaced and the car is ready. It's fixed. I'm happy. Then oddly the check engine light comes on later that day. I make a mental note to go back to Dobbs the next day. Since the car is working well and they told me it was all better, I kinda ignore the light and decide to go out that night. Big mistake. As I am on Forest Park Parkway my radio suddenly dies. Then the lights dim. Then the headlights go out entirely. I am driving on FFP at 10:30 pm with NO lights (interior or exterior). Scariest experience of my life.

Get the car towed back to Dobbs since they have a warranty. Dobbs takes about 2 hours to tell me they can't find anything wrong. Bullshit. The fact that car was towed there means something is definitely wrong. So, I finally say 'screw this' and have the car towed to the dealership, which I should have done in the first place. 

Turns out, Dobbs screwed up my alternator installation which melted my battery fuse box. The dealer had it towed back to Dobbs and they replaced the battery and the alternator (again.)

Ok, fine, maybe the one star could account for the fact that they did make it right without charging me extra. But still. Horrible experience.
If you like middle eastern food, this is somewhat similar. My favorite was the lamb dish, there baklava was great too! Support their business!

First time at Sabrina's, I don't believe it was worth all the hype. The breakfast menu wasn't big enough for someone who was a picky eater. Wound up getting a bagel with cream cheese and some bacon, bacon was delicious. My date got the featured stiffen Challah French Toast. When it came out, I was jealous. But he was over it after a couple bites, too much stuffing. Over all experience was good, may be back, may not. Didn't really win me over. Presentation of the food was a 10/10.

I called to ask for a quote and received a really aggressive response that they couldn't give me a quote. Even upon describing the services required, how big the place is, etc. he couldn't provide a quote. Then had the gall to ask me to name a price. Basically starting a negotiation, of which I had no idea what he was actually going to clean

The call was disrespectful and unprofessional, that left me little faith that this company could provide quality services. Save yourself some time and don't even bother asking for a quote.

I was more than a little hesitant to try Teavana after reading reviews of pushy salespeople. But praise be to Yelp- they must've learned their lesson because I was here two days in a row with two completely different staff and while there was a lot of explaining/demoing there was no pressure. In fact today a wonderful salesperson dissuaded me from buying the overly expensive starter kit and rather get me a tea I liked,  a tin, & tumbler on sale! (Please note this starter setup was HALF the cost of the actual kit)

I was thoroughly impressed with the staff who were able to guess exactly which tea I wanted after I blankly starred at them and said "I dunno I think it's green tea based with fruit..." Even more impressive was the reassurance that you will know if you will like the tea simply by smell, I didn't believe it but totally true.

Cool note: Teavana was purchased by Starbucks last year so those Starbucks gift cards work here and if you have a registered card you can accrue stars here as well!

Easy location, decent rooms (though very warm), tasty breakfast, and the woman in charge of the breakfast was delightful. 

Where they really shone though was when my daughter left her stuffed owl behind. We called and they didn't have it, but said they'd look for it. The day later we got a call that someone had found it in the parking lot and they'd mail it to us. I was dreading the condition of the owl after four days of rain in a parking lot, but when the package arrived they'd obviously washed and dried it before mailing. So sweet.

In a word, disappointing. Comfort food concept well designed but execution did not live up to the hype. Had skirt steak. Small portion for the price and not very tender. Turkey pot pie seemed well received by one of our party but brisket was not a hit. Service was swift at least so we got out quick. Won't be back.

I'm so excited that I don't have to drive into Philly to eat at Sabrina's. The food and servers were great. I think they have gotten better than Green Eggs. The food is always consistent. They picked a great location along many other shopping areas.

They put me in a pedicure chair and I sat there for 45 minutes in cold water while they kept starting in other customers. I finally got up and said forget it and they were like "oh you are next" but didn't seem to care I had already been sitting there for almost an hour.

I always like going to Chase. The food is pretty good (not great, but consistently pretty good), but my wife really likes the atmosphere here so we end up going here quite frequently. If you're wondering about the atomosphere, it's got the whole red booth thing going and 1000s of twinkle lights hanging from all areas of the restaurant. It's a romantic spot for the over 35 crowd. Full bar. Service is hit and miss. Sometimes when we go it's great, other times, it's terrible, but it's a unique looking local restaruant that's pretty good. We've been many times and will continue to go on a regular basis.

This location is close to our home. We don't go very often but have been there maybe a dozen times. 
The inside is clean, the tables are clean and the workers are always nice and respectful. 

The food is pretty good but their blizzards and moolatas are soooo very good!  Trey are always freshly made and always exactly what we order. 

It's a great stop on a hot day to cool down. The Orange Julius is so refreshing any time of the day.

The wings are to die for. 
Artisan pizza is tasty, but small for the price.
Our waitress was kind but did not really seem to know what she was talking about when it came to knowing about the food itself.

Came here for lunch with a large group on a Saturday. They sat us quickly in the dining section. There's a separate entrance to the right for dining in. The place was much bigger than I realized!
I ordered an adult beverage, some sort of alcoholic strawberry lemonade. Delicious! I got the Vito panini: prosciutto, mozeralla, tomato. It was so good! Everyone's food looked good. No complaints. They have some interesting menu choices for pizza. I'd like to go back for the pizza.
The decor is fun. Murals on the walls and ceilings combining local and Italian history. Great neighborhood!
It felt a little crowded, but it could have been because they pushed tables together for out 10+ group.

My husband and I had dinner at Mango once before.  This time we took our daughter.  

The plantain chips and green salsa were delish!  We devoured two baskets and ordered some for take-out.

My daughter ordered squid and loved it.  Since it's not in the normal form of fried calamari, I was surprised she liked it.  

My husband had the flank steak which could have been a little more tender but he still liked it.

I had the chicken skewers with the hot dipping sauce which was very good.  It came with large kernel corn and fried sweet potatoes which were also good.
Nice little coffee and pastry shop. Pretty busy little place run by a sole proprietor. Food was good.

I took the wife out last night for dinner. We stood up front waiting to be seated. Once we got seated they took our drink order the wife decided that she would get a Malibu bay breeze which was made well. We ordered an Appetizer combo of chicken fingers Mozzarella sticks and potato skins. The potato skins were horrible and you could tell we're heated in the microwave. We never got the bbq sauce that we asked for with the chicken. When our entree came out my chicken was terrible. The wife's steak was terrible and had a ton of fat on it. We did get desert and my wife said that the cheesecake was incredible and the mile high Boston cream pie was great. All and all the floor was dirty and the food was terrible minus desert.

Unaccustomed to free parking at a hotel, I was impressed that they offered it. Even more impressive is the lobby. It's funky, clean and modern, yet it's still somehow inviting and warm. The rooms are fairly standard with nice touches like pretty reliable wi-fi, robes, tea, and a mini fridge with complimentary water.

I do have a couple of questions. One, what's with the bathroom door? Perhaps it's is just the room I stayed in, but the last thing I want to do is to wake up staring at the toilet. Yes, closing the pocket door to the loo will take care of that, but come on, that didn't come up at all during the design phase? And two - perhaps I'm tech challenged (having never owned a satellite TV) but does the "high tech" remote need to be more frustrating than setting the time on a 1990 VCR? Heed my warning: if you channel surf to high or too low you might wind up on a "games" station - exit - nowhere! Or you'll be in music central - again with no exit in sight. I just want to watch Law and Order gosh darn it ;p

Next time I'm going to work my darnedest to get a room on a higher level. In a flat town, the last thing you want to be looking at is someone else's roof.

I went to Edley's on a double date and everyone was extremely satisfied with their meal. We split fried pickles and thought that they were very tasty, with both good texture and flavor. 

I ended up having the catfish sandwich and it was great! The texture of the catfish was perfect. The pieces were relatively thick, but they were cooked through with a crisp exterior and a juicy interior. The barbecue sauce was good--not overly sweet and had a little bit of kick. 

We really like the relaxed vibe and the open air setting. It was perfect for a May afternoon and we hope to return in the future.

I mean, what can you say. The place is a kitchy nasty chain. It's so plastic, its like... Las Vegas plastic. The menu is meager, but the shakes aren't bad. I just can't validate going into this place when you're on South Street, with TONS of choices around.

Came here with a group of friends for a birthday dinner celebration.  They were VERY accommodating with our group and super friendly.

We started off at the bar while we waited for the rest of the group to arrive.  I was disappointed in the cocktail menu and I like when restaurants have a variety of unique cocktails with several different liquor bases.  I had the Mint-Basil Lemontini which was so TART I couldn't even finish it.  I watered it down twice with ice cubes to even get part of the drink down during dinner.

Our server was AWESOME.  Was very patient and attentive with our large group and very knowledgeable of the menu and what The Block had to offer.

For appetizers the group that I was sitting near shared the Fried Brussels Sprouts, which were awesome, and the Ale & Cheddar with pretzels, nothing too special in my mind.  When it came to dinner I wasn't really in the mood to eat a huge slab of meat, didn't want a salad and I'm not a fan of fish, so there was limited choices for me . I ended up with just a burger.  Wasn't wowed by it but it was definitely thick and juicy.  Came with a side of garlic fries that had a hint of garlic but wasn't over powering.  Everyone at the table seemed very satisfied with what was put in front of them.

I think if I ever return to the Block, I will be trying the Webster Groves location.  I hear they have a wider variety of menu items, both food and beverages.

Bleh. Big disappointment.

I've been wanting to come here for so long only to be so disappointed. The food was ridiculously overpriced, and the dining experience is extremely long (We probably got there around 7, and didn't get out til almost 11). There's just so much wait time that it's not really worth it.

For our first course we ordered two cheeses that comes with bread, apples, and a small portion of vegetables for dipping. The fiesta cheese was pretty good, however, stay away from the swiss. It's horrible. We tried to keep a good amount of cheese left to dip our entrees in, only to find out, we weren't allowed to. The cheeses had to be cleared for the entrees to come in. So that disappointed us too.

So then our entrees come, and to our surprise every thing was raw and you had to cook it in the broth and oil. It's like hot pot, but not very good. We didn't like that we had to cook ourselves because that only meant we'd have to stay in the restaurant longer. I ordered an individual entree, the seafood trio for $24. It came with tuna, shrimp, and scallops. It looks like a small portion but it's filling after the cheese and salad that comes with it. By the way, it was the most amazing salad I've ever had. I ordered the House salad and the dressing was beyond perfect. 

So anyway, I stuck with the broth because I didn't want to cook my food in the oil. It just didn't look good to me. They give you two kinds of batter, sesame and tempura, which can only be dipped in the oil so I didn't try it. They also give you a variety of dipping sauces - plum, some kind of yogurt, and another which was kind of like cocktail sauce. They also serve broccoli, mushrooms, and potatoes, which go really well with the butter cheese spread. 

We were definitely not leaving until after we had some dessert. We ordered the smores dip, which was flambé'd. It came with a variety of things - strawberries, bananas, rice crispies, cheesecake, little brownies that were so good, and marshmallows. The smallest pot was more than enough for 6 people, so I recommend sticking to one pot to save money. Same goes for the cheese.

All in all, I won't be going back there again unless we're strictly getting dessert only. Go if you have a lot of time and money, otherwise, it's not as great as people say it is.

I ordered the brisket meal to go.  The lean brisket portion was very dry and the fatty portion was just full of fat, there wasn't a happy medium.  The brisket did have good flavoring from the sauce.  The mac & cheese didn't disappoint.  The corn bread was not what I expected, a bit dry but enjoyed the spiciness.

The staff at the Redhound Grille was super friendly and accommodating our request to sit outside. The service at the bar while we waited for our table was quick and friendly. Once we got to the table it was apparent they were running a little behind. Despite the delays, the food was great! I had the BBQ pork sandwich and a my wife had the blackened chicken wrap. Seemed like the place wasn't staffed for the surge of people coming to listen to the live music because by the time we left another couple was leaving the restaurant because no one had been by their table in the 20 they had been seated. Maybe we lucked out, maybe benefited from a good server, who knows? Sometimes a smart server can make all the difference. Since this was our first visit, I can say that it was good enough to go back and try something else off their menu.

Great little spot for brunch.  I stopped here on my last trip to Philadelphia before heading out in the morning, and I have to say the food was good, the drinks were good (as I always have to have my Bloody Mary's with any brunch), and the place was absolutely adorable.  I believe it was once an old bank that has now been renovated into a retro 60's style restaurant that reminds me of something out of the Brady Bunch, but a little cooler.  If you're in the city, why not.

Dirty and gross. Stay clear. This was a friends choice for Sunday brunch. We got the last table for 4 tucked behind the ATM. The drinks were served in small plastic cups. I ordered the Cajun combo. The gumbo was ok, but way too much white rice and served with an empty crab claw. The jambalaya was awful rice with bland tomato
Sauce and no added meat etc. the only thing I liked was the crawfish pie as it had good creamy flavor with extra topping smothered on top.

Excellent. These guys work their BUTTS off. I got the Royal Full Service wash and was not disappointed. The guy who worked on my car event spent extra time trying to buff out stains on my hood because of fallen sap that never went away, he did great. 

I would recommend this place if you dont have anywhere to go in a hurry, they seem to take their time, but only because it is SO busy. 

I will come back whenever my car needs a deep clean, otherwise I'll go somewhere a bit quicker and cheaper.
Came over from Philly to get an oil change and a free car wash. This is my 4th time coming over here try to get it done. Not to crowded today.

Coming from Philly and living 2 minutes away from this Chickies location, theres no other place that makes you feel so close with my fellow philadelphia sports fans. There are a MILLION TVs, so anywhere you sit, you'll have a great view of one of the TVs. The place is pretty huge. the crab fries are obviously a must when you go. I have enjoyed just about everything I have ordered here including the calamari, cheesesteak, mussels marinara, wings, etc. A bar that actually plays the games' on the speakers instead of music, then when a commercial hits during the games...cue the music! They have weekly specials for beers and on big game days.

A need to understand customer service. Very disappointed with the lack of understanding. Traveling down from NY to take my Mother to a musical at the Media Theater. Picked Mom up at the nursing home. Looking to get a reservation at one of my Mom's favorite testaruants.  Unfortunately the person on the phone wouldn't accommodate a reservation for our party of 4. They only take reservations for large parties. Silly

Great view and spot but obviously struggling. Went years ago and loved it returned the other night and our food was less than mediocre. I work in the service industry and have been a waitress for 6 years and hate to complain. My moms fish was super fishy and the batter wasn't even cooked all the way. My brother and me both had average food but nothing great. The server and manager were super understanding and nice tho. The women's bathroom had no lock and no toilet paper holder, just a giant roll of toilet paper on the back of the toilet. We had to take turns with strangers watching the door so we wouldn't be walked in on. The manager told us he was hired in to get it back to the way it used to be. Sad such an awesome view and location is being run down.

It was double date day and my friends, my husband and I had spent the day sampling some wines in some of South Jersey's wineries, so needless to say come dinner time we needed some real food in our bellies other than cheese and crackers. Well the first place we thought of was booked up so we ended up here at West Side Gravy because it looked slow and I have wanted to try it out for awhile. The decor is somewhat cute with the Sailor Jerry type paintings of food, but besides that the place was pretty empty looking. It kinda looked like as if it had just opened and wasn't finished yet. But that didn't matter by the time we got our food. I have just four words to say: Truffle Oil French Fries. These were the most amazing fries I have ever eaten. I swear. I could eat them every day and never get sick of them. And the fried chicken that my friend's husband ordered was the best fried chicken ever, according to him. And he was serious. I got a steak and it was tasty, and everyone else seemed very pleased with their selections too (the other two had sandwiches I think). The prices weren't too bad and it's BYOB so that helps keep costs down. I definitely recommend trying this place out of you haven't yet, it's good for some great home style eats.

Food ok but expensive , nice atmosphere 
Very clean. Great server 
Giving it three stars as too prices for a quick breakfast and food is just ok

I had to revise my review with the new owners, and I think it is a step down (dropped one star).  The kimchee jigae (spicy tofu stew) was pretty similar, though there were noodles in it now.  The jaeyook kimchi bokum (stir fry with spicy pork and kimchi) was ok, but I was disappointed that they put this gigantic block of tofu under the meat, making it look like the plate was piled high when the portion was nowhere near as large as it used to be.  My favorite dish, the octopus rice bowl, is what has gone down hill the most.  

This place is ok now, used to be much better.  Service is still very good, but with the changes in the food I imagine that I will be spending more time at Sammy Chon's KTown BBQ (Korean BBQ just a couple miles down rt. 70).

still BYOB
Great place to grab a beer and some crab fries before the game.

I like crafty drinks and truffle popcorn with some cool lights above my head. In all seriousness you get to drink with one of the better views of downtown, and you're well taken care of. Both Will and Scott were on point at all times.  Thank you!

This place was recommended to us by a friend and oh my gosh was it terrible. First off when you come to a BBQ place your hoping to have a variety of BBQ items to choose from, not here. And to add insult to injury are their sides! Horrible!!! It was kinda expensive for the nothing you get. I got a brisket sandwich that I couldn't finish. It tasted absolutely disgusting, even smothered in their sauce. The staff was really friendly on a positive note.

Although the salesman was friendly enough, The rest of the buying experience was pretty awful.

I regret not walking out and going to another dealer. I was a cash buyer for a new suburban but wanted to take advantage of GM 0% financing and finance 100% of the car. Elco is my usual dealer but I figured I would deal with a place closer to my home... No problem there,, What struck me as low rent was the dealer add on painted pinstripe and door edge protector for about $400. Really? Dealers still do that? I said no way and they removed the charge.. Which actually meant I just did not get the truck  discounted as much as it could have been. I DOUBT they get those things done for free.. MAYBE $100 for both, their cost... I got a few discounts and got a reasonable trade in on my old truck.. The fact that the wholesaler who buys from them was all over it drooling, That tells  me it was probably worth more than I got.

I have excellent credit and easily qualified for the loan as requested. I went to sign the papers and was asked if I wanted an extended warranty, I said yes, ONLY if it was a GMEPP (Formerly GMPP ) plan that is a General Motors product that is essentially an extension of the warranty. I usually get 84 months 75000 miles as I tend to keep my trucks 15 or more years.
When I declined he seems a bit irritated i would not pay for an aftermarket warranty which, usually not worth the paper they are printed on.  I bought a GMEPP from a dealer in North Dakota who was highly recommended on the Corvette Forums, 0 deductible 84 months 75000 miles from General Motors.

I was kind of embarrassed for the sales job applicants that had to fill out applications at a table in the noisy show room and interview in the cubicle next to the one i was spending all day in.. I could hear every cringe worthy question like, " Tell me what your weaknesses are" ect. They did not have a freaking office they could use?

It took SIX HOURS to complete the transaction... The cleaning of the car took a couple of hours... And it was  a half-assed job,, Marks remained on the arm rest and I am still peeling protective plastic from the interior.   Not to mention a ding on drivers door that marked the " Door Edge Protection".

I usually deal with ELCO Cadillac and St Louis Porsche so, in all fairness, my expectations might be too high.  

I blame only myself.


Addendum.    I just found masking  tape marks and overspray on a brand new 69000 dollar vehicle.    Wow.

Oh I adore this place even though it reminds me of Walmart for alcohol. There is an AMAZING selection and unbeatable prices at wine and beyond. Every wine imaginable, they have - I was pleasantly surprised to find out that they carried some of my Okanagan favourites! Absolutely lovely! 

I think one of the best features this place has to offer is the wine tastings and samples of beer they offer! You can also buy large jugs of beer from their taps that are significantly cheaper and refillable - way to save the environment and my wallet wine and beyond! 

In my few trips to this place I've also found some lovely house decor - including a very LARGE wine glass that I intend to fill with wine corks! 

I could wander this store aimlessly for hours - it's heaven!

Food quality is good but the portions are small. The shrimp kabob "entree" contains six shrimp. That will not fill up a normal sized person. That dish costs 12 bucks with tax. Chicken sandwich is slightly better, but still leaves you hungry, and that's for 10 bucks. It's clean, healthy food, but could you give a little more food for those prices please? That being said, it's a good resource in the area and the chicken quesadilla is a hit with kids.

It's OK. The parking lot smells has an alluring smell of urine and a lot of smoke inside the hallways of the hotel rooms. The only thing that was really nice about the stay was the food at BREAKFAST food at Cafe Bellini inside the hotel. The prices were very comfortable. I don't think I would come back here again. The noise level wasn't too bad but the sheets were a bit questionable.

This review is only for their GIANT FORTUNE COOKIES:

I ordered a giant chocolate covered fortune cookie back in October 2013 because my fiance and I wanted a creative way to ask his uncle to marry us.  We thought, "What better way than to put it in a giant fortune cookie!!" I typed out a personalized message when I was ordering the cookie but had some issues with the format of the message. I contacted their customer service and Derek immediately addressed my concerns and helped me with my order!

We were so excited to give my fiance's uncle the fortune cookie a couple of days after it arrived! It came nicely packaged and the fortune cookie looked awesome! The message inside was also formatted exactly how I wanted it to look!

The only negative thing was that the fortune cookie WAS NOT EDIBLE. We tried eating it and it almost hurt our teeth trying to bite into it. Maybe I just got a bad cookie because most of the other reviewers can't stop talking about how amazing they tasted. It was slightly disappointing that we couldn't eat it but we all laughed about it and don't regret ordering the cookie!

5 stars for presentation, customer service, and fast shipping!
1 star for taste - chocolate was good but the cookie was as hard as a rock.

I would probably think twice before ordering another giant fortune cookie though.

I feel bad writing this review because our waiter was really nice. i was so impressed by the uniforms (which shirt, black slacks and ties. There might have even been hats)  The staff here is fresh to death! That being said the food left me thinking I wasnt tasting it right. it's home style cooking, gramma's recipes and what not. So maybe the day I was there gramma didnt add the magical zest that gramma's always have or she ran out of her secret ingredient that she never told your mom about so you would still wanna come to her house on Sundays. I'll try this place again tho. If everyone raves about the fried chicken and I got the off batch I'll try again till we get it right.

Looks so much better than it used to.  Kingsway was a cutting edge mall when it opened in the Medieval Times, but the dingy brick interiors needed a facelift and much more.  I love how they've brightened up the interior and modernized much of the mall.  There are plenty of stores and many of the more popular trendy clothing options are available to satisfy most tastes.  The food court is bright and open and is a welcoming place to rest your tired legs after a busy day of shopping.  In all honesty, I'd say that Southgate is nicer but Kingsway would probably be 2nd best in the city.  Parking is always a bitch on a busy weekend but which mall doesn't suffer from that problem.  

In a nutshell, if you haven't checked out the recent renovations at Kingsway then it's well worth a trip out that way.
Long lines and slightly disorganized in the distribution component.  Food was good, but not the best available at the public market by far as it was rather bland.

Great service. Beautiful lounge. Had the edimame which were delicious and the pobalo dumplings which were...interesting. don't know if I'd order them again but that could just be me. Great sunset view out the windows, friendly staff. Very nice

For a beer and app can't beat it. All kinds of promotions during the sports season. Food is ok not consistent preparations though. The service is indicative of most Florida food joints. Kids without a clue of what service is all about. Ps I had a fork straight from hell. No pun this thing was dangerous! I showed it to the manager (a clueless kid) she took it from me and never saw her again. No comp no apology.

Queso Fundido, spinach & mushroom enchilada, 2 mini chimichangas and chicken tinga taco. To drink was a mango and a peach margarita. 

Service, food, and drinks were great! Immediately seated, owner came to greet us. Our order was taken promptly and the food came very soon after.

Fun atmosphere during busy happy hour on a Wednesday.

I read a lot of reviews on here and decided to give it a try. The bar close to our house (Risky Business on Kolb/Sunrise) had my Steelers on but they don't turn up the volume on the any games. Even one would be nice to get the feel and excitement of the game.
This place has the vibe and feel we wanted! 
The breakfast burger was excellent! Their beers were cold and the service was fantastic! 
It was worth the drive. I'll be back!

Over the last year, service seems to be declining along with food quality (the menu is still great, but the preparation is no longer spot-on).  I no longer consider Brio competition for St. Louis's authentic American-Italian restaurants, but rather a nicer alternative to Macaroni Grill.  I'm rating 4 stars only in the hope they can re-earn the fifth I once granted.
Not good for delivery. Food was cold and soggy. I ordered the salmon bento and stir fry soft shell crab. Horrible. Threw most of it away. Now looking for something else for dinner.
This was the first place I went for restaurant week and oh my god the food was delicious the space within the restaurant was small but cute
Ann Marie in the bar area is wonderful. My son loves the cheese fries and mac and cheese. We enjoy steak and salads. The food is always consistently good.

Thanks to Groupon I tried out this service for the first time Friday.  I love the fact you can sign up online at any time and in this case I got a last minute appointment.  Tracy was a little late but I was overall pleased with how well and fast she cleaned.  Attention to detail is very important to me so it's hard to find someone that can do a good job dusting behind the tv and in the corner of the room.  Tracy did a good job w/o much guidance.  I plan on using the service again for the convenience with booking and I think with a little feedback, Tracy will be a good fit.

We came here by chance when a nearby restaurant was crowded. I would come
back again, although there were a few ways they fell short. Liked the comfortable atmosphere and fun menu. While the menu is heavy on meat, there are several nice salads, a fish special and vegetarian option. First, loved my glass of wine. The beet salad looked beautiful, but two of us agreed that it was too salty. Everyone enjoyed their main courses, but my friend's fish was undercooked and much smaller than the other fishes (mine was perfect). By the time she got a new piece, everyone else was done dinner and they did not offer to take it off or even a comp dessert. Speaking of dessert, the ice cream was amazing.

2 stars for good food that is cheap- but dirty and poor service.  AFTER eating nearly all of it, I asked for a to-go soup container and they gave me a used one that was dirty with a hair in it!!!  When I asked for a new one and showed them no sorry, nothing, just replaced it!  What the!  And then I noticed flies around our table.  Health inspectors might wanna check it out

Quaint romantic spot with awesome food and a decent wine selection.  Expect French Mediterranean tapas and entrees that are quite different from almost any other restaurant in the Bay area.  The creme brulee is also very good.  Highly recommended if you enjoy great food.  But don't expect a happening scene, awesome decor or a great bar because you won't find any of that here.

Stumbled on this little tea room while out for a Sunday morning bike ride.  We were not able to get in for tea as you needed a reservation for Sunday, but we did order coffee and some chocolates and enjoyed them on the outdoor seating.  I look forward to going back to enjoy the rest of what they have to offer.

Was in the mood for a burger to smash with my sisters. We were in the area already so decided to give this place some play. We had no idea of what to do. Stand in line or plant at a table. Walked to the counter greeted by very busy cashiers and servers. Decisions, decisions decisions! Isn't this the beauty of America? In summary, I chose a baja spicy chicken burger. It was average. My sister got something more up my alley. The mushroom melt chicken burger with sweet potato fries. My french fries were not dazzling. They were not better than a fast food spot. I won't blame my bad decisions on my review. I plan on going back to sit ouside again and obviously copy off my sister's order.

Yum yum really good breakfast challah French toast so tender and scrambled eggs simple but done right 
Server forgot butter and jam for toast but otherwise ok 
Will return for lumch

This is a great place for creole soul food in the Marigny, right near all the clubs on Frenchman. A friend we were with who lived here for a while said their fried chicken is among the best in the city. I can testify to their collard greens--excellent--and their jambalaya is hands the down the best I've ever had. I'm used to jambalaya being mostly about the rice, seasonings, and meat, but they pack theirs with about 10 different kinds of fresh vegetables that keeps it lighter and keeps the rice from drying it out.

Great time yesterday at the south Philadelphia location.  the hostesses seated us promptly and when I asked for a table rather than a booth they were very accommodating.  our waitress was very nice and the service is always fast.  Great place to go after a sporting event especially when your team wins!

i love the atmosphere here! they've got booths, high tables, televisions, a huge pizza oven and an overall good vibe. 

i was simple and ordered a garden salad and spaghettic with meatballs. i was actually carbing up for a race the next day and didn't want to over so it...i asked the waiter to please place half of the order in a to-go box which he gladly agreed to...so great!

my dad ordered the pesto chicken pasta which he swallowed up. mom got the chinese chicken salad which she loved. they don't serve garlic bread with pasta orders...something most pizza/pasta places do but i didn't mind.

recommend going here and seeing what they've got to offer!

Would've given 2 stars for the service (4 hours wait for new stickers), but I am giving 3 stars because of the kind service desk employee that waited on us.
