The only issue, at times, is the cashier service.  They can be standoffish, not very warm.  They need to do some customer service training, scripting, etc.  Other than that, everything is great.
Love the cheap margaritas.  Poncho Willis is the best eatery there.  Lots of choices, but the mexican is the best.  Great for locals, cheap night out.

I was torn about whether to give this place one or two stars. Our dining experience was terrible terrible terrible, but you can't beat the location or the open air set up. It really is quite lovely to sit, quietly watching   Jackson Square by that ever-present flickering lamp light. And the interior of the restaurant is lush and lovely and oozes history. It would have been much nicer, of course, to enjoy these things while consuming, say, drinks, or food. After a certain point, I might even have settled for the smallest acknowledgment from any employee.

Sadly, that was not in the cards for us. I'll spare you the details but the gist is that we sat, totally ignored by all the staff, for far longer than is acceptable. Once we were noticed, we waited far too long for drinks and food. The service was terrible terrible terrible. 

The food (we both had red beans, rice, and sausage) was fine, though there was something slightly funky about the parsley garnish. Nothing outstanding, but I wasn't expecting outstanding. Given the size, location, and menu, I was betting on some adequate, tourist grade food, which is exactly what we got. 

So, ultimately two stars for the location, ambiance, and the fact that the food is not terrible. But there is not a chance in hell that I would go back. You can find something better.

Met the owner, a very nice gentleman, the place was spotlessly clean and nicely appointed. Ordered Thai rolls, Spring rolls, and the Garlic/Black Pepper vegetables with beef for take out. Food prep was about 15 minutes, and everything was very good and freshly prepared. Both the spring and Thai rolls appeared to have been made fresh, not the frozen variety so many places use these days. I thought the prices were fair considering the freshness and the quality of the food. I definitely plan on returning to enjoy eating there at the restaurant.

This place is great! We opted for the egg and cheese crossants with a side of potatoes, but were super jealous of the pancake stacks we saw come out for others. Wish we had known before we ordered, they looked so good! Food and service was great. Make sure you get there early as after 10am there is a long wait. We tried to go back another day after 10am and the parking lot was full!

it was a first time visit for me. upon entering, the facade was impressive with the japanese art, wood and stone decor.  the night was getting late so the place was only half packed and customers appeared to be enjoying themselves  it looked to be a mid or higher tier establishment so i had high expectations. 

the 'banana boat' was what i ordered since it was my first visit and thought this would be a good way to get the 'lay of the land' and try a sample of what the place offered.  the 'boat' arrived in the form of a square plate (someone posted a pic above).  immediately, i was unimpressed.  are you kdding me?! the sushi on this plate looked like the type you find at chinese buffets!  the shrimp over the rice ball and spicy tuna roll on here is the standard line-up at all the buffets!  ok. took a bite and it tastes about as good as it looked, meh.

so, if you want to eat meh overpriced sushi then go here or drive a little into the plaza and there is a chinese buffet that will serve you the exact same thing, except you'll only pay about a third of what they charge here.

I came here looking for my wedding gown. The staff was friendly and helpful. Their selection was not huge, but they had some really beautiful dresses. The dressing room is nice and large. I did not get my dress here, but I did purchase a beautiful rhinestone headpiece that I loved. Overall, the prices were decent. Not David's Bridal cheap, but comparable to the average bridal shop.
Every once in awhile I have a craving for this place. It's an okay place. I like the sesame chicken and their egg rolls are great, definitely different than any other Chinese place in St. Louis.

This place is OK- 3.5 stars. We had a large group for brunch and the staff seemed really confused about seating us. We eventually ended up just taking over the restaurant (it was like 12 of us). We actually had a good 'ol time but that was partly because we had such a large group. 

The food was just OK- maybe 3 stars. It might have been better if the place wasn't so crowded. If we were in the neighborhood we might come back.

In my opinion, one of the BEST Indian restaurants in the entire Indianapolis area!  Their garlic naan alone is enough to keep you coming back and every single entrée I've tried has been nothing short of exceptional.  If you like to try a variety of dishes I highly recommend coming during the week for the lunch buffet.  It includes nearly all the traditional favorites including some desserts.

Had lunch with my family (2 adults and 2 kids). We both had pick 2 combos.  Food is good and has been very consistent over my 3-4 visits this year. Kids menu comes with a sundae which they love and my kids eat well here. Any plus for a parent.

Bill: 2 sodas, 2 pick 2 combos, 2 kids meals. $30.83 plus tip

Total tourist trap.  I had an oyster po-boy which was just - not very good. - I've had much better in other places.  My sister had the red beans and rice, which she was not too keen on.  They did make a good hefty hamburger which my niece gulped down, so I'm giving them up to 2 stars for that.  The food did come out quickly which was nice.  In general it seems that the place has survived based on its proximity to the Cathedral, thus attracting tourists, but the food just isn't that authentic.
Pretty Good Ethiopian food  far from Ethiopia. Great service super nice people
Hot donuts. Be careful. Its addictive. and yummy. I don't like the glazed ones though

The sandwiches are minuscule and have a ton of bread compared to sandwich ingredients! I had a chicken, zucchini (who puts zucchini on a sandwich anyhow). Anyhow, I ordered ir and this was a major disappointment. There was probably a couple tablespoons of chicken on 1/2 of what I ate. My husband had procuitto and hi was pretty much the same. 
  So I'd recommend ordering cafe and croissants 
here.

Meun Fan is nice because of its Goleta location. The food is average, but their major downside is their pricing. Highly overpriced for the portions and the Thai food market in general. Facility is clean and the staff is friendly.

Boyfriend and I ordered the Pacific pre fixe meal. The first course was not very pleasant - there was way too much kick in the sauce, but our server was very nice and was able to switch that out for their popular cheddar sauce instead.

The main entree was delicious. I would recommend their curry sauce with the meal. (The Fondue Fusion)

The best part was the dessert. We ordered the Banana's Foster (sp?) and that sauce was delicious, like... omg. Maybe it's because both boyfriend and I like white chocolate, but it was definitely very yummy.

Despite the fact that we came in at 7:45 and they closed at 9, aaaand we stayed way past that time, we didn't feel rushed at all to leave.

It was a lot of food for the both of us - maybe if we ever come back, we'll come back for the dessert.

I tried to like this place. They have good ideas, just very poorly executed. I've made reservations that were ignored. I've tried many menu items. Everything was edible but not much more than that. The food just doesn't make it worthwhile to sit in this strip mall for an hour to wait for a table. Best restaurant in the area? I guess but that's no big accomplishment. All done with this joint!

Met a friend for lunch.  Chips and salsa were fresh and good.  Each had a Margarita which was good as well.  I enjoyed the water pitcher with fresh limes or lemons.  I had a salad with some blackened chicken.  Was a nice meal, nice service.  There was not a wait for tables, so we were seated right away.  Can't remember the bill total, but for going out to a longer lunch it seemed on par with what we expected.
Pretty darn good BBQ. We ordered some pickle chips with our food and it was well worth it. Prices are more than fair and the portions will fill you up.

My husband and I enjoyed a quick trip to New Orleans and chose The Saint hotel. We loved the hotel decor; very chic and clean. The service was great from the valet to the front desk.  We only had trouble when  ordering room service. It was difficult getting through to place the order, only to be hung up on.  Then when it arrived, we had no utensils. Other than that, we thoroughly enjoyed our visit! On a side note...you must eat breakfast at the Ruby Slipper just next door. The Bam Bam Breakfast and shrimp grits were delish!

The best thing about this is the jazz band. They are AMAZING and super entertaining. The boat is nice and the views of the city are exceptional. Its a relaxing ride. We didn't have the dinner so no idea how good that is.

Never again. My co worker and I went to lunch there last week. First off, our silverware was dirty, we immediately told the server to bring us another set (that should have been a red flag). I ordered a turkey breast wrap and my friend ordered the seafood mix. We waited exactly 45 minutes for our food. My wrap was disgusting, it was soggy and the turkey smelled as if it were many days old. The seafood mix is a joke, we counted 5 pieces that consisted of small shrimp and scallops, it costs $19.99!! for only 5 pieces of mediocre "seafood". Too expensive. The server never bothered to check on us. Because we were in my lunch hour, I had to chase her down for my bill (I shouldn't have paid for this disgusting meal). When I went up to pay the $41.00 for this lunch,  the cashier asked how everything had been. I told her horrible. For the inconvenience, she gave us a $2 discount. Like I said, I should have walked out. I learned my lesson, Never again. I went back to work and told all my coworkers to stay away from this place. Also, this place  is dirty! if the eating area is dirty, can you imagine what the kitchen looks like?

My boyfriend ordered wings to go for us for the first time and they were delicious he also ordered us chicken tenders and fries. The mild Wings were finger lickin good, great sauce and the chicken tenders were delicious, fried and battered to perfection. Giving wings over Broadway 4 stars for their fries, they were big and hot but lacking some seasoning, they were a little bland and I was hoping they would be a little more tasty!

I'm honestly not really into classic rock but if I'm around my dad, this is what I go to.  MGK is a pretty good classic rock station. I don't really know what else there is for classic rock in Philly... I guess that's why they reserved the slogan "Philly's Classic rock station."
The quality doesn't seem it's as good as it once was. Maybe it's the new ovens. We now prefer New York New York Pizza down the street off of Big Bend.

Tourist trap. The fudge tastes good and they give you samples. The employees were very pushy. They asked us 3 times to buy their buy 3 get 1 free combo. Our cashier visibly changed his attitude the last time we declined, we felt like we had done something wrong and got drawn into a conversation over why 2 people didn't need 2lbs of fudge. When we looked at the bill he charged us over $20 for one half pound slice of fudge, when he had quoted us $10.99 for a half pound.

Cool place if get lucky and get a parking spot and table, I have tried to come here 2 times before being successful place was slammed on week nights both times causing me to get frustrated and go somewhere else both times, I went on Wednesday even though still packed got lucky I guess cause up front parking and circling a few times and then only waited 5 minutes for a table.
Service was amazing young lady greeted us promptly and provided us with recommendations and didn't rush us either. Had a flight and pretzels to start really good. Ordered burgers and they were good not amazing but not to bad. Will likely try something else next time. There menu is really diverse so won't get bored with choices. 
Overall amazing service and clean establishment. Will try to come back if get lucky again.

What a crock.  My wife and I had two vehicles in need of safety and emission checks.  I, the husband, dropped my vehicle off for the emission check around 9:15am and was told that it would take around an hour.  No big deal.  My wife pulled in 30 minutes later and went in (I had already walked to a nearby store).  They told her they could not guarantee a time for the safety and emission check, but that it should be done by 5:00.  My vehicle was done within 30 minutes, and my wife's wasn't completed until around 3:30.  Now, maybe the safety check does take a bit longer- so that's fine.  That said, they said that her vehicle needed a new brake light and that one of the windshield wiper blades needed to be replaced in order to pass the safety inspection.  They quoted her $250.00 for the repairs.  TWO HUNDRED AND FIFTY DOLLARS.  I went to an auto parts store and bought the necessary bulbs and a wiper blade for a grand total of $37.89.  They told my wife the bulbs were LED- they aren't.  They were going to replace an entire tail light assembly when all that needed to be replaced were three bulbs.  Now, the kicker.  When I took the tail light assembly out to do the (easy) replacement- three of the five bulbs had been unsocketed and pushed back into the lens.  One bulb had been cracked.  Now, I have no proof that the tail light was functioning before.  But I have considerable doubts that the bulbs had been pushed in and broken before I dropped this thing off at Dobbs.

Long story short, this place will get the job done.  They'll just gladly charge you about SEVEN TIMES the cost of the actual repair.  Unbelievable.  Any reputable repair shop would never quote such an astronomical price for such easy and quick repairs.  I will NOT be returning.

Federal is know for its donuts and chicken.  There however is one issue I have with the new chicken sandwich.   There are no other offerings on the menu to balance out the intensity of the flavor of the chicken.   My chicken was a bit dry because it was in the fryer a bit too long but on average the sandwich is phenomenal.  But it's an intense sandwich with a lot of flavor.   I do wish they'd lighten up on the powder flavoring maybe just one side of the chicken?  But in order for me to enjoy this  sandwich to its fullest potential it needs some French fries or some cole slaw something...  The price point is a bit high for $7 but seeing that they spend so much effort behind each sandwich it's a bit to be expected I guess.   However in this town we're not about over paying for food.

This used to be my favorite pizza spot back in college. So many unique pizzas with good bread and ingredients. But now, 8 years later coming back, I was excited to revisit a fond memory and was left disappointed. 

They used to let you choose your bread for the pizza. Multi-grain is definitely different now. I don't know if its because I didn't see any grains at all, just white flour, or if it was the very hard consistency of the bread and pizza in general. It was a pain to chew through even two pieces. I shouldn't have to work this hard for a good pizza dinner! 

Overall, the menu and ingredients are still impressive, but the delivery is not. Call it a change in taste if you will, but I think there's always something to say about the slippery slope of quality most businesses tend to suffer from. And Black Rock is no exception.

This place continued to be below average. Small servings, esp with the meat, and servers who are more in a hurry than to be attentive to the customers needs. Mildly flavorful but when Freebirds arrives from Austin, it will put this place and Chipotle to shame.

Great selection of both wine, beer, and liquors! Staff is always friendly and helpful! Really cool store as it used to be a old bank! They also do delivery which is awesome! As well they have tastings daily and also special events occasionally if you are apart of their royalty program.

Went on Tuesday night for the gourmet pizza night! 

Cost my husband and I $20 for all you can eat pizza, drinks & salad! Not too shabby!

It was fun trying the different flavors of pizza they had available!
Salad bar was fresh clean and had a good selection of toppings! 

The only complaint is the Samoan pizza needed to be cooked longer some of the cheese on top wasn't melted all the way... smh! Other than that everything & everyone was awesome!

We live in walking distance to this restaurant and had high hopes for it when we first moved in. The second time we went, we just ordered drinks and they took forever. The restaurant wasn't even that crowded! We saw the bartenders goofing off while we were waiting for our overpriced drinks.

In my opinion, the food is overpriced for what you get as well. Prices are comparable to TJ's up the street where the environment and the food is much, much better. This place never gives us a good vibe. I don't think we'll be going back. It's a shame!

My review is strictly for the desserts. I had the Creme brûlée and the Chocolate cake. Both were pretty solid. The bartender was attentive. However, he did ask if wanted ice cream with our cake which we said yes to but we didn't get. The decor was nice inside with the lights, too.

Very clean ... however not a very relaxing environment.. cheap prices but I'd rather pay more money and get a better massage... I spent the whole time wondering where the therapist received his training

The food and location are great. We loved the fresh apple cider doughnuts and all enjoyed our lunches. But we can only give 3 stars because the wait was so long to get our food. Most people seemed confused about how to order (including us) and it all seemed very inefficient. Also oddly the outdoor picnic tables are located next to the sewer vents... Not exactly a pleasant smell to accompany your lunch.

So, we check in online and there are 0 parties ahead of us as we walk through the door. We are told ok your next but 4 parties walk in and are seated ahead of us??!? NO, just No!! I have to again get up and tell the hostess 4 parties was seated ahead of us and she says ' oh, I was coming back to get you guys' when was she coming back, after she sat 10 more parties. We had a total wait of abt 20 mins to be seated with her seating all of God's children besides us (who were next). What a bad start off experience!!! Food was ok, we saw our waitress at the beginning to take our order and then never saw her again. Had to ask other waiters for a refill, box and check. There are so many other better choices for food, I mean you are paying good hard earned money, right? Although this experience was not the best, we still have 20% tip. But we will choose another option next time. Also one last thing, floors are very sticky on the bathroom. I almost lost a shoe lol!!

I have mixed emotions reviewing brow art-- 

The Pros: 
-I think it's in a good location, especially if you're a frequent shopper at the Green Hills mall. 

-For $11 for eyebrow threading, it's one of the better deals I've seen in town. 

That's where the pros end. 

The Cons: 
-The ladies aren't particularly friendly. 

-One of the two ladies isn't even all that good-- threading can be somewhat painful, but I've noticed the more experienced you are, the less it hurts-- this one lady probably gave me the most painful eyebrow threading I've had, and I get them done regularly. 

-One Saturday afternoon, the employees apparently brought their young children to work who were screaming and running around the small little space! No one even TRIED to stop them. Don't get me wrong-- I love children, but I didn't think it was an appropriate place to bring your kids, especially allowing them to be so misbehaved. It's one thing if it was one of the clients who dropped in with their kids- but that wasn't the case.

I'd like to give this place 2.5 stars because again I like the location and price. I'm not ruling them out, but if I see kids running around in there again, then I might go elsewhere... for good.

I absolutely love stopping in here for a quick drink and socializing with friends and family. A friend kept mentioning events hosted here and I first visited with family from out of town. The layout is friendly for group outtings. The brewery floor is appealing with areas for guest to view the process without a formal tour. I have become hooked on there Kombucha. As a beer lover it's weird to say that I truly get excited about this place for their Kombucha, it's such a niche in Nashville. They're the only brewery that offers an alternative non-alcoholic beverage adding to the appeal. 
I have yet to try the menu but it's intriguing and unique. Offering a wide array and providing beer lovers a reason to stick around. I could see this as a great date spot for beer and food enthusiasts like me.

We went in for lunch and the food was tasty but our waitress ignored us the whole time we were there. We had to pull over other waitstaff to assist us while our waitress chatted with bar staff with her back to us for the entire hour we were there. We couldn't even get our drinks refilled. Even though we returned to many NOLA restaurants for a repeat visit this was not one of them.

Not bad. Got my first weave ever at this spot. 
Things to remember - if you're a walk-in make sure to go as early as possible. So far I've been there twice and have had to wait at least 2 hours before I was attended to. 

I'll be back - the first stylist I used was V - she did a great job. Jeff was great too...6 weeks later and my friends are still complimenting me on the weave.

On 06/09/17 my wife and I made reservations to celebrate our 13 year anniversary and we couldn't be more happier. As a revolving restaurant there really is not a bad seat but I felt we were given a great view overlooking the gulf just in time to watch the sun set. Our waitress Tracey was so helpful throughout the night. She made great suggestions, took my wife and I picture and constantly made sure we were comfortable.The food was pretty good although my mini pork shank was not what I expected. This was my wife and I second time dinning here and we would gladly do it again.

We pass Sakura a lot while shopping and it always smells wonderful from the outside. We decided to stop in on a Monday evening and my 5 yr old was really excited to do the hibachi. Unfortunately we were told there would be a 20 minute wait and were given a long explanation about only having two cooks in and blah blah. I knew a 20 min wait probably meant 40, and with two small kids that wasn't ideal, so we sat at the regular tables. Needless to say the kids were disappointed. I watched a few more groups come in for hibachi who had to sit on the regular side because of the wait. Which, 10 mins after we sat down, was still "20 mins!"

The waitress was nice. We ordered beef dumplings for an appetizer. My 5 year old and husband were not fans. The waitress forgot my husbands wine but she was nowhere to be found after she took our order. We saw the wine sitting on the bar for a long time. The host, I believe, would bring us our soups and salads but then would run away so quickly we could barely ask him for the things we needed. 

I got filet and chicken with fried rice. I was pretty impressed and thought the food tasted great with a nice presentation on the plate. The rest of the meal went well until we got the bill. Very pricey! But the kicker was....$4 for a tiny plastic kids cup of apple juice??!?!? I thought I was seeing things. My husbands wine was only $2 more! Wow. 

Still undecided about this place. I think I will give the hibachi side a try soon, but will definitely call ahead first!!!

1.  Disfunction with the hostesses to start the night. 
2.  Drinks took 15-20 mins to get.  
3.  The orders took a long time to receive.  
4.  One was wrong and the other was cold.  The waitress asked if I would like my meal reheated in the microwave!  Appetite lost!

We went for first time today at 5pm 
1st host sat us down and nobody came for 15min as i asked a waiter to be our waiter we were seen talking to him when he was rushed off and told that shes got this then left to get a waitress who finally came to rush our order out of us then disappered again we were just about to get up when our drinks came so we said lets see how this goes 
apps good 
salads good cesaer great
Johnny ok steak good but chicken dry 
soup minastrone good but cold and waitress didn,t have cheese then disapeared again
we also had Italian trio, good  
So lets see food good but not handled correctly
We got our check before we were asked if we were finished or wanted desert witch we did, so when we said something to her she just said sorry so we just said forget it then she dissapeared for 20 min this time, really ,never got a refill yet so when we asked for teas to go she came and refilled our table glasses, really ,however now we were laughing 

Wait staff needs to be retrained or fired  

That said will I go back.   Yes but not untill I read better reviews on yelp. yep

Both my wife and I ordered soup at the same time; our bowls came out 10 minutes apart.  My wife's soup came with a piece of aluminum foil in it.  She sent it back and requested another bowl of soup.  We were promtly charged for that second bowl for her...SERIOUSLY!!  My wife ordered linguini with meatballs.  The meatballs were passable but the linguini had not been drained properly so her sauce turned into soup.  First time in over 20 years I have not tipped at least 18%.  This place is in dire need of training and management.

This was absolutely the worst experience ever. My first and my last. Took an hour to get the food then it was cold, we sent it back and got it cold AGAIN! They gave us gift cards but we gave them back and told them to just waive the check. We didn't even eat! 2 hours of sitting there I was sick to my stomach. I know it doesn't happen to everyone or everytime but read these reviews... horrible.

A little on the pricy side, but I guess considering the location. . . Also, the ambiance was nice, and the restaurant had a nice, cozy feel.

The service was really good, and the steak was delicious. We go the burger, which was good and a steak with brussel sprouts and mushrooms as the sides. Neither one of the sides were standouts, and we were saddened when we saw our neighboring table get the mac and cheese, which looked yummy.

We went there because we had a groupon, but will probably not be back.

Legitimately the worst wax I've ever had. Numerous hairs missed, in fact, huge clumps of hair were missed and it started growing back almost immediately. I wish I hadn't paid cash so I could demand a refund. Look elsewhere for a decent or well done Brazilian. Never again.

Have only been here once but can say that we really enjoyed our dinner. Came with a few friends and it was a perfect place to relax, have some drinks and catch up. They have a really good beer selection and the food is great too.

It seems over priced, but the location is good- right on Frenchmen. The feel is more of a lunch place than dinner. Although I might stop here before seeing a show at Snug.

This is a good place for vegetarians to find options for soul food. Our waitress was awesome and helped me find things that I could eat. Hopefully she didn't just tell me it was without meat!? My girlfriend got a shrimp po'boy and I got collards, mac n cheese, red beans, rice, and corn bread. The desserts were great too.

After hearing the hype before visiting Philadelphia - plus  a rave review from a fellow librarian while attending a conference I knew I would have to try Jones out.  My last night in town a group of went to  - always a good thing when they don't *blink* at a party of 8!  I'm sure it helped that it was a Monday night :)   I loved the decor - awesomely done 70s basement goodness - although it must be a bitch to clean the crap from the shag carpet every night.  In particular I loved the lighting, the inset details and the unisex, private bathrooms.   I had the reuben and as far as reubens go it wasn't the best I have ever had, but it was pretty good.  The food is very, very reasonably priced - anytime you can eat somewhere for less than $20 (not booze of course) is a bonus to me!

I'm loving the Hi Watt! It's great to see a new venue that caters the real up-and-comers in the current new music wave. The room has a great, rustic factory vibe with brick walls and wood beams. The stage is super-easy to see, even from extreme angles. Plus, the bartenders are friendly and the drink prices aren't as hard on the wallet as one would expect. I can't wait to see more shows here!

So for service first, i loved this place. Shelly welcomed us off the street and made it seem we could not wait to be her guests. The place is a bit old, the vinyl-topped stools at the counter dated but charming, the ATM machine squatting next to the row of booths an intrusive anachronism, but overall homey and comfortable. The food is southern and just as comfy. I had tender ham hocks and cabbage; my buddy had a meaty and robust fried catfish. Both were tasty as hell and served with a smile and warm conversation. Great lunch at an affordable price, and one of our best experiences in NOLA this trip. Thank you, Shelly, for a great finish to our little tour that day.

Just a warning, THIS PLACE IS CLOSED during the winter. I had heard about this place from the daily show during their visit to the DNC 2016 and I had never heard of water ice before and this was on my must do list when visiting Philly. So after walking here all the way from Pat and Genos I was really disappointed to find it close with no indication why.  Turns out most water ice places are just closed in the winter unless it's inside a mall. But still 1* for no warning ahead of time. I'm not a local, I'm a tourist I shouldn't be just expected to know these things. Their website even said we're open for the season.

The atmosphere is nice.  The food terrific.   The servers extremely friendly .   I was there for breakfast with my husband .  We both enjoyed the Wisconsin omelet.   I had lunch with a good friend I enjoyed the meatloaf sandwich and my friend had the fish tacos both were wonderful!

Trader Joes is an awesome place for all categories of food except produce. It's never that good and so much of it is packaged in styrofoam and plastic wrap so you can't inspect it.

That aside there are so many things here I can't imagine my life without. These include feta with Medeterranian herbs, sweet and hot mustard and a $4.99 bottle of sparkling Contadino Pinot Grigio.

And an added plus at this location is last time I was there they appeared to have returned to self serve beverage samples.

Pretty good pizza.  Very quiet location nestled in the mall.  Doens't deliver, but they do have take out specials.  If you decide to dine in, they also have good specials on beer and pitchers.  The wait staff was very friendly and attentive.

Red Hound's food is decent.  The choice of beers on tap is below average but they have one good IPA.  Last week I ordered a burger with Gorgonzola, a great combination, and a salad.  In particular I asked for the burger MEDIUM.  After I got my beer, the waitress brought utensils and napkin.  I asked for my salad before the burger.  The waitress said NO.  I began watching television (two TVs are at the end of the bar beside each each other).  My burger arrived and I ate the salad while watching the TV, the burger getting cool and the bun soggy.   I was halfway through the burger when I realized that the inside was RAW, not rare, RAW, never mind medium.  I ate around the center and abandoned the sandwich.  In the meantime a couple walked in, sat at the bar, and asked the waitress to change the channel to the ball game, so now the ball game was on TWO TVs beside each other.  I decided to have another beer and asked for the TV to go back to the channel I was watching before the couple came in, but the waitress said NO, the couple had asked to change the channel.  I had my choice of the game on the left TV, the same game on the TV next to it, or move somewhere to watch some other TV.  I complained more about the burger; the waitress said that I should have complained earlier and walked away.  

The next day I called to complain to the owner, but he was gone, so I talked to the manager.  She said she heard about the incident because I did not leave a tip.  The restaurant does not like to serve salads separately because it takes time and the staff is busy.  I pointed out that at the time this happened there were precisely three people at the bar, including me, and the waitress had been idling.  The manager then told me that it would have been OK for me to get the salad and the burger together and then send the burger back to the kitchen while I ate the salad.  If the owner0 should fire the pair.  

There is a competing bar, TJ's, less than half a mile away, with much better food, a wider beer selection, and FAR better wait staff.

They get my order wrong every time I go thru the drive through. This time, it was two kids cokes instead of the lemonades I ordered, and we were missing an order of fries. It's really irritating. I guess I'll have to double check before leaving the drive thru window. Sorry in advance to the customers behind me in line.
Only eaten here once, the food was good. Traditional menu with some stir fry items. Service was good also. I had the pho with all the variety of meats. The beef tendon was not what I was use to.

The apartment was really nice. My friends and I opted for a two bedroom two bathroom and it was really spacious. Within walking distance to everything. Would stay at a Stay Alfred apartment again in the future.
Great service, great menu, great food.  It's a nice atmosphere that is rarely crowded, and definitely one of my favorite restaurants in Media.   I hope this place sticks around for a long time.

Not very good, the bathrooms were sticky and the bathroom handles had something on them.  The Hand Grenade had some stuff floating in it and did not taste very good and I peed bright green for 2 days.  Music was not good and cheesy.  I would not go there.
I love this place food and service is alway spot on. Only thing I wish was if they did the add on lunch special where you get a side of chips and salsa and drink for a few extra bucks

This place does a thorough job cleaning. However, the staff there can be snippy and rude at times. A couple times I wanted to get a full detail and the ticket attendant snippily said "it's too late" the first time I said OK and just got a regular car wash; the second time I came about 1pm and he said the same thing, I asked him what a good time was and he just ignored me and asked what car wash I wanted to get, so the lady at the front desk tells me to come before 12 next time. So my last run in with this same ticket attendant was yesterday, I was pulling up to the car wash under the direction of his hand motion and once I got out he told me in snippy and rude tone not to watch the front of my car and to watch him, Which I don't understand, I'm not going to drive forward when there's a car in front of me under your direction and not look at what I'm doing. So I go to the manager because at this point I know this guy is a bit rude, the manager tells me "well he's been here 19 years and longer than me and I haven't had any complaints" I said "well that doesn't have anything to do with my experience and why I'm standing in your office at this moment and I'm coming to you because I'm giving you an opportunity to rectify it rather than just going to social media or writing a bad review" he says "well you can do that, we have thousands of people come through every day" So here I am! The managers attitude was an "I don't care about your experience because we have enough customers, we can lose one" That will be my last time giving them a chance, I'm sure there are other car washes with thorough work and great customer service skills as well.

I've been sitting at the bar waiting for someone to ask me what I'm having. I waited a good 5 minutes and still no one asked, yet everyone else around me was being served. It wasn't busy at all.  I ordered the pineapple rice and sushi and it was the worst thing I had ever had. The service sucked and so did the food. The sake cafe on Magazine st. is much better.

Top 10 Things About Nomad

10.) Exposed brick walls - They remind me of other red things I like; Tiger Woods' Sunday polo, red folders, red pens to mark up someone's errors, the American flag and of course period blood (aka the red flower).

9.) The bathrooms - A dual-level toilet?!? Say what? When taking care of business, you can choose to sit down with a backrest in the traditional posture and your feet on the floor, or sit up top with a place to dip your feet in the hot summer months. Although the second option requires removing the heavy porcelain lid to the toilet, so if you're a woman this might be a challenge.

8.) Yadda.

7.) Yadda.

6.) Yadda.

5.) Food choices - You can order food in from any other local food spot, because they don't have a kitchen. That's right, you can enjoy pizza, ceviche, chicken mcnuggets, caviar, black truffles, black mambas and even pork belly pancakes. All is within your reach with a quick phone call. Use your imagination!

4.) Blacked out windows - When I first walked by, I didn't even notice the bar. So I kept walking...and walking...and before I knew it I was playing a game of dominoes with some local gentleman on their front porch. It didn't last long - I thought slamming your fist on the table and yelling "dominoes bitches!" was standard, but it seems my timing was off. On my way back up Orange Street luckily I heard the music emitting from inside, which brings us to number 3.

3.) Live Jazz and Blues music - Loves it. I even got to wear my favorite "White People Fucked Up The Blues" t-shirt. This is the real draw at Nomad. It's cozy, has a great beer selection, friendly bartenders and great music soaring through the bar and out the door into the third-rate city Wilmington sky.

2.) Free Goldfish crackers - Confession - I like to get a lttle raw and raunchy sometimes. Maybe dress as a seagull and eat raw fish, flap my wings in public to piss people off, etc. And what is a seagull's second favorite food? Goldfish crackers. I know, because I used to empty whole large cartons onto the beach when I was younger, to draw in every seagull within a 10 block radius to create a diversion to get home. Maybe I upset a few families trying to enjoy the beach without the cackling, cawing and bird shit on their chests, but these are sacrifices you need to make.

1.) I leave number one up to you, the reader. Sometimes it's about the journey, not the destination, sucka.

The restaurant just reopened today from a 2 1/2 week renovation. The restaurant over went a complete transition. The look used to be casual dining with a very busy decor to a classy upscale looking restaurant. Don't worry about the prices, the restaurants is affordable. 

Great semi open kitchen so you can view the cooks in action. There is a glass to keep the noise from entering the dining room. 

Apps are around the $10 area and you can get many main course for $14 - $16. 

The food and service is top notch. There are many dishes to choose from. This has now become my wife and I favourite Italian restaurant in this price range. 

The reason I am giving it 4 out of 5 stars is because they spent the money on renovating the restaurant but our bread came in a broken wicker basket. One of our menus was very worn out around the edges. Not a big deal but don't cheap out on the little things.
Loved the boat and the trip but the supper is trash. I mean come on - this is NOLA where the food is great everywhere... except on this boat. Do yourself a favor and skip the supper.

This review is for those who do their own maintenance on their vehicles. I have no experience with the service department.

As a do-it-yourself kind of guy I like to do all the maintenance on my own vehicles. When I need to buy parts I frequently find myself at this Pep Boys. 

While they usually have whatever part I'm looking for, I would advise caution when getting parts from behind the counter. On too many occasions I've opened the box to find the wrong size parts. This usually causes the person behind the counter confusion only to then realize that some cars have different size parts depending on the model. I find this to be most often the case when I am buying brakes, rotors, or brake shoes.

Also, their prices are not always the best. Many times Autozone or Napa will have the same parts at a cheaper price. Sometimes Autozone or Napa will have a brand name on sale cheaper than the Pep Boys store brand. So shop around before you buy!

Enjoyed a great dinner with my family this Sunday.  
Husband and I had the Turkey dinner.  Delicious. 
Drinks-- Philly Sour and Thyme were both very good.  
Kids had burgers and toasted cheese with tomato soup.  Everyone enjoyed their choice.  
Would definitely return. 
Great atmosphere as well.

The grocery store is great for finding items you can't get at Publix, but the produce is hit or miss so I usually end up at a vegetable stand or a larger grocery store on my way home. The staff is friendly and helpful. 

The cafe is meh. The smoked tofu melt is great, but the vegan breakfast isn't so awesome. The tofu scramble could be much improved with some nutritional yeast, lemon juice, and spices.

This cafe has location going for it, but to the food, I say "meh". Tasted more pre-packaged than fresh. The shrimp po'boy, crawfish pie, gumbo and jambalaya were all disappointing. Very noisy. With all the great places to eat in the city, skip this one.

As usual, i sat at the bar  Good BBQ Nice smoke flavor doesn't really require sauce on the brisket. 2nd best brisket in recent memory. Friendly service.  A little quicker to receive food this time. Quick on the drink refills!!!

Went on NYE 2013/14 and got some oysters and jambalaya. The oysters were actually some of the best I've had, and I'm not really a huge oyster fan. The jambalaya was the best I've had from anywhere. Honestly, I was really surprised. The only reason it falls short of 5 stars was my wife ordered some shrimp that was supposed to be spicy, and it was a little bland. Stick with the jambalaya, gumbo or red beans and rice. I can't speak to the poboys and such. Oh, and the service and location were great, too.

The best part of my dinner this evening was the chips and salsa. I ordered La Bandera (3 different enchiladas) and they were pretty dry. My wife ordered the shrimp fajitas which were tasty, but the portion size was disappointing. It came out on the typical cast iron skillet and only filled about half of it. For almost $16 she got 8 shrimp. If they were tiger prawns then this would be OK, but they weren't. Don't think we'll be returning.

I came to Lao Bar on a Sunday night, so it was very relaxed. It was also one of the only places nearby open on a Sunday! What's up with that! People need to drink everyday of the week, especially right before returning to work on Monday. 

The bartender was nice and helpful. She recommend drinks off the cocktail menu to us, which was very helpful because I have never heard of over 50% of those ingredients. 

The drinks were good although a bit expensive. I really liked the way they decorated the place.

I like this chain and its too bad we don't have it out East.  The burgers are satisfying, the shakes pretty darn good, the price is right, and you can't complain about their hours (they don't close!)

I'd prolly give them 5 stars after a good night out!
Crab cakes with poached eggs are so yummy!we got right in the view was beautiful looking at the st.louis cathedral and Jackson square ,everything was served in a timely manner

First off, a great beer list. It's refreshing to see a restaurant that sees beer for what it is: an amazing complex beverage and not a poor man's wine. I greatly enjoyed pairing beers with appetizers, entree and dessert. It was no easy task with the limited but excellent tap selection and even more extensive bottled collection.

And I haven't even talked about the food yet. Crab cake and flatbread appetizers, excellent. Crispy duck, excellent. All three were well presented and cooked to perfection.

But most excellent, the cinnamon brioche bread pudding with caramelized bananas and banana creme freche. Even my wife who is usually not a fan of bread puddings absolutely loved the dessert. Save room for it.

My only complaint (and this should tell you how good the food was since I'm still giving it four stars) is that the staff is somewhat, shall we say, curt. Almost like they are too busy to care that much about what you want. I can put up with curt if the service is fast, but it wasn't fast enough to put up with curt. I have to knock off a star for service which is a shame because I REALLY wanted to give Alison Two five stars.
OH my gosh!  I love mc Popsicle !  He was awesome!  Also the band was great!  For the first time in 13 years my husband danced with me!

I went to chickie and petes last night for dinner and it was the WORSE experience EVER.  Now don't get me wrong I have  been to chickie and petes on several occasions and it was a great experience BUT last night made me feel like I was on a bad episode of BAR RESCUE.

1.  when we walked in (party of 3) the women at the hostess booth were so busy in their own conversation to even acknowledge that someone walked in. we waited for about 1 minute before even being acknowledge (STRIKE ONE)

2.  after being seated the waitress came right over and asked us what were we drinking.  didn't give us a minute to even look at the menu.  after ordering the drinks (which came out very fast) we order our appetizers.  the appetizers came out very fast which was great.  but we finished the appetizers and waited a good 10 minutes before the waitress came over and took our ENTREE order (STRIKE TWO)

3.  Entrees come out  (which were pretty good) and we finished them.  the waitress never not once came over and asked us how are we, did we need anything, she basically never came to check on us.  when she finally came over we asked for boxes  because we couldn't eat the rest of the food.  she brought us the boxes and left.  for another 15 minutes before we flagged her down and asked her for the check.  (which she should of brought when she gave us the boxes).  (STRIKE THREE)

**oh did I mention that it wasn't crowded?  so there was no need for the POOR customer service.


bottom line -- the FOOD here is GREAT,  but the customer service SUCKS ASS BIG TIME.  The waitress we had was very lazy, unprofessional, and act like she was intimidated by us.  My company does a lot of business with sports bars in Philadelphia and because of last night I will have them reconsider their relationship with CHICKIE and PETES.

I will take GREAT customer service with mediocre food ANY DAY -- over POOR customer service and GREAT food..

I've came here a few times and the last 2 times my order have been incorrect. I checked the receipt and the correct order was listed. I suggest to better train your staff and make sure they are paying attention to detail if not you will continue to lose customers. This is consistent trend.

The sales portion of the store is full of nice clothing with sales associates tending to you as if they are waiters at Antoine's, but everything is certainly overpriced. The rental portion of the store though is abyssmal. I have failed to ever have anything fit remotely correct; plus, the pricing reflects the clothing sales portion but without the service they provide "downstairs."

G.O.B. sale was a waste of time.

Going out of business sales can be good, but store is almost empty & what is left is over priced.  Remember this is all sales final pricing.

I saw ~$200 style tent marked over $300 & "on sale for $268.

I saw the exact same crossbow nerf toy I bought a niece for $10 at Christmas marked $18 & 40% off so $10.80.

Boyfriend and I went to this location the other day, and I have to say, it wasn't my favourite experience. The waitress was alright - not amazing, but not horrible - she had a bad habit of just leaving us for 20 minutes at a time randomly. 
The food is why I'm giving this place two stars. My boyfriend ordered pasta, and I ordered a chicken sandwich. They give massive portions, which I like, however it seems like they're dead set on drowning every dish they serve. Because my side (Caesar salad) had just /too much/ dressing. My boyfriend's "pasta" was more like a stew or a thick soup, there was so much sauce. He was really upset about it, and he's not even the type of person to be picky about food. I offered to bring it up for him to our waitress but he insisted that I just leave it alone. We paid our bill and tipped, then left a little disappointed. Don't think we will be returning to this BPs location again. The quality of food is not as good as some of them I've been to (the Whyte ave location was much better.)

We stopped at this Trader Joe's for a few things. Since we had a big lunch, we were looking for salads for dinner. They have a wonderful selection. We picked out:
Kale and edamame salad,
Grilled chicken and field fresh greens,
Mexicali salad,
Super spinach salad,
Baby spinach and greens salad.

We also stopped to get boxed Shiraz wine which is our 'go to cheap but still very nice' wine. Unfortunately, this store was out of this today.

Service/check out is fast and the variety of what they carry is great. 

One additional thought- they carry the best canned dolmades I've had. I've had better in restaurants but not canned. Delish.

I've only ever ordered buffalo chicken salad and white pizza from here but it's always been consistently good. The salad comes in a garlic bread bowl - veggies are always fresh and crisp with lots of chicken. Usually order through grub hub and it's here in about 30 minutes.
Laid-back, easy atmosphere with a great band. An added bonus: clean lavatories!

I go to the OV El Charro for the veggie enchiladas- so delish. So good that I started eating before I remembered to take a pic. The food is always good the 3 times I've gone but the service is so-so each time. Most recently, it was a Saturday night so they were busy, I get it. But simple requests were forgotten (water) and it took a solid 20 minutes to get a draft beer. I think we were seated for around ten minutes before a server even greeted us. My husband and I were seated at a super tiny table that could hardly fit our chips, plates, cups etc. The table and floor around us was dirty too. I wish I could focus solely on food but ambiance matters to me. Since I like the food, maybe I'll just make sure I go back on a weeknight when it's not so nuts.

Great food, bad service.  Our waitress had family members visiting so she made us wait at least 5 minutes before introducing herself or asking for a drink order.
No refills on water unless we asked.  I ordered a house margarita, which wasn't the best in Tucson...try something different, and when that was gone she never asked if I wanted another or brought more water.  That was offered after our meal was done.  
Waitress also brought veggie enchiladas when I ordered corn tamales.  This was after I asked her opinion and she suggested the corn tamales.  When the bill came we were charged for the more expensive enchiladas so we had to ask for that to be corrected.
Food was great, but if her sevice is the norm then we won't be back or recommend El Charro Cafe to others.
We came at 8:30 on a Friday night so bad service wasn't due to a busy restaurant.

4.5 stars. Service and crabs legs we LOVED. Snow and Dungeness crabs were off the hook. Our server was incredibly helpful and nice. The (curly haired) hostess, however, who worked that night of August 22 can use some serious training. She was really off putting and not welcoming.

We had a great time.  It is a real steam powered riverboat, and the two-hour trip was educational, relaxing, and fun.  I enjoyed seeing all the sights as well as touring the boat.  The engine room was fascinating, but was exceptionally hot.  I can't comment on the food since we didn't eat on board.  I guess my only complaint was that I would have liked a much longer river tour.

We really wanted to love El Charro Cafe, after a friends recommendation, however that just was not the case. From the tasteless food to the substandard service (we were never even acknowledged after being seated, no drinks, no chips and salsa)and many other choices for Mexican food in the Tucson area, I would pass by this overpriced, poor quality "cafe"

We visited To Kai twice.  The first time we were blown away.  The second time it was merely alright.  I think consistency is a sign of a great restaurant.  These guys still have to work on it, but there's a promise.  The rolls are innovative, but their names are annoying (BMW roll, Lexus roll).  I kept wanting to ask where's the Audi and Mercedes rolls.  I would give them another chance.

A large specialty pizza was just $16 after tax, and would easily feed 4 people with a salad or something on the side. We felt like pigging out a bit, so it was just for 2 people accompanied by movie candy;-) We only got through 3/4 of it and were both beyond full.  
The pizza is quite nice, the crust is thin but not crunchy.  Ordered for pickup and it was ready within half an hour.  Got the Scallopini pizza and we both really enjoyed it!   I didn't find it greasy at all, which was nice. We will definitely be ordering again.

It's a tiny unassuming place that we couldn't find at first. I love restaurants in houses so I was really excited when we finally found it. We stepped in and they actually were able to seat us early (8pm instead of our 8:45 reservation). The service really is impeccable. I almost wish I got wine so we could experience the sommelier who seemed amazing.

We ordered a la carte because we are not yet filthy rich. For our antipasti, we got the TEMPURA SOFT SHELL CRAB which was ugh sooo goooood. Then our bread came, which was weird, and I thought the focaccia was to-die-for, the white tuscan not so much. For pastas we tried the SHRIMP & RICOTTA RAVIOLI, which just melted in your mouth but I think it reminded me too much of shrimp shu mai. We also had the DUNGENESS CRAB SPAGHETTI which was really disappointing - really salty, no crab to be seen, and the pasta was really hard and I know that's not what al dente is. Our secondi was the special, a SUCKLING PIG dish that was pretty much perfect, really decadent and crispy and salty and sweet.

So it was a mixed experience. I really expected better pasta (and bigger portions of it), but the antipasti and secondi were amazing. The tables next to us got the tasting menus and wine and would not stop raving about it so that is probably the way to go if you can afford to splurge (more than usual).

We had the 22 oz rib eye done medium rare.  Caesar Salad with anchovies added, broccolini and mashed potatoes.  The steak was done just right and very flavorful. Loved the mashed potatoes and the broccolini.  Service was very good.  Atmosphere was okay but not special.  We will be back for the steak!
Great food, service was perfect! Nice BYOB in the neighborhood without crazy high prices

So many good things to say about Beawon - they're such a pleasant atmosphere of friendly servers, inviting (and well-lit) dining room and their FOOD.  

Bon chons --- awesome!  The different kinds we had were some sort of potato salad, kimchi (of course), fishcake & a few others that are currently slipping my mind.  Loved 'em all & they were quite nice about refilling for seconds :).

I tip-toed past my usual kalbi & soondubu favorites and ventured on instead for some kkanpunggi (a spicy version of sweet & sour chicken) & Jjamppong, a sort of ramen-type dish with seafood.  I really liked the jjamppong, but preferred the broth tossed with rice instead of noodles (perhaps this is a change they could make for you per request?).  Happily slurped up all the goodness, seafood was really fresh, couldn't have asked for a better heart-warming meal.

Staff was extremely helpful and friendly. The outback burger was made perfectly, and the 4 course meals are a very good deal. The only hiccup was that my wife's steak and cheese was dry. The first time she ordered it, it was juicy, but the last two times it came out dry. I'm not sure why that is but will be returning real soon!

The alligator appetizer is delicious! Order it half fried and half grilled. It's so good, you don't even need the dipping sauce....but, the sauce is also good.
The Voodoo Juice drink is one my favorite mixed drink in New Orleans.
I like the location, which is right on Jackson Square by the Cathedral. Plus, the people watching is pretty interesting.
We've enjoyed all the classics-gumbo, jambalaya, red beans & rice with sausage. 
We will definitely be back!

This is less than 5 minutes off of 295 - a good place to stop for lunch if traveling South.  It is close to the Delaware Memorial Bridge.

Italian Kitchen is kid friendly- has chicken fingers, pizza, grilled cheese and French fries.  

They have cold deli and hot deli foods, pizza and soup.

You can pick up soda and bottled water for the road.  They also have coffee to go.

The children voted.  They said we should stop here the next time we are going South.

To answer Greg T's question, YES! I am totally into bowling alleys that haven't been renovated in 30 years. I am not a serious bowler--I go maybe once or twice a year, and I'm looking for a place that's not too crowded or too family friendly/corporatized. When I came here, there was no crowd, the jukebox was playing a lot of Rolling Stones, and there was a little old lady with a big blonde beehive behind the bar. In short, it was awesome, and I plan to return.

My favorite place to have chimichanga in town (so far). For $8, I had 2 medium size chimichangas with crispy wrap. It came with picco de gallo, guacamole, green salad and sour cream. It tastes much better than the food from a chain Mexican restaurant or even local Mexican restaurant in Indy area. I wish they can make it with chorizo...it could be more delicious (and fattening too). 

The kids meal has a standard menu for the price range of $4-$4.5. They offer chicken fingers, quesadilla, hot dogs, grilled cheese and few more. They all come with a soft drink and a bowl of fruit. According to them they taste pretty good. 

In conclusion, the restaurant offers good (and unique) meals for the museum. They offer many different southwestern inspired dishes such as buffalo chili, and they also serve shrimp po'boy sandwich. I will return and try different dishes next time I come back here. Or I will probably just come back here only to order my chimicanga. Yum yum...

I am more impressed with this location each time I go! Maybe I am getting to understand the layout but it seems like the selection and stock are improving. 

Price checking is always a good option but sometimes Canadian Tire just drops their prices and that is when it is time to pounce. Momma likes her deals so I usually shop off season and have a pretty good personal inventory idea of what I need/want and what I don't. 

As far as Automotive goes I have asked a few ordering questions and as per usual with Canadian Tire this department does not receive great reviews from me...or anyone I know. If given the choice on that I may even consider running past the local high school shop class before I walk in here as a female.

The food is good, beer is good. The waitress is friendly ... the place isn't very lively. It looks sad  maybe better music  or drink specials or food specials. It's not over priced, the just place needs SOMETHING. I ordered the BBQ pulled pork sliders, they are tasty. The beer is local, it's pretty good (if you like beer).

Will give 5 star if I stay in the 2nd floor.. But overall a good experience. Breakfast， free bike rental and easy parking and did I mention about the great location ....so even though a bit noise early on from the 2nd floor but after 9pm everything quiet down... Would come back again but stay on the 2nd floor!

I wanted to like this place but it felt more like a mass chain than a great local find. The staff were great and no wait. The custom omelet was not done correctly (tomatoes inside when I asked for none and an onion or two that were not part of my order). Not terrible mistakes, but the worst was that the food came out luke warm. The complimentary corn muffins also seemed old. If you're looking for standard fare, no frills, this place is fine. That said, for the quality they are too expensive for brunch/breakfast in my opinion.

The staff is really helpful impatient especially when you don't know what title you need. It's great that they're spaces that you can review books and read a little bit of the book to see if you will truly enjoy it. The bathrooms are a little unkept.

I absolutely love this Starbucks - servers are friendly, coffee is always well crafted and if you go during down times they're pretty quick. My only gripe with this starbucks is obviously the parking lot is ridiculously small - forget going there in the morning when it's work time. You will never get a parking spot and the drive thru will take forever. The longest I've ever waited in the drive thru is 20 minutes and the only reason I stayed is because I couldn't get out of the line due to the high curb. 

Now if you can go after the morning craziness the service is superb. Also the pastries and breakfast sandwiches are fresh. I've had the croissants, very berry coffee cake, banana bread, brownies, bacon & gouda breakfast sandwich (this was just okay), veggie & monterey jack breakfast sandwich, turkey bacon and white cheddar breakfast sandwich and never left there disappointed.

Not a bad place for regular diner fare. Friendly staff. A little cramped inside. Ceiling tiles were sagging. Seats and all felt cheap. Stupid cheap meals(a good thing). I don't really feel strongly about this place to continue writi

Not impressed at all. Tiny rolls for a hefty price plus very bland. Ordered three specialty rolls among other things and paid over $100. So not worth it. Gave an extra star for nice staff. Won't be going back as there are other places that are much better.

Have been coming here for years. Glad to finally see a decent cider on the list instead if woodchuck and magners. Good has always been fairly consistent,  and the new decor is a welcome change as they bring a little city atmosphere into the burbs similar to Nectar. 

Not to keen on seeing the prices increase beyond normal bar/pub type prices. Burgers/sandwiches easily reaching the teens is a bit much. Have had the same if not better food at Kelly's for less. Still some of the better fajitas in the area which is my GF go to,  minus the tortillas of course. And Angry Orchard cider is a welcome addition. Miss the Dara Estelle beer though. 

Still good for catching a bite during a game and the beer selection is always vast for the non-glutarded. Would love to see some back to back big screens in the main dining room. Would make for a nice divider and not have to wonder what people are cheering about. 

Would love to see Marathon Grill take Food Source's location and bring some nice competition to them.  Iron Hill will do the same when it opens in Ardmore.

Stopped at this diner because of all the great reviews, however, it didn't live up to the reviews I read, at least not for us. 

One, as soon as you walk in there was a bucket & newspapers because the roof was leaking. Now, this is not uncommon weather for it to constantly rain. If you looked up at the ceiling you could see how the tiles were holding on to water as if could fall at any second. 

Second, food was flavorless. However, food arrived promptly and the service was okay.

Great happy hour featuring creative drinks.  Inexpensive, but quality cocktails.  Pictured are the jalapeño strawberry margarita and the black widow.  Easy to get to in the CBD and ample parking.  Huge bar and spacious seating area.  Small plates are perfect for sharing.  Tacos are $2 on Thursday Caliente Nights.

What can I say.... I paid 10$ to park then waited over 20 minutes for a server. Finally after flagging down two waitresses I got the managers attention and was informed it was shift change and a waitress would be over shortly. Needless to say I left. I can't imagine the service would get any better as the meal went on. The manager refused to refund my parking and offered nothing other than a pitiful explanation that it was shift change.
I'm shocked at the good reviews here. Service was good the staff is very nice. I can eat dried out chicken at home. Very bland. Overpriced. Not enough parking. Won't be back.
Fantastic French pastries. The almond croissant vaporized and made me eat a second one.

We visited on Sunday, January 15th and we were excited to try out Philly's newest addition to the gayborhood. The atmosphere of the place is OK, if not a little sterile. In fact, sterile might be the best description of this place as a whole. There was no real culture or heart beat. The whole experience was a little drab. The drinks were basic and a touch over priced with a mild pour. The bar tenders weren't especially friendly and the time it took to get a drink while it was only moderately busy didn't bode well for what it would be like if the place was packed. 
The place could really benefit from something unique, rather that is a craft cocktail list, a look, an aesthetic...something. 
We didn't try the food, but even the menu was a bit of a yawn. The one bright spot of our visit was the greeter/ID checker at the door. They were very warm and energetic when we entered and upon leaving. We stayed for 1 drink and left, not sure we would go back.

When it comes to great flavor the smoked fish has it all.
Woo hoo! The coleslaw was perfect the German potato salad was very tasty. 
Just remember to bring enough cash for the meal and the tip.

Don't go...Its a scam. Food is awful and is very very very expensive and they don't post their prices. Three other buffet style places within five miles much better and cheaper. They charge little kids big prices and depending upon day charge different.

Went there for bottomless bellinis over brunch as a party of two. There was a bit of wait despite making a reservation but nothing to write home about. 

Our waiter was attentive and polite without being overbearing. He kept the champagne and juice flowing as soon as prompted which was great. 

As for the food, we started with the monkey bread - which is basically a shareable, rippable cinnamon bread that comes with a sugary dipping sauce. Think cinnabon. Personally, I'm not a huge fan of desert foods in general, let alone at noon on a Sunday, but my friend seemed to enjoy it. 

For the entree, we both ordered juevos rancheros (I know, sue us). While not incredibly delicious, the juevos rancheros certainly weren't bad. I'd give them 3 out of 5 stars. The reason I gave Jones 4/5 stars was due to the good service and reasonable price - and the food wasn't bad at all!

Would definitely come back to try some of their other stuff on the menu and for bottomless bellinis.

I'd been to Sage once before, more than a year and a half ago.  I shouldn't have waited to come back.  I was with a party of 6 and had a reso that I made earlier in the day.  We were seated quickly; the hostess knew our reservation.  We ordered the calamari per the waiter's suggestion, and we were all impressed: this stuff is where it's at, no where else have I seen breaded and fried calamari that is tossed in the sauce (a sweet creamy sauce that complemented the squid well).  The wine list isn't extensive, but it's got everything you might want - and all bottles were approachable.  
I ended up having the ribeye with candied yams and green beans.  Everything had tremendous flavor.  My one complaint would be that the yams were legitimately covered in caramel, which seemed like a little much.  It didn't stop me from eating them, though.  
The portions are pretty big, regardless of what you get.  I tried a bit of the steak and shrimp penne which had a peppery heat that wasn't shy.  Everyone at the table was very pleased with their dishes.  Including the cedar plank salmon, the Seafood Cipellini and the crab cake sandwiches.  
They have some great dessert selections, too.  All for $2!  I had an apple cranberry crumble and tried the creme brule - both very good.

Our server was very friendly and attentive, though he also was good at knowing when to hang back.  He needs to be trained on bottle service (didn't present the bottle and vintage, first pour didn't go to the person who ordered the wine and he finished that pour before serving the rest of the table).  I appreciated his suggestions, his patience and helpfulness.  

Some guests from either the AB visitor center or upstairs came by the bar drunk and caused some distractions (falling off bar stools) and increased the volume of the place considerably.

A hole in the wall you'll want to visit again and again. Found Please U on Yelp and decided to visit during my trip to New Orleans. We had wonderful waffles and the best biscuits. Walked out spending $12 for breakfast. Friendly staff and delicious food. Locals all around!

This place is horrible I watched them microwave all our food in the plastic containers and handle trash while cooking and handling food I didn't even eat the food I ordered I suggest you don't go here

We stopped in here for breakfast on a Saturday morning. It was busy, but not packed, and we were seated immediately.

The service was attentive and friendly, and the food was very good. I had eggs benedict, and the hollandaise sauce was really tasty. My wife tried the special of the day, which was a pumpkin omelet, and she enjoyed it.

My only complaint is that the food was a tad expensive and the portions a little small, but delicious nonetheless. We will definitely stop in again when we're in the area.

Mother of pearl! That was a greasy cheeseburger! Clogged arteries aside, the food here was yummy! You order your food and they bring it out you 5-10 mins later and enjoy! I had a cheeseburger and smash burger fries which have garlic and something else (can't remember what!) on them, but they were delicious- not soggy! We also had the fried onions which were really good as well. I got the smaller sized burger which filled me just fine (and I am a big eater), so I can't imagine how big the regular sized one is!

I like it, I think the concept is awesome. They wait kills me though. I'm sure give a bit more time and things will calm down some. I will definitely stop by once in a while but I also really like another local ice cream shop in Seminole heights area that makes unique flavors too. That one is still a favorite, time will tell if icesmiles will steal my heart. 
The ice cream is very creamy and the flavors do evolve, they say they add new flavors every 1-2 weeks. I like that they add toppings too.

The bouncers are rude and disrespectful. The customer service in this place is an absolute ZERO! For someone in the customer service industry i was appalled by how myself and my group of 10 LADIES were treated. No wonder this club was dead. Nobody wants to spend the prices to be treated like your club is on 4th Street. Beautiful facility, but ruined by the bouncer. Let me tell you how It started, upon entering i decided not to coat check. No big deal but as a few minutes passed i changed my mind. I walked to the guy at the entrance who was too busy talking to his girlfriend to even actually assist me. He finally yelled at me and told me i would have to exit and RE PAY and re enter in order to coat check. What? What terrible customer service! I have been in hotel hospitality the past 10 years and i have never witnessed such terrible disrespect. When i asked him if he could assist me since coat check was right there he put his hand in my face and said "no" and his girlfriend snickered behind him. I just dropped $$ on drinks for all my girls and tipped the valet $200 i am not afraid to drop money where people have excellent customer service. I will NOT be back to this facility. It is over priced and ran by a bunch of trash. Welcome to reno, you can build a vegas night club but you can't take trash and put it in a suit and expect it to be as classy.

If you're going to get comfort food, why not get Stephen Starr comfort food? I've lost track of his proliferation of establishments since I moved away, but, as a random restaurant choice, this was a good one. The four of us all got drinks, entrees and desserts, and left happy. Everyone got different cocktails, and we were each pleased with our choices. We went poultry-heavy: two orders of chicken pot pie, one of chicken and waffles, and one of fried chicken (mine). The pot pie was deemed wanting salt, but was otherwise well-received. My fried chicken was delicious: perfectly crispy on the outside, succulent on the inside. The accompanying fries were well-done, too. 

As for dessert, I can say only this: S'MORES. Get the s'mores, everyone; they are amazing and perfect, and I would eat them every day without complaint, I think. So, overall, a good meal, and won't break the bank. Keep it up, Mr. Starr.
Ask for Gerri. Gerri is awesome. Desire has a great happy hour. $6 for 6 oysters and $3 draft beer, Monday-Friday 3pm-6pm.

Had the Alligator Meatballs with marinara over pasta and it was delicious. Everyone I was with really enjoyed their meal. I would say a great New Orleans style meal at a reasonable price. Staff was fine also.

Pretty good food. I'm not a huge fan of the whole standing in line and ordering every ingredient of my meal but I figured I'd try it! I got 2 drunken yardbird tacos and a side of chips and queso. I thought it was a little pricey-- $13 for everything with a drink. They do give you a huge portion of chips and queso  though, I'd need another person to actually finish them! Moe's still has better queso

I have been here 3 different times.  The first visit (underarm). This visit was complimentary and my esthetician was Gwen. Why didn't anyone warn me?!?!.  I wasn't ready!!  The first rip...Oh...Lordt the pain was unbearable, but I was satisfied with her high attention to detail and perfectionism. She was thorough and efficient, making sure she got every single hair.

Second visit, (chin and upper lip). I believe her name was Celeste. She wasn't exactly warm and receptive, but rather matter-of-fact and about her business.She was thorough, detail oriented and efficient. She definitely knew her stuff and I was out in 15 minutes.  I think it was her last day or last week at this location before she moved on to a different location. 

Third visit (side burns). I believe I had Jenny.  She was warm, receptive, and very friendly. She was efficient and had me out in 15 minutes.  

Overall, I had a pretty good experience and I would definitely go back. The prices are reasonable and the attentiveness and attention to detail is bar none (I can only speak on the ladies who waxed me). The only suggestion I have for this location would be to improve their customer service/receptionist.  There were too many times where my appointment was scheduled for the wrong time/day, my name/appt couldn't be found in the system, and I was put on obscene hold times.

Wanted to love this place as we just moved from Portland and the Flying Pies are AMAZING. Totally different pizza and ingredients, this one was meh. Service was extremely slow but they were busy. Ordered breadsticks and waited 30 min, never came. Pizza took a bit longer. The Portland locations have amazing sausage with LOTS of it on the pizzas. This was very sparse and crust is totally differen,t being just ok. Will try the Fairview location but if you are expecting the best pizza like the Oregon locations have, dont.

Not bad but not great. We arrived around 6 and went to the roof top. We were wanting a table outside and the waitress was pretty rude about letting us know where we could not sit. Funny thing is that when we left an hour later it was still empty. Not a great start Blake. Let's break it down:

Atmosphere 3 stars - nothing special about the interior or rooftop. The downstairs area has a nice stage but the entire restaurant has a commercial feel to it. I considered it average for Broadway. 

Service 4 Stars - our waitress was very pleasant. She was helpful and attentive. No issue here. 

Food 4 stars - it's bar food but pretty darn good. I consider it above average. I had the trio sliders. The pork was very tasty, the bologna sandwich (yes they still make these) a we really good and clever. Lastly, the hamburger was solid. My wife had the pimento cheese wrapped in mozzarella. Really well done. 

Value 3 Stars - for basically two appetizers and four beers we left with a $60 bill. Alittle high for what you get but considering it is in the heart of broadway, probably pretty typical. 

All in all, it was ok. I would give it 3.5 but yelp won't allow me to do that. It's definitely slightly above average and I would come back.

Came here way late in the night and big props to all the staff who dedicate their nocturnal time to serving and feeding those like me who decide to eat out around 3am. Great service for being surprisingly busy at the hour and the food was pretty good going by diner standards. 

I ordered the corn beef hash which came with breakfast potatoes and an egg. It was yummy except I didn't particularly like the crispy, fried outside of the corn beef hash. I grew up eating it more on the runny/ taco meat texture style so that's just what I'm used to. Anyways, great place and I'd stop by again anytime!
Good neighborhood hardware store: makes keys too. Their staff is friendly ,and the store is pet friendly.

I was here with a group of colleagues for work & had a (weekday) dinner here. Our group comprised of every possible culture you can think of.

There are plenty of sushi restaurants but this location is nice because it's nice and close to where we need it to be .
The location is right by the University of Alberta campus, right beside the remedy Cafe.closer to Whyte Avenue, although if you just crossed the bridge across the river valley you can reach downtown very quickly.there is plenty of parking in the back, if you happen to not find enough parking in the front right near the door of the restaurant.  we were lucky and found a parking spot right by the door :)

I like the variety and I like the atmosphere.we were sitting in the corner booth with the benches where you could choose to Kneel or just sit normally. I also like the skylight so you can see outdoors.

We ordered quite a bit of food and I especially enjoyed the sushi boat. the soup was pretty good the tea was standard.most importantly, the food was fresh.the fish was excellent.
