This Moe's is disgusting! I have been to the Moe's in Deptford many times and have always had a pleasant experience. This was my first and ONLY time at the sicklerville location and it was appalling. FIRST I ordered the close talker and the lettuce was basically all orange and rusty. Mainly all of the ingredients did not taste fresh, far from actually. Then as I'm getting my drink the floor was ridiculously sticky! My shoes were sticky the rest of the day. It was disgusting. The floors were gross, the tables were gross, everything was gross. I love Moe's but I will never return to this location!

We found this place on Yelp and went for dinner one evening. We ordered crab rangoon, drunken noodles, hawaiian fried rice and panang curry. Food came pretty fast and was just okay. The staff was quite friendly and responsive. I think I would do take out next time, but not worthy of dine-in.
Nice little place in Carp.  Great food. The chia seeds dessert is a bit pricey at $4.25 though.

I am a ridiculously huge fan of taco bell, in the sense that I could eat it for lunch almost every day and then go back for dinner.  This particular taco bell is the worst i have ever, ever been to.    The wait times are just outragous, whether in store or in the drive-through.  I dont know if it is the staff, or lack of management, or both combined, but something definitely needs to be changed at this location.  I will say this is the first time in about the 50+ times i have been there that my order was completely correct.  But hell, it should have been considering i was there for 25 minutes or more.  I should not be able to go to a sit down restaurant and potentially get my food quicker than i would through this fast food joint.

Wanted to try some local flavours and got inside this simple looking place, I was surprised! Food was very good, large dishes and good prices. Try the fried chicken livers!!!
The only disappointment is the cocktails... Not updated, too sweet and not interesting. Stick to the food.

Short: 

Good food, Good service, small parking lot.

Long:  

This place is smaller than the one located at Carrollwood, but is much closer for me.  The food here is just as good as the other.  This time I got a brisket sandwich.  It was cook perfectly, wasn't dry at all.  

The style is still the same as most BBQ places, cafeteria style.  You got up order the food, and they load up your tray.  The food is prepared and ready to go on their hot table, so it definitely a great place to grab some good food quick.  

All their sides are delicious, I haven't been disappointed yet by any of the sides I've gotten.  Its hard to choose what sides to get.  They even offer to let you get a sample of a side if you can't decide.  As for meat, I recommend trying the brisket, it is my favorite meat choice over there.

Really enjoyed this place. We all did the fixed menu, and it was a lot of food, but tasty. The fish and steaks were good, as were the sides. Waiter was knowledgeable, and attentive. Very enjoyable evening.

As consistent as it gets!

I have eaten from Jones (in house and delivery) countless times and I have to say they are always very good.

I usually go with the Hummus and Pita and the Matzo Ball Soup, but decided to try the calamari salad, which I heard was very good.

Let me just say, it was way better than "good."  It was great. The server followed the instructions of no tentacles and that made it even better.  I hate wasting food and I despise the tentacles, so I was in heaven.

The dressing is out of this world and the calamari was lightly fried to perfection.  The little pieces of corn added a nice flavor as well.  

HHHHMMMMMMM...I think I know what I am having for dinner tonight!
Awesome crepes! Great service and fresh tasting crepes. I had a strawberry, French cream, chocolate sauce, and sugar crepe. It was awesome!

Nice family run business atmosphere with very friendly waiters. We liked the original twists to the recipes, for example the Mediterranean salad with crab and avocado was excellent. we are not big fans of fried or cheesy food and usually stay away from the chain Italian options for that reason but we would strongly recommend this place due to the options available.

I placed an online order and had the pickup location as here since it was closest to me.  I didn't get any car work done so I cant say much about their services.  When I pulled up I was worried I was in the wrong place because it is just an auto body shop and not a store.  I went in and they had my order all ready to go.  Just picked it up and left.  I got windshield wipers and unfortunately one had been opened before and the size was incorrect.  I went back and they said they didn't have any more of that brand and to go to their actual store location.  It turns out that the store location deliverers it to this location for the customer to pick up.  They were nice about it and said it wouldn't be a problem to change it out.  The idea was nice for the convenience, but the convenience didn't work out since I got the wrong wiper blade.  The store wasn't too far down the road so I headed there.  The staff was nice about it and didn't give me a hard time.

Food is ok, probably 3 stars out of 5. However, service is horrible. They have no basic manner. We came in during lunch hour when more than half of the restaurant was empty. When we asked how long is the wait (there were about 10 people waiting in line), the waiter just turned away without even responding.

Subsequent visits have been quick (blood testing and family planning consultations; no more than a few minutes of waiting in either waiting or exam room for either) and pleasant. Dr. Borgia was even willing to wait for my fiance since he was running late to our fertility appointment as he had required personal matters to attend to. 

My only quip is maybe the administrative side is a little understaffed. I was on hold with a recording telling me I had 7 callers ahead of me when I was calling to ask a question.

Still grateful for discovering them.

I used to go there when I was living in the East coast, so when I heard about a new one being opened in New Orleans, I just can't wait to check it out. My husband and I went there on  a Sunday so it was quite crowded but all the games are so new and we had tons of fun just getting few drinks and playing some old fashion arcade machines! I didn't try their food but it seems like they have large variety of dishes based on the menu. The price is not bad either. We spent $40 for 250 credits and there is a reward special when you join the first time.  Also, there is a $10 parking special as well and I don't think the parking special will last forever, so take advantage of it while you can!  

The only thing that I had a problem with is the attitudes that the bartenders had. They seemed to me that they didn't wanna be there and it was too much troubles to take our orders.

Overall, I still enjoyed a lot during my first time visit of this location and I am looking forward to go again!
Zero stars, this place is a train wreck. Not enough waiters and the food is terrible. Will be gone in less than a couple months. RIP Bagger #Fail

I always have a great experience when I visit BevMo. The staff is always pretty friendly and knowledgeable. I love the selection they have and for the most part, the prices are reasonable. The only issue that I have is that they are not open 24/7, but I suppose that is only in a perfect world.
How do people like this?!?! The music is the most terrible noise I've ever heard. I think it made my ears bleed.

Breakfast was great, we even went two times on our vacation. As for the service, it was horrible. The first day I asked a waiter where we go to be seated, he gave me nothing but attitude and asked us what does it look like? And then pointed us to a table that was just cleaned. I asked nicely there was no need for the attitude. The second time we knew what to do, but our waitress had absolutely no personality, it really seemed like she just didn't care. I asked for water when we first ordered our drinks and I didn't get it until we paid. Every time she walked by I reminded her, but still had to wait. That meal we found a hair in our potatoes as well. It was pretty nasty but they were kind enough to take that item off the bill. And the last thing we all really disliked about this place was that they automatically put an 18% gratuity on your check. That service should not be tipped that much. Especially since it was so slow and it wasn't that packed while we were waiting for our food. If you don't really care about the quality of service then go here the food is excellent. Just check it before you start eating it.

I've been to this place a few times to get eggs and meats and while sometimes there is a wait (I mean, it's a busy market on a Saturday or Sunday), I've always received great services and their prices are on point for what I need. Most recently I went in for chicken and red meat for kabobs on the grill and asked for some guidance on best red meat to use. I was not only guided to a great item, but the gentleman who was assisting me also precut all the meat into the perfect size pieces for my kabobs. They turned out great. This is my go to spot in the Market!

Self-bikini waxing is one of the few skills of which late pregnancy has robbed me, so the positive Yelp reviews led me to try the services at European Wax Center.

I was very pleased by how easy it was to get a next-day appointment for a Saturday morning, though this was probably helped by the fact that I had no preference for who performed my wax.  I showed up a few minutes before my appointment and received a friendly greeting with just a touch of the "hard sell" for some of their package deals before I was led back for my wax.  To be fair, the packages did appear to offer great prices if one were going to be going for regular waxing, and they were not pushy when I declined.  As an added bonus, I was able to take advantage of their 50% off your first wax deal without buying a package.

The wax experience itself was solidly above average.  It seemed a little unusual that my waxer (I forget her name) remained in the room while I depantsed and got situated on the table, but it's not like I was preparing to be modest.  She worked quickly and was appropriately chatty to reduce the awkwardness.  The wax was a comfortable temperature, there was NO double dipping, and only a few of the pulls caused noticeable discomfort.  When I got home I found only some minimal wax residue, minimal skin irritation, and zero stray hairs even though there was no tweezing done after the wax.

As an aside, I don't know why their special proprietary wax formulation has to have so much coloring in it.  It goes on so thick and glossy that I felt a little like I was being prepped to appear in a porn version of Blue Man Group.

I live down the street from Edleys and to be honest the first time I went I would've given this place a 1 star review (went in 2013). BUT a friend of mines wanted to eat there a few weeks ago so we went and I ended up getting the chicken sandwich and it was really good. The staff is awesome and they're very friendly!

Tonight I ordered through the "favor" app and I got the chicken sandwich with banana pudding and mac & cheese. Of course the chicken sandwich was awesome, banana pudding was average, the mac & cheese was disgusting. It was flavorless and whatever was sprinkled on top was gross. I think next time I will just stick to the chicken sandwich.

If you like slow service and surly wait staff, this is the place for you. The food is decent, but it's no fun when you have to keep asking for service like a napkin, possibly a fork and knife. The drinks took 15 minutes to arrive. They forgot to put our food order in so we had to wait 20 more minutes. We won't go back there again.

Enjoyable atmosphere, good music/musicians, & a super sweet waitress made this place enjoyable. I was really hoping I would love this place but the menu is limited and our food wasn't great. We got the fried pickles as an appetizer and the Nashville Hot Chicken & biscuit with Fries and tator-tots for dinner. The biscuit buns were great, I actually think I would've enjoyed them alone with butter and jelly more than I did on the sandwich. The chicken was hard to chew and not very spicy. The French fries were nothing to write home about but the tarot-tots were great! They're homemade and much better than the boring steak fries. The fried pickles were good, large portion so easy to share with a group of 5-6! Overall just an ok experience, I'd probably pick a different place next time to get the real honky-Tonk Nashville vibes.

Called on a Wednesday to schedule an appt. They asked if I would be waiting for the car, I said yes. They said it would be best to schedule on Friday as I would not have to wait as long. I came 15 min before my 9am appt. and 2 hours later they still have not gotten to the car. They estimate another 30 min to get to it and about an hour for the PA state inspection. I seem to recall this happening last time I was in for inspection so I should have known.
French crepes, french service. Took forever to get our food while the wait staff noisily bused and washed dishes next to our table. Not worth the 2 hours or the headache.

My brother and I were excited to try this place for the first time for lunch today.  Got there at 10:50 (open at 11:00) and no sign of anyone around.  Finally at 11:00, someone came rushing in the side door and opened the front door.  Was not impressed when entering.  Musty smelling and not very clean.  My brother and I both ordered the luncheon cannelloni and side salad.  Side salad was great.  Cannelloni was ok, but I've tasted better.  When they brought it out, it appeared to be steaming, but upon cutting it, it was just lukewarm and the noodles were a little undercooked.  They need to clean the place up before I go back.

I was surprised at how good the Mandalay chicken was! I will definitely go back for more of that. They give you a huge heap of fried rice which is so-so. It didn't have much flavor and was a bit disappointing. Could eat the chicken all day and all night tho.

This is a cute little place-- very reminiscent of the pizza parlor I used to visit as a kid.  They have a nice outdoor seating area that is very cozy, too.  Overall the food was OK.  Service was OK, too.  I asked for ranch and the server totally forgot about it.  They have some good deals with the Axxess card, so it's definitely worth a trip if you have that.  The leftover pizza tasted differently than any I had had before--it left a layer of fat on my tongue.

Ive eaten here many times and really enjoyed the food but they ust have changed hands or something  because the salads were soggy - took it back and the second one was absolutely no different...the chicken is really not the original chickenďl!...disappointed

When they first opened, I came here a couple of times for dinner. Initially, they were almost as good as Minado's (another big-name buffet) but recently, the selection and quality has downgraded a lot. Don't know who's in management now but it sure looks like they're trying to cut costs down and have become very Americanized with their menu. I'm pretty sure I can get fries at McDonald's. Why bother having sushi buffet in their name if the Americanized foods outnumbers the sushi they offer? Was looking forward towards some beef short rib and seafood soup and of course an ample variety of sushi - but those are no longer offered. Me and my group arrived about 3 hours before closing time on a Thursday night while it was still sort of packed (we had to park in the back). However, it appeared that they stopped making sushi around that time, leaving only a couple plain pieces on the serving plates. I'm sure the 10 cooks they got behind the island could've replenished that. The price has went down slightly but the selection went down greatly. 

PROS:
Very attentive and sharp-looking waiters.
Good ambience, interior design.
Nice hostess.
Nice bathroom.

CONS:
Limited sushi selection. No premium rolls.
A lot of offerings are Americanized or fried everything.
No green tea ice cream or yogurt.
Dishes are not replenished fast enough when empty. 

My suggestion: GO TO MINADO'S to get your money's worth. 

Until then, I would not be returning until I hear that this place has improved.

Actual service received was adequate, the atmosphere in which it was provided was not. 
While sitting on a glorifed bar stool for 30 minutes of brow shaping (via tweezing) only two feet away from the receptionist desk, I was distracted by less than hushed social and work conflcit discussions from the receptionist & staff members. 
Regardless of the service all guests should be granted the privacy, relaxation, and comfort they deserve. The spa requests no cell phone use to preserve the "tranquil, relaxing atmosphere", practice what you preach.

It was busy this time but considering this is like my tenth time here, I've come to have very low expectations. There is a honda dealership near by and coffee isn't so great. Worth walking to Dunkin for coffee. However if you order anything but coffee be aware. I often get the wrong items, wrong quantity or even don't get things. Today was particularly bad. It's Mother's Day and it seems they have been stockpiling the items because the muffins were sooooo dry. As in they crumbled just getting the wrapper off the bottom. It was like eating croutons that were muffin flavor. Very dissatisfied. Coffee was good as always so whatever. Good stinks but coffee is close to where I drop of car so most likely I'll be back. Something better than nothing.

After walking around last night we decided to stop in and have a late meal. 

First thing that stood out was the friendly and attentive service. I wish I could remember the young lady's name but she was great. 

The beer was ice cold which hit the spot on the a hot night in New Orleans. 

Gumbo and shrimp poboy were very good. 

Not really a fan of the deserts but other than that will definitely be back.

I didn't spend much time in here but only to grab a road Hand Grenade. You can get it with or without ice.. I opted for ice because it was hotter than my body could handle, and was happy. You have to try one of these since they're advertised all over Bourbon.

My advice? Get 2.. but not 4 :)

Was here with a reserved group.  Was very noisy, took the server quite a while to get our check (was one that we all paid together)  Love the one downtown but the food here was not very good.  Chips were soggy and salsa no kick.  Have been here before and was good food and service.  Seems this location is quite inconsistent on food and service.

I am a huge fan of Bully's food. I think they have delicious pulled pork and sides, but their service tends to be slow and not good quality. We had our rehearsal dinner here and while the food was delicious, they only had one person working the entire back room (catering for about 40 people). Drinks were not being served, we ran out of food (even though we paid for it, it was very disorganized. I would definitely recommend eating here if you are looking for good bbq, but I think they need to reconsider their catering and poor service.

My husband and I came here for Memorial Day weekend. It was surprisingly not busy and my husband decided to come here based on the burger reviews. They helped us out fairly quickly. I was surprised a bit by the prices, yet I naively figured that it must mean the food is made with extra care. My husband ordered a burger and I ordered the roasted chicken. The burger, based on my husband's review, was delicious. My chicken, on the other hand, was a disaster. At first glance the chicken looked huge, so seeing some burned sections didn't bother me as much, that was until I noticed that what wasn't burned was pure bone. The couple of bites I took of it ended up being extremely dry and had no other flavor except for burnt. I mainly ate the mashed potatoes, which was also a disappointment, and considered the $22+ we spent on my meal alone, as a lost. Maybe I was unfortunate and the chef simply got distracted, yet for their meal prices I expected so much more. In regards to the service, like I mentioned, we got greeted and seated quickly. The only negative thing I saw was that some of the employees who I think had just ended their shift were sitting in the bar talking with the staff that was still on the clock and they complaint throughout our entire meal about their work and other guests. I didn't find it appropriate to be talking badly about previous guests while current ones were eating their dinner just 10 feet away.

No major complaints about the service, the ambiance, the view. I doubt I'll be suggesting anyone go out of their way to come here though. My NY strip was requested MR and came out the MW side of M. That is pretty much unacceptable in my book considering that I don't often go out looking to pay for steak.. and this is a steakhouse.

On a more positive note, the creme brulee was excellent for dessert, and my friend appeared to enjoy the sauce his mussels came in.

The shop was busy when I entered so I was not greeted.  I noticed a sign in sheet on the counter and being reasonably intelligent I signed in on the next available slot.  After waiting for over twenty minutes I rechecked and found that my name had been inadvertently crossed off.  Not wanting to make a scene with a person I expected to then cut my hair I left.  I am probably not going to give them a second chance.  On the plus side I did enjoy watching some March Madness action while I waited.

I am giving one star only because the woman who runs this place was nice enough. First off, what she is selling on Groupon as a luxury, hour long, herbal detox wrap, is really the ItWorks product, a multilevel marketing product that has become rather big right now, however I have friends who distribute for them, could have had a party at my house and got the wrap for free. Also I am pretty sure it is against their regulations to use Groupon to sell, also no where on her website or Groupon did it say ItWorks, but I will be contacting them later today. Secondly, she literally took me to the bathroom, applied the wrap and sent me on my way...not the relaxing "hour long herbal wrap" experience that I had I visioned when I purchased this deal and proceeded to hire a babysitter for my 2 year old. All in all I feel totally ripped off. I will be contacting groupon as well as ItWorks and hopefully this scammer gets shut down.
Worst sushi I've ever had. Worse than all you can eat sushi. The fish is gross, the rolls are terrible. They fall apart. It's not good sushi at all.

I have been platinum player for years there food is great customer service is good but gaming machines are a joke I have noticed in last couple months$200 lasts no less than 45 minutes and I was only playing$0.45 a hand I am seriously considering going to different Casinos I love going there for everything but this place is becoming corporate nightmare and it's far away from being what it used to be

Barone's has all the right ingredients for the possibility of a successful office party.  The restaurant is divided into several separate spaces, so we were able to get crazy with our annual re-gifting game without annoying the rest of the diners.  The pasta dishes were delicious.  They accommodated requests for gift and wine tables to be set up before we arrived.

Unfortunately, there were far more cons than pros.

Barone's seriously lacks organization and professionalism for large group planning.  When I called to ask about pricing packages, I was told that tax would be waived if we paid in cash.  This meant that the total cost per person fell within my budget goal, and so I went ahead and made a reservation.  A couple weeks later, when I stopped in to choose the menu, I was told that the person I spoke with on the phone was misinformed, and that we would be charged 5% tax rather than 7%.  Since this would raise the cost per person by a dollar, and I was not going to go around asking everyone who had already RSVP'd to give me another dollar, I had to go back the next day to speak with a manager.

I explained my concerns to the manager, hoping that he'd lower the cost of the dinner so that the 5% increase in price wouldn't change my total cost per person.  My main concern was that since my total cost included a tip, the restaurant would take what they were owed, but the servers wouldn't receive their 20% cut.  The manager assured me that it would not be an issue because HE would be the server the evening of our event, and since he understood the situation, he just wouldn't take tip.  "Seriously, don't worry about it," I was assured.

So the night of the party came, and there were several red flags from the start.  First, the manager was nowhere to be seen.  It would have been nice to be greeted by someone who was familiar with all of my requests upon arrival, perhaps a review of the menu that had been chosen.  But when I got there, the hostess simply pointed me in the direction of the back room and I was on my own.

Said room happened to be freezing.  I mentioned it to a worker, and was told that the heat was just turned on for that room.  The room never warmed to a comfortable temperature, and I actually had a few diners who ate their entire dinners with their winter coats on.

The bathroom that was at the back of our room was gross.  It looked like someone had given himself a haircut in there before we arrived, little snips of hair all over the sink, and a canister of hair pomade on the counter.  A friend had to wipe up the sink before the rest of the party arrived.  There were also no paper towels and the room was running low on toilet paper.  It took several requests before these changes were made.

The service itself was good.  From what I experienced at my own table, drinks and food arrived in a timely manner, and the servers were friendly.  Aside from the pasta course, no one really seemed impressed by their food.  Portions were huge, but taste was forgettable.  The manager finally showed up about halfway through our dinner to ask how we wanted our dessert served, cut up all the cakes for us, and then disappeared again.

More issues, or maybe the same issue, arrived when it was time to pay.  We were charged for an extra diner; I mistakenly said that we had 31, and that is how many the manager put on our bill, despite the fact that I know he MUST have counted how many plates went out so that he'd be able to tally up our dues later on.  He had to know I had miscounted.

Oh, and hey, remember when I was told that gratuity would NOT be included and that the manager would be our server so we shouldn't worry about an inadequate tip?  Yeah, well gratuity WAS included, and we had not collected enough money to meet the total.  I had to ask the poor server (who was NOT the manager) to remove the gratuity from the check so that we could afford the bill, and then I ended up throwing in some extra cash that I (luckily, and uncharacteristically) had in my wallet to come closer to what the total should have been with a 20% tip.  *MAJOR GUILT*

The manager made an appearance again as we were on our way out, and we pointed to a large bottle of wine that had been opened but not otherwise touched, offering it to him and his servers.  "Ugh, I don't drink wine.  That stuff is like grape juice," he scoffed, as his cell phone rang with some trancey club beats.  Sorry playa.  I'll leave you to your Red Bull and vodka and fist pump my way out the door.  And my coworkers and I will NOT be back for next year's winter soiree.

Let's be honest, anything that isn't a carwash or used car lot is a win as an addition to 12south.  We've got a long way to go but we're getting there.  I must say that we were incredibly excited to have another dinner option within walking distance and the food at Edley's does not disappoint.  It is EXCELLENT.  The nachos with chicken are amazing.  The brisket (only available at lunch) is phenomenal.  The sides are approximately 1000 times better than Jack's (which isn't saying much because Jack's are an atrocity).  We have been several times and love it except their one HUGE detractor:  The Service Model.

It 's easy enough to figure out where and how to order but where to sit, now that is another question entirely.  It is relatively small inside when the weather isn't warm (and the deck an option).  Lately, at dinner time, you have numerous groups of people with one person in line ordering and 3 others hovering, death-staring, and annoying the crap out of those actually eating.  When a table actually opens up, the Black Friday rush to claim it is unbearable.  We've left several times to burger up or mafioza's where they take names...on a list...like adults.  Cafeteria style?  Really?   Come on gang.  We had enough of that in high school.

Don't go with kids, don't go after five with more than four, be prepared for an adventure.

First, I would like to state that my family and I have long used Enterprise exclusively for our rental car needs. As a frequent traveler, I often find myself having to use rental cars, and due to an unstable car used for local travel, my father also uses rental cars often when going on longer trips.

Yesterday, my father planned to make a cross-state trip in order to attend a funeral of a family member. Being that my father does not carry a credit card or cell phone, he arranged to have someone from Enterprise pick him up at home. For the car, he planned to use a check as a deposit and pay in cash. We have done this on many, many occasions, and never encountered any problems.

Once at Enterprise, located some three miles from our home, my father was told that they would not accept his check, and that furthermore, they would not allow him to even use a phone to make a call so that someone could pick him up. As anyone who has been in 90 degree heat in St. Louis can attest, this left him with a very, very uncomfortable walk home. What makes his particular situation much worse, however, is that last year he had open heart surgery following a stroke, and that he has moreover been unable to walk at full capacity for years due to physical trauma that left several of his vertebrae out of alignment. 

Despite his pleas for the very basic request for a single phone call (they even give you one of these in prison), and despite the horrific heat and an explanation of his physical condition, the representative from Enterprise told my father to take a hike. Thus not only was my father not able to attend the funeral of a family member, but he was also put at very grave physical risk. 

Needless to say I will never, ever use Enterprise again, and I will actively discourage everyone I know from doing so.

Meh.

My coworkers and I ordered about 10 roast beef sandwiches today.  I have eaten here in the past.  The quality went down considerably.  The cheese on my sandwich wasn't even melted.  They skimped on the meat and overall it wasn't a very good sandwich.  Even the horseradish tasted bland.  It added some moisture but barely any heat.

I got the impression that this place has turned into another American business trying to increase its margins by sacrificing quality.

But then again food in this entire area is pretty terrible.

2 hour 45 minute wait to get tires replaced (with an appointment):

I arrive early for a 9 o'clock appointment to replace all 4 of my tires. At the front desk, I'm told that it will be at least 1 1/2 to 2 hours before the car will be done. The front desk person explains that he has multiple cars to service before he can even get to mine. In my head I'm wondering, "what's the point in making an appointment then?" 

He literally has 5 cars to service before getting to mine. I think this could be due to the fact that Pep Boys has a 30 minute oil change promise. If you don't have your oil changed in 30 minutes or less, you get a voucher for a free oil change in the future. So anyone who drops their car off for an (unscheduled) oil change gets help before scheduled services. After all, appointments are a guaranteed payment to them, whereas the oil change folks only pay if they get quick service. 

My car gets pulled into the service area at 10:15. I look over every 15 minutes or so and it looks as if no one is even near the car. It seems as if they simply moved the car to give the impression of progress. I didn't leave until 11:45.

Bar far some of the worst food I've ever had.  I ordered the jambalaya, I've heard that it is a traditional New Orleans dish, and I was clear that it was not fresh and had been sitting out.  The rice was mushy and cold.  For the price it was not worth it at all.  I will go somewhere else for dinner to make up for the experience.

Dam I was so happy with this food last night. Today is a different story.  I'm sicker than a dog. Working my ass off muscling my way through this.  The day is almost over that's what I'm pushing for.  The mana from heaven turned or to be the mana from hell.  Montezuma's reveng, Bubble guts like the volcanoe is active all day.  I would never eat there again.  Whoever runs the joint needs to manage the food better because the only thing you got running was my asshole. Thanks jas. Never again.  Don't recommend this to anybody.

I have had a good experience the few times I have been here. Except yesterday. We went for dinner at 7 and our food took about an hour or more to come out. 
One of us ordered a salad and when we asked around the hour mark if our food was going to be coming she said she didn't want to rush us because he got that salad, then she said it was because of something else...which didn't make sense either. 


The waitress didn't bring me a refill of my drink I had to ask her.  She did bring me a refill without asking after we were all done with dinner and we were paying (to make up for forgetting earlier).  I didn't need it and it was wasted. I didn't even accept it as I don't want to waste food. 

She asked if we wanted more bread, we said yes (because dinner was taking so long) but then she forgot to bring it and I had to ask her for the bread again. 

She forgot to bring a side we asked for with dinner. We had to remind her. 

The restaurant was half full so I am not sure what the problem was. Every time I asked her for something for the second time she kept saying, "of course that would be my pleasure" as if I didn't already request it?? weird.  If it was busy, ok,  but it was half empty. 

The food was still good that's why I still gave it 3 stars but the service was very poor yesterday. 

I am not sure I will go back. I actually think it was the longest I ever waited for food. Oh well.

Things have changed. This place use to be my favorite. So good and fast. Every time I go there it's a completely new staff and they seem to get younger and dumber every time. The food isn't even the same. It's so bland now. No worth the wait or the hassle of dealing with the young staff who are definitely not trained.

Service was the best part of the dining experience. The food was decent but nothing to write home about. I had the Two for Tuscon lunch which had a pretty good corn tamale but the beans, rice, and enchilada were just like every other average Mexican restaurant in the world. 

If you are trying to impress your date this is probably not the first choice, but if you want to spend a 10 bucks for a decent lunch then this is the place to eat.

S-L-O-W, apathetic service. Three people behind the counter, and my drink came up before I even got to the counter.  I wanted to beg for them to hurry up. It was all I could do not to yell- please, pay attention, take care of the customers standing right in front of you!  But I didn't.  And I stood in line 10 mins, while they helped the two people in front of me.

This particular location was a bit dirty as well.  Not my favorite Starbucks, that's for sure.

Beautiful view next to the drawbridge with plenty of activity on the water and we had a very sweet waitress that was extremely helpful. The beer selection was just ok, but on par for the area in general (there are few craft beers). My wife and I ordered the blackened grouper on a chopped salad and at the time I thought it was not fresh grouper, but might have just been frozen,  because it was not very good. I now believe that it was Tilapia as we had grouper several times in the next few days. All of the grouper we had at other restaurants in the area were much better. I suppose it's possible the cook just made an error. We also thought it quite strange that the chopped salad was actually NOT chopped. It was mixed leafy field greens, but it was still good, although my wife thought the dressing tasted bottled and not made by the house. I would go back because of the location, friendly staff and entertainment value of the water, boats and bridge.

I have been curious about this place for a while, but got invited to lunch there yesterday.  This is my experience.

At 11:40 the parking lot was completely empty and we thought perhaps it was not open.  On the way over I joked that churches make me uncomfortable...in a few moments I was about to find out just how prescient my statement was.

The door was cracked open so the four of us escorted ourselves inside and walked into a completely empty building.  There was no host, no servers, no diners, and dark mahogany chairs that looked like they must cost a fortune.  The whole decor was remarkably dark including the soaring vaults and ceiling joists.  It reminded me of the blackness of my soul.  Not a good feeling.  

We waited for a few minutes and a hostess eventually came to seat us.  When greeting us she said "Oh, we thought it was going to be a quiet day because of all the rain!"  Apparently 4 customers at lunchtime is not a slow day at Redemption.

We were seated in the middle of the room and the first thing that came to mind was the eerie yellow lighting caused by the sun shining through the stained glass.  When I say "stained glass" I do not mean pretty religious renderings in vibrant color, I mean that the glass just had a yellow tinge as if it was stained.  

The lighting would continue to affect me and one fellow diner throughout the entire meal, which lasted approximately an hour and a half due to slow cooked meals, an apparent server crash upon attempted bill payment.  The hue of the sun coming through the glass affected me the way fluorescent lighting affects some people/causes migraines/anxiety--if you are one of those people I caution you not to eat here during the day.  

Okay, so now the good stuff.  Despite the anxiety onsets causing me to want to get up and move swiftly towards the exit punctuating the entire meal, the meal itself was quite good.

The warm, fresh bread that they brought out twice during the meal was that perfect combination of crusty on the outside, chewy/warm/moist on the inside.  I ordered a cup of gumbo to start the meal and it was served in what appeared to be a pewter mortar (although I hope for health reasons it was not pewter) which stayed bloody scorching hot throughout the entire cautious slurping process.  They could have dumped this stuff onto medieval castle invaders and melted them promptly.

Three of us ordered a paneed chicken breast with a bacon cream reduction, wilted spinach, and mac & cheese.  The fourth ordered a pecan crusted catfish over crawfish risotto.  The meal took approximately 45 minutes to arrive, which isn't unheard of, but is sad in a place where you are the only customers.  

Everything was pretty good.  Nobody had that "OMG mmmmmmm" experience that occurs so frequently in this city, but everybody agreed that it was all yum-o.  After the wait food always tastes a lot better.  

Concerned that we were embarking on a 2 hour lunch service that we were not sure our bosses would agree we were entitled to, we implored our waiter to bring us our check.  We split it 4 ways, which sometimes takes a little while to punch in, but after 20 or so minutes the waiter returned and said there was a "blackout" and the system had to be re-set  (feeling trapped, anxiety rising, can't get out of here, agonizing jaundice lighting).

Finally got our tabs, $17.00 a piece, over-tipped, made a b-line for the parking lot.  

If the restaurant is called Redemption, parking lot should be called Freedom.
Tried to like this place, but the owners can be quite rude. I have learned our lesson and will be staying away from here. I like to support friendlier local businesses.

Omg. No...Just no. I ordered a cheesesteak pizza with fried onion chopped small. I received a burnt , hard large disc of mess. It was so hard and dry i was speechless. I took the loss and never ordered again.

I was truly disappointed at the serving sizes and also the quality of the food for the price. Also you could see that there was some sort of black grime on the floor, the whole establishment was very unsanitary.
Mozzarella sticks and crab cheese fries were excellent but waiting for a drink at the bar to wash it down was painful. Need more bartenders on a Sat night.

You average pub. Nothing special, but nothing bad either. The place itself is pretty cool, lots of TVs so good when there is a game on.

I went for lunch one day and it was right after a VIllanova basketball game, so the place got mobbed within 20 minutes (the game was at the Wells Fargo center so I guess they all headed over after that). 

I have a turkey wrap, pretty good. Fries were very good. They had a good selection of beers on tap as well. I also like the big windows out to Lancaster Ave, there isn't much to look at, but it opens the place up alot. 

Descent spot to grab a few drinks.

The food here is a three, however the service and approach to the business is akin to one of those rest stops on the Turnpike.   This restaurant is staffed by a very young, inexperienced and unprofessional staff, probably due to the steady flow of lunchtime traffic from the several larger companies in the immediate area.   Don't get me wrong; if the food were a five, I'd sit on the floor - but the kitchen is a far cry from that. 

There is far better in the immediate area, for about the same money.

Disgusting. Pillows were stained with big yellow puddles of slobber, (not mine) and stunk horribly. The stains were in plain sight as the pillow cases were tattered and didn't completely encase the yuck. I used rolled towels as  my pillow. Planned to stay there for 3 nights, left as soon as the sun came up. So gross... Bad odors, bathroom nasty. ..hair on the bathroom floor, also not mine
The duck fries are a must but one hair in my Mac and cheese and well to the " do not go list " it has become

If you are looking for a random spot to find random stuff this it the place for you.

Everything is in impressive order and is all priced.  Unfortunately things are often over priced and the owner will not be talked down.
This place has gone downhill in cleanliness. Who wants to do their was in a dirty place?? Owner needs to get on this.

Thought I'd update ... installed the control board myself ... not too difficult. The job took me about 2 hours ... one to map the wiring, 30 min to gather confidence, 30 min to wire. If I were a contractor, I would guess about one hour needed with travel time. I'm sticking with the my original impression about the contractor's quote.
Having car trouble is frustrating enough .. and then you go here. Come on guys, good customer service isn't that hard.

I've been here multiple times and always love their convenient location and decent drinks (they can make a good Irish Car Bomb). We came here often to throw get togethers or just to meet people prior to going to the movies. The food isn't bad at all and it's rather convenient getting drunk and playing a bunch of games to break up whatever dullness you're having with the crowd you're with. So after starting this review I realize I have very little to write. So I'm going to assume that this review will be meant to inform anyone that they can expect a goods drink and a decent atmosphere. A huge bonus is the fire pit outside to keep you warm during the "cold" Tucson nights.

If you are going to be charging $27 for any plate of food, even if it is a special, I expect not to see trash behind a tree, or piles of dishes waiting on a service table. Your food is good. But dining out isn't just about the food. It's about the ambiance and the experience, and if you aren't focused on those details then how can I take your food and your prices seriously?

I have NEVER given any review on Yelp before. Mainly because I don't think it's any of business either way. However I wanted to give this review because I think you (The Block) deserves praise and customers, and I think cleaning up these details will do you good. 

By the way, your service was good. So Kudos.

Chill spot... open air, good ambience.  Tasty food, not upscale which is fine.  I had it as a solid 3... until it took 20mins to cash out when a total of 8 people were there.  Couldn't tell if the barkeep was with it... Super disappointing.

Are trip didn't start well when walking in and a lady was returning a pizza. She claimed It was the worst pizza she ever had. (she wasn't far off)! It was editable at best, the fries where cold, and no taste to them at all. I had got a calzone which was full of ricotta and pineapple but not mozzarella. The pineapple taste like the can it came in and the marinara sauce on the side was uneatable. I would never go there again.

Three of us had dinner here before the Philly pops concert. I am being generous giving them 3 stars.  I had a glass of wine for 12 bucks. We all shared a potato pancake appetizer. It was delicious but since we had no forks we had to wait forever for the waitress to notice. Actually, one of the managers helped us.  I had the meatlloaf platter. The meatloaf was good, but the gravy was reall really sweet. My dining partners had a turkey club and the other had a cheeseburger. There was nothing special about the food and the service was poor. They just too busy. With only one glass of wine the check was 70.00. We could have had the same dinner at Oregon diner for half the price.

This was my favorite pizza place to go, till today! I called in advance to place my order like I always do. When I came for pick up, they tell told me they didn't have my order? Manager Stevie tells me they can place the order and I have wait 25-30 mins for a pie. Hell no!!!! I called in advance so I wouldn't have to wait and someone there never placed my order! I am a very dissatisfied customer and I didn't like how this situation was handled.

I have had good food and one decent service experience here, but it's been a long time since either of those things have happened here.  We arrived here at 5:00pm on a Sunday and ordered 2 appetizers and a salad.  After inquiring about the long wait, our server seemed unconcerned and told us they were busy.  It took 50 minutes for our food to come out, with no apology.  My meal was cold.  I informed the manager of our long wait and cold food, and although she offered to comp my meal, she did not offer any other solutions or see if I wanted anything else.  It's a shame because we like this place and would like to continue to support our neighborhood restaurant.

Really not that good. Ate here with my sister for brunch today and was really underwhelmed. We both ordered omelettes that were overdone (hers contained maybe a crumble of feta at most).  Our toast came out with no butter, which we had to ask for unfortunately. Jones is also pretty pricey for what you get. Four dollars for poor quality drip coffee is absurd.  Can't say I'd recommend this place for brunch at least.

Inconsistent...WE were so excited to try this lovely, atmospheric restaurant.  The setting and our waiter were wonderful BUT our food (overall) batted about 50%.  The hamburger was terrific; one dish with pork tenderloin was great ...but the "Ways and Means" dish was not good ...and the fries (served with the hamburger) were cold.  The cheese plate appetizer was disappointing...old grapes and very little fruit...(way too skimpy)...  The bread pudding was very good...the custard was cold-refrigerated... and only fair.  Gosh ... this restaurant COULD be great!!!  But...we were disappointed....

The place is cute enough.... I guess. The tables are a little too close together for my taste, but I guess that doesn't matter because the place was empty!!! So then, please tell me WHY, with so much space in the place, our waitress crammed  other patrons at the 3 tables closest to ME??  That's a serious pet peeve of mine. I like to BREATHE, and I honestly felt suffocated.

Their crepes fall into 5 different categories :Les Bourgeoises (rich and light taste),  "Les Classiques (traditional taste), Les Savoureuses , ( seafood delight ), Les Tentantes (very creative mix), and Les Rebelle (Revolutionary, delicious taste,  Pizza Crêpes). Then,of course, you have the brunch menu and the sweet crepes.

I ordered  the Chatelet" off the brunch menu. It's a savory crepe with scrambled eggs, smoked salmon, swiss cheese with boiled sautéed potatoes for $13.00. I thought it might taste better w/ onions, so I asked for them, and I was right. It was DELICIOUS! My potatos were just ok.

My bf ordered a sweet crepe with banana, strawberry and nutella. he wasn't impressed. He thought it was too "crispy." We both ordered black tea to drink, but they did have a variety of loose teas to choose from as well.

Overall, I'd say, the place is far too overpriced, because it simply doesn't have the ambience to charge so much money. They're also pretty stingy on the portions.

We will returm, but RELUCTANTLY!  lol

I've been to a few hibachi restaurants , this was my least favorite , to start I made a reservation they wouldn't let us four people sit down , we had one person on the way who was stuck in traffic and got a little lost , there was plenty of tables open and they could have allowed us to sit then we weren't seated until 30 mins after the reservations , the guy that cooked the food was not very entertaining honestly the only reason they got 3 stars is cause the waitress was a sweet heart and the rice was delicious.

I love the food here, especially the small pizza lunch special. However, the service here is ways so slow. I can never pick up my food at the estimated time I'm given online and I'm constantly having to try and grab someone's attention from the back to be helped. They always seem understaffed, which doesn't make sense for a lunch rush. The only thing keeping me coming is my love for their deep dish pizza with turbo crust. If you want to eat here frustration-free, I suggest you come at a time that other people normally don't eat around.

We just finished our stay at The Saint and we couldn't get out of there fast enough!  We stayed 6 nights and I think this was the worst hotel we've ever stayed in. The  bed was rock hard and so uncomfortable that we had trouble sleeping. We asked for a different room and were told that the would sort it out and call us...they never did call. We followed up with the manager who said she would see what she could do for us. We never heard from her again either. I should say that she offered us a free breakfast buffet for that morning. It was 10:55 when she offered it and breakfast ended at 11. Total slap in the face. 

While our room was clean, we had to request shampoo, etc to be replenished.  They didn't automatically do that.  Our belongings were shoved around to make room for towels, etc and dirty face clothes were left hanging in the bathroom day after day. 

While the location of the hotel is fantastic, we will never stay here again. Stuff can happen during a stay so it becomes about how the staff deals with it. The staff at The Saint just didn't care.
Ok the picture of that hamburger was exactly how it tasted I don't think I ever had a hamburger that was as bad as this one I sent it back and I won't be back. 711 has better burgers

I have been to the location in Philly but this was my first visit here. The décor is modern and spacious, but lacks the old school feel of the original. Our waiter was very knowledgeable about the menu and attentive. The wine list is fare, but I would like to see a few more Italian wine offerings. We started with the fried calamari and it was by far the best in Media. Very fresh with a light breading, so you could actually taste the squid. The marinara sauce accompanying it was good as well. My wife choose the braciole, which is a special they have every Sunday. This was her first time having the dish, and it may be her last. The meat was a little tough, and the stuffing had a somewhat overpowering taste of spices, primarily rosemary, that threw the flavor off. I personally liked it, and ended up eating most of it as she ate my chicken parm. My dish was excellent. The chicken was tender and moist, with a very flavorful breading. I had a side of gnocchi with it and they were delicious. They were cooked just right, and were not too dense. All in all a great dining experience. It seems every time we go for Italian in Media we end up at Fellini's, but we will definitely be back to Spasso!

I've been coming here for a few months for facials and massages.  Ike and Heather do a great job. Today I booked a spa day for my mother and I. It was a facial, massage and pedicures. It was scheduled to last 2.75 hours. Each person that service did a great job, absolutely no complaints about that. My complaint is that we were there 5 hours. Scheduling was a huge issue, I'm not sure I would book a spa day there again. The spa did comp a manicure that I had added in at the last minute but come on, five hours!!!

Literally the worst rental company in SB! I have a pretty high tolerance for awful rental companies, having lived in SB for 5 years and dealing with one scummy slum lord to the next but... This place should be ashamed to call themselves a rental management company.. If housing wasn't so hard to come by in SB, and people ACTUALLY had a choice instead of having their hand forced financially, I know this "Co." would be out of business.
I just recently was able to get off the hook of living in one of their rentals. The rental itself was nice and spacious (no problem with the property). 
My issues lay entirely with the rental company. First off, they are practically unreachable at all times. I called multiples times during different weekdays, and was never able to speak to my property manager. I think he maybe returned 1 call to me out of the countless messages left.  Secondly, when it came to having new roommates move in/out, they somehow lost our numerous roommate swap applications sent in! Then a few months later, acted surprised that the person who had applied many times (and told they had been approved) was residing in the apartment. 
I had a sketchy roommate move in, without Bartlein providing any kind of background. When this person did not pay rent for 2 months, I began to feel uncomfortable and provided an ultimatum for either moving out ASAP or providing owed rent and moving out at an agreed time. When I contacted Bartlein for help on this matter, they acted as if it was something they had never dealt with. I ended up paying to have the locks changed myself. I then paid to have 3 extra copies made so I could provide them to Bartlein to be up front about everything. One of their star employees told me that I would be responsible for a charge for them to make copies of the key. Seriously, these people are useless.
This brings me to the staff... Where do you find these people?! I hope future employers of Bartlein employees look at this yelp page. These employees are literally all awful. Everytime I went in, no one had any idea what was going on, everyone looked just as confused (if not more confused) than I was. Never had any problems solved by going into their office.
Needless to say, I am relieved and grateful for having moved out. Of course Bartlein kept 2/3 of my security deposit (charging me to repair broken stove, garbage disposal) that they themselves had no fixed, even after I put in multiple service requests. Honestly, losing all that money was painful, but I am okay knowing I flushed over $1600 down the toilet, but no longer have to deal with Bartlein ever again.
If you're reading this and considering renting from them, listen to allllll of these reviews! It's not worth it!

I ordered buffalo wings fried hard & a salad with romaine lettuce. My salad was delivered iceberg lettuce who still uses that it has no nutritional value besides I specifically said romaine. The wings looked like they had already cooked them had the sauce on them and placed back in the fryer they had this weird taste and looked like something from an alien movie.
So now I drive to the Sorranos to take it back I asked for the manager he made every excuse in the book. He said we usually don't give money back it's too much. How did he think I felt paying that much for gross food. I really don't like leaving negative reviews but the manager really got under my skin. Won't be ordering here again!!

A decent little place in Philly's gayborhood.  But nothing to write home about.  I do have to say, the food was actually pretty good.  They have tasty wood fired pizzas for $8 or something and hummus is so good.  But the only employee working was the bartender, who also had to wait the tables.  He gave us attitude for ordering or for not having a full list of what we might possibly want all ready for his convenience.  He actually said "I hope you aren't going to make me come back again" or something like that.  Kinda of sarcastic and annoying.  I overheard him with the same attitude at the next table. They all stared with a WTF look when he walked away.

So all in all, pretty good food and great prices but they could work on their service.
The owners need to fix the place up, the food is good though the employees are nice but not as efficient.

If I could give zero stars, I would. I have to say, I'm very upset with this hotel. I walked in and immediately smelled feces, number one. The girl that was operating the elevator was deplorable and ASTOUNDINGLY rude. The manager came out dressed as though he had just came from the gym - this is not a professional establishment. The room was atrocious and was overpriced - I could go on forever. Do not stay here.
Can't say this place isn't to bad. tried a few of the signature and was pleased. the portion size is average.  Did a pick up and the food was ready when I arrived.

Everything about the food at this place was mediocre.  We ordered the red beans and rice and cajun sampler, which included jambalaya, crawfish pie, and gumbo.  We also ordered fried calamari in case my daughter didn't like her red beans and rice.  First the gumbo came out.  It was decent gumbo, nothing special.  I ate it all and it was mildly spicy, but was not nearly the best gumbo I've ever had and this was my first time in Louisiana.  Then came the red beans and rice.  Once again it was okay.  The sausage was dried and it was smoked sausage not andouille. The flavor was rather bland.  Again not terrible just nothing special.  Blues City Deli back home in St. Louis is decidedly better.  The jambalaya was dry and lacking in flavor as well.  The Zataranes I make on my stove is better.  The crawfish pie was actually the best item by far.  Golden flakey pie stuffed with crawfish and the crawfish in a cream sauce poured over the top.  Nothing we had was insulting but I'm sure there is far better cajun food in the French Quarter. On a positive note the view on Jackson square was excellent.

My boyfriend and I stopped in for lunch recently. I immediately LOVED the concept: comfort food and comfort style. I loved the interior design, and got giddy when we were seated at a two-person booth. I dug it, despite the tight space. 

Given this excitement, I wish I could give it 3.5 stars. IMO, the food was okay but not great. We split the tuna tacos and the breakfast burrito. The tuna was good, but the cold ingredients were a bit odd and awkward in the hard shell taco. The breakfast burrito was good, but again, not great. (I did like the addition of tater tots) The bloody mary I had was pretty tasty, though, and I'd say that overall we left satisfied.

Had high hopes for this place, love Santa Barbara.  We got a deal through Living Social or Groupon or one of those for half off.  Sounded awesome.  Called, made the reservation, asked about pets and was told pets are okay.  We get there, with our little, non-shedding dog, and are told they have a no pet policy.  Now we didn't just walk our dog though the lobby for all to see, we snuck him in in his carrier, and snuck him out and in for each walk.  We go to check out, all's well, had to pay the room tax, expected, and we were on our way.  The next day I notice there's a $100 charge on my credit card from them I knew nothing about.  I called them and they said there's a $100 cleaning fee if you have a pet because they don't allow pets.  I said there was nothing to clean, and he didn't disturb anyone.  They intentionally did not mention this to me in person at any point while we were there, shady to say the least.  Strikes one and two...

The room we got was the smallest I've ever stayed in, no doubt because we got it for half price.  First floor, right next to the front desk and lobby with a view of the parking lot, where all could see in if you didn't close the shutters.  The toilet was horrible, had to flush twice, holding the handle the whole time, every time no matter what business you did.  Strike three...

I will say the outside of this place was nice, and the decor was fine.

All in all a negative experience.  I will not recommend this place to anyone and will tell everyone I know who visits Santa Barbara to avoid this place like the plague.

I've gotten LASEK but wanted to wear my old frames to accessorize here and there so I came here to get a price quote on how much it would be to have the prescription taken out. My frames are rimless and my prescription was pretty strong (I was legally blind) so I had already paid extra to have the drilling done directly into lenses and also to have them thinned out as much as possible. With this in mind, I was already prepared to have to pay more than traditional rimmed frames even to just have the prescription removed. I was coldly greeted by a very snobbish woman who looked at me and then looked at the frames briefly and told me it would cost a lost. She basically profiled me and assumed that it would be "a lot" for me. This woman had no idea what my finances were like, nevermind the fact that she didn't have the common sense to think that I might at least be somewhat aware that rimless frames cost more to work with being that I already had a pair right there in my hand with a prescription in them. I asked if she could just give me a price instead of just saying "a lot" as if she was aware of my bank statement. She shuffled off the back where I'm sure she pretended to act like she was asking one of the people that puts prescriptions in frames (not sure what they are called). She comes back and tells me $200 which is actually way less than what I was expecting and readily prepared to pay if necessary. I would have gladly taken the offer but her obvious profiling and atrocious attitude turned me completely away. The nerve of her to treat a customer that way not knowing a single thing about them or what they can afford. I will never come back to this place and would strongly urge others not to as well. For Eyes down the street is a much nicer and welcoming establishment.

Went for BRUNCH, which was delicious. 3 stars for the food, 3 1/2 stars because you get to make your own bloody mary! My dreams came true! I didn't have to hope that they would spice the rim with old bay, or include baby gherkins on a toothpick, I got to CHOOSE!! Genius move, Jones. 

The brunch items all come with TATER TOTS! Way to make me so ecstatic during a brutal hangover. 

The LATKES are addicting. Give me a good latke with some delicious applesauce and a little creme fraiche, and I'll give you a kiss and a good bootyshake. (Those latkes go straight to my booty)

The omelette was definitely solid, though our friend's huevos rancheros were looking pretty tempting. It took everything I had not to reach across the table and grab a bite. 

Jones has a great diner feel, without being a gross greasy diner. I highly recommend, and I'll definitely go back.

Would not return. Grilled pork was like warm. Obviously microwaved. Sent it back. Microwaved it again. Got it back hot. But it was one of the toughest, dryest cuts of pork I have ever had. Terrible. They also gave us chicken instead of beef for our other dish. The only thai iced tea they offer is in a can. It was clean and the furnishings were up to date. That's the reason for two stars. But they seriously need to rethink food and service quality if they are going to stay in business.

I love Jones most for their baked mac & cheese and tomato soup/grilled cheese combo. Their take on American food is spot-on. 

My only complaint- it's a little steep for frequent lunches and I've only been to dinner a couple of times because of the high prices, but the food is always great, the service is great, and the atmosphere is great, so at least you're not wasting your money! 

Special Note: If you can get a seat by the fireplace, it's even better! If you can't get a table, the bar is usually available.

Special Treat: Sometimes it's nice to walk here from a neighboring restaurant and just get dessert- they are sooo goood!!

DISGUSTING!!! I ordered out last night: Sweet/Sour Chicken, Chicken Fried Rice and Egg Rolls. The egg rolls had some weird meat, wasn't pork, I can tell you that. Needless to say, they didn't taste very good, and this was the best part of the order. The chicken fried rice was steamed rice, not fried, still white not even browned. The chicken was rough and not looking too good. The sweet/sour chicken was horrible. The chicken pieces were thin little strips with way too much batter. It was all fried batter and very little chicken. This is the type of chicken I would expect to get at a KFC. This is the first time I ordered food and threw it all out. I can't believe people eat here. I am new to STL and if anyone can let me know where to go for good Chinese food, I would greatly appreciate it.
Could contend for the best thin in STL if they would give you an option to have something other than that awful provel cheese.  Won't come back.

Took the Sunday brunch cruise and was pretty disappointed. As many people have already stated, the food is terrible, but the service was great. If you're dying to be surrounded by a horde of enormous sweaty people on a vessel, moving down a river, then this is your jam. I feel like they may have over booked it, because once the band came on, there was zero floor space around them. Speaking of the band... holy boring. They looked like they were at their own funeral. I'm pretty sure the horn player fell asleep a time or two. I was looking forward to hearing some loud lively stuff, but these guys couldn't draw a crowd to save their lives. Save your money and head on over to Frenchmen St. It's a better, less-touristy vibe, with much better music and food.

A group of us visited La Casona for a friend's birthday.  The mofongo is amazing, great red sangria and wonderful little empanadillas for an appetizer.  The portion size is huge at a very low price.  I got the shrimpt mofongo.  Others at the table got chicken and steak mofongo.  No complaints except for the fried plantains which were dry and had zero flavor.  We had to soak them in the garlic dipping sauce to make them worth eating.  

Service leaves something to be desired but I don't think the intention is to be rude, just not top notch.  You can tell this is a family run business as the chef came from the kitchen to chat with the diners and the older gentleman behind the bar.  It seemed like a mother, father and son dynamic.  The female was extremely nice and came to the table to make sure the food was ok and to tell our friend happy birthday.  One odd note, they seem to close whenever they want.  We arrive at 7pm and the place was full with exception of three tables.  As soon as we came in, they turned the "open" sign to "closed" and would not accept any more diners.  

All in all, great experience and would definitely go back and recommend to others.

I will never (never say never) eat here again. Our table of four was expecting a fabulous experience in a classic Italian setting. What happened was quite the contrary. The service was untrained and uneven. The main course came before the appetizers (the amount of mussels for cost was pathetic). All of the orders had food delivered incorrectly and had to be taken back for adjustments. The "steamed" vegetables were raw, the Mahi Mahi was so dry it couldn't be swallowed. I sent my dinner back refusing any more indignation and was still charged. With much discussion with management and server to adjust an inflated, incorrect bill, and very little apologies, we left shaken and unhappy.

OMG. I finally gave it a try and what a waste of my time! Me and my significant other decided to give ourselves a break from looking at houses for hours. We came here found a table and nothing else was accomplished except I did get my sweet tea. It was downhill from there. We got there at 3 and we left at 3:52. We never got our drinks we ordered with my sweet tea. All the manager could say was the bar is backed up I'm sorry. We never got our apps or any other food. Only the sweet tea in 45 mins. No drinks we ordered or anything else. Our server walked around like she wasn't bothered and didn't have a care in the world. Just taking a stroll around the area and had such a calm passive attitude about how long it was taking. Horrible first experience. Won't be going back even with the kids.

Food was fairly good. ( Chefs obviously know what they are doing, & use care. ) The big salad I had was good, except it was mostly iceberg lettuce. In 2016, other options like redhead, or spinach would be great. Our waitress wasn't friendly. Her name was Tracy.

The place has a cute atmosphere. Our waitress was really nice. I was not impressed with the food. There were only a handful of people there. Everything we orderd was barely warm, except the grits. My pancake was not fully cooked in the middle. The hollandaise sauce is served room temperature, which we thought was strange.

Tom and I stopped by for dinner around 5:30 this past Friday, there was only 2 other people seated at the time, the hostess asked us if we had a reservation and checked her incoming reservations then politely told us we could sit anywhere. After that it took 5 minutes before anyone acknowledged us again despite the place being empty and there being 2 other serving staff but I did notice a couple of people coming in to pick up take out. The decor is quaint put the place is small and tables are close together so as it began to fill up it did get a little loud. When we finally did order we started with the Duck Fries and Mac and Cheese. The Duck Fries were good, the portion is definitely enough for 2-3 people. I was disappointed with the Mac & Cheese it was just ok  it reminded me of Kraft Mac and cheese it had a runny cheesy consistency I was looking forward to a baked Mac & cheese. Tom like the Mac & Cheese so it really is about your personal preference. Our server read off a long list of specials & I was impressed that she remembered them all,me on the other hand I forgot them & ended  having to get her to remind me again because she disappeared for 10 minutes. I ended up ordering the blackened ravioli which I found out when they brought them out had hot peppers on them, they kindly took them back so I ordered the Ribeye which was worth the $40.00 price tag. Not only was it cooked to perfection the seasoning was out of this world. Since the specials are recited to you and not written down I was surprised when I got the check at the $40 price but it was a good cut of meet that was accompanied with some potatoes and mushroom. Tom had the Northeast Burger and said he liked the Burgers at Gallo's Burger Barn better, again it is all about preference. In conclusion I had a good enough experience that I would try the place again Tom said he would pass, he felt like it was too loud, wait staff could have been more attentive and specials should be written down with the prices published. We both mentioned that to a gentleman who may have been the manager and he said we can always go to their Twitter page to look them up. Not the response that I expected but this is the age of Social Media & I don't think the gentleman was trying to be rude. Sorry I forgot to take photos we were starving by the time we got our food.

if only I could give 0 stars. service was terrible. food was terrible and cold. eggs benedict was not near what a benedict should be, bland and the eggs were hard. the only thing good about this place is the location.

We (party of 5) visited this establishment on Saturday, June 6, 2015 for my sisters birthday and was excited to see, meet and taste the food of Chef Milly's, as we rooted for him on Hells Kitchen.  To start off we had a 9pm reservation and was not seated until at least 10:30pm (we waited patiently, some of us anyway).  Then once we were seated we waited again for anyone to even acknowledge our table.  Someone finally came and bought over silverware and stated they would bring back bread.  We finally received the bread I would say about 30-45 minutes later sitting in a whole stick of melted butter. If it had not been for the birthday cupcakes we bought with us we would have starved to death. I believe it was his brother but not sure who finally came to wait on our table (Ty?).  He was superb, very apologetic, and gracious, because we could tell he was not suppose to be waiting tables.  We ordered appetizers which came out in a reasonable amount of time I would say (Wings, Crab Fries, and Shrimp) all superb, the wings are the BOMB!  Finally we ordered our entrees.  Shortly after placing our order the waiter came to tell us several of our orders weren't available (thad just about ran out of everything we came for mainly being the fried lobster) . The food came out about 12am one by one.  Needless to say we didn't get back to our hotel until 1:27AM from being here since 9PM.  I understand this was only day 6 open but the service was the absolute worst.  I wish I could give them a better review but 2 stars is the best I can do.  The food is ok, I ordered the crabcakes with seafood mac n'cheese, and corn on the cob.  Oh and Chef Milly does come out from the kitchen but not to greet his guests...WOW.

Hi...just an update..... They sent there tech out to fix this......now we are back to the same problem.....bed collapsing
Now my dresser is collapsing also.....

This FURNITURE needs a recall before someone gets hurt..... 
I am so disappointed. Someone please call me 618-719-3717

I discovered this super hidden bar during an adventure to the Homedale Inn with a group of friends.  This bar is further down behind Homedale Inn and we just saw it as we were turning around and decided we had to check it out.  We came in here and there were only around two people in the entire place so it seemed a bit spooky.  It looks like a place that only people that live in the immediate area would even know about or go to.  I've never met anybody else that has even heard of this place or been here.  It looks like a decent place to hang out but it is just very far off the beaten path.  If there were more people here and I would have stayed for longer than one drink this might actually have been a fantastic place but from my short visit there wasn't much going on here.

Crab fries

Buffalo tenders

Decent service

What's up with the leggings?
Clean bathrooms 
Interesting sinks in the bar area
Hyoooge
Taxi crab for 10$
Not too far of a walk to the ballpark

I've been to Dööners several times and the food isn't super bad, but coming from Germany, I am German and grew up there, the Döner there is extremely bland and boring. The sauce, which is the best part for me in Döner stores, is completely tasteless. The meat was very dry and hard to swallow. The person working there was nice but didn't listen to anything I said and put things on my Döner that I didn't want even though I repeated myself 3 times. 
They also do not have onions, which are very traditionally on EVERY Döner anywhere else in the world, their own picture even has onions on it. They only have a onion-tomato mixture which is disgusting to someone like me who doesn't like tomatoes. 
And no A/C either, in FLORIDA! 
Lastly, the price. Usually, anywhere else, a Döner is about $6, but not here. They are trying to be super expensive with $17 for 2. And mine only had sauce and very little meat on it. I guess those prices are not going to the missing A/C......Needless to say, a waste of your money and I'd never go back there again.
Not that great tasting food.  Gulab jamun is the saving grace of otherwise very average tasting food

I'm on the fence about Edison's...the reason being is the set up. While I think it's awesome that they can accommodate people of all ages, I think there needs to be a separation of bar area to the entertainment area. With the number of small children running around, it's not unlikely that they've overheard conversations or seen the mannerism of adults drinking. Upon our visit there, I observed a group of younger men being loud and vulgar towards the entrance of the bar which isn't far from the rest of the gaming or bowling area. I was also told by our friend that the bathrooms which are also used by children had vomit from which I assume was an adult having a little too much to drink.

Food: while refillable beverages is a plus, I thought the food was lacking. I ordered their version of the sampler and after ordering chicken fingers, the dip, and the potato skins and I think I'll stray away from this next time we go. I'm not sure if our waitress was new or just uninformed/not familiar with the menu. I've worked in the food business for over 8 years and knowing the menu and the size of the portions is key. I'm honest when I say that in every restaurant that I've worked in that I've tried everything on the menu...that way I know how it looks when it comes to the window, how to describe the tastes, and suggesting food to new customers. The potato skins where average, the chicken tenders were not the size anticipated giving the waitresses description, and I was disappointed by the chips and their version of queso dip. The queso dip serving dish was the equivalent of a serving dish that you would get when you asked for a side of ranch and it wasn't even full. You could tell that the dip wasn't heated up to full temp because the top layer had already begun to harden when it came out. The chips were WAY over salted. I mistakenly picked up one that had the underside covered in a layer of salt and was simply inedible, good thing they give you an over sized portion of chips to accompany the small portion of salsa.
