They told me they could install new dealer "take-offs" over the phone, and when I arrived they wouldn't. When I asked for the district managers phone number they gave me 1-800-pep-boys. I stood in the store and called and they proceeded to call a police officer to try to intimidate me to hang-up and leave. Take your chances with this store if you want, but if they are in the wrong, you are out of luck, because they don't own their mistakes, let alone correct them. Btw, the guy on the phone at corporate was good, but I haven't received the callback that I was supposed to get 4+ hours ago.

The room was nice and the neighborhood was good. For the money though, I would have to complain about a few things: 

- The front desk guy when we got there (5-30-09 at about noon) was kind of a jerk. He seemed put out by being asked if we could check in early. I understand if that's not possible. That is not the problem, but to be borderline rude about things is just not OK with me when I am spending $300+ on a room after taxes etc. 

- Maybe I am spoiled being from CA, but when I think of a breakfast being included, I don't think of microwave-yourself oatmeal, plastic bottles of OJ, and such exotic fruits as apples. 

- The room we stayed in had apparently been trashed by someone before who stole the iPod docking station and broke one of the lamps and the handle to the closet by the front door. 

I would recommend this place to people that are (for lack of a better term) "Old Money/Country Club" types and who are used to people that work the front desk who haven't seen their own feet in years because their nose is stuck so high in the air. Seriously, I have never been treated so badly by someone holding an entry level position in my life. If this isn't your cup of tea, do what I'm going to do next time I'm in Philly and stay a few blocks to the East in a hotel that is closer to Washington Sq park. I hear the neighborhood is slightly less snooty there.  It could hardly get any worse.

Since moving from the promise land (Texas) my husband and I have been watering at the mouth for some REAL.... GOOD... BBQ. We 've tried some places (for example, Whitts) and were left longing for more. Nothing was satisfying our inner Texan longing for BBQ. Last weekend we arrived at Edley's. Besides being disappointed that they only do Brisket at lunch (that seems to be running trend around these parts) we loved our Ribs and sides! The sauce hit the spot and i was tempted to start drinking it from the bottle, the baked beans disappeared into my mouth before my husband could have another taste and the corn bread was perfectly accented with jalapeno... It hit all of our Texas BBQ high notes but with a trendy atmosphere flare that only Nashville can deliver.

I've left a previous post about cat before, who has since left EWC. My new wax lady is also great. But this review is on their receptionists.

I find them cold, rude, unwelcoming, and generally uninterested in anything I'm saying. Some of them are great, but it seems whenever I call into the store I'm greeted by the same girl who always makes it sound like I'm annoying her by asking a question. She actually has no warmth or friendliness at all when talking to you, and it actually pisses me of and makes me want to frequent a different wax salon. This is a service industry, so when you expect a certain kind of service and you're met with abrupt and rude service, it leaves a bad taste in your mouth. Once my package is over, I'm not sure I'll be buying another.

I like Bank of America and their Clayton and Frontenac locations are great, but this one has an ATM that needs to be fixed. The first time I tried to withdraw money I couldn't get a receipt for my transaction and the second time it was malfunctioning altogether.

This is my mom's favorite restaurant and bar in Reno.  It's a perfect place to go and watch any game.  The service is prompt and the waitresses never leave you with an empty glass.  Typical bar food, so don't go here if you're on a diet!  Everything I've ordered from wings to a burger was good....but the atmosphere is what makes this a great place to watch a game!

Every year we purchase multiple gift cards for Brio as mother's day presents. We have been doing this for years. The last two times I have been to this Brio to purchase gift cards or have lunch, I have stood at the counter and been ignored as multiple employees have either walked past or helped people that have come in after me. Today I told the host what had happened and he laughed. Their food is decent. Their service leaves a lot to be desired. I would not recommend going to this Brio, especially when there are numerous options in the same plaza with great food and service.
A nearly complete failure. Bad service. Bad food. Something is seriously wrong here. I have eaten at the other locations and they were much better.

Jess was fun and attentive as bartenders go.. She definitely had her regulars (small town bar) but she and they made me feel like one of the crew. Food was okay, sauce was good but my bacon was burned and part of the pork was dry.. Overall it was an enjoyable time. Good place to catch a ballgame.

Poor service from host through lunch. 
We had a large group (13) so they made us split up. They refused to even seat us near each other even though there were enough empty tables to put both groups next to each other. We were all seated by 2:50. Not even during a lunch rush. The party of 6 had menus when they sat down and had their drink order take right away and good order fairly quickly. The party of seven had no menus  for over 20 minutes and only after we asked 3x. No one took our drink order until after we had been seated for 30 minutes and I spoke to an employee standing by the doorway to the elevator.  We did not actually order our food or our first beverages until 3:25.  My first beverage was made incorrectly. Our waiter said he knew that but did nothing to correct the issue. 

A trainer came over to inform us our server was in training and today was his last day of training so he was trying to go solo. She told US we needed to give him a hard time for the errors. I told her that is not our place. She needed to address it and she may want to give him more support in training. 

Food finally came around 4:00. The party of 5 was completely done eating. So we're the smaller parties they say at the two empty tables near us. Most things came out correctly except for the smoked chicken. Two of us ordered 1/2 orders. Both orders came out full. The manager eventually reduced our food bill (not including any drinks) by 50%.  We finally got our bill about 4:35. But our waiter did not fix what he charged for the chicken so we still paid more than we should have.  

We finally got out of there about 2:45. Almost 2 hours after being seated. We did package up our leftovers and shared with Nashville's homeless. Saw a lot of need along Broadway. 

All in all, not the experience I expected and not a place I would suggest to others without a lot of warning about what could happen.

I absolutely love this place! Food is very good.... beer is cold and a great variety. Facility is clean. Service is extremely inconsistent.  I have been to this location several times and each time it is like going to a different restaurant.  For the first time we had a great server Rachel!  Although the service was slow she was great.   Again out order was wrong,  but she actually caught it before we could say anything.   She apologized and coolrrected it immediately.  Not sure if the problem is corporate or local management but someone needs to address the inconsistency in everything from service to portion size to timing.  Overall if you have time it is worth the wait, but don't go starving or you may not make it before you are initially served.  12 minutes from sitting to getting my beer.

What is GREAT about this Starbucks is its location, as Gerald S noted.   Given the dearth of decent "to go" coffee purveyors in this area, Starbucks is a nice addition to this neighborhood.   As other Yelpers have noted, this is a relatively small store.  It has comfy chairs and is a nice place to meet a friend for coffee and convo. The staff is pleasant and helpful even if they are not always burdened with a sense of urgency.

This place isn't bad. I went in late one afternoon to get a takeout Mexican and rainbow roll. They got it ready for me in about 20 minutes (longer than the 5 they said it would take...). They were really nice and gave me lots of extra soy sauce, wasabi, and some containers to mix it up in on the go when I asked for it. The fish was fresh and the rolls were tasty but they weren't rolled up very tightly and so they kept falling apart when I tried to dip them in the soy sauce. This was especially a problem with the tempura flakes in the Mexican roll.. 
The price wasn't bad for the quality (compared to other, worse places around town) - I think it was about $8 per roll and they were 8 big pieces each. Kyoto does the job in a pinch.
The food was not so great.the desserts were good. I was wondering if the owner ever smiles or properly recieves his customers. He was definitely not welcoming. Won't be going there again

Very average pizza including the tasteless crust. On top of that, I ordered their 10" "everything" pizza with additional anchovies for $1.50 extra. Got home and no anchovies. The prices are are high for the quality and taste IMHO. Smokey Mountain Pizza on Fairview is much much better both in crust, Smokey flavor and price. That's where I'm going next time.

The food is good, the service is mediocre at best. We came at a slow time on a Sunday, and our server neglected to give us plates, silverware, and napkins. He was inattentive, neglected to refill drinks, and did not once check on how we were doing or if we needed anything. I won't be rushing back.
Another burger joint in town.  Burger and fries eaten were OK.  I will return to try the signature milk shakes

This was The "second chance" for Anthony's  and I will never return.  Both times the pizza was very dry and over cooked. The salad was incomplete at best! Pricey and DEFINITELY not worth it! Sad because I live so close!

Was it here that Abraham Lincoln asked, upon ordering coffee, "If this is coffee, bring me tea. If this is tea, bring me coffee." ?

Maybe the food is good, but don't buy the coffee unless you like over-priced hot brown water.

Well at least Jones is pretty consistent. But then again, you'd expect that out of a Steven Starr establishment.

Came back, brought my parents, and enjoyed another evening of comfort foods.

This time I was smart and ordered their Mac & Cheese to share with everyone. Honestly, I wasn't too impressed. It tasted just like Velveeta to me, same sharp cheese and thick consistency. I don't really mind Velveeta, I just was expecting something more...

My grilled cheese and tomato soup were very satisfying and my step-father practically licked his thanksgiving dinner plate clean. So all in all it was a successful trip. I just don't see the hype in their Mac & Cheese, o well...

Jones is definitely average quality, not many complaints, but nothing to really rave over either. We went as a group of five with a reservation so we didn't have to wait long for a table. They have a limited menu during certain times, so be careful of that. It was very busy and loud, but this wasn't an issue for us. We would go back if there weren't any other options, but considering the area, there are many other better options.
yeah i wasn't that impressed the last time    i went ,   i been to this one  4  times and the  last  2  have not been  the best . i will be choosing  a  different korean place

This organization is unbelievable.  You complain about how they overcharge on a credit card and then you get a private message that they want to help you - and you never hear from these idiots again.  All I can say is watch your credit card statement after you leave as they slammed a promotional card that I had and unfortunately, I have no recourse.  WILL NEVER BE SUCKERED INTO THIS JOINT AGAIN!
Just not good compared to other places around. I personally like the sweeter "pinched" crab Rangoon, not the folded ones like they have. I also like big egg rolls not the small bready ones.
Do not visit for the food.  It's very poor quality.  In addition they don't have many gluten free options either.  Our group enjoyed having cocktails on the rooftop and that's all I would recommend.
Service was great. Burgers were okay.  Not much on the order of onion rings.  Fries were the best part.

My husband and I had dinner at Chase recently and while the atmosphere was interesting the rest of it was a let down.  The service was not very good, though I must admit the server seemed to be aware of the fact that everything was slow and seemed to feel badly about it.  The food was only mediocre and my husband got very sick from eating it.

We enjoyed eating downstairs in the open area lakeside with a light breeze and lovely views. Everything and everyone working here is still relatively or brand new, so there are some glitches to be overcome. Friendly, affordable and comfortable - enjoy Felix's open air.
Hands down worst service of any restaurant I've been to in STL. Food was decent, but service was enough to never return.

Just went there and it was frustrating, a staff full of cuban old ladies thinking the store is theirs, they priced checked two items i had with price tags!!  -making me think im a thief or something SMH!

I ordered a spaghetti with meatballs platter and it was so bland that it reminded me of hospital food. Sauce was brown, not red. Meatballs were frozen style. Just very bad for the price. So disappointing.

WORST SERVICE EVER. and i mean ever. 

After someone took my food by accident, I asked them to make another one for me (and I waited ANOTHER 5 minutes). They misunderstood and didn't add chicken to the pizza I ordered. So the employee rolled his eyes at me and gave me this look. 
So after 15 minutes of waiting...I got my food.

I have never been treated this way at ANY fast food place even. No one apologized. 

Now I go out of my way NOT to go to Cosi. They don't care about their customers. They just make the money. But hey...students need food...so they go to Cosi.

Is it too much to ask for employees to be respectful?

I hadn't been here in a few years, but really used to enjoy their crispy  thin crust pizza.  So, I ordered one to go last night. Got it home and it was so soggy that you literally needed a spoon to eat it. So much for their crispy, thin crust and the toppings were very sparse.
   I ended up throwing it away... waste of $20.  .

Terrible experience. Came for lunch on 2/12. Arrived at 12:30. Sat on the patio. Sat at about the same time as two other tables. Sat for about 5 minutes with the last party's dirty dishes on the table. Waitress finally removed the dirty dishes and brought two waters. Didn't come back for another 10 to 15 minutes to take our lunch order. Ordered the pretzel, Buffalo chicken sandwich and peel and eat shrimp (two of us). We now waited for about 15 minutes for the pretzel. An appetizer, waiting 15 minutes (and all it is was a frozen pretzel, not that hard). After receiving the pretzel and finishing it, waited another 20 minutes for our food. 20 minutes for a sandwich and a few shrimp (which literally requires placing them on a plate). About 4 tables that were sat around us at the same time or AFTER we did received their food before we did. I stopped the waitress who kept just walking by and asked what was taking so long. She so stupidly said, "I'll go check, not sure what's taking so long." Food still didn't come out for another 10 minutes. Peel and eat shrimp were fine. (They weren't for me). Chicken sandwich was disgusting. So much grease, it poured in my mouth the first (and only) bite. The batter was undercooked and just falling off the chicken. It was as if it was battered too much and just soaked in under temperature oil. Asked the waitress to send over the manager. This is a key point here. The manager acted dumber than the waitress. Literally seemed extremely unintelligent. Stuttering and mumbling, uhhh, ummm, not really sure. At one point he actually said, "uhhh, well it is a fried chicken sandwich." I mean really. This is your manager? This is your best guy on the floor? Shameful. After mumbling he said, "I'll take care of this for you" and walked away. Never to return. We sat there like idiots for the next 10 minutes without  the waitress or manager telling us we didn't have to pay. This was the worst restaurant experience I've had in recent memory. I will not return and I will not recommend anyone go here.

(November 17) I have been to Wings over Broadway a couple times. Recently, I have been dying to get back out to them and gorge on their wings. In my opinion, best wings there is in Tucson, although I haven't tried every wing place. Overall, the service is good, not excellent but just good. As much as I love this small community restaurant, I always get sick after eating there. It's not just sick and feeling off with a stomach ache, it is completely sick with food poisoning for a few days to a week long, sick. Although I love the food, I will not be returning due to getting sick all four times that I have been there. I don't know if it's just me, or if there are others who have got sick? Three stars because I loved this place and food is great, but I couldn't get over that I got food poisoning more then once here. It's very disappointing.

Decent but nothing special. If you're looking for a place to eat oysters and drink beer, come here for happy hour as Desire does have some good happy hour beer & oyster specials ($4 Abita drafts and $1 oysters if I'm not mistaken). There are many better tasting and cheaper options for food & drink outside of happy hour in the Quarter.

Standard Hampton.

Decent bed, a choice of pillows.  Room was pretty standard, but it did not have a fridge.  

Internet horrible.  I really wish Hotels would get better internet in this day and age!

Came here on a slow Wednesday night, but the place filled up fast.  I ordered sushi rolls and we had udon soup and salad.  I would say the sushi was slightly above average--nothing spectacular.  The udon was small for the price, but was pretty good.  I don't understand the wild raves about this place, but maybe I ordered the wrong thing.  It's an average sushi spot, as far as I can tell.

This used to be one of my favorite thrift stores because it always had very unique, well taken care of items. I loved how much stuff that was always stocked on its shelves. I could spend hours in this place and still not be able to see everything, which made the thrill of the hunt very enjoyable. However, since they change management the shelves are much more sparse. The few items they do have are still nice quality.

Paid $20 for a fill. I've gone before for a full set by Minh and that lasted like two and a half weeks. This one an older woman did them and they are all very nice but nice doesn't equal quality. While they did stay extra long to do my nails because I got there twenty to close, they let me go without my nails even being fully painted and thus they were smudged badly. On top of that within three days I had two broken nails. Very disappointed. As a college student who works hard and I have rare little luxuries such as getting my nails done, and when I do spend my time and money to have them done, it is very irritating to have them break within days.

The food wasn't terrible. I had the spinach and artichoke dip (delicious, they poured a hefty amount of olive oil on the bread which is always good.) My chicken was cooked perfectly, the pasta was sub par though, quite bland.  I would tell you how my dessert was, but my server thrusted the bill in my face before asking us if we wanted dessert. We did mention it before we ordered our food though. She seemed preoccupied talking to another table about her honeymoon at Yellowstone. Go for the food, expect bad service and a terrible old fashion.
Stoped at this place passing through P. A. I must say it's a pretty cool place a definite college town sports bar. I tried the alligator tacos and I enjoyed them. Good vibes...

Staff isn't very friendly inside they look very annoyed every time I've been inside, not very inviting to sit and relax and drink or eat, usually the drive through seems better to use at this location

We stopped by for lunch kind of late, maybe around 1:45 PM.  We were the only customers at that time.  The music was blaring!!  Really, really loud!!  I asked, very politely, if the music could be turned a bit softer so that we could have a conversation over lunch.  The cashier told us "The music can't be adjusted!"
Of course music can be adjusted!!  It can be turned off!!
Not caring about the customer at all.
Not going back.

We were pretty disappointed in Whetstone given the published reviews recently and can't say that is at all what we saw or experienced ourselves. Service was lacking to the point we needed to step inside to request basics like silverware, condiments, and the even bill. The afternoon menu was also very disappointing. The portions were way less than we expected given the price and fairly weak in flavor. We live in the neighborhood and will not be returning but who knows, maybe things will get better.

I came here tonight for dinner with four gal pals. It was my first visit to The Block. The experience was awful. I was the first of the group to arrive. Let me start by saying that the whole point of a hostess is to 1.) greet you at the door and welcome you to the restaurant, and 2.) select a table for you and seat your table. The hostess here did neither. When I approached her station at the door, the hostess seemed scared and nervous as if I had three eyes and was speaking a different language. I found this odd but told her that I'd like a table for five people. She seemed awkward and said "oh, uh, I don't know...um... let me check with the manager." Huh? Check with the manager? Aren't you the hostess?! This set the tone for the rest of our evening....

She went to get the manager, who also seemed jittery, and told me and a friend who had arrived that it should be ten minutes for us to get a table because they were waiting on a table who had paid their bill to leave. She told us that we could go to the bar while we waited.  We were standing at a long table in the bar area, which had no stools. The bar area waiter came by promptly to get our drink orders and we told him that we were  wating on a table. After more than ten minutes, the rest of our friends arrived and we were still not seated at a table. A friend who had just arrived asked the manager if we could order food and eat at the table that we were standing at, and she agreed and told us that we could pull chairs up to the table. Now, while it's good that she advised us that we could just sit there, why didn't she offer that to begin with?

Our entire group ordered the Block Burger medium well. The burger comes with fries and I asked if I could substitute my fries for green beans and was told by the waiter that it would be a $2 upcharge, which was fine. While ordering, I asked the waiter if the burger came with the traditional lettuce, tomato, onions, mustard, etc and was flatly told no. I asked if I could get that and he said sure. What he didn't say was that this addition of toppings would cost $2. I didn't find that out until I got my bill. SMH. I also should add, that the lettuce, tomato, and onions were chopped as though they were prepared for a salad and not a burger.

All of our burgers came out at different temperatures. Mine came closer to the well done side than the medium side (there was no pink at all in the burger), while a friend had a burger that was medium rare. It was completely pink on the inside. I didn't complain to the waiter about my burger, but my friend justifiedly alerted the waiter and he took her meal away to accomodate the error. He did not apologize. After the rest of our group was almost done with our meals, my friend still had not received her meal back so she went to talk to the manager about it. Shortly after, a brand new burger was brought out. It was cooked medium well, as requested. Right after she received it, the manager came to her and asked "how is your burger? good. good." and then she walked away. She didn't even wait for my friend to answer her question! My friend was quite upset about the long wait that she had for her corrected burger to arrive, as we were all finishing up when she finally got her burger. However, her bill was not accomodated for the inconvenience which my friend expected after talking to the manager. She asked the waiter, and was told "unfortunately we can't do anything about the bill because you ate the entire burger." Wow! I understand that not all restaurants/managers comp bills for ill-prepared meat dishes, but that response was strange.

While I've heard that the happy hour at the Block is pretty good, I don't think I'll ever return due to the poor service that we received tonight. From start to finish, the wait staff and manager seemed rushed and seemed like they did not care much for providing us with a good dining experience. I don't understand why the vibe was as strange as it was. Although the burgers and my green beans were all well-seasoned, I think the strange vibe of the staff, unspecified $2 upcharges, and poor service will prevent me from returning.

The hot chicken sandwich was pretty darn good, but the green beans, Mac & cheese, and coleslaw weren't up to par. Pretty disappointed by them. Didn't even eat over one bite.

Not horrible, but not worth coming back for. 

Coming from West TN, I had high standards and they clearly were not met. My husband had the pork tacos and said they were lame and didn't have much flavor. 

Also, we went in for brisket but it's not served past noon.

We ordered over 150.00 worth of food today takeout.  I complained that the wings were dry, and that I had ordered them with mild sause. Granted 3 hours had gone by before I could sit down to eat, so the staff explained to me that the wings absorbed all the sause. I questioned why the foil the wings were sitting on was completely dry, and was told it too absorbed the sause! LOL. They only offered to pour more sause on them.   Needless to say I left them the wings.  If this is how they treat customers with this kind of tab, I can only imagine if you only buy a sandwich.

Been meaning to try for a while now, finally had the chance to stop in with bae. 

Atmosphere is nice, music was great and staff was attentive and friendly!

I got the pierogis (apparently a new addition) and a side of braised green beans and tomatoes. Pierogis should have been great but I was underwhelmed. The meat inside was yummy, but the dish was cold when it came out and it generally lacked flavor. The green beans were also cold and tasted like frozen steamed green beans covered in Ragu sauce. 

Bae had the burger and enjoyed it-nothing exceptional though. 

I really wanted this place to be great! Hoping the menu gets better if I decide to stop in again.

Only giving this place two stars for its' cleanliness and appearance. Other than that, the customer service here is terrible. Upon arrival, I had to wait about 20 minutes at the clerk's stand until someone finally came to help. When finally getting to our room after a poor initial customer experience, come to find out we didn't even get the room we had reserved. If you're into good customer service and experience, I wouldn't recommend

Went to this location before and liked it fine, but I went just now and TERRIBLE. The person who took my order was perfectly nice,  the person at the window didn't look at me, overcharged me, did not give me a receipt, and closed the window in my face without speaking to me. The store was not busy at the time so I can't imagine she was helping someone else. On top of all that, my iced coffee was no good. Three ice cubes floating in warm coffee is not iced coffee! I didn't have time to go in and try to fix it or I would've asked for a manager. UGH.

I had one of the salmon teriyaki bento box dinners, and my husband had sushi: eel roll, yellowtail roll, Philadelphia roll, and spicy tuna roll.  The restaurant itself is a little worn at the edges....dried leaf decorations on the shoji window coverings, curling acoustic panels on the ceiling, many with water stains, and radio playing in the background.  The outside of the restaurant makes it looks like an abandoned building (boarded up windows), my hubby and I didn't actually know it was a functioning restaurant for the first few months we drove by.  The food was edible but not great.  The salmon was covered in copious amounts of onions and mushrooms, and the teriyaki sauce was watery. The salad dressing was good, but I wasn't expecting fried rice and potstickers in a bento box.  Anyway, the restaurant is relocating, as the building owner decided not to renew the lease...look for a new restaurant here in the near-ish future. I hope they renovate the building, it needs it.

The location on Canal street is great. Its a 4 star hotel on priceline. But I don't get it. I think they were going for a modern decor but it seems cheap to me. And the purple lights and black and white pics of strippers in the hall gives it a brothel feel. The perfume smell in the hall makes me sick and adds to the french brothel ambiance.  

The staff is very nice.

When I walked into my room all of the drawers were open which seems odd. I was happy to see the keurig in my room but they only had tea and decaf available. I tried the decaf. It wasn't very good. I was disappointed

The alarm cord is too short for the alarm to be beside the bed

An alarm went off and I just had to evacuate the hotel in my PJ's. Fortunately it was a false alarm

Decent food. Literally the worst service I have ever come across. I have had about 10 terrible experiences at is location. Each time I think they are playing a joke on me. I guess they stay open because of idiots like me who hope it is just a fluke. My husband waited an hour for our online order tonight, and naturally it was missing a few things. Never again.

Not your typical hotel bar. The atmosphere is lively and offers some nice nighttimme views of the city. If you are into hand crafted cocktails give it a try.  If you prefer beer or wine then I would head downstairs to Cerulean.

Boooooo-ring.

This dog park is fine enough if there's nothing closer. It's fenced-in and off leash, so it's a safe place to go, but my dog couldn't have been more bored. 

This is basically a ring walking trail with a couple short trails cutting through it. There is nowhere for the dogs to congregate and play, and nowhere to throw a ball and really let your dog tear off. 

I went here 3 times, trying to give it a fair share. First time, I liked it, although the dog was bored. Second time, okay the dog is really bored. Third time, okay now the dog and I are bored, and it's a little awkward having to walk behind/next to someone you don't know. Because all there is to do here is walk the circuit as many times as your brain can handle it. My dog stopped at every gate wanting to leave, and had more fun walking the trails outside of the dog park area. 

There aren't many fenced-in places in south Jersey, but if your dog is well-behaved, you'd fair better in an open field or sports field. We just opt for Cooper River Dog Run instead. It's small but long so he can chase a ball. And the usuals there tend to be cuckoos instead of clique-y, so it's more comfortable of a scene.
Service and food under par. They capitalize on their location and forget about the quality of the food and service. Do not order jambalaya, dry and  tastes like nothing.

I can't stand this place.  Called and was cursed out for no reason.  I guess we were ordering to much and the guy couldn't keep up so he started yelling at me. The pizza is to sweet anyway so screw them. Uncle nicks has better pizza and they're crust is way tastier.
Very poor service and pathetic quality of food. Would never come back here and also not recommend for any of them this place.

I purchased a batch of these cookies and some of the cookies contained sayings from someone else's order  (various orders). It made handing out the cookies kind of scary since I never knew what someone would get when they cracked one open (one of the sayings, for example, seemed to belong to a bachelorette party). I contacted the company about four times via both email and telephone with absolutely no response.

I've been to this place twice, with two separate female barbers, and won't be going back.  Each time, I've asked for the sides to be buzzed and a trim on the top.  It's a pretty easy request.  

Both times, the barber missed TONS of stray hairs on the top.  I kept pointing them out, and she obligingly cut them, but it shouldn't be on me to make sure the barber does a competent job.  Last time and the most recent time, I had to get my girlfriend to take a pair of scissors and trim all the stray hairs that they missed.  I forget the name of the first barber, but Christine was the one who screwed up this time.  I looked in the mirror and found small patches of hair that she missed with the clippers all over my head because she didn't do a thorough job.  

If I'm going to pay good money for a haircut, I shouldn't have to fix it myself afterward.  The barbers here are more concerned about gossiping and chatting than focusing on the task at hand.  They also resisted what I wanted, and I had to specifically request that they please do what I'm paying them to do.  

If they screw up my relatively simple request, don't trust them to do something more complicated.

Wouldn't eat here again. Small portions for the price. Food had zero flavor and the service was nonexistent. We never had our waters refilled nor did our waiter even check on us. I doubt the voodoo juice had any alcohol in it and the jambalaya was dry and was a small serving for $16. Disappointed that we had lunch here.

I have been coming to Arner's for 30+ years and unfortunately, it seems to have taken a turn for the worse.  First you have to understand this restaurant is (was?) a Delaware staple, a go to place for good service and food...having seen the remodel after fire damage and it just keeping up with the times, I have to say I was super disappointed!  

I took my son for a special Sunday morning breakfast.  After driving for 45 minutes, we arrived just before 10am.  The first thing that struck me was the empty parking lot, I totally expected to get there and wait for 30+ minutes for a table but not this day.  We got sat right away; I only saw about 7-10 other tables sat.  Server's timing was fine and we ordered right away.  

My son ordered hot chocolate which isn't something he get regularly so this was a big deal that he was allowed to have it and of course he wanted whipped cream.  Then the hot chocolate arrived, it had a clump of white stuff on top which looked like sour cream, he took a small bite (because it didn't even have enough to cover the top of the hot chocolate so a big bite wasn't possible) and I was sure he was going to make an "oh my gosh, I just ate sour cream," face but he made a "oh my gosh, I just ate weird whipped cream," face.  I tasted it to be sure and although it wasn't sour, it wasn't whipped cream but more like part way whipped cream so I mixed it into the hot chocolate but the unmixed, solid cream bits were too much to over come and he didn't touch it again.

We ordered creamed chip beef, strawberry waffle and an omelet.  The omelet was fine; cooked well and tasty.  However, the waffle was covered by the same sorry excuse for whipped cream as the hot chocolate; we scrapped it to the side and enjoyed the waffle all the same.  The whipped cream looked like an attempt at homemade whipped cream gone wrong or from way too many minutes ago as it was part thick clumps and part runny sauce.  The creamed chip beef was more like lumpy sauce with a few pieces of chipped beef (very few); the taste was ok but it obviously had been sitting around long enough to coagulate making it thick and gloopy (if that's a word?)...a little thinning with some milk would have made all the difference.  Because nothing tasted bad and it was all edible, I didn't mention it to the server.  BUT, she never asked why it wasn't eaten either which I feel would have been appropriate for her to do.

As we sat and waited for the check, my son asked what was under the booth across the isle.  After looking at the baseboard (which he was talking about) I explained to him it was just not clean and had lots of splatter on it.  So, of course, now I look around and notice lots of things which were not clean...food splatter on the booth walls/glass, small food/paper debris all over the floor, dirty light fixtures, server area was messy...basically this place needs to close and deep clean the dinning room; things needs to be scrubbed, not wiped off, scrubbed!  If this is what the dining room looks like what on earth does the kitchen look like?  On a side note, watching servers fix their hair and wipe at their eyes/noses is gross...they need to be told not to do this in the dining room.

When I paid, the cashier asked me how everything was and I looked at her, shook my head and told her it was very disappointing...here's the kicker, she said NOTHING after that!!  She didn't ask me what was wrong, what happened, why I was disappointed, NOTHING.  I responded to her question and told her I was disappointed and she said nothing in response.  This is such a shame that an institution, a local business, has let things go so far wrong that the employees don't even care but a guess if the owners don't care, why should they????

was at the Blue Duck tonight was very hungry ordered the chicken wings for a app . and the tagliatelle well this waitress brought me my chicken wings as my entree, my husband ordered the scallops as a special which came out first we shared it then she brought generals tso's chicken wings with my husbands burger . my tagliatelle never came out when she came to are table she said well its finish time it was 10 minutes to 10 which is closeing time i had a reservation for 915 pm what a disappointment left hungry but didn't complain just felt cheated never gone back
Never answer the phone, the website is impossible, the food is decent but you can't order it so that's irrelevant!!!! So annoyed.

Ordered food online for pick up. Paid $34 online by inputting card number. Husband went to pick it up. They went over the order then told him it would be $47 so he swiped his card. I assumed they would know we paid online since it was done through THEIR website and there is no option to pay at the restaurant. So now we have paid $80 for $34 worth of food. They dont open for a few more hours, but trust me that manager will hear from me and our money WILL be refunded on the original form of payment....not a gift card. We will NOT be back to this location. They have been open for over a month so we assumed that would be enough time to work out the kinks. Apparently not. Be very cautious of online ordering.

ETA: It took over two weeks to be refunded our money. I had to bring in proof several times. I was never given an apology for the extreme inconvenience. The response was essentially "Ooops". Terrible customer service.

My family has been to To-Kai many times. It is family friendly and the food is excellent. The ambiance is not great and the place could be a little cleaner and definitely needs an update but if you are going for the food you will not be disappointed.

I ordered off Skip the Dishes and it was over an hour late. The fault was 100% with the restaurant and they kept giving Skip the Dishes inaccurate information (food is on the way, we're handing it over shortly, etc). Taking to the driver, I guess the restaurant kept screwing up orders.

I tried calling the restaurant at one point and never got a call back.

When I finally got the food, it was cold, the chicken was dry and over cooked, the ginger beef was all breading, and they never gave me the lemon sauce for the lemon chicken.

It was just a terrible experience overall. It's made worse by the fact that they made the delivery driver sit there for an hour, which stopped him from making more money.
So very disappointed- booked this through Open Table at 9am - friends came up from Chattanooga- got a call at 3:50 saying no reservation. Not good!

First by letting me say I am a Starbucks addict! I eat drink or work at some Starbucks location almost every single day. This time I didn't go in but just grab myself some coffee in the drive-through. This Starbucks drive-through is not necessarily ideal. You have to basically go through the line of fire from the drive-through line to get to the front door if you decided you wanted to go inside instead of using the drive-through. This location is also connected to body pages, which..., If you have some dry cleaning and want to grab a coffee is absolutely ideal! However the layout for the drive-through and parking for the guests that are going into the location is a bit dangerous and a bit muddled up. You have people there parking across the street that are trying to get into the entrance you have people from the drive-through trying to get out of the drive-through and into the drive through and all of them have to cross in the same general area. Unfortunately this is the only drive-through Starbucks within quite some ways... And if it's snowing or raining and you're in a hurry this is going to be your best bet. The people here are always absolutely amazing, like all Starbucks! So I give them three stars for the drive-through but five stars for the staff.

Omg this place is filthy! They don't disinfect their tools or files! Acrylic dust all over everything! Two stars because the girl was nice and the polish job looks ok I guess. Paid $12 for basically a polish change because I wouldn't let her cut my cuticles with a dirty tool!
Was good, but not great.  I thought the gumbo was too salty for my taste.  Oysters were good.  Their Desire hot sauce wasn't bad either.

The food was great and our waiter was super friendly, but the hostess was a nightmare. We got a table for 4 but one of the members of our party was parking, so we were told we couldn't be seated until everyone was present. After explaining that the other member was parking and might just grab drinks we were told that "we can seat you but the person not here won't be able to order" and took their menu away. Very rude, very unprofessional. Definitely won't be back. We were thankful to the waiter for getting her some water and a add for our pitcher of mimosa!
Really wish they would bring the sweet potato fries back. Not sure why they rebranded in what seems to be for the worse...

Fool me once, shame on you.  Fool me FOUR TIMES, shame on me!

I don't know why we keep revisiting Land of Smile.  Wait.  I know.  It's ridiculously convenient.  It's clean.  It's friendly.

The food is just beyond mediocre.

That, and it's overpriced. 

Thai isn't that unique anymore and I guess I'm too used Thai places being along the same lines as Chinese takeout places to put up with such prices.  

After giving it a couple tries, we're more than willing to drive another 5 minutes to the next Thai place that we feel are more worth it.
Its okay for a chain.  Not a first choice but tolerable for company lunch meetings.

I had been wanting to come here for some time now..after having such a nice experience at their old location ('A Peace of Sole' near 4th and South). The hubby and I finally made it out on a Saturday nite and found the restaurant to be busy but not crowded. It was warm for the end of winter (75 degrees that day) and the windows were opened to allow a slight breeze to blow. It was lovely. 

We started with the seafood salad and it was a very "healthy" serving. That, along with the delish buttered, garlic and oldbay rolls they serve could have been a meal by itself..Tri color rotini pasta, shrimp and imitation crab, celery and seasonings mixed with a creamy parmesan dressing. Lemme tell ya, it was the bee's knees!

Then onto dinner...I was undecided between the fried whiting and the stuffed turkey chops (spinach, cheese, roasted red peppers, mushroom gravy) so I convinced the dude to order the turkey chops so I could sample both..Yeah, greedy I know- so what!

We couldn't have been happier with the meal. Both came accompanied by two sides: corn, roasted potatoes, sauteed broccoli, orange rice. I think you could get mac-n-cheese or collards for an additional cost but we didn't order either this time. I was on the fence about ordering the cherry iced tea but it was on point. 

The meal came to about $40 - not too shabby, eh? With our $25 Restaurant.com gift certificate, we only had to pay $15 + tip, right??? WRONG! Little did we know that they no longer accepted Restaurant.com gift certs and when we explained this to our server, she pointed to a sign that was 8 1/2 x 11 (Landscape view) on a podium in the corner that read "We no longer accept Restaurant.com coupons". Are you serious?? I came here to eat not read little posters so I surely didn't notice that throughout the entire dinner. The hubby didn't either because the big screen TV's were blasting the NCAA game and he barely paid ME any attention, so the sign didn't stand a chance!

Al in all, the server gave us a 20% discount on the bill (not a lot of help when you expect to pay $15). That was the best she could do but you'd best believe that I contacted Restaurant.com as soon as I got home and got a replacement  cert. According to Sole II Soul, they cancelled their contract with Restaurant.com but the website continues to sell their certificates.. So BUYER BEWARE!!

All in all, I had a good time. Yeah, I left with the figurative "bad taste in my mouth". Even though they could've had better signage or more notice before we ordered AND devoured such a delicious, savory meal that we were actually going to have to pay for it - the service, food and atmosphere couldn't be beat. I'll be back!

We ate dinner here- it was pretty empty when we arrived but was packed by the time we left (we eat early with the two small kids). It was quite a walk from our hotel, but worth it. The building in general and restaurant decor is a bit old/dated, as were the wait staff uniforms/dress code. It is small, but cozy, no complaints. We ordered: fried chicken with red beans/rice/mac'n'cheese, crowder peas/okra/rice with smoked sausage, and crawfish etouffee. The boys loved the chicken, but to the adults it was borderline too salty. The crawfish etouffee was excellent- at the top other list of what we've had to date (and that includes multiple iterations of this dish). The crowder peas and okra were great by themselves and with the smoked sausage which was awesome. My mouth is watering just thinking about it again. The kids' mac and cheese was also great ( although stingy small portion) - they put some kind of a crumb on it which is nice. Cornbread was crumbly and came with ample butter - a definite must. The wait staff were nice, good service. All in all a solid meal. I saw another table order the bread pudding and it looked good- would definitely give it a try on a second visit.

Went to Gusto last night after a two year gap to see if things had improved. Food was actually quite tasty and service was good. We had Ceasar salad, tasty but slightly wilted, pasta bolognese and eggplant parmigiana. The pasta was a pleasant surprise and the eggplant was tasty with a crispy crust. Overall a good experience, but wish they could do something to improve the dining ambience relative to the prices. They charge about 30% higher than the place appears it should. Personally I am ok with the prices but don't like the vibe.

WORST MCDONALDS EVER!! Wait times in drive thru are up to 15 sometimes 20 Minutes Iv been short changed 3 times now by the same older Philippine lady that receives payments at first window. And then when you call her out on it she just stares at the screen and pretends to be sorry I'm under the influence that this is her scam. Also could explain the long wait times. My kid doesn't like mustard on his burger and the other one doesn't like lettuce I ask for this every time and have received maybe once correct outta the dozen times Iv been. I live in the area so I enjoy my coffee before work 15 min for an incorrect coffee order gets old fast. I will be traveling to the Weber greens McDonald's from now on. I only gave them one star because it wouldn't let me give zero.

My GF and I decided to get dinner here on a whim because we were hungry, and well, it was open. We were both distinctly underwhelmed with the food and the service. Our waiter took our order, but never came back to check on us until it was time to pay. The place was definitely kid friendly, which is fine, but it was downright raucous and noisy. My dinner was a squid ink pasta with scallops and shrimp, and the pasta was mostly stuck together (although the sauce was nice), and there were two small shrimp and two small scallops - all for 19 bucks. Next time we'll keep on walking.

Would give a negative star if I could. Haven't had a good bar experience in about 4 attempts. Slow is an understatement. If they have more than 1 bartender working that's a good night. They should train all of the staff to at least pour a beer. Last but certainly not least... the entire building smells like a toilet. It's like they are trying their hardest to fail. From the day they opened its been terrible.

Very creative decor. Nice staff. Big selection
of things to eat and drink.
I bought a new IPhone from them three weeks ago, and today it didn't work.  Even though it was under warranty, they refused to replace it and told me to go to an Apple store.

Took my wife here because she loves the carne seca. I'm not a fan. It's too dry. I tried the steak this time, but it was too well done and had no flavor. Chips and salsa are good, as are the charro beans and calbacitas, but they overcook their main courses.

Terrible, terrible, terrible service. Good food. Waited 1.5 hours to eat. Had to ask for refills EVERYTIME. Feeling neglected. Terrible service. Wish I wasn't so hungry :(

The manager or whoever was in charge came out twice to apologize, after we flagged someone down to ask. They said they were going to take care of the check. But upon receiving the check it was only discounted a small percentage. I forget, like 10-20%. Regardless, I wrote this place off

Watch your statement!!! They changed my $11 tip to $14 probably thinking I wouldn't notice! I will never be returning. Food was okay, but lacking in spices so I might have given them another shot but not after altering my payment. Beware!

First visit wasn't anything to write home about but stopped in for appetizers. The calamari was awful and when they ask if everything was okay we were just ignored by management. We will never go there again

Will not eat here again! I must admit I have had some very nice meals here in the past. Their basil chicken is very good, tasty and nice bit of spiciness. Last night we ordered basil chicken and kung pao chicken. Could not eat it! The kung pao was tasteless and the meat was absolutely horrible. Not one piece of chicken was edible. Grizzle and chewiness in every bite.
Basil chicken still tasted good but the chicken was the same as the other dish.

Exhausted from a long drive from NY to Nashville, we refused to venture far from the homestead but insisted on still eating well, and Edley's seemed like our best option on a night where everything else on 12th seemed closed. 
$20.21 for hot chicken (weak in heat but decent flavor) and a pork sandwich with some sides and drinks. Sat outside under space heaters and it wasn't so terrible on a winter evening.
PS they let their POS upstage them with first line of receipt as 'micros demo system'

The food was mediocre at best.  I was with a party of 6 and one of my friends wasn't even served until after we had all eaten and then the food was much too salty to eat.  It seemed one waitress was handling the majority of the over crowded restaurant.

Bathroom was very nasty!  Poor experience here.  Too many restaurants in New Orleans to have to stop here again.

Maru Global is apparently an average starting point for those who have never experienced takoyaki- however I admit that I've gotten a few raw ones on occassion.

Also the food is not exactly cheap, the decor sucks and size of the place leave a lot to be desired.

Not happy. Although I typically go to the Italian market or Callowhill I thought I would come to Collingswood with friends who were having their first experience here. I called ahead at 1015am and was told there would be a half hour wait plus 15 minutes when I arrived. When we got there I was told they had no notice of my call even though I received a text. Now it is 1150am and we are still waiting for a table. Unacceptable and technology is no excuse. Very disappointed that I decided to bring my friends here. 
After being seated managers apologized and brought out French toast. The food was as good as it usually is but some kinks need to be worked out. Service was mediocre. I'll be back in a few months when things are running smoothly and noise machines are in place.

This is my second time to eat at Brio. It has been in two different cities and as a whole, the chain has sub par food with poor service. Avoid the eggs benedictano and pollo al forno flatbread. There as too many good Italian place in STL and this place should be avoided.

this place is an OK place to live. there are a few good and a few bad things. lets start with good... nice pool, good parking, playground, affordable. now..for the bad...1. rude loud neighbors, people smoking weed outside like its no big deal, they are always turning the water off and sometimes its out for the entire day and night...

Literally worst service ever. Goodish food, very skimpy wine pours, cute spot except for the extremely creepy bathroom. Overall 2 stars because I repeat: worst.service.ever. We had to go inside to ask our server for wine after 12 minutes of sitting with empty wine and water glasses, mid meal. On a Sunday. Not super busy. Never going there again.

One afternoon I was having a craving for a hamburger.  Now, my frequent dining companion does not really care for hamburgers, but the fry menu was enticing enough to intrigue both of us.

I ordered a plain cheeseburger that came out dressed.  FAIL.  However, after removing the extra "stuff" the flavor of the burger was exactly what I was looking for... you know... grilled BEEF.  The rosemary fries are always a refreshing twist on the old favorite, and I you can't go wrong with a hand dipped Oreo milk shake.  I love that they bring out the metal cup with that tiny bit extra that won't fit in the glass.

So, yes, Smashburger is a chain, and yes they screwed up my order, but in the end it hit the spot.  I don't do the burger and fries thing often, but at least I know where I can go next time it comes up.

The Nashvegas is one of my favorite burgers in Nashville. But what happened here? Not only was the bun smashed paper thin, the burger was well done (not medium as ordered), and there was barely any BBQ sauce. I usually  go to the one on 8th, guess I'll stick to that one from now on. 

This was to go, or I would have sent it back. Which I've only done once in my life.

Ninki gets 4 stars for fresh fish, friendly service and a knowledgeable staff. I have to take a star away for a poorly rolled (very loose) sushi roll that wouldn't hold together, forcing me to dissect it, which is embarassing and I hate to do. 

However, 20% off on Wednesdays is certainly a draw, and I have never in my life, across the many many sushi joints I've been to, seen a flaming sushi roll. 

Overall, this place will teach you not to turn your nose up at a sushi place in a shopping center.

Over rated and resting on its Laurels. To have a store adjacent to restaurant is part of the "franchising" approach of NOLA food establishments. 

Came in for an afternoon snack, with dinner at another place later in the evening.  Gumbo was so-so, Oakland's Brown Sugar Cafe is far superior. Fried pickles, skinny and fried to long --aka almost burnt.  

The best NOLA cooking I had were in people's home during the holidays. The variety and quality exceeds those found in run of the mill places.

Yes, I am a San Francisco foodie and expected better from moderately priced restaurants such as TPC.

Food is okay, for mass-produced, commissary-prepared ingredients that are cooked and/or assembled on site, as it is in most large, corporate establishments like this one. Nothing is particularly good, and nothing is particularly bad. 

I imagine the decision where to go vs Chipotle, Qdoba, Moe's, and Blue Coast largely comes down to price and/or convenience. For me, these four in the area are pretty much interchangeable otherwise, besides little things like one's chips are slightly better, etc. This location does have a Coca-Cola Freestyle machine, FWIW.

Qdoba has recently started to offer all add-ons for no additional charge -- even guacamole. It will be interesting to see if and how this new wrinkle may change the level of business -- I've never seen this location in any danger of being even half full.

My husband and I went on a Friday night around 9 for our first visit. I like the atmosphere and feel the location of the restaurant is perfect for a BBQ joint since there isn't much like it in the Belmont/12th/Green Hills area. Other than the appearance of the place, not much else impressed me. We ordered fried pickles as an appetizer and they were waaay too greasy to even eat, and this comes from someone who is not afraid of a little grease here & there. When we ordered they were out of turkey, brisket and some other meat so I couldn't try the nachos like I'd wanted. I ordered a chicken sandwich which came with coleslaw on the bun. The coleslaw was delicious but couldn't hide the ever-so-intense dryness of the chicken. We went outside to eat and the tables hadn't been cleared, we had to ask a staff member to do it for us. The staff was kind and friendly enough and the side dishes as well as the pork my boy had were all good but nothing to write home about. I can think of 3 superior BBQ restaurants in Nashville that I'd prefer to spend my $ at. I may give them another shot but I just wouldn't try chicken again. Maybe next time they won't be out of brisket.

I will concur with the other reviews regarding poor service. The lady who took our order was ok but the lady who made our burritos was extremely rude to my wife and also had soap suds all over her forearm and thankfully it didn't touch our burrito. This is the closest qdoba to our home but next time will go somewhere else

Hmm.. Definitely one of those places that doesn't live up to the decor. I was expecting so much more and was dying to try this place. We had a vanilla/vanilla cupcake and a vanilla/raspberry cupcake. They both just seemed "meh". I have to say the icing is fit for a wedding cake with fondant and shouldn't be on a cupcake because it is just too overpowering and too rich. The cake itself also seemed old tasting. I'm not going to go get another one. However, I would return again and again to Ramona Susan's. Their cupcakes have always been on point and I find myself craving another one or would go get their butter cake before returning to Cake life. We also tried the salted caramel brownie and it was totally stale. So disappointing!
Great comfort food, good service, and reasonable prices. Never had a bad meal here.

i really wanted to like this place. I got the Philly style one. it was soggy and super buttery for some reason. maybe it was the mustardy mayo sauce that was on it. pretty bland. I ended up picking through the other half after eating the first and just ended up throwing away half of it. I'll have to try the Komchi version next time...if I go back.

Worst pho I've had in a while.  The tendon is harder than plastic.  They need to take that crash of the menu, if they don't know how to cook it.  It's an offense to be served it.  The pho is also overly oily.  The flashy ads on the front of the store are a facade hides that this place is rather bad.

For fast food, I love the options -  the variety of healthier meal choices is a real bonus. The reason for the low score is due to the cleanliness of the tables.  The dining area stays perpetually dirty, even when the restaurant is not busy.

Not a great experience!!! I've been here a couple of times and haven't had much to complain about, I've really enjoyed their food and services, but that changed when I planned a larger group event. With the increase in guests their care of us got exponentially worse. Every other time I've been here there has only been two ladies and they seemed to handle us well no hick ups. This time I made a 15 person table the first thing that happened was when we arrived they miscounted our table setting and it took about 10 minutes to get it handled and with three different servers that were not communicating to each other we kept getting more chairs and more plates and settings that had to be removed or brought back. The service this time seemed subpar and almost crass. I don't know if it was because they knew we had to pay a 18% gratuity anyway, but they were not very hospitable and I even felt ignored, they barely looked at us and often overlooked us for other smaller groups. One serve even said "I barely saw you out of the corner of my eye" because they never looked at our table directly. The food was sloppily presented Family style which I did not expect considering it was always plated individually before, I don't mind the sharing but the way it was arranged made my event feel slapdash and childish. We were constantly having to reorder things from different servers because the other would forget. What really pushed the Brunch to a bad place was that a plate of half burnt scones was dropped off, not only did it look bad, but it smelled worse and to have that placed in front of us was unappetizing and a huge oversight by the staff. One of my guests was also horribly talked down to by the staff in a very berating way when she asked for butter for her scone the server which I think was the owner stopped and said "you are in a tea house and you don't want butter, here we use Devonshire cream and not butter for our scones" this I feel was not the way to talk to a customer and embarrass them in front of a large group.  I had high hopes for this Brunch, but my hope was sorely misplaced. I don't think I will be hosting a get together here again because the service and attention to detail was so poor.

The most D.E.P.L.O.R.A.B.L.E. dining experience I have ever had. Service is non existant...al of the severs stand around in the bar, talking about the guy they were with last night...then...six TV's...I am watching one...and a guy just walks up and changes the channel...3 laps to go in the race...never asked if anyone was watching...put them all on the game he and the cooks wanted to watch...no manners or consideration.The place is a "roadhouse"....but there is crap on the floor that has been here...awhile. A good team is assembled by a manager who cares...and then rides herd on the emplyees....Manager walks around just watching it all...smdh.

First off, the noise level is off the charts. There is no dress code, each guy walking in came in sweat pants and flip flops. Turn off. We ordered shredded duck pappardelle and prosciutto wrapped duck breast in balsamic glaze. The duck breast was cooked perfectly and was juicy and delicious.  The sauce on the paperdellle pasta was so salty that it was as inedible.  I like salt on my food but I could not stomach this entree. It was a major disappointment after hearing glowing reviews. I will NOT be going back..

Where do I start? The initial cleanup was back in December and they did a half ass job putting a humidifier. I called in January and February to find out about part 2. In march I found out it was still wet and started to smell so bad I was throwing up. They came out and determined they needed to put another humidifier in. Now on to part 2. The original guy was fired so I was lost in the sauce. They finally sent someone out that said I would have it done the next Monday morning. No one showed. When I called he said I needed to pay them everything first before they came out. Unusual but ok you never said this before. I paid and then the following week someone came out. Mind you this is May by now. They came out did a half ass job and I told them I was unhappy. I would not sign a finished work order until they fixed it. He told me 3x someone would call me back. I called last Wednesday and he said he would call me Thursday to schedule. I called him today a week later and told them they need to be here by Monday. They stained my carpet and stained my chimney and never cleaned up. I'm filing a bbb complaint as well!

Update* guy comes today and in the middle of us talking he interrupts me talking to take a phone call from another customer. Steps outside and gives me the one finger like hold on. How unprofessional! At this point it's like I'm bothering him more than he is supposed to be here helping the situation

Update** you guys say refer to your sister company but your sister company doesn't have a yelp page for reviews or contact. Could this possibly be the reason why those of us with bad reviews post here? Smh this is ridiculous

If you are a fan of Collingswood restaurants this is one to try. You are not going to get a particularly bad meal but maybe just not the best meal. They need to work on being a little more memorable. I do think its worth a try and you should form your own opinion on this one.
The reason I gave them 3 stars was because I tasted more bread than fillings in my sandwich. I got the Westside Turkey Club and felt there could have been a little more turkey in there. I do not like when restaurants pack the meat in there either but there was just one weak layer. From the little turkey I had it was tender and tasty. The raisin bread was really good but I think it would have been better if it were toasted. 
We ordered desert, we were handed a desert menu with no prices. I got the flourless chocolate tort, my friend got the fruit compote. Of course I ate all mine, lol, although it was just maybe a little dry or something not all together great. She liked hers.
Now the shock! When we got the bill I was curious why it was so high. My tort was $10! And hers $7!!! Mine was more than the sandwich I ordered!
I felt a little ripped off. They need to put prices on their desert menu ASAP. I would have never ordered it had I known it was priced so high. Way too high for what you got. 
This place has so much potential, loved the vintage decor and tables! They have a great food concept!! They just need to tweak. My suggestion is they offer a complimentary small basket of their homemade chips when seated(to be fair no free refill), slightly up the meat in their sandwiches, and offer specialty pies ($5-6) for desert. Pie would go much better with the rest of the comfort foods they offer.

This is for "The Lookout" which should be called the Watchout. IT IS NOT OLE RED. We made a reservation but didn't realize The Lookout was not part of Ole Red. We were whisked through Ole Red which looked fun and had a great menu. The Lookout is like a lame hotel bar and does NOT play country music. Our server was wonderful but she had to shoo people away from our table when we arrived. People who continued to stand behind us the entire time and blocked our view. AWKWARD. The food was overpriced and not good. The wine was warm. The table was dirty. The view was okay, I watched people at the bar across the street having fun and wished I was there instead. The worst part? The stench of rotten trash blowing up from the street to the table. Is this a "thing" in Nashville or were the garbage collectors on strike? Every dumpster was overflowing. I became nauseous from it and we had to leave. So sad that this was how we had to spend our last night in Nashville, we were having a great time until this.

Been going to Jones on and off for about 18 months since we live in the area. No more. 
No one seems to attend to the complaint that this place is deafening. In fact we left early on one visit because the noise level was so aversive that our ears were ringing and it was impossible to carry on a conversation.
The food continues to be predictable and OK but so overpriced for essentially diner food.
The service is chaotic and staff is not trained competently. Recently our party of 8 tried to get in on a weekday at 5 pm and the host was so scattered that he barely knew what he was doing with a line that was out the door. He looked like a deer in the headlights. Not confidence inspiring. And the people kept waiting while he dithered. Then come to find out that they NOW have changed to taking reservations. Way to go Jones -- should have done that years ago not changed the rules midstream. Needless to say, we did not have reservations and they missed out on feeding 8 hungry people. This place is on its demise I think and I further think that Starr needs to attend to the complaints that people who know food and service and ambiance are posting here and on other venues. I don't see that happening.

This is our second time going to capital cellars. I'm leaving my review based on price point and comparison to other similar priced venuses such as Brick 29, Chandlers, Ruth Chris and Alavita.  We had a salmon fish and a seafood risotto dish.  For appatizer we had a bowl of French onion soup, no doubt the best thing the entire night.  The salmon should have never made it to our table. Even a new chef would have know it was way over cooked and extremely dry.  The seafood risotto was ok but nothing I'd pay $20+ for.  This was our second go at the place and for sure our last.  I always give a place two attempts to get it right, capital failed on both.

I don't get the hype at all. Place looks like a college hang out. It's dirty, uncomfortable, chaotic, freezing cold. Food was "fine" but certainly not anything to get excited about. Coffee was decent, but far from the best I've ever had. Maybe I'm missing something.

Went in with some trepidation given the gimmick of a spinning dining room and was pleasantly surprised. While I could still do without the moving floor I had an enjoyable meal. Our waitress Darlena was fantastic. The food was a bit over priced but pretty good. I had a jerk steak special which was very tasty. Friends had coconut crusted grouper which they very much enjoyed. The place is bit of a cheese fest but once you get past that you realize it's kinda fun.

This is a nice track but the service is horrible. We paid for two races. The first race we waited 20 minutes for. The second race was not until 2 hours later. I think this is ridiculous. I am not sure if this is a scam to try and get you to spend more money in the arcade or what but I would never return he again even if it was free.

This is a great place to go for mexican food and magarita's. The food is great -my personal favorite are the steak fajitas! The margaritas are good enough to sing about!! Best part is the pricing is 1st-date reasonable! 
I am not usually a fan of the 'chain' mexican restaurants but this place does a lot of things right!

I don't even know where to begin. This is the closest KFC to my house and it is the biggest disappointment. Every time I go there, there is always something wrong. For instance one night they were out of chicken tenders and mashed potatoes. Almost every time I go there they forget something in my order or get it wrong. Like ordering a number six and a number eight and only getting one number 6. Or not getting any sauces that I asked for. Or 4 getting my cookie AND my sauce.... AND my utensils in the same order. And there's always a weird smell coming from the window when I pull up for the drive thru... And when I peek in it just looks like a mess. Of course the floor looks disgusting. Honestly I don't even know why I come back. If I could give them zero Stars I would but fortunately I can't so they get one star. And I guess that one star accounts for the fact that everyone is always friendly. At least they're nice.

I have been to West Side Gravy 4 times now, and really, really wanted to like this joint. I just do not. 

I don't understand cuisine in Collingswood. It has simply left me underwhelmed. If a few of these places (including West Side) were in the city, they would never have made it. 

The food is ok (probably 3 stars- good, but nothing to go gaga over) but the service sucks. Today we went for brunch, were the first ones there, the doors were locked (at 11 am!... I think it is supposed to open at 10?), the server who greeted us was surly, and there were no brunch menus. Instead, there were specials which had to be recited by the server. Can you say awkward? No, I dont want grilled cheese and tomato soup on Sunday morning, thank you. I want to sit down and look at a menu and decide what I want. That's how it is done. Or so I thought. 

And yes, the other times i have been there the service has been below par as well. I dont get it- here is this cute little shop, really cool decor, certainly acceptable menu items, and yet I feel like they are doing me a favor by serving me. 

Anyway, I know I am alone in not loving this place. It simply hasn't worked out for me.

I happened upon this little bakery in Oliver while waiting to meet with someone over at the McDonald's and decided to pop in to see if they had hamburger buns in an attempt to save myself a trip over to Safeway.
 
I got service immediately and was greeted by a very enthusiastic young woman. I did end up up purchasing a package based solely on the fact they smelt so darn good but was slightly shocked at the price. They are smaller than your typical grocery chain buns but the price jump obviously comes from the fact they are organic.

Upon using them with our burgers however I was quite surprised with how much better they tasted then the prior mentioned grocery store chain buns so I must admit I am torn about my experience. If value and quantity is what is important to you then I recommend sticking with the big grocery stores but if you want something high quality and healthy and can handle paying a bit more these guys are a great choice.

I will definitely come back for a coffee and individual baked good if I am in the neighborhood again but I don't think my wallet could handle regular purchases here.

Very prompt curbside takeout! Our order is always correct and cooked just the way we ordered it :-) ... Thanks for the coupons on your app! We always use them!
2nd time I went .... HORRIBLE FOOD!!!

After all the hype I had to try and my chicken salad sandwich was really good! I was not a fan of the duck fat fries or the quack and cheese. The taste of duck fat was strong and left a bad taste in my mouth. My bf had the fried boneless chicken breast sandwich which was also just ok. I probably won't go back.

Easily the worst pizza in town. Wings were the only thing good. Crust is pre made and frozen. Pizza toppings are extremely low quality and almost non existent. Cheese is pre shredded and plasticky. Poppers are frozen, bought product, not house made. 

Why send complimentary cookies that are so poorly made?

My boyfriend and I had polar opposite experiences here, on the same visit together.  So I would give it one star, he would give it 5..... which averages to three.  

It was our first time trying this place.  Customer service was good - everyone says aloha!  Food came out quickly.

We started out with onion rings.  They were ok, but nothing special. 

I ordered a grilled cheese and it was yucky :(  I can't describe what the deal was, but I had my boyfriend check it out and he thinks it had a ton of butter on it, and that's what made it taste bad.  It smelled just like it tasted too - a weird dusty old taste. My boyfriend said it was like movie theater popcorn butter.  I couldn't finish it, each bite was just gross.  I figured my boyfriend would eat it for me or want to take it home for later since he eats ANYTHING - Old food, food that's been sitting out, anything.... he's like a garbage disposal - But even he thought it was nasty.  So that was a bummer.  I got fries with it, and they were alright.

But out of the onion rings, grilled cheese, and fries I really wasn't digging any of it, and don't want to go back to have more. 

My boyfriend got the Colby burger and he loved it.  He said it was the best burger he has ever had.  He ordered it with mushrooms, avocado, and bacon and it was huge.  He couldn't stop talking about what a great place this was and how great his food was.  

I mean he was REALLY in love with this place...  He is the type of guy who is super stingy with tips and hates to tip for any sort of service, even though I feel like especially at restaurants, it is expected.  But he said that he wanted to give our guy an EXTRA big tip!  I have never heard those words come out of his mouth before.  He is also the type who is very hard to please at restaurants... every little thing bugs him, he always says he never wants to return because of some crazy little thing.  But here he said that he WANTS to go back and that he had an awesome experience (for example, he liked how the server asked for permission before taking his plate away - told you he's a hard-ass!). 

So I'm really dreading going back, but I think we will be doing that soon.  I guess I should have figured that at a place called "cheeseburger" you should probably just order a cheeseburger instead of trying something that isn't the restaurant's forte.

This latest visit to Brio was the opposite of my last visit. Last time I liked my food and the service was bad. This time my food was ok and the service was better. I had the Pasta Brio and I was surprised to find the noodles a bit mushy and not al dente. For bearing the restaurant's name, I thought this would be a better pasta dish. It was very one note and just didn't really do anything for me. 

The service this time was decent. We sat in the bar area as opposed to the dining room last time. The bar was faster. We had to remind the server of a couple things but she had a lot going on.

Decent food, nice outdoor courtyard seating, live music was playing I'm not sure if it's every night but they did last Friday nite. The alcoholic drinks aren't all that bartender could do better. Unsure of the dress code most people we were dressy.

I have always loved Chipotle, and have never had a problem at any of the locations. However, now I am apprehensive about going back. I stopped in the Evesham Township, NJ location last night to pick up dinner. I ordered a bowl with chicken. When I got home and proceeded to eat my dinner I was greatly disappointed. The rice was terrible! Most of it was hard and crunchy. I ended up throwing out the bowl and cooking something else for dinner.

The food was good...the service was not.  Wouldn't come back based on this experience.
