Well it's definitely not Louie's anymore. 
Food prices are 20-25% higher, no meaningful drink specials and food quality has dropped tremendously. Took away family atmosphere and booths to enlarge bar. Got to see my first bar fight since college (my kids unfortunately saw it too) with 3 sheriffs showing up. Todd Johnson the majority owner was there when it happened. 
Our neighborhood bar and grill is now just the hood bar...
The sandwiches they sell are good but when you find hair in the food after waiting an hour for delivery  !horrible!  They need to work on cleanliness and speed. Thank you for reading

Not a lot of tables for seating and the wait is long. The flavors are unique and crafty. I can see why there's so much hype for this trendy place ! But if you just want a quick sweet treat better off getting a frosty at Wendy's across the street. If you don't mind waiting get a cutesy little ice smile treat !

Yummy, but average.  Found on Yelp and glad it was close to home.  They claim to be food from The Hill and it was a lot like Mom's Deli.  The food was ok, wait kinda stunk, but the guy who took our order and delivered food to the table was awesome. Very nice and fun.
Hate to complain, but the food just wasn't good. Nobody in my family enjoyed it. The music was great, and the service was average. Could've gotten better food at McDonald's to be completely honest.

This is one of the most profoundly poor experiences I have ever had with a meal involving sushi.  The fish was not fresh, the rice was still warm around it, the teriyaki chicken clearly came from a bird that celebrated it's 65th birthday yesterday.  Even the batter around the tempura was not a traiditionally made batter- it was the type of batter that you get from cafeteria fried fish.  Only thing worth noting was that they got good color on the gyoza when frying it and the sauce it came with was good.  I would never send anyone I liked to this restaurant.

Well I used to recommend this place but after going the last couple of times I have been dissapointed.

I ordered the shrimp bucatini and it literally tasted just like eating tomato sauce. It had no blend of flavors or any discernable quality.  

The crab cakes are about the only thing here that I would still recommend.

My family and I haven't been here in about 5 yrs ... we will not go back again.. I don't know if there is new management or what, but the ceiling has a huge hole in it right near the tables where we sat.. it was constantly leaking and no bucket was being used. You can't tell me no one noticed it ??  Also the bathrooms were a mess.. the floors were sticky and one of the toilets wouldn't flush.. the water was leaking out of the joints and running all over the back of the toilet. 2 our if the 3 sinks were broken. We were really disappointed in the look and feel of this place... very slow service when it was dead also..  sorry but go elsewhere..

Disappointing first time experience. We bought tickets to see The Weekend perform, but he flaked. Not the club's fault, but they tried to make up for it and failed.

They tried to do damage control by allowing ticket purchasers free admission to the club - they had Jayceeoh DJ the night away, but it sucked. There was one point where he was trying to switch the song and it got stuck / stalled. I added a video of it; before the clip starts, it was going on for at least 1 minute. Lol. 

The drinks were full of ice and I believe $12-14 each...typical and overpriced. 

Our group of 6 stayed there no longer than an hour. Overall it was decent, but nothing amazing.

i like to support small neighborhood businesses; however today i received the worst pedicure of my life at violet salon. my feet are rough and bleeding (no lie) and there is polish on my toes instead of just the nails. when i complained, the same woman was sent back to correct it, but couldn't understand why i didn't want her near my feet with a sharp object. i was still expected to pay full price for this "service." essentially i have spent $20 today for a good bleeding. i hope the time i took to write this review can spare others the same experience.

My doctor is good, but every time I'm actually sick, even for a chronic condition that only requires a refill, dealing with their staff is a nightmare. I've been misdiagnosed by a PA, hung up on while a receptionist was fighting with her significant other on the phone for choosing the 'emergency' line during an emergency, and denied a prescription causing me needless suffering and multiple phone calls. If you aren't able to help me when I'm sick, why are you my doctor?

We found this place by luck (more of a BAD LUCK). The restaurant seemed nice and cozy - it was almost a perfect place to enjoy a meal with the family after a "hard" day under the sun at the beach. We orderred some apetizer and entrée as soon as we got seated. The apetizer came out rather quickly, and they were excellent! Especially the white wine clams. The buffalo wings and calamari were good too. Unfortunately, it was all downhill from there. The entrées took almost 2 HOURS to be served. Granted, there were 7 people in our party, and the kitchen might have had to wait a little for all of the dishes to be ready for serving all at once, which I could understand  and appreciate. But I didn't  think it was the case because the 6-person party next to us and couple of smaller parties around us got their food way before us even though  they placed their orders after us. We queried the nice waitress multiple  times as we started to lose our patience, and she had no clue as to why it took so long. Finally, the food came out, only after we had lost our cool and had to express some discontent to the waitress. Well, besides the ungodly long wait, the entrées were not too far from a total disappointment! The pizza was of Pizza Hut quality, and the crust was noticeably stale as if it was pulled out of the oven centuries ago. The meatball spaghetti was a dish of disaster mainly because of the mediocre marinara sauce on top of a clump of inadequately cooked noodles. Other dishes were aceptable but barely noteworthy. Overall, in plain language, THIS PLACE SUCKS. It pains me to even give it a one-star. It is a disgrace that this restaurant bares the name of "Italian Gourmet." This $200 dinner was a total failure!!!

I love ML Rose. We have been eating here for years. They have a phenomenal beer selection and tons of tvs. I am disappointed to say that when I had a baby and we brought her here the other night (1.5 year old) for a casual dinner, there was no kids menu or high chairs. We eat out a lot and this is the only place we have ever been that doesn't even have high chairs. What?!

I had to try this because a friend told me it was the best fish spread. I found the fish spread to be very fishy and that overpowered the taste. It looks much darker than most fish spreads I am used to but I don't know what kind of fish they use. My daughter and husband also tried it and didn't like it. I am not sure if they use liquid smoke but the smoke flavor was also off putting to me. I also ordered German potato salad and it was very dry. It had a slight vinegar taste but not the sweet sour taste you expect from German salad. It seems as though they skimped on the sauce to make it cheaper.
Location, location, location is what they have going for them.  The rest was underwhelming...  The space and bathroom were rather dirty and the beer was unimpressive.

Update - New Owner - Used to be 5 stars, now 1
Unfortunately the owner of Joto's in Tampa bought this location. We have been several times since and it has gotten worse each time. We are very sad, it was our favorite sushi place. Cost cutting is in full effect, very little avocado on the rolls, rolls fall apart, fish was so cold like it just came out of the refrigerator. After our last visit, I told my wife I never want to go back there. 

As someone else mentioned, new sushi chefs and only a couple of the same people that used to work there.

My wife and I have been going to Joto's for 4 years and it is one of our favorite sushi places in Tampa Bay. First off the sushi is great - we love the volcano (best ever) and the food is always fresh. They have a nice selection of sake to choose from as well. We have never tried anything off of their regular menu...just the sushi.
The staff at Joto's is FANTASTIC. From the guys who seat people and bus the tables, the ladies who wait on the customers in their kimonos and the sushi chefs preparing the food. We have always felt very welcome and comfortable there.
The atmosphere is great too. It is very calming and a perfect ambience for enjoying sushi with a special person!

The service was horrible. We waiting on a table forever luckily the waitress came over and cleaned one for us.(due to the large group we needed a big table) The kitchen staff trying to rush out messing up everyone food. The silverware was so dirty. It wasn't as packed today thank god. They really need to step it up with the kitchen staff. And yes it wasn't that packed yet we waited to be seated and they had 7/8 empty tables.  The games was fun and the kids did have a blast besides the bad food experience.

I really should have used my better judgement and ruled against eating at a joint on Bourbon Street.  None of the employees seemed particularly pleased to be there, and it was still early in the night.  I feel bad for the customers who had to deal with them 3-4 hours later.

The food was fine, but really wasn't anything to write home about.  No wonder the place was half-empty during Mardi Gras.  The inside is decorated classy, perhaps to justify the inflated prices, but the food certainly isn't worth it.

And I'm not sure whether would consider this a plus or a minus, but the restaurant is open to Bourbon Street, so you get to enjoy the freak show with your meal.

Went to look at dishwashers.  My husband and I need one, and my mom needs one as well.   And they were offering 40% off all dishwashers. 

There was a particular model on sale for $199, and only one left in stock.  Mom wanted that one.  She went to purchase it and was told, while they accept MasterCard, they only accept SEARS MasterCard.  Of course, they offered to let her apply for one at the counter.  She refused. 

I don't have a Sears card either, but we decided to go look at the other Sears Outlet...., the one on Rt 70 in Cherry Hill.  Great store, and I will be reviewing that next, but the point is, they accept all credit cards!  (Plus they had about  10 of the model dishwasher mom wanted.)

So I would advise you to avoid this store.  I don't know what their agenda is, but it seems to me that policies on credit cards should not vary from store to store.  I mean, it's Sears!  Shouldn't it be consistent?   I think these sales people get bonuses for signing people up or have some other agenda. Either way, it does not instill trust.
I used to love this place until the last time. I got food poisoning. I went to the mall right after and within 1 hour I was seriously sick! Never again!

I had so much hope for this place...but it was not good.  Apparently I don't know what  Paneed means because to deep fry a lamb chop is truly hearbreaking!  
Our server seemed realitively new, she couldn't tell me what cut of lamb chop was offered.  I don't like to order loin chops at restaurants, lucky she found out they were rib chops...yeah!  And medium rare please as the chef "recommends".  
My date had the stuffed salmon.  And then...our server asked for a temp on that too?  I found that questionable to doubtful that was going to happen.  
I am a formally trained chef. My boyfriend looks at me with question and I know disappointment looms.  I suggest he get it medium, knowing this is impossible.  If the fish is medium the stuffing will be cold.  But hey they offered, so give that a spin!
Imagine the absolute horror on my face when my entree arrived.  Why would you deep fry a lamb chop?  And they were so tiny and thin...just one well done, very crunchy, flavorless bite per very long well frenched bone.  Now back to that medium salmon...it was well done.  As I suspected.  Very monochromatic and fishy in nature. 
Needless to say our meals were expensive for the quality.  It was very hard to justify the cost and leave feeling hungry.  It's a beautiful space but a culinary fail.   There is way better food within blocks.

My only complaint with this location is the disgusting bathroom. Any other Target close by such as Sicklerville & Deptford are way cleaner!  This bathroom has only 3 stalls, the toilet seats look like they need to be replaced and the entire restroom is obviously never cleaned during the day. 

They are currently renovating the entire store and hopefully they make the bathroom bigger with new toilet seats at the very least.

Truly terrible service. The hipsters have the urgency of a tortoise and couldn't be less engaging or friendly. We waited forever for an old fashioned cocktail and after watching a majority of the staff work to do menial work like stack crates I eventually called them over to tell them I had been waiting 20 minutes for a drink though the place was virtually empty. All were confused and them took another 10 minutes to figure out what to do about being slow and incompetent. Would never return.

there was 3 of us for a late lunch about 3:30, the waitress handed us a menu for early bird specials and they didn't have half the stuff on the menu, I asked for an order of goat cheese bruschetta and the waitress told me they didn't have goat cheese so I asked to substitute it for mozzarella and she said we arnt allowed to do that, she ended up placing the order for me and it was good, we then asked about the soup of the day and she replied with "we don't make soups during the summer due to the fact that TJ will just throw it away" we then proceeded to order our meal. we then waited for an hour and then she finally came back and we asked what was going on and she replied with "sorry our kitchen staff just got here" i also asked for a straw that i waited 20 minutes for and then i finally asked another waitress. we finally got out food 1 hr and 45 mins later!!!! she was very apologetic but don't open your doors if you don't have people to make that food.

Do not go here!!!!   Be aware of the shot girls. The cops even call them shots-titutes. Insane. I was forced to pay my way out of jail. Please do not go here. Please enjoy all the rest of New Orleans, it is a good place, just not here.

With a reservation from start to finish was over 75 minutes for an acrylic fill in.  The polish was tacky and never dried. I ruined my manicure on the way home.  I had to take it off when I got home and paint them again. Maybe it was old polish but that is not what I paid for

This place doesn't even deserve one star. The icing on the cake of my horrible experience was getting served cold lasagna. It had definitely been sitting out for some time and not under any heat. The waitress was slow and rude. The only good part of my meal was the salad because it actually came out quickly and isn't meant to be warm. Manager was polite and attempted to apologize but at that point I just wanted to leave. This place left a bad taste in my mouth in more ways than one.

I will not use them again.  My cleaner cancelled with less than two hours' notice before the cleaning was supposed to begin.  I was left high and dry on a weekend on which I was having guests.  This is completely unacceptable. HJ needs to get its act together.

(I live in Philadelphia - Yelp profile has old information)

My father and I were attending a legal seminar down the street and thought we found a bargain.  This was most frightening and unaccommodating looking establishment I have ever seen. We drove into the horrible collapsing labyrinth of a parking garage to find apparent prostitutes sitting in their car and people fighting.  I like to use yelp, but I recommend that you read and believe any of the worst reviews on trip advisor.  We left that parking garage and never looked back.

Slightly overrated but not bad. I respect that it's been open for so long and continues to operate at relatively low prices.

That being said, I would have preferred more variety. My cherry and pineapple ice was quite good but I've had better in Philly.

I would come back here but I wouldn't seek it. There must be better places to get italian ice around these parts!

Ok! Coming along. This visit upgrades Spasso to a place where you actually might try eating!

Visited midweek with about 6 people this time, and, got the standard start: we wait five minutes in the foyer while staff mill about a not-close-to-full restaurant doing I don't know what. After being seated, though, everything went right.

Our server, a lady with a European accent, was much more attentive and welcoming this time. That helps! She was nice enough to make a reasonable suggestion for someone who had a sense of what she wanted but didn't want to traverse the menu to find it. The waitress assured her they could make what she was describing.

The wine list has enough selections that you should be able to find something you like. We got some bread, appetizers, drinks, etc. and were neither being hurried along nor ignored. Another positive, and 180 degrees from prior visits.

I think it may be wise to order the specials, as they seem to put some attention and care into those. We all found the entrees to be flavorful and attractively presented. Another positive is we did not experience the "dumped from a jar" feel of some of the accouterments like the last couple of times. 

So I upgrade Spasso to "worth a try", but be ready for a hit or miss experience. So far, for what it's worth, Wednesday has proved to be our best night there. Sunday looks like a no-fly zone.

This was the worst experience I have ever had. The wings were nothing special, the service is horrible, it is so hot due to the lack of air conditioning, and the restaurant is very dirty. I do not recommend this place to anyone..

I used to LOVE Jones but the food has been sub-par recently. Today, my pancakes were delicious bus my fiancé had a pretty mediocre omelette and we paid $5 for a "fruit bowl" containing a handful of grapes and banana slices topped with four raspberries. It was a dish appropriate for a preschooler. Not impressed but I love the decor so I'll probably give it another chance.

So disappointed in this cafe. For starters our server Kate had the personality of a slug. No smile, no kind words, she only spoke when it was absolutely necessary. The server brought 2 plates with ice cold fries. The french bread requested for the red beans and rice was bought out with globs of ice cold margarine. Overall food and service was absolutely sub- standard. Most definitely will not return.

$35 for a hair cut and the place does not take credit cards.  Add in an annoyingly gay barber and that about sums up my experience.

I won't be be back, but I doubt they will care.  They have enough people who will come for Dominic.

Many things are relative. So is this place: there is a dim sum/Cantonese restaurant place next door, and these appear to be the "authentic two" in Northeast Philly on Bustleton Avenue. However, I highly prefer and recommend the place next door called Ruby Garden over this place.

Long wait on a weekend for dim sum - with inefficient service and low selection (comparing to next door). Questionable sanitary practices, especially in the summer when they leave the door open. They are more eager to seat you than serving you real food.

In the beginning when this place opened this was an iffy okay-ish place. But now - stay away for dim sum. Dinner time is somewhat okay-ish, but for the prices, taste and service - go next door or risk being frustrated. Why settle for "meh" when you can have okay to good next door? Gambling may be exciting, but not on food.

Only go to this location if you want cold food that takes 60min to come to your table, if you want one of your party members to not get their food, no refills on water or any other drinks. Other businesses should go here to learn how to run a business to the ground. The new server was the only person competent at his job.

Horrible, 

Was advised to sit at the bar to wait for a seat. Then was told to reserve a seat you must wait outside. Ridiculous and unprofessional , I would. Not advise trying, when Oceania is accommodating and just a block away.

Nice looking location! Unfortunately, they have only been open less than a month and I've already gotten snotty attitude ridden service. Not something you want to deal with early in the morning. I miss the staff from the one recently closed down the road already.

This is probably the worst place I used I got a general cleaning and still seeing roaches two days after they came by and sprayed. The lady was literally done in about 20 minutes I figured I'd give him the benefit of a doubt since it was done so quickly. I paid $65 I could have bought my own supplies and done it myself.

I did the tourist thing here and at Pat O's. At least the notorious hand grenades- even though obviously sweet- had a decent taste to them and pack a punch. Think midori sours spiked with 151. Ok to try once.

I have been in contact with owner of the Company, he made the situation right.  Can't say too much more though this was the process I went through. Looking at the reviews to the right, makes me say hmmmm. 
Marie DeMaria 
Real Estate Broker 
Baird and Warner

I've been here a couple of times and found the food is just okay. The tuck special is probably the best sandwich, but only available during lunch which is a bummer. The egg on top really makes this sandwich! The chicken sandwich is also pretty good, especially with the white sauce, but not too memorable. The most disappointing thing about this place is their ribs. They are terrible... not tender, dry, and doesn't fall off the bones like a good bbq ribs should be. The smokey flavors there but the execution on cooking it was not. Ambiance is okay, you order in line (can be really long during meal times) and then find a table for the to bring your food to you. We got yelled at for not doing that; went with another couple and had the SOs order food, while we sat at a table and waited...but NOOO you can't do that!! Geez.

This location is in a perfect spot - Busy part of Baltimore pike.

They have a nice comfy waiting area.  Comfy chairs.. coffee and cold drinks.  

A nice large TV is there to kill the time.

It's ok.  I spent an hour before finding out my tire couldn't be repaired.  Came back later for a new tire.    I was then told I would be charged $8 to clean the corosion on my tire ??  I think on a $200+ tire that the $8 felt like a nickle and dime charge.

Anyway the service was good and the tire arrive faster then anticiapted.
They were invited to join our school event with 15% of their proceeds benefiting the school. They were the busiest truck and never donated the amount they agreed too. Total bummer!

Went on a Wednesday night and did their special.  App, entrée, and dessert for $18.  Caesar salad was good, steak and sausage pasta was great and chocolate brownie was OK.  Service was horrible, so the drop in score.

They cancelled on my 9-months-pregnant-wife and I the night before we needed them at our house to clean for the baby shower that weekend and didn't have a backup they could send us. Definitely left us hanging with no explanation and a terrible compensation offer. We actually had to push for a refund and dispute that they'd already charged us when they said they hadn't. So: they can't cover no-shows, can't manage their accounts, and don't understand how to work with a customer who depends on them. Nice.

Not bad! Had the monkey bread ($8.50), Fried Chicken and Waffles ($15) and mac and cheese ($8??). The monkey bread was excellent. Essentially it is like a cinnamon bun except not curled up with the hard crispy parts (except for the outside). I wouldn't mind just eating a couple of those as a meal. The interior was very soft and moist and very yummy. 
The chicken and waffles was okay. I would say that the Green Eggs Cafe has a better dish, although it will be slightly smaller. It had two large pieces of boned chicken but the dish just didn't taste that special to me. And the gravy was essentially thick chicken noodle soup which is not something I really seek out. 
Same with the mac and cheese. It was decent, but I wouldn't order it again. 

Service was good, wait to get a table was only 20 minutes for Sunday Brunch even though it was pretty packed and the food came out fast as well. 

I would check this place out again though because some of the other items on the menu sounded good. 

Food Quality/Taste = 3 Stars
Portion Size Given Price = 3 Stars
Price = 3 Stars
Experience with Staff = 4 Stars
Decor = 4 Stars
Wait Time = 5 Stars
Jesus Christ this place is terrible. Waited half an hour for a beer while the place was dead. Food was microwaved. Poor waitress tried her best but management is clearly out to lunch.
The food here is ok, because the dipping sauce does not taste like the sauce in JP. The dish "seafood and yasai" is too salty.

I have to begin this review by saying that I have never reviewed a restaurant online before but I felt it my duty to try to save someone else from the same mistake. 
 I ordered a steak sandwich with mushrooms, cheese and onion rings. My husband ordered a roast beef plate with mashed potatoes. 
1. my steak sandwich had a small piece of steak(the menu said 6 oz so I understand that). However, the steak had so much grissle(I think that's how you spell it) that I could only actually chew and swallow a few bites. 2. The mushrooms on the sandwich were cold, canned mushrooms. I'm not sure if it's a gourmet rule on cooking but I have never ordered a steak sandwich and the restaurant put canned mushrooms on it.
3. Because they put canned mushrooms on the sandwich, the piece(sandwich slice size) of cheese didn't melt so envision a grissly(sp) steak sandwich, cold watery canned mushrooms, a hard piece of unmelted cheese and bread that was twice the size of anything contained in it.
3. My onion rings( that i had to pay $2 extra for) were cold.  
My steak sandwich and onion rings came to $10.50.....RIDICULOUS
My husband fared slightly better as only his mashed potatoes were cold. His was just a smaller size plate though, no where near worth the price. He paid for it with the fact that he couldn't get them to refill his tea. 
The total cost for the 1 tea and 2 meals, $26.00. For that amount of money we could have went to several places in the area and got FAR better food. Joey b's, pasta house, affton cafe...just to name a few within a few miles. 
Also to note that because, even though it was rainy and 48 degrees, they had the door open because "it was being fixed". The man who was fixing it was chatting on the phone on the porch the whole time we tried to eat.
If the owner reads this, my suggestion to them would be, cut the menu down to a few things and make them quality food.  I suspect the lack of quality is due to trying to have too much variety.  
Save your money and go to one of the other places around.

I attended a wedding at this Venue this past weekend. The venue is beautiful and the women's bathroom decor is gorgeous. But unfortunately the good parts ended there... The drinks were watered down the food lacked any type of seasoning and the DJ played one or two songs from the years 2017 & 2018. A bad DJ can definitely ruin an event. Luckily my family knows how to party regardless so we still had fun. If you are in the market for a wedding venue and can afford a place like Falls Manor, you might want to invest your cash somewhere else.

The pizza was good... not the best i have had seemed like too much sauce and not enough cheese. But i do have to say i had it delivered and they were at my house before the online time said it would be, and it was still hot. All in all ill still order from here.
Piss poor service.  The quality and the size of the roll was disappointing for a place that specialize in sushi.  I tried and will never go back.

We stopped in for dinner on a Wednesday night after dropping our son off at college.  We sat in their lively outdoor patio and tried several of their small plates.

Apparently the menu outside is a big different than inside.  We tried a number of small plates including grilled shrimp (yum), crab cakes - supposedly their specialty (ho-hum) and wanted to try some of their specialty drinks but they were out of basil (wtf?).

A+ on the atmosphere, OK on the service and decent food.  Will give them another chance to impress when we are back for a visit.

OK~ I get it donuts are great, nice to be able to get a parking. Service is friendly and place is cool. But 2.50 for a donut?? $20 for a dozen! At least offer a free cup of coffee with that dozen.  I hear the chicken is awesome but I was in such shock after my donut tab I had to go sit down and re-group. Next time I'll get chicken but definitely skip the donut$.
Slightly above average food and relatively below average service in a pretty generic environment. One of the most unremarkable dining experiences I have had.

busy place, service was fast pizza was ood   hat the hot pepper and saugage pie...they do 
mean hot peppers  they were hot...just wish they would of cleaned the seeds out 1st..personal prefference   suprised got a ice tea and it was in a bottle of tea ( coke makes them) not fresh
brewed..

Well, now that they have gotten more popular so I guess they think it's safe to cut back a bit; less meat, less bread, and omg, it's mostly iceberg lettuce now.

Do I still it's worth it?  Definitely.  I guess I'm a little spoiled because I was there when they were first opened, the servings were much better, and they used romaine lettuce only.

The meat on the spit could be better.  El Greco is a Greek place in Sarasota.  They have meat on spit as well but somehow theirs doesn't look dried out on the spit.  You can see because it's visible from the window as you walk by the restaurant.

Love the staff!

Very fun, best way to describe this retro 1960/70 through back is a diner. A Disneyfied experience down to the green shag carpet in the dining room. Friendly service, our only choice was the brunch menu Saturday  midday. Food was good, ample and homey. Nothing cutting edge, but the idea of a through back is not to take you forward. Quality fires and ingredients. Value is there. Share to maximize the dollar.
I would never go to the place again. The food is processed right out of the can. Chicken wings have wings, the stuffed crab cake was extremely cold. Please save your money and don't waste your time.

I was excited to see a somewhat local restaurant right off the highway as we drove through Nashville. We were trying to avoid fast food and look for more unique, local food joints to enjoy and keep the trip fresh. 

We were excited when we first walked into M.L. Rose as it has an awesome vibe and was clearly a popular choice for the local crowd. Excellent beer menu and a fun selection of burgers. 

I liked my burger but felt like it was overpriced for the size of the burger and toppings - can't remember what it was called but it had a sweet potato bun, tobacco onion straws, pimento cheese. Great concept but just a pit too expensive at $12.

My partner had the breakfast burger...eggs needed to be cooked a bit more as they were clear in parts but also pretty tasty. 

The main problem was that it took for-ev-er to get our food and then our check. We were trying to stick with a timeline and were running pretty late by the time we left. It was busy so understandably, it took some time to do right, but 30 minutes for our burgers was just too much.
Great new Orleans dinner , food WAs good, not greasy at all, friendly staff if ur in the garden district Check it out. Plenty of other places for bfast in the city, there is nothing to special here

Large assortment of food but am annoyed that the lunch buffet price changes frequently....this winter it was $9.95 for a time but today it was back to $12.95.
We found their roll menu average and a bit heavy.  Hot buffet has American fried selections. Seems more like a $9.95 value to us...not planning to go back as frequently.

My family and I have been going here for a long time.  They have a consistent wait staff who are always very polite, it depends on when you go for fresh food.  The Sat. $10 lunch or lunch time is the best value. 

I've had one bad experience but we went back and they were super nice and accommodating to us.

I stopped in yesterday and picked up the BLT that everyone is talking about.  I also grabed a turkey club sandwich too.  The place is roomy and nice.  There was only one other customer there so I didn't have to wait long to order or to get my food.

The sandwiches were good, but not spectacular.  The cajun bacon BLT was interesting.  I felt the bacon was more sweet than spicey.  The club was good too, but I've definately had better.  The size of the sandwiches were small compared to what you would receive from other sammie spots.  

Prices are cheap, the menu is expansive, and the food was good.  I'll be back when in the area.

I really wish they were more family friendly in the evenings. My son likes their burgers and it would be great to go there after his baseball games (etc.). We like to eat out for dinners, and unfortunately the restaurant's policy doesn't make that possible.

The food is good; the beer selection is awesome. Sometimes the service is a bit slow; haphazard is really the better description.

Overall I really like the restaurant; I haven't eaten anything there that I don't like, and we love Monday night bingo!

This place was just average mainly based on our server who was rude.

Greeted with if you want breakfast you don't have time to look at the menu as I need to enter it now. Felt rushed and after 2 times of saying I needed to look, I just responded I'll order from the lunch because I just don't know. I had never been there.

Husband ordered a spicy sausage poboy which he said was good. There was an extra charge to add cheese and this was not communicated up front. I guess because we were so rushed.

I ordered the Cajun pasta which had good flavor but you could tell seemed mass produced. So much sauce just poured over some noodles.

This places get business because of location to Jackson Square but I wouldn't go out of my way to eat there.

Fun for the family, if you don't eat

Good Laser tag, bowling, and video games. The kids had a blast. The service in the restaurant was slooooowwww.  The chips were so stale I sent them back. The tortillas were also stale. The mahi was cold. So were the potato skins. Also they were dry. The sour cream had a funny taste. In a nearly empty restaurant, our food for a table of 3 took 25 minutes at least. I'm guessing most of it was made right away based on the temp and the rest came much later. Fortunately my daughter's chicken strips and fries were hot. Although, she was so biliary playing, she didn't notice.

Must've stopped in here at the wrong time -- Mardi Gras. Since so many fools were acting like fools around the corner on Bourbon, Pravda was pretty laid back. Just what myself and a comrade were looking for at the time.

Bit of a disconnect though. The place was half empty, and the bartender wasn't really busy, but when I asked about any specialties, either that he made or house drinks, he totally punted. Not rude, just kind of lame. I didn't know a thing about the absinthe here until reading these other reviews. So I wound up with a pretty weak Jack & Coke in a sad little plastic cup. Buyer's remorse kicked in with the very first sip.

The interior was cool enough, but there's no shortage of places with a funky pseudo-dive vibe. Definitely preferred the R Bar and One Eyed Jack's(even more when it was Shim Sham Club) to this place. Oh well.

I guess this is the best indoor go karting will get. I found it expensive and a little bit of a hassle. You have to pay and subscribe for a year license and for like 14 laps (1 race) was pretty outrageous for the price they want. There are better karting experiences which can go much faster if you're a speed junkie. Keeping track of lap times and tracking them all is a cool thing though.

This hotel establishment was a bit to hipster for us. The lobby was extremely loud we assume because there was a restaurant on one side and bar lounge on the other. The front desk personnel were a bit snotty and were not attentive mostly cause they were busy on their phones and messing with paperwork. The only person we felt was attentive was the gentleman that took our bags to hold cause we got their early. Once we got to our room we were pleased with the "look" of the room but the bed felt horrible! It felt like a cheap box spring...no cushion at all, we had ask for extra pillows and even then it did not help! Asked for a late check out and it was denied

Horrible! Joke of a business. Bought a groupon and will probably have to fight to get it refunded. 

I guess there is only one person who can provide the groupon service who doesn't answer her phone & only texted to say she couldn't help me. She is also working during the day at a different job so she can't even talk to me to help.

She also mentioned that Adonias phone was broke? So it is impossible to make an appointment or use this Groupon basically.

VERY unprofessional!

Had shellac done during my mani...I was assured it would take no longer than 30 minutes as I was on my lunch break. 50 minutes later, she was taking off the polish because she needed it up. Within 3 days the shellac (which should last 2 weeks) came off. Very disappointed!

Nice service. Had a small tea selection for high tea. I went with a friend of mine because we were already dressed nice and felt like treating ourselves. Overall, a nice experience. The high tea has an assortment of tea sandwiches and scones and pastries. They all taste fairly well. The scones are very nice. The tea room I was used to going to (not in Tampa) is casual and laid back and run by the sweetest Scottish ladies. So it is different to come here because it is a bit classy and I felt the need to use more manners. :)

Attending "Dining under the Stars" in Media this past Wednesday Night. What a great experience. The dining experience on the other hand, not so much. 

Our waiter was rude and pushy/rushy. I will give him the benefit of the doubt, although we had our reservations at 8:45 and looking around, it didn't seem to be that "busy".

I ordered the gnoicchis with red meat sauce. SPECIFICALLY asking for them to be cooked firm. He assured us that is how they are made. Come to find out, They were not. In fact, they had a texture like mashed potatoes. 

Would not recommend.

First time in New Orleans, walked in here the ambiance was nice there was nice music on the streets but the Food here was horrible.  I'd never come here again or recommend to anyone.The mac and cheese tasted like kraft. The greens had no seasonings. Gumbo was bad. I've had way better food in Minnesota. Everything tasted like it came out of a box. The fried chicken was good though.
Food was slopped on plate, asparagus was over-cooked and fish was not filleted properly.   We found this restaurant on a walk-by.  We should have kept walking.   Disappointed.

The food is a bit more plentiful than many of the takeout places around,however they sometimes have the same issue as most of the places in that the order takers are easily distracted thereby messing our orders up no matter how many times we repeat...

The hostess/manager was polite and was able to seat us fast.  The place felt a little hospital-ish.  It was very bright and all white tile.  The oysters were good, and came quickly.  The po-boys and shrimp and grits were both good.  

We went here, as all the other places on Bourbon Street had long lines.  We'd probably go there again, under similar circumstances.  Not a memorable dinner, rather a quick and easy bite out.
Ordered through uber eats . I didn't receive my sauce or the chips I ordered. The 4 wings I did receive were breaded too much and very tasteless. Not a fan , I will not order from here again .

Worst choice of the trip...

I'll keep this short.  We walked into Dave & Busters a few hours before a flight to get a couple of drinks and have some fun with the games.  Several of us bought cards (required to play the games, of course), at a $14 minimum -- $10 in games, plus a $2 new card fee and a $2 "super charge" fee... no kidding; I was wondering why they didn't charge me for the rust-proofing.

15 minutes after we arrived, the power went out.  A flustered manager told us that his system wasn't working with the power out, so he could not refund our money.  It was unclear if we would be able to get our money back when the power was eventually restored, as he would not answer that question directly and suggested we come back the next day to play some games.  With a flight leaving 4 hours later, that wasn't really an option.

This turned out to be a complete waste of 30 minutes and $14.  Bummer.

It's been a while since I've dealt with really poor customer service, but look no further if that's your thing.
Food is good. But service is one of the worse I've ever had. I highly doubt we will be back again.

Our server was awful and because of her inattentive nature created a stressful dining experience. The food was pretty good. The rolls and dipping herbs were tasty. Our appetizer was really great and we enjoyed the honey orange glaze dip for the shrimp. The salad is usually a delicious delight; however, the salad had way too much salad dressing and made the typically tasty salad a disappointment. Our food was served timely and was nice. My meal was the salmon with vegetables and asparagus, my spouse chose the steak with grilled vegetables and seasoned potatoes. The potatoes were way overloaded in seasoning and made eating them a chore, just like the salad. Our server did not check on us and we needed drink refills. The three women seated at the table beside us had a different server who was very attentive to their needs. We usually get dessert but was so aggravated by the end of our meal, we opted out of dessert. Good service and appropriate seasoning is essential in making happy patrons in any dining experience.
OK food bad crews and horrible customer service at Lowery zoo...  Not sufficient signage to charge extra for credit card but wanted to charge additional $1.00 for a $8.99 plate

1 hour and 45 minutes.
That's how long it took to get our dinner and eat it.
And the restaurant was only about  1/2 full.  No refills on salsa or chips in our wait time. 
And the table was in need of a good cleaning not just wiping off.  Sticky.  The whole top.
Try Lupita's on Thornydale instead.  Better food and price.  And timing.  But no the big name.

I saw that they had new management and decided to give them a try.  To go order: the server told me she will take care of everything and that all my silverware was in the bag when the order came up.  My fault, I did not check the bag, but for business travelers, we don't have silver where in our hotel room.  I called and told them that I didn't get any silverware and they told me that I should have checked and "too bad". Will it go back... customer lost

DO NOT live here they are infested with mice and do absolutely nothing about it. We caught 3 in one day. Contacted maintenance several times since December. They brought ONE trap and did nothing else. Earlier in the year their solution was putting a sign up saying don't leave bird feed out because it attracts mice.Would never recommend ANYONE to live here.


Update
Called over 13 days ago about mice, have caught several more. Have not received a call, email or visit from maintenance. Very disappointing that they do not care about their tenants.
Waited an hour for a calzone and 2 slices of plain pizza. Missed two movie times as a result. To top it off, they never put our order in so we were sitting there for no reason. Never again.

I visited this place because, hey I figured its nice to support family owned places. Boy was I wrong. The place is way over priced. Next time I'm going to Costco save some money and my taste buds, and buy some wedges @ Vons! psshh.... 10 bucks for a handful of wedges! Are you kidding me? Never again, and I would never recommend.

Oh my lord! This place just delivered my family uncooked pork in the lomien. I call them up, they said sometimes we make mistakes. I say refund my money, they say we don't do that. I say I'm calling board of health department Monday morning. She says do what you want, we don't care. They are way higher priced then the others also. Eat at your own risk!

Interesting.

It feels like a good ol Harley bar with it's dressed up dressed down image, nice on the inside (looks completely redone, but is not all flashy) and "Rustic" on the outside.

I wouldn't call it a dive, but then again maybe I would....I dunno.

It's worth checking out if you like sports and cheap brews, the people seem rather friendly and very down to earth.... I only caught a white-trash side about twice... pretty good I would say.

This is a long-standing concept for fund raising and TN Flavors still has room for improvement.

I think it's important that the event be held where the funds raised are used--the monies raised here benefit the Nashville State Community College (NSCC) Foundation which was established in 1994 as an independent 501c3 nonprofit organization and operates for the sole benefit and support of NSCC and its students.  Folks can see the "who" (students), the "what" (education), and the "where" (Nashville State Community College) first hand and know that their contributions are well spent.  

That being said, it would seem that somewhere on campus there is a larger, open area that could accommodate the growth that this event has seen.  My event organization background tells me that there is a psychological edge to a shoulder-to-shoulder event that screams, "EVERYONE is here--THIS is the place to be."  But in my opinion, particularly for an event format such as this, it will hurt attendance in the long-run.  Folks don't want to have to fight to get to what they have donated funds to get!  When food and drink are involved, there needs to be table tops (you can forgo the seats at tables to prevent folks from just camping out), fringe seating for those needing to take a break but not blocking people-flow, a LOT of trash receptacles, and, for this event, more signage INSIDE and on each floor to direct patrons to food areas.  We would have missed out on 2-3 entire rooms of exhibitors had I not seen a few people come in and out of a remote hallway.  This is a detriment to both the exhibitors AND the patrons!  

I think that multiple levels of booths also added to confusion and frustration on the part of event-goers.  We had VIP tickets (these were an amazing gift!) and that gave us a yellow wristband and we were told that there was a VIP room on the third level...reality was that there were several booths in an open area and no oversight as to VIP status treatment--anyone was served.  I think that those who contributed such large sums of money should be made to feel as special as they are for their generosity.

I wish that some of the exhibitors had kept their offerings more simple--it would have been easier for them and just as appreciated by patrons.  Many of the samples were prepared on the spot, which added to long lines and that was an annoyance for many.  I know that the Chefs and food establishments want to show off their skills, variety, and adaptability, but this was not the place...too many people, too little time.  My favorite thing was some FABULOUS guacamole on a chip from Saint Añejo!!  Simple, but *outstanding* flavor. And then there were Christie cookies--so amazing!  YUM!

Overall, I thought the event was good.  Loved meeting new vendors (there are times I have to have events catered for work so I am always looking) and experimenting with some really unique flavors.  

Keep this event and grow it to the fabulous heights it can go.  You have a real good one here--and the real winners are the students!
Stay away from this Moe's. Filthy, I really question the food safety if the restaurant is this dirty...Shane on owners, management, and staff.

Place was dirty. There were 4 employees: two were doing nothing, one made our food (not great BTW), and the last one was doing something with the food. One employee asked for help and was flat out told no by the female employee. You could feel the tension and disconnect in the teamwork. 

There was only 1 other family there. My husband and I couldn't find a place to sit without him taking napkins and cleaning up a table himself. After the workers saw his cell phone recording the establishment, they started to clean up minutes later. Their definition of cleaning consisted of picking up the trash on the table, I didn't see anyone wipe down the tables, and neither one swept. It still looks dirty inside. 

As for the food, the guy kept asking me how to make it. My husband told him that he wanted it like the picture but that didn't help. Then the same person who made the food rang us up to pay and repeatedly asked me what food I ordered (I ordered two things). The chips were completely, and my nachos toppings were all piled on top of each other rather than spread out. 

The whole experience was terrible. Definitely going to be avoiding this establishment. I WASTED MY MONEY COMING HERE. 

I feel bad for the owners because their employees are taking advantage and need retraining!

The employees are rude and mumble so much I could barely understand them. I went twice in a month and both times my orders were wrong when I clearly said what I wanted to buy. The kitchen looked dirty and unkept when I pulled up to pay and as I was driving up to speaker the lady inside was yelling for help. The rest of the staff were in back smoking and playing. I will not waste my money at this location for food cooked in a dirty kitchen by employees who clearly don't like their job and can't even fake a smile.

Food was mediocre, service was horrible! We had a reservation however we weren't seated until an hour/hour and a half later.  By the time we finally got our food, the staff started to move the tables and turn the space into a night club, so we literally were eating on the dance floor! We had to yell over the music to have a conversation.  When we asked to speak to the manager, he immediately came to our table with a confrontational attitude and seemed unfazed by our dissatisfaction.  He even yelled at someone else in my party who told him this was unacceptable....
Absolutely horrible, will not be going back!

This was my favorite casino to gamble & dine at! I've won the most money here than any other place & used to visit for any meal of the day. Unfortunately, one day, I put cash in a machine, & no credit came up on the screen. I immediately pushed the help button, & an attendant came to assist me. I told them what happened, in hopes they could figure out my issue, but instead he accused me of never having put money in because if I did, the credit would have come up. I was appalled, as yes, this was my issue... with the machine. I asked if he could review the cameras, because I would never lie, & I'm sorry for the trouble, I at least just want my money back. I offered to go to a different machine & all. He proceeded to begin inserting vouchers from his pocket into the machine & point as the credits went up from his vouchers. "Seeee? You put money in, you can play. You don't put money in, you can't." He treated me like a child, & when I demanded a supervisor, he claimed he was the supervisor, so I left immediately WITHOUT my money, & never returned. I was, & still am very disappointed in how I was treated.

The live music was good but was expecting more Latin/Mexican music. There was a mixture of r&b, oldies and pop. The salsa tasted straight from a jar. The chips and French fries were very greasy and tasted like they'd been fried in old oil. The mango margarita was good! I got the three enchiladas. Chicken, beans and ground beef. My fiancé got the shrimp Yolanda. All with no flavor or heat. A little adobo could have gone a long way. The server only came by 2 times the whole visit. I still feel 2 stars is very generous.

George W. Bush, one of our great nations 45 greatest Presidents once said, "Fool me once, shame on, shame on you - fool me twice - won't get fooled again."

A year ago my wife and I went to The Block for happy hour and appetizers. Every appetizer was as mediocre as the next. Fast forward to last night. We were going to dinner with two other couples and the first two restaurants we decided on were booked solid. Before I could react The Block was decided upon as the restaurant of choice. Mainly because they didn't have a wait. On a Saturday night. Against my wife's insistence on demanding the group choose another restaurant I thought we'd give the Block another chance. We got fooled again.

Pros: The waiter seemed an affable fellow. And that's all I have to say about that.

Now on to the meal. In one word: AWFUL! I ordered the "grass fed butchers cut" steak, medium rare. The steak was actually cooked to perfection but it was a tough, tasteless, laughably bad hunk of beef devoid of any flavor. I should've known better because the steak came with a side of "The Block's special steak sauce", AKA ketchup. Steak sauce is good for one thing and one thing only: masking the flavor of a terrible steak. But in perfect harmony with its accompanying steak the steak sauce lacked any flavor and failed miserably in its attempt to mask the crappiness of my steak. My arm is still tired from having to saw my way through this tough leathery piece of meat. 

My wife ordered the scallops w/ pork belly. The scallops came from the sea and tasted like the sea. If you've ever gone on vacation to the ocean and inadvertently opened your mouth letting in salt water that's what the scallops tasted like. If you're like me or any other non native to salt water animal you usually puke a little in your mouth after inhaling the salt water. Other than that sensation in eating the scallops they were great. The side of pork belly was rubbery with a hard crusted plastic like exterior. It was tasteless but now I know what eating a tide pod feels like.

On to the dessert. A chocolate ganache pudding with roasted marshmallows that had to be good, right? You can't screw up chocolate, can you The Block. Well, I'm here to tell you that the Block did the unthinkable and screwed up a chocolate dessert. The "pudding" was a hard gritty piece of chocolate that we had to use all our strength to chisel away at much the same way John Rambo had to chisel away aimlessly at limestone while he was in the prison work camp at the beginning of Rambo First Blood: Part II. 

When my wife and I left and got in the car there was stunned silence. Finally she broke the silence by berating me, "you should've spoke up, you should've said something when your friends decided on that place." A nice emasculation to cap a wonderful $150 meal. 

My first piece of advice for The Block would be to close and save the good hard working citizens of St. Louis the time and money. The Rams skipping town, Ferguson/Stockley verdict riots, decaying riverfront, highest murder rate in the nation, this restaurant, we've been through enough. Failing that advice The Block should change their theme to more of a prison type atmosphere that's more indicative of the food they serve. The waiter is your warden for the night and each table should have a poster of either Rita Hayworth, Marilyn Monroe or Raquel Welch hanging over it. The you know what you have to crawl through to get to the other side is actually eating their food. After you leave the restaurant you get whisked away to a beach in Mexico where you and a smiling Morgan Freeman meet and hug, knowing you'll never have to eat at The Block again.

In the words of Pete Townsend and the voice of Roger Daltrey, "We don't get fooled again, no no!"

Boo hoo what a dissapointment. 45 min wait to get in....45 min to get our order taken 45 min to get bland, boring food. Mac and cheese looked like the kraft kind, but worse. What a waste of time and money.

It pains me to write a review update, for a restaurant I have been to before (where I had a memorable experience) and reduce the rating. 

I get to New Orleans 5 to 6 times per year, and this time I was craving the fried chicken livers with sweet hot pepper jelly....previously it was amazing...

The livers were crisply fried, looked beautiful,  and were properly cooked....HOWEVER  the sweet hot pepper jelly turned out to be watery, runny, zero flavor without heat....

Sidenote: Service was excellent.

I asked my waiter to bring another small dish of pepper jelly....he checked and told me that it was all the same. ....
Disappointing. 

With so many places to eat here, I  have to think hard about coming back

Walked in and there were two waitstaff and a bunch of dirty tables.  The waitresses didn't seem to care to see 10 tables needing to be busses.  They were busy taking orders but not in a hurry to clean any tables.  Where's the manager!?
This is a really cute restaurant. The service is great and fast. Unfortunately, the food is mediocre. The catfish and the ceiling gumbo are pretty good though.

When I heard that a takoyaki place was opening up in Philly, I was ecstatic to see what they tasted like outside of my freezer burt version I get from H Mart. But I think the word "aight" is more appropriate in this case than "A-OK". I ordered the Japanese burrito bento, and the Philly cheesesteak Maru Balls.

I'm not sure what was supposed to be in that burrito, but it tasted like a whole lot of rice with a helping of salty paste stuffed in the corner. The bento also came with your standard salad w/ ginger dressing, and a MASSIVE helping of [rather mediocre] potato salad, of all things. The dessert takoyaki was dough with chocolate sauce inside it, I might have liked it better if it had some kind of frosting or sweet dip to balance out the bitterness, but that's just personal preference. I did appreciate the 2 pieces of Hi-Chew though. :)

I was more pleased with the cheesesteak. Another dish that's "aight" on it's own, but once I drizzled some ketchup over them they were perfect. I still want to try their take on traditional takoyaki, but I'm not in any hurry to get another one of those bentos. If you need a larger meal for lunch, get a larger order of the Maru Balls.

Also, I was almost shocked when I saw what I saw upon entering, this place needs a serious dose of feng shui!!

This place is ok went here a few times while staying at the hotel.  They have a nice breakfast set up in the morning with an omelet station.  Went there later on also so drinks and app's.  The bartender(s) (all of them) are pretty slow and drinks prices are up there, but the food was pretty good, steak tips were very good, and they had football on, so I was ok.  I would mostly just go here if staying at the hotel because it is super easy and fairly good food.

Tried one dish: broccoli with zoodles ("noodles" made from zucchini) and potatoes. Salmon was added. The salmon was very good, the zoodles alright, the broccoli was yellowed tinged with age and tasteless. The home fried potatoes were mushy and also tasteless. Based on this dish I would not return to BFF, of course others may be better.

Anyone who walks into an AJ's should know they aren't walking out without taking a significant hit to the wallet, but even with that said, I think there is a bit of "buyer beware" to be mindful of here.  My wife and I stopped here to check out their specialty selections and found it to be a very mixed bag.  The sushi bar was an excellent find (love the dragon roll) but the Bistro was a total disappointment.  Their website said "five-star quality" and while of course that was a bit of an exaggeration, the selection of hot entrees we found didn't even approach two-star quality level.  

On the plus side though, the dessert and coffee bar was outstanding.  The selection is simply amazing... from chocolates, to pastries, to fruits, coffee, tea, hot cocoa, you name it.  We should have just stuck to sushi and dessert.  Those were definitely the highlights.

As a grocery store, I would only see myself shopping here to take advantage of their butcher and seafood selections.  There were some cuts and items here that you simply can't find at your typical grocery store.  But outside of that, you're just paying for marked up Cheerios.  

So if you stick to those specialties AJ's does well, you will feel like you got your money's worth.

I always find it odd when I ask a server at a somewhat nice place what a beer or wine tastes like or compares to and they don't know. I asked about one beer here, and because I knew I was going to be doing a heavy night of drinking I wanted something light, and she said "It tastes like a craft beer." Oh. Ok. Thanks for really painting a picture.

This was my first time trying crawfish, and it was very good. I liked the spices they used, and would get them again. I also ordered the Fried Green Tomatoes, but found them to be very bland. Overall, the place was just ok.

This appears to be a regular restaurant from the outside, but once inside, completely fast-food set up. Upon visiting for the first time, one doesn't know whether to be seated or wait for a host/hostess. By standing around you then realize that at the back there is an "order here" sign. OK. No one was in line, nor were we greeted, and had no idea what was on the menu, so we asked for one. We were told the menus were back along the wall - no problem if you've been in before and know where they are. But not a logical set up for new customers. The menus are difficult to locate and not easily identifiable. We ordered water to drink, and the woman who took our order did not give us cups, or indicate what was the procedure. Nor did she let us know if we should come back to the counter to pick up our food, or if it would be brought to our table. I finally went up and got cups and as I was filling them, several new customers asked me if I knew the procedure (seating, ordering) as it was also confusing to them. The pulled pork sandwich was good, but the ribs were very tough and tasted as though "liquid smoke" had been poured over them. The taste stayed all night, and it wasn't good. For some reason Edley's is charging $7 for a "basket" of fried okra, which was actually about half full (you could hold the entire amount in two hands) and way over-fried and hard. There are some good bbq places in Nashville. This one is by far, to me, the worst.

I always come here and I'm extremely disappointed with the way everything is. I been waiting for my food for about 45 minutes they said they had no chicken. They never told us they didn't have any ready. We went to ask and they told us 15 minutes it's been 45 minutes and they still haven't bought our food out. Everything seems very dirty, I will definitely notify the county so that they can come check this restaurant. I'm definetely not coming back here anymore. Horrible service.
Thin , undercooked,  soggy greasy garbage. Don't expect a refund either. They had to make it so your Shit Out of Luck.
Everyone says this is a great authentic Mexican restaurant. We had one good bartender and a dirty table. Service took forever. Food okay. Not impressed at all. Not even a crowded time. Disappointed.

Good, but room for improvement. 

We came for dinner, in contrast to the numerous brunch reviews. Note that breakfast dishes are available all day, which is a nice feature- nothing sucks quite like sleeping in and missing the cutoff for breakfast. 

The interior is nice, with the original pressed-tin ceiling from when this was a Woolworth's. There are a preponderance of hard surfaces, which contributes to the noise complaints other Yelpers have mentioned. It wasn't too bad when we went there, because the place wasn't that busy, but a full house would definitely be raucous. 

The food was quite good - it didn't knock my socks off, but it was certainly tasty. 

The weakest aspect was the service. In particular, the staging of the courses. One member of our party had an appetizer that was markedly larger than the rest, which took more time to eat. The entrées arrived while he was still finishing it, and rather than fading back to the kitchen to wait, they just piled them on the table. Making things worse, after the appetizer was finished, they never came back to remove it - it just sat there taking up space until the end of the meal. Very careless. 

I would go back to Sabrina's and recommend it to others, but if they fix some of the rough edges, I would rate them higher.

No high chair or booster seats, no changing table in bathroom, no sides besides Mac n Cheese. I'm a chips and pizza girl personally. 
We ordered a whole pie -half bacon chix ranch , half supreme. Wasn't bad. Just wasn't the piping hot gooey pizza I had been craving. Spent $31 on the pizza an order of Mac n cheese, 2 fountain drinks and a juice pouch for our toddler. That is $10 more than I wanted to spend but that's not their fault., we'll blame the fiancé.

just had my first visit to this range. the visit cost about $40(this included, $15 for the range rental, ear protection, 2 targets and a box of 9mm ammo. i brought my own gun).
the staff was very helpful -- both down in the shop and up in the range.
the range was hot and humid -- though it was the hottest week of the summer. but it was very uncomfortable. 
the target delivery system was fantastic, i loved being able to adjust the distance by exact feet.

not much else to it. it was a good experience, but not great. i'm going to check out some other ranges before i head back.

Pretty good, ate here twice while in INDY for the race.  The omelet with cheese was little lane, two pieces of cheese slices tossed on top, is that crap even cheese?  Other then that, food was good. Place was clean, wait staff friendly and on top of service.  Try using shredded cheese and mix it into the omelet

The first part of the process was great. The team that came to my place when it flooded was impeccable. They were quick and got that process completed as fast as possible. They did a great job with fans, dehumidifiers, keeping communication with the process. The latter half, I wish I could say the same. A few day paint job took a few weeks. Painters made it known they were getting paid by the hour, so they didn't seem like they were in a hurry to get the job done. Paint job was fine. I just wish it didn't take weeks to do.

Wings here are delicious! The service may be a bit slow, but I say it is worth the wait. Just be prepared. Don't mind the flies...they may bother you but they won't eat much...there is a bright green neon paper plastered on the front door stating that this business is currently operating without a permit...

As a small business owner myself, I'm usually not one to post a review after just one visit, everybody should get the benefit of the doubt. But my dinner at this place was shockingly awful. The wonton soup tasted like hot water and grass - absolutely no flavor and not even a shaker of pepper made any difference. I don't ever remember having to chew soup in my life. The Coke was flat, so I told the server -- who then naturally brought out a flat Dr. Pepper as a replacement. The chicken fried rice looked and tasted like a big plate of hot cotton: lumpy and white and left an awful taste in my mouth and made me feel like I'd been punched in the stomach. Maybe I came on an off night, but man, this place was baaaaad.

Came here for an event and had buffet here. I came here multiple times and I feel like the food is getting worst and worst. Its neat that you can make your own shabu shabu here but the broth is not good...unless you boil it for 30 minutes with some soy sauce and meats. This place is clean and big for a buffet but it lacks variety of the food. Stay away. REALLY stay away from the soba noodles. It tastes so bad! I never had a soba noodles that blend in my life. Their sushi and rolls are okay but not the freshest. If you get the salmon right when they put it on the platter, expected to be little frozen. But in the end, you get what you pay for!

it was many many years and after learning of the new place in Berwyn from Mainline Today we went, we then learned the owners had this same location for awhile now but under two different names. Visited both and both were good but the Binni and Flynns name I hope will be their "third time is a charm" place. 
  Started wit complimetary chips and salsa ( roja ) then a couple of margiritas, both delicious. Table of four had the tacos for two with chicken and beef, a beef chimichanga and the beef tacos calliente. food was good, hot in temp., and spice, altought I believe they should spice dishes a little different so they can be different in flavor profiles, they were the same. Finishe with the " fried ice cream" my son lovedit.
  Service was attentive but not rushed, helpfull and not over bearing. Over all we enjoyed.
This seems like the unattractive sibling of Elaia. I tried several small plates. The flavors are good and chef is definitely experimenting but it doesn't leave its mark.

I was very excited to check out the new location when I went for a massage today.  Unfortunately, it was a huge let down - it's basically same as their old space, just with more wasted space.  I don't get why they made the move and didn't make any changes.

First, it is LOUD, LOUD, LOUD.  The front desk ladies do not lower their voices as in most spas, nor does anyone else (customers sitting around, make up lady, estheticians in the open areas, etc.).  Even though the waiting area is off to a side, it is in no way sheltered from the hubub of the elevators, the front desk, etc.  Really not a relaxing spa environment.  Further, all the floors are wood with nothing to absorb sound.  The constant clomping, of the staff in particular, who run around like crazy only adding to the stressful atmosphere, is very disruptive.  I've never been to a spa where the staff can wear non-soft soled shoes.  I even complained about it during my massage - my masseuse explained that there is an office just next to my massage room and apologized for the disturbance.  

The only somewhat quiet space I found was tucked into the new dry sauna, which was relatively nice.  The downside is that the way the bathroom is set up, every time someone comes into the bathroom, the sauna door clacks on its hinges.  And again, the staff clomps around and doesn't seem to make any effort to make their tidying up and cleaning quiet.  I was also looking forward to the steam shower, but I couldn't figure out how to work it.  I asked one of the women bustling in and out of the bathroom and she was also perplexed and not too excited to be helping, but got it to work for a few minutes.  The water was so hot I couldn't really enjoy the shower, and I couldn't figure how to cool it off, so I just got out after a few minutes.  Granted, these are new features - I can understand some learning time, but you would think all the staff would have been trained on this and would be able to clearly explain to me how to use and adjust the equipment.  

Then, decor.  I'm guessing they were going for "glam." It feels a bit like Sephora with a lot of black and gloss.  There's even an awful print of "Chanel" over the couch in the waiting area with lots of coffee table books about fashion in Paris.  There is also a violent looking sculpture on another wall of dead twigs.  I can understand this look over in the make up area, but it's just not relaxing for the spa waiting area.  

Lastly, my masseuse, Heidi - she gets FIVE STARS.  This was my 4th visit with her and I have been consistently amazed by her talent.  She's a fantastic listener and has a real skill for healing with her hands.  She is the only reason I go here.

I mentioned how loud it was at check out and they didn't really seem to care.  Maybe there's nothing they can do, but someone needs to hear this (hint - managers and owners).  Overall, if you want to shop for pricey make up and skin care, you might like Rescue Spa.  If you want a full, relaxing spa experience, go somewhere else.

I hate to give such a low rating for a place that has great review, but I want the readers to get a good idea of what they are walking into. This place is not family oriented, needs lot of organization, decoration and presentation is a mess. They barely have 10 tables and how they manage their kitchen supply is questionable. 

We live in the northeast and decided to check this place out. So the first time we visited them, the place was packed and we had to go back. The second time I called in for reservation to which they said I do not need one that day. We have heard great reviews about their duck dishes and so we had decided to order Duck fries and Quack and cheese. As soon as we sat down the waitress informed us that they are out of several dishes especially all the ones that contain duck (which is why we came there in the first place). 

I have never seen a restaurant with such high rating running out of key supplies (especially their main dish) - Just bad management. They had every opportunity to inform me regarding the mess they were in that day when I called earlier. Both the times I visited them, I was taking my wife out. Both the times I arranged babysitters to make it happen. Really mad that I could not have the time with my wife like I desired to have.

Running a restaurant is not all about having one great chef; the presentation matters, the surrounding matters, customer service matters. They need to operate as a well oiled machine to deserve higher rating. 

We settled for their signature burgers which was good.... and so the one star.

This place is good for take outs and not for families. It is not family friendly.

I will never associate myself with this company again. I experienced blatant homophobia from this company when trying to book a venue . I was shocked, offended, and saddened. I had very high expectations. I will look else where, I suggest you do as well!

Currently, Banks St. Bar "and Grill" is my neighborhood bar located very close to my house.  Every time I go in I'm reminded of a quote from The Simpsons:

Moe: Oh, everybody is going to family restaurants these days, tsk.  Seems nobody wants to hang out in a dank pit no more.
Carl: You ain't thinking of getting rid of the dank, are you, Moe?
Moe: Ehh, maybe I am.
Carl: Oh, but Moe: the dank.  The dank!

Dank comes to this bar to find itself.

Also, where the "grill" part of this name comes from is a total mystery to me.  There's a fantastic pizza joint attached called Lazarro's (see my review), but pizza is a far cry from grill.  Sometimes, from my houes (yes, that close), I've seen them grilling outside for Saints games or something, but I think the name is a little misleading.

The TVs are kinda old, so watching games here isn't the best.  I did watch the final world cup match and it was okay, but only because I didn't really care.  I've never watched a Saints game there.  Finn's is a better bar for that, IMO.

However, the beers are cheap and you can't beat the dank with a stick.

the atmosphere, menu and food were, in general, very good.  the mushroom soup was very good, the ahi tuna was different and could barely detect the tuna taste. we both enjoyed our entrees.  our service was very spotty.  waited a long time to order.  the side of vegetables did not come out until we were nearly finished eating our meal.  we got the dessert medley, fair at best.
I would return, my wife would not.

I like the ice cream here bc it's really creative and pretty good but what really put me off was the smell. It might just be me but I don't know It smells kind of dingy to me. But it looks very clean so I was confused. Other than that this place is cute and I like the flavor options they have.

First visit on a not-so-busy Saturday night.   Three stars, according to Yelp, mean 'A-OK'.  Maybe B minus-OK in my estimation.
Locals will remember past culinary iterations in this space that were just meh.
For starters, the space is handsome but with some kitschy touches that will wear thin over time.  Whetstone has the look of a first post-grad project conceived by a marketing/restaurant design major.
The bar is smaller now when compared to the prior tenant's design, and this is because of the addition of a crudo/cheese station.  This concept is a culinary peeve for me, since ANYONE can pull together an excellent crudo/cheese plate at home for a fraction of the cost.  You can, Blanche.  You can.  

Moving along.

A particular aspect about this place that the current and previous owners keep failing to address is the lack of noise-abating construction materials.  At points, it was too loud with the place only half full.  A particularly chatty threesome ten feet away really drove that point home. 

This city is full of trendy places like this, and Whetstone, like all the rest, unfortunately fails to take the extra steps to create a marginally more quiet dining experience.  Skip this place on weekends if you hate the din as much as I do.

Onward.

My partner had the Caesar Salad, and I had the Whetstone salad, both of which were plentiful and quite good.  Only complaint is that the croutons in both were well past their prime and inedible.  Zero crunch.  Tough as licorice.
My partner had the seared Icelandic cod with clams (6 oz if that matters to you...), and he said it was a four-star dish.
On to my burger, listed as 'Dad's Burger' on the menu.  It was offered as a single patty (about 4-5 oz according to our server), or double patty (about $3 more) of La Frieda beef, which is a well-regarded meat purveyor.
Ok.  I get it.   Americans frequently ruin hamburgers by mashing the heck out of them with a spatula on a barbecue.  And yes, fattier is tastier, and cooking over a barbecue grill unfortunately lets all that unctuous goodness drip through the grill into oblivion.
However, the Dad's Burger, in spite of its carnal pedigree, was just plain drippy bovine bloviation that overwhelmed the meager bun and made me pine for the much-lauded burger at Rouge on Rittenhouse.  
Whatever toppings were on the Dad's Burger just got lost in the muck.  We all know that a proper burger is a thick, gently packed thing of beauty with some juice in evidence.  Not here, however.
I opted for the fries (half salad is also offered), which were just too friggin' salty.  Oversalting is a given here, as much as anywhere else, so no surprise.
I may give Whetstone another try, but there is nothing new going on here.

Disappointed for several reasons.  A Sunday night with a reservation, we were seated and our waitress seemed very busy ,popping in and out several times before i had a chance to ask what the soup of the day was.  She brought us bread and we had to go find our own silverware.   The soups were good, artichoke dip was fine, but half of  my tilapia was not cooked thru.
