It was not flaking and just a thick raw chunk.  I could eat half and did not want a new meal, but I thought the chef should know.  My son thought his strip steak was a little charred on the outside and pretty rare inside- almost too thick to cook it well.  His mashed potatoes you could cut with a knife. Very hard, not creamy at all. We accepted a dessert as their apology for the tilapia.
I probably don't need to go back... The bar and outdoor seating looked nice if just going for drinks.

I don't think I got a very fair representation of Sparkles. We came around 2 o'clock and there were only two flavors of cupcakes left. There was a plain chocolate and a plain vanilla. They were not the best cupcakes I have ever had. They were a little dry, but still far better than Gigi's. I cannot stand an over abundance of frosting, and Sparkles cupcakes have just the right amount.

The customer service was great and definately gets 5 stars. We will have to try back another day when the cupcakes are fresh, and fully stocked :)

I ate here based on the recommendation of someone who lives here. I had a paradise Burger. It came highly recommended but I found it quite boring. There was a little too much char on the burger. The bun was a little small and soggy. The pickles were outstanding. I could eat those all day long. 

The shoestring fries were ok, not spectacular. The ranch was watery. 

Since I don't live here, I likely won't return. It was just ok overall.

i heard they clean guns....DONT DO IT...I had the PAGC clean my gun 9mm and went to another gun range to shoot, and what happen?!!! my gun jammed, several times..ok i went back and had them look at it. ok im not thinking, that machine u put my gun in is doing something to it..ok went again to another range guess what it jammed again, went back and was told the gun (that they sold me and said was best for me) was a piece of junk..what one guy said was for me another said was a piece of junk..this place is the worse..dont waste ur money..

I ordered takeout despite knowing from reviews it would take a while.

I had to call three times for someone to pick up the phone. Once they did, they were nice, took my order, and told me my food would be ready in 20 minutes.

45 minutes later, my husband came home with what he was told was our order- my husband confirmed what we had ordered too! We ordered two soups and ended up with one completely different one and a shrimp rice platter. We still don't understand how.

Despite the food roulette that we played, we ate what we were given bc I'm pregnant and we have a toddler to also feed. The food we received was tasty- no complaints despite the wait and mixup- so we are hopeful that once this place figures out a system, it will be a staple in our foodventures in Fairmount.

For now we have learned our lesson and will try again perhaps in two months. We can drive to south philly, eat, and come back in the time that we waited for our wrong order today.

The Boyfriend and I went to Dale's one night when we were in search of a place with a salad bar. We were sold on this place once we saw that you could get a free appetizer just for checking-in. The menu has a lot on it. So much that I almost wasn't sure what to get but I ended up settling on the meatloaf with corn and mac and cheese. The boyfriend got the turkey manhatten. We also both got the salad bar because well... that is what we came for.

Now, don't get me wrong I liked my entree and most of the other food. However, the salad bar left a lot to be desired. Also, the price for the salad bar vs. what is available is not worth it. It was $4 to add on the salad bar and definitely not worth the price. If you get the salad bar by itself it is almost $9, again not worth the price. That being said, I wouldn't come back for the salad bar but...I might come back for breakfast or something. The decor was kitschy and not my style but the food was decent and for the price the portions were huge.
The service is so bad that it's an embarrassment. I called three times today to find out if a certain battery is in stock. I was put on hold three times for a half an hour each. Horrible.

So I think Brio has the potential to be good, but i just happened to choose the wrong dish. I went not on my own accord but with my mentor who suggested we go there.  I read the description gargandelli carbonara pasta with wood fired chicken, bacon, and asparagus and I was like I know I'm going to LOVE this! The sauce had a weird taste so I couldn't really appreciate all the other delish items like the bacon and chicken. I would go back and try another dish but only if I was forced to, I'm not itching to go back anytime soon.

I do not now but the communication issue is with this Chick-fil-A as compared to the rest that I've been to. Ordered a $40 meal for the family via the Chick-fil-A app. When I arrive to pick it up I informed the first employee that I need to add an additional shake. I've been informed the second employee that I needed to purchase an additional shake. And then I am formed the third employee that I needed to buy an additional shake. They were so confused about the simple request that I ended up driving away.

The food is average. I have had better food in NOLA. It has turned into a tourist spot and is just not as good anymore. I like the fact that they are open late on the weekend so it's a option to eat when other places are closed. It just would be my first choice. The food is cold and the service is subpar.

There was no steak or lobster on the menu when we went, on a Sunday night. 

The fried pickles were amazing and so were the greens; however that's where the goodness ended. The meatloaf was disappointing and even nasty. The mac-n-cheese was dry, old, and flavorless. 

I'm giving 3 stars because my companions got the fried combo platter and said it was great!

I really want to give Edley's five stars, because I love the bar-b-que.  However, the set up and service really hold this place back.

I have been to Edley's several times.  My husband and I love to take our baby, since it is family friendly.  I love, love, love the salad.  It is huge! Although I'm not a huge fan of fried food, the fried pickles are the best I have ever had!  They are a must!  

The set up still needs work.  I am not sure who came up with the idea to order food and drinks in separate places, but it does not work for Edley's!    If the bar is full, it will take you longer to get a drink than it does to get your food.  I have also had bad experiences with the service every time I have been.  I think socializing with each other is more important to the workers than customer service is.  

Nonetheless...I still go back.  Edley's is close to my house, has good food, and is family friendly.

I've never even had the food truck, but I can tell they made absolutely no changes. The food isn't even decent for being a brick and mortar "restaurant." It tastes exactly how I imagine the food truck food to taste: cheap, fattening, with hardly any effort put in to make it, but hey, if that's your thing, then to each his own.

The inside is so tiny. I can only imagine what this place looks like during the lunch rush.

I can only see Mc Mart's value as a food truck for people who work nearby to get a quick lunch at, not as a legitimate counter-serve restaurant (so if you have more than a half hour, you're better off going somewhere else).

I had a horrible experience at Rescue. I decided to splurge on an expensive mani/pedi to see what it was like. I arrived early, as instructed, paid and sat down to wait. After 20 minutes, I was told that "the girls had been very busy."  I asked for a refund because given the expense for this service, continuing to  wait for  them was ridiculous. I will never return.

Updated: 6/12/15
I feel like the guys here value their job, they mentioned they are overwhelmed with recalls, service calls, phone calls, etc. but sometimes customer service is really important, it's what keeps people coming back. I read over a lot of the reviews people have left and it seems that if you bought your car here, it's great reviews, collision receives mediocre reviews and the service department ehhh. Maybe those who have great service don't leave reviews and maybe they need encouraged to do so. I don't know the thousands of peoples overall opinion of Don Brown Chevy but from yelp it's just an average A-Ok. 

Over the several weeks from the very start of getting the bumper fixed to picking up the car today, I heard from many employees that the collision and service is two different entity's and one doesn't have much to do with the other... I'd think it would be nice if both departments communicated. You have one name to your business, Don Brown, but communication wise it's two different parts of town and two different businesses. My review starts off pretty bad but today, they did ok. I don't think one person here particularly is bad, I just think the location needs better communication which is why I rerated it at a 2 being Meh. I've experienced better. 

6.4.15 Can we get a 0 star?  I took my car to the collision center to have the bumper repaired. I got the estimate 2 months ago and they honored that but when I got my estimate they stated it would be a 1 week (5 businesses days) turnaround. I called on the 7th business day and they said it would be ready the next day. I had also requested when I dropped it off for an oil change be done and look at a few other issues I was having. So when I called Wednesday (8th Business Day) they said there was no guarantee that the oil change would be done that day and that they were very swamped. I had a hard time even getting to talk to someone after I'd get transferred, then I'd eventually get transferred back to the person who originally transferred me, beyond aggravating. It was AWEFUL!  I've never seen a business that wouldn't be able to get that done when the car has been there for 8 days. I had a rental car that was being paid for by insurance so it's not like I could just leave it there any longer although by the time they closed on Wednesday I had to wait until Thursday to pick up my car. I wish I would have inspected it before I left. A part they had to totally replaced is chipped and the bumper has gaps on the sides where it wasn't fit properly on the car. I called and left a message for customer service to return my call and still 3 weeks later have yet to get a call. I am to a point where I don't even want to step foot in that place ever again. It took me 40 minutes when I picked up my car on Thursday for them to bring it from service where it was waiting for an oil change back to the collision center. If you've ever been there you'd know it's not across town. Totally ridiculous in my opinion. I wish your customer service and communication was better but you guys are lacking!

Update Monday 6.8.15 
Thursday 6.4.15 I received a call from the customer service department stating that my call must have gotten lost in a black hole after I posted the previous review. (I'm sorry but someone had to have gotten it.... Technology can suck sometimes but it's also easier to not deal with a complaint.) I told the Customer Service Rep, about what happened. She offered a free oil change since I still needed one - I get those with my car's warranty, then offered a $20 gas card, said that's fine but then after her telling me that they want to make things right and not lose a customer, I agreed to bring my car in for the servicing. She then offered a free rental. She stated that someone from service would be calling Friday 6/5/15 to go into more detail with me. I was ready to update Thursday with a more positive review but then Friday came and Friday went with no call. I understand they can be busy, I kept hearing about how many recalls they have. But it doesn't take 5 minutes to call,  I called Monday 6.8.15 to inform the customer service person that I didn't receive a call. He called a few minutes later.  I went over everything with him that I needed serviced/looked at. 

Dropped my car off Tuesday (next day). Re-went over everything with the service department, said it would be a few days. He explained everything and went into detail if there would be any possible cost. Rental car was waiting.

6/12/15 - Friday
I received a call on 6/11/15 stating that my car was finished but they wanted to look over it. Received a call today 6/12/15 stating it was ready they gave me a heads up on if I owed anything. Went in and everything was quick and done. So far so good on everything! Thanks for making it right Service Department and Customer Service.

A very small store with a very limited inventory.  I also went in on a Saturday and there were maybe two employees, both of whom did their best to ignore me.  I will not be coming back to this store.  I drove out to the JoAnn's in Marple and not only was it way bigger and organized, but the staff were very helpful and friendly.

Dining for only one should be easier.  None of the entrees are served in smaller portions thus limiting my meal to just one item. As an out of towner doggy bagging is not an option when staying at a hotel without means to reheat the next day.  3 stars for service for not offering half portions, though the food was tasty and merited another star. Tuesday night, I was the only one in the restaurant, but 2-3 take out orders were picked up.

This place smelled like a gym and was incredibly dirty.  Layers of fuzz on the counter where you fix up your coffee.  The floors looked like they had not been washed in a year.  I wanted to bolt the second I walked in the door.  But, my husband wanted some iced tea so we waited in line for a while and I got to observe the surroundings, which only reconfirmed why I will never be back.

The pluses are the people who work there were incredibly friendly and they have extensive vegan and gluten free options if you lean that way.

I went to Gators Dec.10 to enjoy some holiday time and watch the boats in the parade and have dinner, I approached a 4 top that was empty and a man asked me if my party was all hereI responded that, my boyfriend was buying drinks at the bar and my friends were close by. He rudely informed me that I could not sit with my drinks or a menu until all 4 of us where there.
When I questioned that  he repeated it again only angrier and said "It's business" When my husband approached the table he got the same rudeness. We walked a few feet where we would wait, He came over to us,,all puffed up. We walked away and when upstairs where we could get away from him His name is Tom and he happens to be the General Manager! 
What an example for his young employees.

The Greater Reno Grub and Gripe Group gathered at Zagol for its latest Chowdown. Overall we enjoyed the food. The manner in which it is served was maybe a little off putting but we quickly overcame it.
This is a restaurant that opened a couple of years ago on East Fourth Street near downtown Reno. It's in a new building in an area targeted for redevelopment by the city of Reno.
I looked over the menu and found nothing I would object to so when it came to ordered I deferred to others. Robin pretty much handled the ordering and she did a good job. She made a point of ordering food that would be a contrast. She noted one previous dinner there where too much of the food seemed similar.
But because Robin did the ordering I can't really tell you much about what we ordered. We had the combination dish and we got two beef dishes and three vegetable dishes. Robin also got some cottage cheese that was a nice mix with the spicier dishes. 
We placed our order and the food arrived a short time later. If you're not familiar with Ethiopian food it would help to look at my pictures. All five portions of food and the cottage cheese are served on a platter on a big sheet of soft bread called injera. Additionally, there is wrapped bread served in a plate. One member of our group asked, "Are there plates?" The answer is no. You take some of the bread on the side, tear off a bit and use it to pick up meat or vegetables. As the food gets mushed together eventually it's easier to pick it up together.
I can't describe each individual dish, but all were good. And the cottage cheese is a nice addition, too. We didn't feel full after we finished the first so we ordered a lamb dish and it was also good. Also lots of good wine. Robin and Fred brought a nice 2010 Twisted Oak verdelho and a 2009 Dillian primitivo. I brought a 2010 Ametzoi Rubentis, a Basque rose that's a little more rounded than the 2009 I brought to the Chowdown at the Santa Fe.
Service was good. Our water glasses were kept full.
We didn't order anything else but the also offer Ethiopian beer and Ethiopian coffee.

First, we LOVE the wings here and visit this place pretty regularly HOWEVER, the service seems to have declined to a level that has made us seek out another wing place to give our business to.   A couple of months ago I called to place a call in order and was told to hold.  The person who answered put the phone down and I waited for 10 minutes before giving up.   Last Friday, I went in to pick my order up and the person who rang me up couldn't be bothered to interrupt his conversation with another employee so he had to run my card twice because the first time it was done incorrectly. Finally, last night  I called to place an order and AGAIN was left on hold for over 11 minutes.  I ended up ordering from a place that is 5 minutes out of my way but seemed to be worth the drive since that establishment picked up on the second ring and took my order immediately.   Again, great food but not good enough to deal with the inconsistent service.

I've been wanting to come here for a long time being a mid city resident.  HOWEVER, the trout was sub par and came with pea risotto (really??).  And apparently, the chef is a little snobby (go figure).  I am currently on blood thinners and my dish came with asparagus.  I asked if it could be substituted and was told "Chef doesn't like to do that."  Whatevs....Guess what...They didn't substitute for anything.  I will not be returning....Unless someone else pays for my dinner...Sorry.  I really was looking forward to being impressed.

Omg!! I walked in and the smell of dirty mop and wet rags was so strong I lost my appetite as soon as I walked in. It was highly disappointing because I love Pizza Hut!! With sadness and disgust I turned right around and walked out! My ride home I was nauseous!! Not cool!

Black Rock Pizza is a difficult pizzeria to rate. 

The best thing about Black Rock Pizza is their product. Their pizza is really very good, not the best I've ever had but very close. Their service is fine, although to be fair they've never been terribly busy on my visits. The atmosphere reminds me of Shakey's Pizza, and might even be a step down from lowly Round Table Pizza. But the worst part about Black Rock Pizza is their prices.

Black Rock Pizza offers two sizes: personal and large. If you've had a large pizza anywhere else, you'll be sorely disappointed by its diminutive size.  For roughly 2/3 the size of competing 'large' pizzas, you can expect to pay $19-$22.

Their pizza selection is probably the best you'll find in Reno. They offer three different styles: tomato sauce, olive oil, or bbq. All crusts are (very) thin, so expect to eat at least one more slice than normal.

Overall, I rate Black Rock Pizza with three stars. Their pizza rates a 4.5, but everything else about them is a solid 2.5 at best. I might go again, but only if someone else is buying.
Nothing exciting here. The food was good and quick. The location is nice and we didn't wait at all for a table on a Saturday night.

No way am I ever coming back here. I will purposely walk out of my way to avoid the area when i'm at this particular mall, even upstairs to avoid their very presence. I asked for a small bag of tea, and ended up being charged $150 dollars for the largest bag they carry. It all happened so fast, and now I'm out a full day's paycheck for something I cannot return after the three minute bait and switch routine I was just given. If you like your space, and hate to be oversold- avoid these guys.

I found the entire experience so unpleasant that  even though i now have a $150 credit to the store (because of course, once you cross the store's threshold- all tea is un-returnable, even in my case of the about-face return i did just moments after purchasing my overpriced dried goods) i will not be back. Hopefully, I can pass it off to another tea enthusiast, hopefully far from the pushy and borderline assaulting employees of La Encantada.

The cold brew coffee is either not cold brewed, a terrible brand of coffee, or it was brewed days ago. It is so incredibly stale. I am pretty upset I paid $3 for this. 

Everything else though, is delicious.

$6.70 for a 24 oz blended snickers. This is $2 more than any other coffee shop I have gone to in the entire valley for the exact same drink. Never in my life did I expect to pay almost $7 for a coffee. 

The staff is very sweet but doesn't seem to know what they are doing. I haven't been out of the drive through yet any sooner than 10 min for one drink. I have also seen other people behind me back out of the drive through and leave because they were frustrated on multiple occasions. Hopefully with time they will get a lot quicker and more efficient. I don't see this new location lasting otherwise.

The room was clean and the housekeeping responsive. Valet service was terrible.  It was obvious the gentlemen had not received proper training.  Wi-Fi in room was very spotty and intermittent. A/C was ok.  Ice machine inoperable on our floor.  Ice machine missing on floor below us.  Lobby was trendy and front desk personnel pleasant.   Location of hotel allows you to be close enough to the French Quarter without having to endure the stench and filth everyday.

Stopped in on a drive buy.  Just happened to read your reviews while I waited 10 minutes for a waitress to show up at my table.  I honestly didn't think it could be as bad as the reviewers were saying.  I WAS AMAZED HOW SPOT ON THEY WERE.   The waitress wax clueless,  this food was cold and tasteless.   This stop was a waste of time and money.  If you are driving past ......... Keep on trucking!!!!!!!

What a Stinky restaurant.  The restroom is right smack in the middle restaurant when the door open, you can see the toilet and  the smell was disgusting.  I had been to several third world countries and I had never seen a set up like this.  Talking about losing appetite.  Hope I'm not the only one that don't like to eat close to where I take a dump.

Id like to actually rate how the music or drinks were here since it came highly reccommended, but I cant. The barely old enough to drink himself bartender decided to not take my out of state Id for some reason (im 32), so we never got in the door. Maybe it was because I was with two people of color? Avoid this place.

Let me start by saying that they weren't having a good day b/c they were having computer problem, but with that being said... Overall service was average. They were very welcoming despite their circumstances, but that quickly changed when they lost our food ticket. We had already been sitting their for at least 45 minutes. They stated that they would give us some cake for waiting, but that NEVER happen. People that came in well after us, who were taking out, were offered sample sides. The food was nothing to praise. The fried wings take 20 minutes to cook, but they weren't even fried hard when I got them. Their Mac & Cheese was ok, but wasn't really soul food style. The cabbage tasted like vinegar and over cooked to me. My sister had a hamburger that didn't even look good. Honestly the best part was the center cut ribs. The meat came right off the bone, very tender. The BBQ sauce was also GREAT! I would only recommend this spot for the center cut ribs. If I was back in town, I wouldn't revisit this spot.
I ordered delivery from mikes and the minimum order was $20 plus a $2 delivery fee. The food took forever and it was cold. Never ordering again.

We hit this place around 3:30 for a late lunch. Unassuming is exactly the way to describe this place. Service was friendly and prompt. Food was above average, but not really stellar. I had tri-tip sandwich and my wife had the Paradise burger. We shared an order of fries - again, decent, but nothing to write home about. All-in-all, reasonably priced appeal ling place to eat.

I was not impressed with Smashburger. I figured I'd give it a try since it's so close to my apartment, but I regret not going the extra distance to a better burger place. 

I got the "NOLA" burger, which comes topped with a fried green tomato and creole mustard. It was nowhere near as good as it sounded.

 It's as though they literally smashed the burger. It was flat like a fast food cheeseburger, the bun was so soggy, the lettuce was completely wilted and starting to turn brown. I figured I'd might as well try to eat it (broke and hungry sacrifice) and when I took my first bite, it was full of paper. A receipt to be exact, for another order, in my burger. The bite of meat I did get was extremely dry. I'm not much of a fast food person, but would have preferred a McD's dollar menu burger over the one I was given.

But, on a positive note, the Smash Fries were pretty tasty. A little too greasy, but still well seasoned.
Go somewhere else, unless you're really hungry. Sorry to say. Cool costumes, but ehhhh. Coming from a Cajun born Louisianian...

Being a huge Burger fan I rushed at the opportunity to try this new spot on one of my favorite streets out. One thing that struck me as odd is that they have different burger choices, but the menu doesn't do a good job or a job at all of distinguishing the differences, at least not when you're standing in line looking at the menu board. What I mean is when I went I had never been to a Smashburger so I didn't know the difference between a NOLA burger a classic and an All American.

I was tempted to ask the staff but considering how long the line and waits were the staff behind the counter was a bit rushed and I overheard someone in front of me ask the same question and get a rushed unclear answer.

I'm willing to forgive that however. I was overall pleased with the burger......I got the NOLA burger. It was good, not Earth shattering and IMO not better some other burger places I've had (5 Guys, or even Good Stuff Eatery in DC). 

All in all a good burger place, but nothing extraordinary at least in my humble opinion.
The sandwich was okay, despite the fact I forgot to eat it on the plane (in my defense, I was intently watching "X-Men: First Class").  So well done, and whatnot.

Jones was very nice for dinner. That's about all I can say, though. It had good food with good cocktails and the ambiance was fantastic. I'm a big fan of the ski-lodge reminiscent decor. 

However, the food was nothing special, though definitely on the pricey side of things. I feel like Jones does comfort food extremely well, but there are cheaper places to eat with similar dishes of similar quality. You are definitely paying a premium for decor and location. That fact is fine with me, but it means that I'm more likely to go somewhere with more interesting food if I want to spend $35 on dinner.
Cheesesteaks are the best in town, if you can get there when they are open. They are lazy with their hours and close randomly as they wish. Not very customer friendly.

AC wasn't working, turns out my fuse box was turned off. They charged me $174 to turn it back on. Shame on me for not checking, but I would have been a good customer if they bothered to return my phone calls asking why they charged so much.

This place is disgusting!  I walked in to a bad smell to the room. Then my sister found holes all over the blankets and pillows. The wifi is crappy and doesn't even work. Don't come here!! Worst hotel I have been to!! The room feels dirty and I felt the need to wash my hand every time I touched something.

Terrible. Truly terrible. I won't be getting pizza from this place again. 
I am vegan and I eat a lot of pizza. Cheese-less pizza is NOT hard to get right but somehow Black Rock pizza missed the mark entirely. 
I ordered the Tomato Sunrise (which is really a Margherita pizza) with garlic/olive oil sauce because the Spicy Sicilian red sauce contains Parmesan cheese. I got it on the Multi-grain crust. 
This was the ugliest pizza I've ever seen. A pale crust with about 12 pieces of chopped tomato and 3 or 4 strands of basil. The crust tasted like it was from frozen, no flavor, gooey AND dry at the same time. Don't know how they pulled that off. 
I took a photo of it and really wish I could upload it here. Words don't do justice the the ugliness of this thing that I can't even bring myself to call pizza. 
Reno vegans, do yourself a favor and stick with Pie-Face or Brew Brother's at the Eldorado. Both of these places do vegan pizza right.

Strange hotel. The breakfast area is very small and clearly not enough for how many guests stay. The breakfast area runs out of food every 2 minutes. They insist on only putting out 6 sausages at a time and then 3 people have to ask to refill it before someone does anything. Just very odd. The rooms are okay but our shower floor was nearly unusable. It needs to be refinished and repaired. Housekeeping knocks on your door even if you have a sign up(happened twice). The dryers are hit or miss with actually drying clothes. Our room had one coffee cup and a stack of plates. Just a lot of weird things that add up to a sub par stay.

First of all, this fingerprint service is actually embedded within an insurance agency.  I understand that the fingerprinting business is a side gig, and that it has unexpectedly caused this agency frustration and been detrimental to their ability to conduct business.  However, this is a terrible place to get fingerprinting done.  The employees are rude and treat us as a nuisance, even commenting that our fingerprinting is interfering with their insurance business.  All of us waiting were professionals in the field of psychology, education, and even coaching, and did not expect to be treated so rudely.  In fact, the Identogo website lists this location as a service provider but does not give any indication that it is located inside of another business.  The office environment itself was dark and dirty, and the employees rude and discourteous to each other and the fingerprinting customers.  They do not greet you, and have posted the sign below indicating that walk-ins will only be serviced in the afternoon.  This differs from the Identogo website information, which clearly lists 9:00 am - 11:00 am as walk in hours.  Bottom line - this agency took a contract to provide fingerprint services, now is wishing they did not have this hassle, and will take it out on you if you go there.

There just isn't much that I can say on a positive note about the food. I am a burger LOVER and was so thrilled when this place opened down the street from school.  I popped in on my lunch break (1 1/2 hours) and decided to dine-in.  I was impressed with the selection of hand crafted soda and asked the waitress if any of the other flavors came in diet. Her response: Oh, yes. All of our sodas are caffeine free! Uhm. OK.  I ordered the Trainwreck and believe me, it's a fitting name for it.  Worst. Burger. Ever.  It was all one note. There was cheese, fries, meat, some wacky sauce, and who knows what else, but I couldn't taste any of it.  It was just bland and boring.  Most importantly, they were slow.  i sat in the dining room for a good 30 minutes and counted the number of people that I saw with food.  Two tables, 3 people.  That's it. I waited so long that  I asked for my food to go.  I barely made it back in time for class.  Go to Red Robin, go to 5 Guys. Heck, go to McDonald's...just don't eat here!

We have been customers for many years.  We also go to the Anthony's in Florida.  Unfortunately our last few experiences have not been great.  Our pizza wasn't well done - it was totally burnt. (We did take out).  For almost $30 for a pizza - we expect that they are not frugal with toppings - it was not the quality we are used to.  I don't know if management has changed but even the guy who checked us out forgot to give us the hot pepper he said he was putting in our bag.

Fucking piss poor service  mediocre food  This burger king need not too be in service any longer. Don't  even bother on wasting your time  eating here nor stopping at this establishment unless you have to use the rest room.  I rather eat gas station food, fast food Isn't not rocket science  HOW DO YOU SCREW UP  MEAT,  BREAD ,CHEESE AND TOPPINGS      just PATHETIC

The worst hair salon in the world. It's nasty. The boy who did my hair don't follow my instructions and he cut my hair super short. They aren't professional at all. The prices are different from a customer to another even for the same presentation. Don't waist your money. I'm really disappointed

The service is exactly what I expect from a fast food joint -- slow and lousy.  

Food is served lukewarm.  Double check anything you get from the drive thru.  Don't expect fries to be cooked thoroughly.  And don't dare ask what kind of salad dressing they have!

WARNING: 15% Gratuity Will Be Added For Parties of 3  

Unconscionable adj. referring to a contract or bargain which is so unfair to a party that no reasonable or informed person would agree to it. It is similar to an "adhesion contract," in which one party has taken advantage of a person dealing from weakness.

We ate here Memorial weekend. When our bill came we discovered a 15% gratuity was added. I'm accustomed to paying gratuity for parties of usually 6 or more, but 3, three, thres, trois?!?! 

Service was below average.  I wasn't even escorted to the restroom. (No joke, there are restaurants in NOLA that will escort you to the restroom (ie. Commander's Palace & Mr. B's)). 

Cafe Pontalba does not get 2 stars because of the 15% gratuity.

It gets 2 stars because "Meh. I've experienced better."

Drinks are pricey. 
Music is extra loud but played good songs.
No cover.
Cute bartenders.
Fast service. 
Strong drinks.

This is the most money hungry place!!!! when you order wings 20 plus I think you should be able to get half plain and half with sauce if f you like... they will charge you a extra $2 for a container.... the food is here OK nothing special or extra. I've ordered here 2 different times and had bad experiences (1st time order was completely wrong Not to mention they put mustard on the food and I'm severely allergic) The only good thing on the menu is the salad with the garlic bread.

The experience is what you are paying for and not the food. I believe I read somewhere that the tour is 3 hours long with a lunch. Sadly, our tour was only 2 hours long and we left 30 mins late so it was more like a hour and a half. The food was what I expected. Fried "probably frozen" catfish fillets, dry corn bread, cole slaw, okra and bread pudding. Nothing spectacular but it got the job done. Always wanted to be on a river boat so it was a fun one time experience.

Gators is a staple in our band viewing and listening options. We recently visited and saw a new band called Flyte Band Tampa. They definitely made the night great. They played a fun mix of songs that you could dance to and did it well. We'd definitely come back to see them again. It's too bad that the food seems to be only fair. It used to be better but has been slipping on taste and quality as of late. I'm not saying it's bad but rather it has slipped to being simply "bar fare", perhaps it can rise again. We shall see.

My order never showed up. Got a conformation email from fedex though but no package. Emailed rebel several times but got no response. Mistakes happen but the least you could do is contact a customer back and tell them u cant help. 

I should have been supporting my local homebrew store instead of ordering online anyway. 

Northern brewer might be a better option for anyone without a local homebrew store.

If I could give this less than 1 star I would. The service here is unacceptable and they shouldn't be allowed to operate. We bussed our own table. Took us over an hour to get our food. Our waitress was so rude. No one came to check on us. I will not be back

I ordered a onsie cake for a baby shower and it didnt look anything like the picture I showed them. It didnt look like a perfessional cake it looked like i made it at home. The taste was just ok. I would never go here again and pay $55.00 for a cake I could have made myself.

Came in and the guy just looked at me like I was lost. wasn't phased so I asked him if I could sit anywhere. Took a seat and my partner and I got a menu. So far okay :) 

Asked for the drinks he had and he pointed at the fridge. Umm okay... So I got up and looked at the fridge and picked out a drink. He looked as confused as I did. Well anyway we got our order and the food was okay. I've been to a lot of Pho restaurants in town and I can safely label it a 3 star place to eat. 

Today I had the flame broiler pork; however it tasted like it had been sitting out for a while (since it was a little cold). The rice was on point today woo! Love good rice, there fish sauce is different, liked that. Hmm what else... Mmm that's about it. It was an okay experience the server/host is not the most experienced but he gets the job done. 4 stars if I would have had a hot meal, 5 if the service was awesome.

I had the opportunity to try Ka'Chi at the South Street Night Market on 8/15. It was on my "let's try list"  for the evening based on the brief description on the Night Market website and I happened to stumble on it at the right time. Well not the right time, because I waited for over an hour. But I was hungry and the menu was intriguing. 

Since I knew I didn't have the time to hit up another truck I decided to try two of the menu items and went for the Bulgogi taco and the fried kimchi rice balls. I added the pineapple habanero sauce and Gochujang to both. Let's start with the taco. It came with the beef and the red cabbage slaw. Even with the two sauces, for whatever reason the flavors just didn't mesh to me. Towards the end, a piece of the bulgogi did fall out and I sampled it by itself. It was a tasty little piece of meat. I think I would have been happy with a chunk of that and some of the pineapple habanero sauce. As forewarned, both sauces did have a nice little kick to them. The rice balls were just okay. I was expecting them have a little more flavor on their own but they were fairly bland. I could kind of taste the cheese but I was expecting a little more of a meltiness to the dish that just wasn't there. I'm not sure what kind of cheese it was...maybe it wasn't supposed to get melty? I added a little extra of the pineapple sauce and I enjoyed it a little more. The kimchi rice balls came with two balls and the the taco was one per order. For the $8 I paid, I think the proportions were appropriate. 

I'm glad I waited for it however it wasn't necessarily worth the wait. I would eat there again sans the long line however I don't love it enough to dedicate another hour or so of my life.
Dumplings are pricey for their size and quality.  You can go to the large Asian grocery store next door and get frozen dumplings that are comparable.

Don't do it!  Don't spend a dime in this place.  Even though they're in the perfect location, right on a (relatively) quiet corner of Jackson Square, they seem to have everything going for them.  Yet, the service is weak, the food is weaker still, and the prices are high for what you get.  The location and the ambiance will get you to eat here once because it just looks so right; but I guarantee you that you won't go back.  This place is the quintessential tourist trap - why would anyone go back?

I love the food but the wait is RIDICULOUS! It's nearly impossible to get in and out on my HOUR lunch and I call ahead with my order. It happens ever time I go. What's interesting is the place is almost empty on lunch, yet service is at a CRAWL!! If the sushi wasn't so good I'd give them only 2 stars. I'll be back, just not on my lunch.

Saw an opera there last night.  The opera was excellent, but I could not enjoy it because I was shivering from the continuous draft of cold air from the non-stop air conditioning.  Room temperature in the lodge area must have been in the low 60's.  OK for hot August nights but tough to take in November!

Love Chick-fil-A ?  I do, but re-think about going to this new location? why?  poor traffic design, parking lot a joke?  Can't get out at the light between here and Starbucks? Contact the following to complain:

pcoderre@castoinfo.com

In a town filled with amazing taco joints, unfortunately this is on the more mediocre end of things. The service is quite good and the location is nice. However, the tacos just aren't as flavorful as you want. Hopefully they will succeed in this location. It might be a decent idea to go back to the recipe book...

Overall quality of food and taste were reasonably good. Conveniently located, price is acceptable.

My disappointment is unfriendliness of their service. My  visit was busy busy Saturday night, so I did not mind longer wait or table being little bit dirty. My family and I did not complain at all and rather we enjoyed the food. But person serving food including cashier were so unwelcoming. 

I would think what makes eating-out fun is whole experience from walking in, enjoying the meal, and interaction....

It was a nice stop on a beautiful day. I'm not going to post much on this place as I have plans to return next weekend. But I had the Grouper tacos, the fish was cold so I was not impressed. A couple of my friends had the cheeseburger and love it. So I'll update this again after next weekend.

All I can say is what happened? We used to go to this place when it was located on Harrison and 22nd. They moved and we were really bummed. We have been dining here since they moved and it seems to have gone down hill. We thought it was just growing pains so we have over looked some things but last night was it for us. 

We ordered two appetizers ( Sw Potato Fries and Fried Zucchini) 
Grilled Chicken platter with side salad
Steak platter for husband
Hot tea and Iced tea

Appetizers never came out
Salads were served but were the very basic for 2.99 Iceberg Lettuce, a sliver or two of onion, tomato slice and a small little thing of ranch dressing. 
barely through my small dinner salad the plates with dinner came out and were delivered by our waitress as if they were coming in for a landing as she put them on the table without waiting for anyone to move a single plate that they were eating from. 
ice tea was not refilled, my hot tea was not refilled nor was i asked if I would like more hot water. 

I didn't eat much of my dinner but out waitress would not have noticed.We saw her plenty when we were between the salad and dinner but  we did not see her after plates were served. We waited and waited for a ticket , she looked our direction and could not tell we were ready to leave i guess so I flagged another waitress who noticed the uneaten meal and asked if I wanted a box. I told her no, the chicken was dry, veggies were drowned in butter and mushy. Just a check please. I also pointed out the appetizers were not delivered. Our waitress did come by with the check at last and offered free desert and did remove charges for my meal and appetizers which was fabulous. I would say I would give it another go but previous experiences combined with other reviews and our experience last night make this a NO GO for future outings. Off we go to find another favorite.  

Kickers was it wasn't that busy!!! hmmm....

I was really excited to eat here as had heard good things about it.  I have to say I was not fully impressed.  We had a 6 p.m. reservation and the dining room was initially empty but eventually filled up. I wanted to try the pork tenderloin but apparently it wasn't "brined" enough yet so I would have had to wait until 8 o'clock to get it so I ended up getting the crab cake sandwich, which was okay, it had a lot of red pepper sauce and the bun was overly big, but I don't eat the bun anyway. My date got the Seafood Capellini and that was good.  I would have to be talked into going back again for the cost I think that being out of pork tenderloin was a bit odd. Service was good our waiter tried to be sympathetic about being out of items, which after speaking with some friends at work seems to be the norm.

Place was a bit of a dive, but the artist was good. Disappointed in the beer selection and it was all bottles. Bartender was very nice and charged my cell phone for me when she heard me say my battery was low.

If you're looking for a place for a great salad this is not the place. Like most places I've ordered from, only the fried food is good. But isn't it always... 
The lettuce is rotten. I ordered a cob salad that included brown lettuce, avocados like rubber, and raw bacon. The garlic knots were also stale. Stick to fried food if you order from here.

I had high hopes for Praline Connection, having been recommended it by a couple of local people I had met in New Orleans, unfortunately my experience failed to live up to the expectation.
Prior to arriving at the restaurant, I called ahead to confirm that they were still open and to check if they were still serving the full menu (it was memorial day and another restaurant had ran out of many dishes, so i wanted to avoid disappointment). Upon arriving, we waited 20 minutes for drinks and our orders to be taken, even though there was as many staff as there was tables. When our orders were eventually taken, I was informed that much of the menu was unavailable such as shrimp, fish and strangely, french fries..
I ordered the gumbo, which was ok and the fried stuffed crab which was nothing special. The waitress got our order wrong and brought out two of the same dish, which I refused to pay for. 
Service can be just as important as the food and ours was poor. The food wasn't great and I was restricted due to unavailability, as a result, im giving two stars.

In the past this has been a great place. However, the service this time was dreadful and the food not the same quality. Others in my party had the Paradise Burger and were satisfied with their meal. I on the other hand had the cioppino, it was by far the worst I have had anywhere. There was no broth and it seemed they reheated it in the microwave. The center was too hot to eat and the rest was luke warm at best.

This would have all been fine if not for the lack of service. Even after we let them know  one of our party was diabetic and needed to eat it still took an hour to get our food.

There for corned beef and cabbage for st. Patrick's day. Took my 94 year old mom. Meal was great, but given plastics wear- which my mother struggled with- asked if she could have regular silverware- very disappointed we were refused our request. I had to cut her meal up for her- how embarrassing for her! Shame on you Beanies- up to now you were a favorite

A star for the outdoor patio, and a star for a decent oak-grilled burger.

But then . . . inattentive service, average food at best, and draft beer served small glasses just makes me wish I hadn't tried a new restaurant.

One last comment . . . the mussel broth tasted like rinse water from a stockpot. Really Disappointing!

Life is too short to review 1 and 2 star establishments.

My daughter and I had lunch here on a Tuesday . I had been here twice before, but since those visits, the menu has been changed. It is now a very Mediterranean menu. We ordered a zucchini salad and a smoked trout sandwich. It took 20 minutes to receive our food. The trout tasted very fishy and therefore I only ate a third of the sandwich . The zucchini was overwhelmed with dill and had a slimey texture, so almost that entire dish was left uneaten. We paid the $33 dollar bill and left. My daughter wanted to get the taste out of her mouth so  we stopped at Taco Bell for lunch after lunch. I'm sad that the menu changed at olio and that the service was terribly slow with no apology . I also am sad that I did not leave when I discovered that the menu had changed .

They wanted over $1125 to replace an ignition switch.  I had the switch replaced for $245 at another local shop.  Pep boys wanted to sell me an entire steering wheel column to replace a single ignition switch rod.  

This was after 4 days and multiple calls requesting a quote.  They failed to return my calls.  Originally they wanted me to just drop the car off before giving me an estimate.  I am glad I didn't, and that I went to another shop for a second opinion.

Food is ok, not too bad, not too good.  They do offer a vegetarian plate with choice of four side dishes.  I only asked if any were made with meat.  I dared not asked if any were made with a meat based broth...I was already starving and figured I take the risk.  The white beans were delish  Cabbage was a bit greasy.  i don't know where the recipe for the mac cheese, greens and cornbread came from, but try again! My partner enjoyed the crawfish etoufee.

I'm happy Denise got the bar opened, it's a beautiful place but it has some kinks that need to be worked out if they intend to survive among the sea of other Gayborhood bars.

Bartender: my friends and I sat at the bar our entire visit. Bartender was friendly but our cosmos were watered down and not cheap either. We quickly switched to wine. No one drinks a cosmo like it's juice unless it really is well juice. We ordered food at the bar but had to ask for the necessary utensils to eat with anything other our hands. 

Venue Seating: they need heat lamps near the bar area because front doorl lets in a gust of cold air every time someone comes in and if there is a line you're freezing your butt off. We had to keep our coats on the entire time. Also there is no coat check or anywhere to hang your coats yet they have signs stating to wear, hold or sit on your coat only. Ummm what the hell. Not cool. People wear coats in the winter time. This isn't miami beach.

Food: service was extremely slow for it not to be crowded. We ordered the nachos which were perfect. The mozzarella fries were meh. Flavorless and lacking in enough mozzarella. We'll stick to the nachos. Portions are on the small side considering the prices.

Overall it was an okay experience. They have a lot of kinks to work out if they intend to survive. We'll visit for karaoke night. Hopefully things improve from there.

Great waffles but... the eggs and hashbrowns were mushy and the coffee was old. Asked for warm coffee and they had to heat it up in a coffee cup. The staff, despite not telling my coffee, was very friendly and polite. The restaurant was also very clean.

Slow, Shoddy workmanship.
I made an appointment online with TirePlus at 152 E Dekalb Pike King of Prussia Pa for 5:00 PM to have a new set of tires installed and an oil change. I arrived at 4:40 and was ignored by 2 workers behind the desk while they played a game on their phones. A third worker was helping another customer at the front desk. After 3-4 minutes I was helped. The TiresPlus closes at 7:00 PM. I expected my vehicle to be completed by 7:00. I was not told that, but I had the reasonable expectation It would be done by closing. I have bought many, many tires in my life and 2 hours is more than enough time to install 4 tires. The Installers were done at 7:53 PM. That is over 3 hours to install 4 tires and an oil change. I was given the oil change for free and a coupon for a second oil change for free but I will not be using the free oil change coupon. I cannot afford them to take so long to do common tasks. I will not be back for the free oil change or for the complimentary tire rotations. There are plenty of other tire facilities to use.
After riding 300 miles on these tires it became obvious that the wheels were balanced incorrectly. I have had the wheels rebalanced at another facility.

Food is pretty good for the price, quick, and good for lunch!!! I hate to say this, but the place smelled like fish oil or something when I last went there - it was overpowering and made me upset. Not sure what is going on, but they don't have an email address for contact on their website. Not my problem - but if it continues, will not be going back.

They served my daughter moldy bread on her Steak Stack sandwich. The mold I lie to you not was the size of a quarter and was paisley shaped. How could the kitchen staff miss that since the sandwich is grilled.   That means it was assembled, buttered and grilled and no one saw that big piece of mold! Really Zoe's kitchen.   Shame on you.   She was supposed to get fresh fruit, they served her pasta salad instead.   When I told the manager she confirmed that it was indeed mold on the bread and they the kitchen staff failed to inform her that they ran out of fruit.   Then magically fruit appears and they say they made it just for my daughter. No, you made it because it should have been available to serve for customers according to the the menu.   The manager gave us a free entree and hummus card.   She should have compensated us our lunch.   Like I would really come back!

For the 3rd time I've ordered my favorite meal from this Taco Bell, and they just can't pull of the correct ingredients with their equipment. I get a #6, which is 2 chalupa's, hard taco, and a drink. I always get chicken, no tomatoes, add nacho cheese, hard taco and MTN DEW BAJA BLAST with hot sauce. Tonight I got beef chalupas, tomatoes, onions, (don't come with it in the first place) a soft taco, an Ice tea of all things, and surprisingly hot sauce. And the fact that chicken and nacho cheese cost $2.50 more is not why I'm mad, all 3 times there was nobody in line, and not even inside. So from now on I'm going to the one and keystone and 62st street. Sure I'm going to wait in like for 20+ minuets but at least I know my order will be correct. Even when it's a cluster at 1am with all the drunks, I'd rather deal with that than a terribly inaccurate order of Castleton tbell.

This is probably one of my most fav places to go play games back in college.   Tho I'm more an adult now (lol), I still enjoy the arcade.  It has gotten pricier than I remembered.  We no longer get tickets cause everything is now put onto the players card.  The games are newer and it looks great in here. But the food is not something I would come to D&B for.  We were hungry after the game at the superdome and decided to eat here instead of at the stadium.  We ordered the bacon wrapped shrimp and steak pasta for me and ribs/chicken wing combo. Let's dive in. 
The pasta dish was super bland.  I had to add salt to it.  Normally I don't even like to add salt to anything ever.  The pasta was soft. The steak medallions were slightly over cooked and the bacon was tasty.  I love the added spinach with my pasta topped with fried spinach.  I miss the blackened chicken pasta! For dessert, we had the Bookie. 

My husband said his ribs were tough to eat and the buffalo wings were too salty.  It's as if the buffalo wings just came out of the oven.  They use bottled ranch instead of making their own.  The fries were soggy as well.  

The Bookie tower for dessert was ok.  They did break the tower down so we can share.  One brownie/cookie topped with a bluebell vanilla scoop of ice cream. You can tell that they had just microwaved a frozen Bookie.  It was hard to eat after a few mins. 

But our server was sweet and provided excellent service. She checked on us constantly as she walked by. Overall we would come back for the games but not for food.

We walked in and several tables were dirty, not to mention it felt rather humid inside. I mean I get it's a tropical place.... but I don't want to feel the humidity. Lol. I order and found that they were widely backed up with the orders. I don't know if the were understaffed or just busy? It was rough. It's an open kitchen so I could see cooks standing around ‍. The drink area and sauces bar were a mess... obviously because they were busy? After waiting 15 minutes, they started on my order . I got my order 23 minutes after ordering it. ‍. This place needs some help. A for effort?

I really don't know why I've been here more than once...probably because I am a broke college kid. Anyway, it makes me sick thinking that I've returned after the service I've gotten. The place is just a mess. Hair in food on more than one occasion, the salad bar is gross, and any food from here other than breakfast makes me wanna barf. 

I think the worse experience I've ever had here was when my friends and I came for the All-You-Can-Eat wings. The wings weren't bad, but they cut off our group after our second plate each (there are about 4 to
5 wings on each plate). The waitress claimed they were running out, and that she had other tables to serve. She was SO
rude, and assumed entitlement since we were a group of teenagers. After tolerating her annoyed sighs and one too many eye rolls, I spoke with the manager. I told her about our horrible service. I must say, she was very kind and professional. She called in another cook (who was apparently bringing more wings? I don't understand to this day what was up with that), but I insisted that we had lost our appetite. Instead, each of my friends received a free scoop of ice cream. Needless to say, we didn't tip over 10% and I won't be back. 

Spend your money elsewhere.

I had the worst chinese of my life here. That says a lot considering I have had chinese food in places like Utah and upstate New Hampshire. It was so greasy and foul I ended up throwing it out. I probably should have known better given the skeezy neon lights.

The eggrolls tasted like they had been fried, then thrown aside for 6 or so hours, and then refried in really dirty oil. So gross.

The chicken with broccoli was a greasy mess. It tasted like it had been reheated as well. The kung pow chicken was also swimming in old grease. 

I have heard other people say they like it, but I really don't think I am going to give them another chance. I'll stick with Chinatown.

Overall a sub par experience, bordering and teetering on a 2 star rating. Decided to try Yoko with all the great reviews about it. Easy to find in a shopping plaza off the herded path. 

Entered the restaurant and beatles music was playing in the background. It seemed only about 5 songs were playing till it was repeated over and over. Our waitress didn't speak any English, which was rather annoying. She really didn't understand us, and we had trouble understanding her. 

Ordered the miso soup which was very tasty. Had sushi which was flavorful but, ok. I was expecting from some of the reviewers, fresh fish, but it wasn't as fresh as I had hoped. I also had the chicken and rice, no kobe beef which I was disappointed with. Bathrooms were very clean and fresh. 

This place was very cold, but the end of the night, most customers had jackets on and were freezing. I had complained, but was ignored. Very expensive as well for sub-par food. The place was dead for a Saturday night, maybe word is getting around.

Most likely would not return.

No problems with the actual restaurant but chickies and Pete's stands at bb&t pavilion and Lincoln financial field are absolutely absurd. $14 for fries plus another $3 for cheese cup is just outrageous. Used to be a strong supporter of crabfries but I will never drop that absurd amount of money on just a small cup of fries.

I have been wanting to try Vetri for years. Maybe my hopes were too high, but I was a bit disappointed - especially given the $155 price tag. 

The service was excellent, particularly the wine service. 

The food, on the other hand, was uneven. The scallop and the gnocchi dishes were incredible, but the other dishes - wild boar pasta, roast, some awful bone marrow dish my friend received, and the desserts were not good enough to warrant the price tag. Moreover, I felt like each dish was heavier than the next. I wish the menu was better balanced with a few more dishes that had a lighter touch. After the scallops, it was all meat, pasta and heavy sauces. 

Finally, I was with two friends and I hated that we each received something different at each course and we had no input on who received what. I understand respectingt eh vision of the chef, but what about serving the tastes of the customers?  

I go to a lot of high end restaurants and a lot of multi course dinners. I'm not uncomfortable spending well over $150 for a meal, BUT, I also expect a meal in this price range to be amazing and I was far from amazed at Vetri. 

And I'm actually a little surprised it has done this well for so long. There are so many other great choices in Philly. I expect to pick one of me the next time I am looking for a good upscale meal in the area.

Was headed to the Asian Market earlier today and as many times as I've been I always said I wanted to try Beawon. My wife and I went mainly to get some bulgogi. Then we looked at the menu! This place is expensive ! I skipped the bulgogi as I didn't feel like spending $48 for lunch. We went with the shrimp tempura which was decent. The vegetable and scallion pancake and dumplings were pretty bad. Should have just went home. I'll never go back.
Conveniently located, this 7-11 delivers beer, gas and chips. What else do you need? I can't think of anything!

The quality of the food was good enough but the restaurant week options were overpriced. We made reservations a week in advance and still had to wait an additional 20 minutes. Very cramped and loud. The waitress offered complimentary vegetables for the table and then charged us $9 for a cup of brussel sprouts! The drinks were weak and not worth the prices. I would not recommend this place again.

Not bad but not great. Locals would say it's over priced, but tourists will find the prices an improvement from Bourbon street.

Unfortunately, the mistake I made on my last visit was nobody's but my own.

I ordered a dish called the Connection Platter- a gigantic serving of ALL THINGS SOUTHERN AND FRIED.

Deep fried catfish, wings, okra, pickles, sausage and yes, chicken livers - enough for 4. It's everything you'd imagine, and yes, they do provide ample warning. Nonetheless, we went for it and an hour later, regretted every bite. Again, this is nobody's fault but my own.

But don't hold my sorry bout of bad ordering disuade you - it's not that bad. ... Besides, the desserts rock and the prices are reasonable, especially the badass bread pudding drenched in praline sauce.

PS. I've also heard their fried chicken is TO-DIE-FOR.

It smelled literally like a toilet.
We got shakes to go (they were pretty good) but couldn't stay because of the smell.

best coffee on the main line, but really meh service... and that has been the case for years...  typically takes 5-10 minutes to get a drink (including a double espresso) with NO ONE ahead of you in line

Walked in the door and it was a few before we got seated. Been here before several times and it was amazing. This time seems a bit different from the get go. After being seated, it took a while to order and to receive the appetizer after the order. Ordered the Tumble Weeds (fried artichoke hearts)and they were great, dipped in blue cheese dressing. As the pizza arrived, it looked nothing like the described pizza on the order which was the Lobo. After asking the waitress about the pizza she said she couldn't find the receipt. However it was taken care of and the pizza came shortly after that. The manager turned out to be our server whom really handled the issue. The pizza was very delicious. I would still recommend this joint to most of my friends.

Well, I have been visiting Arner's for many years, but had been out of state for a few years now.  Their salad bar was one to come visit with their homemade bread with whipped butter that you slice yourself.  NOPE none -- some (you can get anywhere) rolls were in their place.  Salad bar was ok.  Now let us talk desserts,  I love lemon meringue pie.   Big piece of pie, but the filling if homemade they need to fire the baker! OMG  lumpy lemon pie??!!!!????  New Owners? don't know.  Is this why we did not wait in a long line, which was par the course for this restaurant?

Chickie's and Pete's is an artery clogging experience. They are known for their crab fries. They taste super good. And, they are accompanied by melted cheese.

The crinkle cut fries are cooked just right. But they give you so many fries, you might gain ten pounds and have a heart attack in one sitting.

Part of me feels they'd be better off giving not so generous portions. As good as they taste in the beginning, they start making you feel like a pig by the end. And, man, that salt kicks in and all of the sudden you start asking yourself, what am I doing to myself.

So yeah, eat a little, you're alright. Down a whole serving, you're hurtin' for certain.

I know it's dumb to review a Walmart, and even dumber to visit a Walmart. But, I was in the area for the weekend helping my dad, and he sent me here for a few things.

Pulling up, I thought the Walmart had closed and moved. I honesly thought I was in the wrong place and was totally confused. The outside looks like it's being torn down, and there was no signage at all. As I walked up, I saw a small Walmart sign by the door. What is up with this, Walmart? 

Inside, I didn't get any farther than the frozen food section, which is right when you walk in, so that was convenient. I was looking for a very specific frozen pizza, which they didn't have. Sigh...I used my dad's debit card, and the cashier didn't question it. Is that good or bad?

To be fair, this place is more bearable than my local South Philly Walmart (a.k.a. hell on earth.) BUT, that Walmart deserves negative 10 stars, which makes this one-star review pretty reasonable.

We have been here many times. Food is generally pretty good, freshly prepared and they have a Coke Freestyle machine. The front end of the restaurant is usually a little messy but on today's visit it was even worse and not at a busy time, 4pm, and there were at least 5 employees working. We were the only customers in the restaurant. There were 11 dirty tables. The area around the waste bins was scattered with food and the counter area in front of the drink and condiment area was dirty. There was a partially completed catering order on one of the benches. I mentioned this to an employee at the register who seemed to be in charge and he said that he would look into it. Well, it would not take much "looking into" because it was plain to see that this store needs a manager and employees who care about cleanliness and providing their customers a clean place to eat. It's often thought provoking when the visible areas of a restaurant are dirty...what about the areas that the customer doesn't see? The next time I come here to eat I will walk out if I'm seeing the same thing I saw today.
Brunch is my jam here but even if it's not busy be prepared to wait about 40 minutes for your food. Makes no sense at all... Ate here with a friend and their food came out about 10 mins before mine.

This club is simply disgusting; dirty and smelly; staff is incompetent and careless, only priority is to sell more. 
Classes held in the sweltering room, floor is slippery, due to no air conditioning, multiple complains from the instructor and members are ignored.  It's just a disaster waiting to happen.
I never see floor being mopped or mats being disinfected, there is no wipes in the room, and mats are heavily used by extremely sweaty people every day.
 staff refuses to turn on air conditioning.
Went here on a recommendation. Looked promising since all the locals go here. 4 of us all did not care so much for our dishes. Mostly they were too salty. My po'boy was a bit dry and bland.

This is the first Ethiopian restaurant that I've had since moving from Atlanta, Georgia. I have to say back home we have the benefit of a more diverse population, so we have quite a few places to pick from-they carry more sauces like Awaze and some even have breakfast! 

The one thing I've noticed in all of the Ethiopian restaurants I've tried is the kitchen staff is a family, so these family recipes differ a little bit from one to another. I would've preferred a little more garlic and maybe a few more menu options, but the meal definitely satisfied my Ethiopian fix. The older lady who runs/owns the shop was so sweet and had amazing customer service even on New Year's eve.

I ordered gomen, (collard greens), miser (lentil soup) and kitfo (seasoned ground beef) and they were all pretty good. Originally, I wanted shiro instead of the collard greens, but they were out. Maybe you should call in advance for that one, since the side is considered a delicacy in their culture. 

Good experience overall. Hopefully in the future they'll add fish and a few more veggies to their menu :)

add 3-5 hours to whatever ur wait time is. Dont bother making an appointment itll still be 4 hours for an oil change.  How do u screw up a rotation?  They managed to leave something metal in a tire and  when i brought it back they said it was nothing...but it wasnt there anymore..
They say theyll do work....est. 2 hours..then 6 hours  goes by and uh oh they dont have part. 

Because i have a sears card and they r open on sundays we keep going back.  Awesome

Service was good.  We sat in the sushi/non-hibachi area, they have some nice HDTVs to watch the night game.  As for the food, I'd say it was good overall.  I ordered 3 sushi rolls which tasted very fresh.  I'd recommend the Mexican roll if you're into spicy tuna.  Appetizers were also okay, although I won't be ordering the shumai again.  The yakitori was good, big chunks of white meat but a little light on sauce.

Overall, not a bad choice if you're feeling for sushi.

If you like the smooth creamy texture of Palaak Paneer, do NOT order it here. The paneer has small chunks of diced ginger and pods of spices. I found the texture off putting, and would not order again. I've had the tiki masala before and that was good. So will go towards meat if you go here and the garlic naan is good.

Walked all the way thru the store and then had to ask for help. Nobody greeted us even after we asked for help. Lots of workers walking around talking to each other. When the girl said she could, we gave her what we were looking for, she said go over toward the corner & we could see what they had. She would be with us in a while. 10 minutes later we went back to find her on the phone. Had to ask for someone else to help. After we found what we where looking for we found out that to get our table we would have to go to south St. Louis warehouse to get it, or pay $70 to have it delivered not assembled and $35 to have it assembled. I'm very sure that we will never return or buy from this company again.

My experience with this location online has provided me with all I need to know on why people should not join this location.  I witness one of their trainers, who promotes he works for LA Fitness and vice versa, calling people names online, shaming, bullying, and have a recent Instagram photo where he is binge drinking. If this is the representative of their location, you should not chance joining this location.

Bland. Everything was bland. I have eaten there twice and both times was disappointed. Even their beer is bland - tried one of their pale ales and the flavor was shallow and fake. They were out of the local brew I originally ordered. Lettuce wraps lacked flavor with slimy chicken. "Spicy" peanut chicken noodles had no flavor. Boring food, boring atmosphere, just boring! Won't be back.

My husband I went here for lunch and not only was the service really slow (think: took 20 minutes to get refills, and Diet Coke obviously needed to be changed) when our food finally arrived the chicken on my Caesar salad was either really underdone or a terrible cut of meat. Gross. Will not be returning.

Needed a few things, was in the area so stopped in this store.

Safeway never seems to have any real competitive prices on things I buy, in other words not cheap.

We wanted to purchase something from the bakery department of which there was need for an employee's assistance.

Trying to get someone's attention in the department was unsuccessful. So I found another employee and asked him to assist. He did get another from the deli and we got what we wanted and went on our way.

We were told no one was working in the bakery.

It took a few minutes for the person to be found and brought over to help and this gave us time to actually look over the department. Man this place needs a good scrub, what we saw could not have been the result of one days neglect. We almost left before we were actually helped, but braved it and hope we did not kill anyone that received what we bought.

Perhaps someone should be working the department if for no other reason to clean it up.

Never have I ever seen a department store in such bad shape.  I came here looking for jeans and when I got to that section,  I was horrified.  None of the jeans were folded,  nor were they organized by brand,  or size.  The employees ACTIVELY ignored me,  and went out of their way to not help me.  I will never come back to this store. Savers is cleaner than this,  and when I shop at department stores,  I want a clean, organized,  friendly store.  This store is not any of these.
After having some cheese steaks at Jim's, we walked over for dessert. Sundaes, Milk Shakes and fries hit the spot.

When I went there the restaurant was nearly empty and was an hour for closing. I ordered some simple dishes nothing I cannot cook myself. The food took nearly 30 minutes to come out. When it did come out they also brought me my check. The waiter then told me the kitchen is now closed and I must pay my bill. Then even more irritating the waiter came back and told me I have to tip now and that it couldn't wait for me to even eat my food. As for the food it was horrible and the meat was undercooked. I would never go back there again.

Upgraded to a 3 rating because the food is really good.  this place can easily be a 4. Chicken is always really good.  customers and staff are fun and prices are spot on. But what do they have against bartenders?  They have a bar with 8 stools and 4 high top tables in the bar area. 11  cold beers on tap and 8 sodas. Yet for some reason they don't have a bartender. If you sit at the bar, you have to flag down someone to get a beer. Take care of your bar patrons and this place will be Tremendous.
I thought it was too expensive. But i did end up with a red and black metal bookcase that looks great in my kitchen.

I have given Finish Line 2 times getting their pit boss special, and have also been there twice with my old car before I got rid of it, every time they miss large swathes of my car on the exterior and interior. Overall the car gets washed but my car is left with bugs on the front of the car that aren't removed, grime still underneath my rear spoiler that actually drips down because the car isn't done and dried properly. I opened my trunk up and around the edges (on the body) there are tons of stuff that was missed! (and I paid for interior wash as well)

Interior wise, they consistently miss things like hairs/dust on my dash and miss large portions of dust on my gauge cluster (every time now), also don't vacuum my seats thorough enough. I am a stickler but for the prices they charge it should be done properly. 

My grille was missed completely, right above the grille on the front lip (I drove a '16 Challenger, been there twice with this car) wasn't even touched. 

They're nice people wise but their service (I have found at least) is lacking.

It's a cute corner coffee shop. I've never been too impressed with their drinks tbh, I wouldn't recommend going there for that at all. But their pastries are legit especially the banana bread and cookies. I'd *suggest* they get vegan cookies for the lactose intolerant folks, but otherwise the place is pretty cool. It's more of a "oh crap what's close by" than a "let's drive here" kind of place. Mornings it's full of construction workers- no harm to them, I get it, but there's no where to really sit or stand that's not in the way of someone else. One up point the people who work behind the counter are all genuine and friendly, I've never had a bad experience due to them. But I'm most excited to see how they'll grow and hopefully how their reputation will improve.

We loved the service here. Our waiter was one of the kindest, most attentive servers we had when we visited New Orleans.

However, it didn't cancel out the food.

The historic story of the building that houses Cafe Pontalba (take a History Tour for that story - it's a fun one!) is rich and beautiful, but unfortunately, the food at the Cafe is not.

I had crabcakes that were much more cake and much less crab. They were semi-warm, which is something you never want for seafood, mushy, and just overall kind of boring.

The presentation was sloppy - I've seen better plating at a Waffle House.

My partner had a spicy pasta dish, that was so bland he took about four bites of it and said, "Let's just go somewhere else."

The food really was that bad - we left Pontalba and went elsewhere. We'll not be coming back.

Avoid this place unless you have a few hours to waste and you don't plan on eating.  There was no hostess to seat us.  We and other guests just stood around looking for a table.  When a table finally opened, we sat down.  The table was filthy so I cleaned it the best I could with the napkins available at the table.  We waited 25 minutes for a waitress to even come to our table which we had to share with 4 strangers.  My companion went to the front to ask if we were to order at the register or was there wait staff.  Another 10 minutes passed and a disheveled waitress finally appeared.  She did not bring a cloth to wipe the table but was quick to grab her tip from the previous guests.  When we tried to order we were told they were out of bread so we couldn't order any sandwiches which terribly disappointed me as I was craving a pastrami on rye sandwich.  We re-chose items from the menu which didn't require bread and proceeded to wait another 45 minutes but still hadn't gotten any food.  In all that time, the waitress never came back to give us a status on our order.  We'd had enough by then and just paid for our sodas and left.  Absolutely terrible service.  Nearly 2 hours wasted from my day and I didn't even get to eat!  Skip this place if you value your time and want to eat.

Meh. The atmosphere is nice. And the food was okay (not bad, but not fantastic for a $20 chicken pot pie). But we waited 45 minutes outside because of how crowded the waiting area was. There were 6 of us and when we were finally seated we were brought to a 4 person booth. Very cramped. And part of the booth was broken. I couldn't even finish my meal because I had no space to cut anything. I gave up. It was an okay experience, but I'd never bring a party of more than 4 people. Ideally two.

I called this place asking for their hours and was met quite rudely by a woman  who answered the phone.  Giving the benefit of the doubt I decided to try their bbq anyway.
