-Pot Roast (w/ roasted veggies & mashed potatoes): First of all, let me just say... those mashed potatoes were to die for. I believe they're homemade too. As for my pot roast, it was very tasty, just could of been a tad more tender. I feel like a good pot roast will pull apart with your fork and just melt in your mouth.
-I was also really tempted by the Meatloaf and Thanksgiving Dinner when I saw others around me eating it.

Dessert
-Apple Pie served warm with vanilla ice cream and caramel sauce. Enough said.

Overall, I was quite satisfied with the food. But some things could of been tweaked a little. I'm ready for the Mac & Cheese on my next, hopefully quieter, trip!

I just went here for the pie. That's it. The coconut custard looked and smelled like eggs not coconut. The pecan pie we bought for 16 dollars was soggy, and the filling had the consistency of bread pudding. The crust under the pie was both soggy AND burned. I am SEVERELY disappointed. This was my first time at this establishment and will be my last.

Worst experience ever! I bring a celebrity picture with me to do the eyebrow threading, the lady told me she can do eyebrow wax only. Ok, wax. She said she couldn't wax my eyebrow to that style. And she tried her 'freestyle '. I told her that I will have an important life event 2days later with this weird eyebrow, she was laughing. She really pissed me off . I threaded my eyebrow at different cities with that celebrity's picture, nobody said they couldn't do that. How they can get these good views here?

This was not a good place. The waitress was nice but the food was not good at all. They forgot our coffees and even forgot to put cheese on my omelet. When I said something about it they just slapped some slices of cheese on top and wasn't even  melted. I don't recommend this place to my enemy. The bathroom is in the kitchen with no lock on the door. This seem unsanitary.

This joint continually blasts loud rap music around the clock, disturbing the peace and quiet with this sonic garbage of dozens and dozens of travelers waiting for their plane to depart. 

Turn down your damn loud speakers, you self-centered noise-polluters!!!!

Talk about overrated! When I relocated to Nashville from Arizona, I had high hopes for the BBQ scene. But time & time again, I've been disappointed, especially by Edley's. They seem to always be out of the staples: ribs & brisket. And even when you can get your hands on those rarities, they're average. I do like the potato salad & the drink specials. But overall, don't waste your time & don't buy into the hype!

I rolled up to moes 12/3/14 it was accessible for people with disabilities. The service was good n the fellas seemed nice enough. The food was ok not bad. Guess it all depends on what u order on what. The ingredients seemed fresh. N good. The hot salsa had a little heat but not much flavor. Should have asked which one they preferred. Overall Moes is a good spot for a burrito or quick Mexican fix.

Paradise Cafe has always been consistent and good.  Unfortunately the last few times I have eaten there, the service has been horrible.  No refills, forgotten items and lackadaisical servers seems to be the norm.

Just had dinner at The Candlewyck and was very disappointed. The tomato bisque soup which used to be delicious was so early as was the tenderloin stir fry. Such a shame.  We've been going to the. Candlewyck for over 30 years. I hope this was just a bad day and not an indication of the food quality.

Was here on a Tuesday night. The bartender was nice, let us linger as closing time (10pm?) approached. Unfortunately the wine was terrible as were the nachos. They were soaking in an unidentifiable wet white clumpy cheese- We were told the cheese was a 'house blend.' It was not good.

A local recommended this place to us. Some dishes were great, others not so much. 

We tried the sampler, alligator sausage, crawfish etouffee, gumbo, jambalaya, bread pudding, and of course, a hurricane. So, we got a good mix of everything.

Excellent gumbo, fried gator sausage, and the best bread pudding!! I guess it really depends on what you order here. Can't go wrong with a hurricane!!!

Place is nice and clean, staff is patient and friendly but each time we have come we have waited more than 1 hour for our appointment. That's not acceptable. Looks like they are taking in more patients than they can manage. I will look for an alternative for my son.

Ken,

You've lied to me a number of times. First off, you said your name was Tim. Secondly,i got oil changes at Firestone; meanwhile, Goodyear replaced my tire. Thirdly, in my complaint to the BBB, you said we made an appointment and we never did. Fourthly, Firestone DOES NOT use nitrogen air for their tires. Fifthly, you said you could replacement the valve but at a cost. And finally, you did not check with where you purchased my speciality tire ($300) that you had to order of whether they gave you the N2 valve. So any way you cut it, you are responsible for it but refuse to own up to it. That is why i will never step foot in your shop again.

This place is terrible. Awful service, had to wait 10 minutes to be seated then other 10 for the waitress to even show up. Food wasn't bad mozzarella cubes were really good, shrimp and grits were to spicy and cheesy grits with that didn't taste that good but it was okay. After eating waitress took at least 15 minutes for the Check and another 20 for to come back and get it. Overall bad experience highly recommend not going there.

Seated immediately. Very friendly service. Sadly the positive feedback ends here. The food was just not great. Very wet eggs. An egg white omelet that wasn't an omelet by any definition. Soggy hash browns. Beyond overlooked home fries with raw bell peppers and onions. Raw Granny Smith apple pancake. And a 45 minute wait for the food to be served.

third time at smashburger is my last time at smashburger. Long wait for grilled chicken and veggie burger...got smashburger instead of veggie and waited again. Everything was over salted to the point of being unpleasant. Somebody has a heavy hand or it is getting salted twice. Drank a ton of water once home..lips burning from the salt. The workers here are all too friendly with each other. Whilst ordering, Antoine, our order taker would not look at us and was carrying on a conversation with a co-worker instead of uttering anything to us. The woman wiping down tables is very friendly but the disgusting rag that she is wiping every ketchup bottle with needs to be thrown in the laundry bin. It still seemed dirty and had that dirty smell, the outside around the door area hadn't been swept in weeks and there is now a nice greasy trail that leads to their already filthy trash cans. Simple daily cleaning would go a long way. I no longer like smashburger.

I have been coming to GSR for a very long time, to the point that many of the employees know me or my husband. I have been to Lex many times, mostly getting a VIP but sometimes just to enjoy a nice out. My most recent visit to see Fetty Wap was horrible, it's sad to me that an establishment like Lex doesn't have a better management and is completely unorganized.  To start we got in the VIP line and our ticket that had our table # was lost, so we waited a good 45 minutes to even get through the door.  I had already given them my credit card when we got there. When the hostess came to our table and took our order she asked for my card, I told her I had already given it and she said ok she would look in the back. She came back about 15 minutes later and said they couldn't find it. Me and my husband had to go out to the front and ask around to look for it. Apparently it was randomly in on of the black books they carry out front.  So now the hostess leaves to get our drinks.  They never brought our mixers or our ice so the hostess had to get everything herself, she was actually working very hard.  I appreciated her. We finally get to having a drink about 2 hours later. 
If that were the last issue I don't think I would be so frustrated. I read another review where the manager responded they don't deny guest water.  I walked to the empty front bar to ask for water and the bartender told me they don't provide water I would have to buy it. After I just paid for my VIP, I see one of the front guys I believe his name was Roman and I tell him. He said don't worry I got you, he sends a bucket of water with a receipt for $36 I told the random hostess I'm not paying for this I just wanted one water cup for my husband who wasn't feeling well. She gets Roman and he acts like he had never spoke to me and didn't know what I was talking about and the girl snatches the water off the table and walks away.

Where do i start very bad bad bad customer service and none of the games work properly. Ordered the salmon meal with the rice and spinach. The first rice was cold and hard told the server about it then she said she would get me a new order that was fine. Next order was worst not only was it cold and hard it was stuck together. I asked to speak to a manager. Server told me she was getting one. I waited there a total of 27 mins with no manager. The server just continue to seat ppl and never even came back to my table. I got up to ask her where the manager is she said i told him its just busy so he will get to you when he can.. wow it was a Monday night and it wasnt that busy. I walked around myself and found a manager. He told me he was the manager of the game room but he would take care of it. He said he would take the food part off... how is this acceptable?

 I have wasted over 1 hour of my time and you say you can take it off. He did not try to accommodate me at all. I felt like they didnt care to have me as a customer. I will not be returning and i advise ppl to stay away from this place. Games are super over priced and the food is just awful. Service is the worst ive ever recieved in my life!! Stay away from this place!!!!!!

I live in New Orleans so I know what true, authentic NOLA food should taste like. This was my first time eating at The Praline Connection, so I was pretty excited. That excitement quickly vanished when no one greeted us and we waited at the door staring at workers passing us by. We ended up having to walk to the kitchen door to ASK to be seated. We were finally seated and waited about 20 minutes before our server came to our table (only because we had to ask where our server was). After waiting forever, we finally placed our order. The food was NOT freshly prepared; it was obvious it was pre-cooked, refrigerated and then heated up. My meatloaf was dried up and extremely hard on one side (like it has been in the fridge for a long time and that was the side that was sitting up in the fridge). The macaroni was soggy and tasted old. This place seems like a tourist trap to feed visitors who don't know any better in regards to food quality. I spent nearly a $100 on old, warmed up food. My sister got her praline cheesecake to go, and all they put in the box was the cheesecake slice and no praline sauce. I'm telling everyone I know about the poor-quality food at this place. It's ashame you all feed people old food...enough for someone to get sick! You won't have to worry about me spending another dollar at your establishment; I'll get higher quality from a Stoffer's frozen dish. Simply ridiculous! We visited Saturday afternoon, and it'll be our last visit. I'm not spending a large amount of money on stale, unfresh food that could potentially make me ill from unfreshness. It's a shame tourists don't know any better when it comes to what good nola food is, because it's definitely not this place.

We were there for lunch, when the restaurant first opened for the day.  We were the only table there for the first 45 minutes.  Ten minutes after we ordered we were required to change our order because they were "out of fish."  30 minutes later, the tacos came out.  The shrimp tacos were good (they were only out of mahi mahi, apparently).  The chips (not free) and the guacamole were good.  The other tacos were not.  This place just isn't ready yet to be a restaurant, and certainly not to compete with Taco Mamacita.  Maybe the bar scene is better.

I thought the first time I went there, they were just having a bad day.  So I returned a second time.  Never again!! The service is a joke...20 mins to get my order and it was wrong. The stupid leading the stupid.  Management should be ashamed for hiring the most incompetent people in Indianapolis.  One question for the employees... What are you going to do with your life if  you can't even make a taco???
This restaurant has the worst service ever! They did not get our coffee out until 45 minutes after we ordered it.

They don't even deserve one star.

Both customer service representatives, Gabrielle and Michelle, are very rude! 

I ordered silver candle holders and most of them were sent to me tarnished. I spent the night prior to my wedding polishing them and they only offered to take $30 off my bill

When they say no refund, they mean it.  My wedding venue advised me that I could not rent outside silverware after I had already made a down payment with Preferred Party Place. I explained this to Michelle and she refused to refund my down payment.

Whereas, another vendor did so, without a problem.

I would never rent from this company and would not advise advise anyone to do so either.
Poor customer service i ask for extra hot chili oil she said they are expensive and only game me 1? Never coming here again.plus i dont know if their asian accent but really they are rude.

Usually this taco bell is fine.  Nothing stands out, negative or positive.  But I have to laugh when I go an hour before closing, when it's dead and people are ready to go home, and I'm told "computers are down" so I can't order anything. This has happened twice.

Right.

As an aside, I am a professional who works very late hours.  When I go through a drive through at 3 am, it's not because I was out partying all night. It's because I just finished a 19 hour work day, I'm hungry, and i'm tired.    But I can promise you that at the end of that 19 hours, I treat every single one of my clients with the same pleasant attitude I did at the start of that day.     

I get that working nights might suck, and you only do it for the shift premium, but there's no reason for being rude, lazy, or a liar just because you're tired. If you can't hack it, change your hours.   Your customers are paying the same money they are during the day, and they should get the same level of service.   This seems to be a trending problem among late-night places around here.

I go to a lot of cons, and thus, have experienced a lot of Convention Centres. I went here for the 2007 NASFiC which was also Archon for the year and was simply unimpressed with the space. It's sad because I really would have liked it. 

The first thing is that it's located in the middle of freakin' nowhere from the point of view of someone who isn't from around those parts. It's about 30 minutes out of town, with no direct way to get there except for SuperShuttle, which costs a ton. A Taxi will run you 'OH MY GOD!" and if you try to take the lightrail you're a fool. Just a fool. 

Now, once you're there, there's everything you need to keep yourself from experience too much of the world outside the convention center area. There are varying levels of restaurant within walking distance and a dozen or so hotels. I stayed at the Drury Inn. There's a Mexican place, whcih is OK, and there's a White Castle, which I put a hurt on the first day I was there. There's a lovely Rudy Tuesday, and after years of seeing their commercials in a state that has no Ruby Tuesday, I was glad to get to see one in person. 

The real problem is the Convention Centre itself. The rooms are weirdly set-up and I had a real problem finding the rooms where the various programmes were. The signage was poor and at a couple of points it seemed to indicate that the same room was in two different places. The thing could handle a good-sized con, but it also had some serious bottlenecks that you don't have to deal with at the best CCs.

It's kept up well yet there are homeless people there and each time I go dog owners of large dogs think it's ok to bring their dogs into the medium dog (40 lb and under) section. It ruins my experience because I will not bring my dog or small kids into that area where there are Great Danes and other large dogs. Other dog parks around have friendly people who listen to the dog park rules.

Got there early 5pm for dinner was not crowded at meaning there were only three other groups in the restaurant. Took 15 minutes to get our drinks another 15 to order our food. 45 minutes later our food finally arrived. Average at best. Won't be coming back to jones anytime soon if ever. Nothing special about the food. Menu hasn't changed over the year.

GREAT place to watch the game!!!! And that is about it!

Crab fries... Yeah.  They are tasty, but all they are is (admitedly decent) fries with Old Bay Seasoning on them.  Not all that magical. 

The muscles are good - and spicy.  In fact, just about everything on the menu is spicy.  I understand that the goal of the menu is to sell beer, but come on!  Not everyone wants one spicy dish after another!  It becomes too much.

My friend who cannot handle spicy food was there with us to watch the game.  And that was when we found out she did not like spicy food.  We never knew this before.  So, because she was not eating , she opted to only drink soda (drinking on an empty stomach is not smart, after all).  The bill came and they charged her for every soda.  Classy.

But a GREAT place to watch the game!!!  FRIGGING WOOT!

For something billed as a Jazz Cruise, the band didn't play very much. The cruise was good. We were not allowed in the dining room on time for our 7:45 seating. The food was good. We ordered drinks right after we sat down, but they never arrived. When we tried to visit the gift shop after dinner, it was closed - well before we docked.

Business travelers looking for a late dinner find this name on Yelp, find the restaurant in a dark strip mall, and think, "Hey, why not."  (Or more like "Too late to bother with anywhere else.")  Oh Yoko! turned out to have spotty service and some unavailable menu items (a couple of patrons arriving after us were told "no sushi, we're out of rice"--what?!)

But delicious food makes up for a lot of faults, and the sushi here was pretty good.  We tried several of the rolls, including a number from the specials list, and were generally impressed.  The sushi was tasty, had some creative combinations, and were artfully presented.  Also, those wasabi fries are pretty addictive.

I absolutely love boats and anything to do with the water but I feel like I had more fun waiting in line to board than when I was actually on it. I enjoyed listening to the steam music they had playing while boarding. You had the option of just going on the cruise itself or having lunch on the cruise. We chose to have lunch, which I wish we hadn't done because as traditional as it was, it didn't taste all that great. Especially for the price we paid. A couple of the servers also seemed to have a bit of an attitude. I found the actual ride quite boring. I didn't care to be on the boat any longer but of course I couldn't get off so I stayed in the dining area where the jazz musicians were performing. The performance was tremendous! I found this to be the best part of the ride although I did end up catching myself falling asleep every once in a while but most likely because of my lack of sleep. Lol I personally didn't think this experience was fulfilling compared to the price I paid. There are better, cheaper things to do and eat in Nola, but I guess it was an alright one-time experience.

Horrible!!! I went for a mani pedi and I got ripped off! Never got a massage on either feet or hands. The acrylic tips I got are super thick and unrealistic. Not to mention it now have an infection on my right pointer finger!! I would not recommend this place to anyone! I could have saved 50$$ and did a better job myself.

This place is extremely overrated. The food is average- nothing special. But then you're paying more for simple homemade American food. I just can't justify the spend on something so average. Why spend $8 on mac and cheese when I can make a pretty awesome mac and cheese at home for $3?

Our annual holiday luncheon was at Redemption today. When we were deciding on a location for our party we checked into a few places. Our admin staff sent us the menu and everyone liked what they saw so we settled on Redemption. We called to make the reservations and were told that a banquet menu would be offered due to the size of our party (14 people).

At that point, we said thank you but we were going to pass because we wanted to order from the lunch menu. The person taking the reservation quickly reversed and said ordering from the lunch menu would not be a problem. We made the reservation for 12:30pm today but were called later and asked if 1pm was acceptable. We agreed and planned accordingly.

We arrived today at about 1:05pm, no later than 1:10pm. We were seated and saw that we had banquet menus at our place settings. Our office manager, who made the reservation, asked about this and we were offered lunch menus. 

We ordered drinks, appetizers, and entrees and thus began a 2+ hour fiasco. 

The appetizers took an hour to arrive. My colleagues did seem to enjoy theirs as did I. However, it was lukewarm. 

The office manager was a little upset with a couple of service related issues and that it was after 2:30 pm and our entrees had not yet appeared. She asked to speak to a manager. One of the wait staff went into the kitchen and got the "chef". Said office manager gets up and goes off to the hallway by the restrooms with him and lodges her complaint. 

So what does the chef do? He harangues her about our party being "late" for the 1 o'clock reservation and says that the entree preparation is being delayed further by her asking to speak to a manager who also happened to be the person making us lunch (as if she would know that). He also managed to throw in the irrelevant points that he was also a "manager" and "part owner". The only thing that would have made it better is if he'd said it with a thick french accent while throwing his hands in the air in outrage.

We finally got our entrees near 3:00pm and were able to resume our lives.

My takeaway is-

The owners need to find someone who is capable of managing the expectations of the customers spending the money. Our party overlooked quite a few things without complaint; getting drinks spilled all over 2 of our staff (no biggie, mistakes happen, hey why are none of the waitstaff helping clean it up?), 15 minute waits for a single glass of wine while when we were the ONLY customers in the place, etc.

The owners need to hire people who are  thoughtful, considerate, and professional. The "chef" was not it. 

PS - We liked the food! Thus 2 stars.

Cajun Combo had a cup of gumbo which was good, followed by a plate with a scoop of white rice, scoop of Jambalaya, beans with sausage and beef, and a piece of grilled sausage.  The jambalya (seasoned rice had sausage slices) was too spicy for my taste buds!  I ate everything else.
Blackened Alligator appetizer was very good.  It tasted like the dark meat of pork.  It came with a lemon wedge and dipping sauce.
My husband had the barbecue shrimp appetizer which was more like a boil using oil and seasonings with whole shrimps...head and all. That came with slices of Italian bread to dip.  Not bad.
My DD had the Catfish Pecan which was a fried filet covered by a lemony sauce with chopped pecans. That came with overcooked string beans and a scoop of mashed red potatoes, skin included.
Service was very good.  Gratuity is added to the bill but I added more since he was friendly, attentive, and recommended the blackened alligator over the fried one.
We sat with a view of St. Louis Cathedral as the sun set.  Awesome!
Oh and the prices were quite reasonable.

Went early on Saturday night. They were busy!! A lot of big parties. Service was a little slow but our waiter was accommodating. food was good (chicken parmigiana / Pappardelle Porcini), but not extraordinary. Desserts (tiramisu / chocolate mouse pie) were good. Apparently they have a prix fix menu on Weds in winter. I would give that a try, but would not go back for a date.

I got the turkey leg with squash and cornbread as the two sides. Interest pricing here...the entrees are an amazing deal (the turkey leg is like half the size of my arm and only $10! So awesome!) while the sides are like the biggest rip off ever. $3 for a fistful of squash?! And another $2 bucks if I want cornbread?! A lot of people have been talking about the cornbread but I'll just set it straight. Unless you like dry, stale, under seasoned, not very tasty cornbread DONT GET IT. To put it in perspective, I like Boston market cornbread better. Yeah that's right, you can get better cornbread at Boston market. it's not worth it. 

So with a great deal on entrees and terrible deal on sides it ends up being about decent meal for ~$16-17 bucks. 

I was pretty turned off by the cornbread but overall it was a good meal. Turkey leg for $10 is great. easily could eat that for two meals and be very happy

Experience this restaurant Only if you have over an hour to wait for a breakfast. A group of us made reservations to dine at this restaurant on Sunday morning for brunch. The first couple arrived and was told that there was no champagne left for Sunday brunch mimosas (at 11:00am), but they could buy a $55.00 bottle of champagne if they so chose. Further, the server stated that they would not get more champagne as it would not be cost effective for them- the restaurant. I was shocked to hear this from a restaurant at any time, let alone these economic times. I arrived last in the party as I was parking. I was never offered a menu, never offered water and was basically ignored for not having arrived with the rest. The server finally offered to take a drink order from me. She returned with their "Special House Bloody Mary," which basically was a standard bloody Mary mix with an unreasonable amount of horseradish and Worcestershire sauce in the bottom. In short, my lips were numb from the drink. 
After an hour and 15 minutes, our breakfasts came out in three groupings, as the one end of the table began eating 10 minutes before the rest of the table. The eggs were greasy, the grits were palatable at best. And, no, I was never offered a drink re-fill or a glass of water the entire time we were there. No, the restaurant was not full. No, there were no waiting lines. No, the bar was not packed. There was simply plenty of very poor service, and management to fill all the empty tables and bar stools.
We eat out more than twice a week, from the Dave's local breakfast joint to Bern's last weekend and Donatello's this weekend. If a restaurant does not have service, they have nothing, especially in this economy.

I've been here more than a couple of times because my mom is addicted to their chips and salsa...

Service - Every server I've had has been great. The only thing is that they do sometimes have to be reminded to fill up the chips, but as soon as you let them now they bring them out pretty fast.

Atmosphere - It's nice, I guess. It's a chain so it just looks like they tried a little too hard to be "MEXICAN!" with the clay pots on the wall and the mural in the back. It can get loud when busy, and sometimes the floors look a little messy, but nothing too bad.

Food - I'll do this by the dishes I've had. :D

Chips & Salsa: Mmmmm, so good. They bring them out warm and crispy. Sometimes you get a chip that's overly salty, but I just brush the salt off and eat it. The salsa is not chunky (just how I like it) and deliciously spicy. You can get a big bag of chips and salsa to go anytime for like $3.

Taco Melts (Chicken): OH MY GOD. The chicken is in this insanely delicious queso sauce & then topped with even more cheese. It's greasy in a good way. They serve it with either soft flour tortillas or crispy shells. The shells are not like usual hard taco shells, they seem more light if that makes sense.

Chicken & Cheese Empanadas: I've gotten this with the create-your-own-combo before. They're just okay. The crust is pretty good and flaky, but it's just dripping in grease.

Chicken Flauta: These were super lame the two times I got them. They are really dry and about the width of a tube of chapstick. The ends were burnt both times too. 

Class Burrito (Chicken, of course): This is what I get almost every time I go. The chicken is sooo tender and has this spicy flavor to it that is amazing. I always get the queso on top and it's tasty.

Refried Beans & Rice: Your choice of refried beans or black beans comes with almost every meal, I think. The refried beans are pretty good, not anything special but they do the job. The rice has some corn and peppers in it and is pretty good.

It's not "authentic" by any means, but you definitely could do worse.

Sign says they open at 7:30. At 7:40 a guy comes to unlock the door and then walks away. I open the door and walk in saying "good morning" to no reply. I just bought a quick set of jumper cables. Had to wait in line for them to open registers, then answer phone calls. I was only customer in the store but took 25 minutes to get jumper cables.

I have gone there many times with a group of friends over the years just come along. But last night would be the last time for me. They regularly screwed up my orders and the servers would have a bad attitudes about them screwing up.  While the name implies something nice this place is anything but. 

Best go somewhere else.

Fui con mi familia a comer a este rest. Q nos habian recomendado pero el mesero nos trató de la patada muy malcriado y hasta tirando los platos al ponerlos en la mesa. Una experiencia horrible no volvemos ni lo recomendamos

Smash Burger offers good, if predictable burgers. It is important to bear in mind that it is closer to fast-food in style than a gourmet burger joint. This is evinced by the fact that, instead of ordering a thicker patty to get more food, you need to either order a double patty or an additional burger.  However, the burgers are still of substantially better quality than you get from McDonald's or Burger King, but don't expect a half-pound of cooked-to-order angus or filet. 

For what it is, Smash Burger is good. The prices are about what you'd expect, perhaps a little high but not "hamburger on a silver platter" prices you get at the gourmet joints. It is what it is.

The recording on their phone says they close at 10:30 pm, so I hope on 10W to get there around 9:40.  They were closed.  I called again while there.  Asked for the manager and was placed on hold for over 5 minutes.  Then, the call ended.  Called back and was told the manager was 'in a conference.'  At 9:45, she's in a conference? I asked.  He said, 'yes, with a customer.'  I advised there were only two people in the restaurant and they were out on the patio, so who was she meeting with?  Suddenly, "Kim" was available and came to the phone.  I asked, "why are you closed if you advertise you close at 10:30?"  Her response,"oh no, we are open until 10.  I explain that I just left because I was told you were closed and the doors were locked.  She said "oh no, we are open."  I advised again, I just left there, two people on the patio and Amman sitting off to the side.  The doors were locked and I was told you were closed.  She apologized and said ahead only been there 2 weeks.

Awful! I got the Italian Hoagie. The meat was brown, the cheese tasted off. Not made fresh. When we ordered at 9:20 pm, they had already cleaned their fryer, so we couldn't order fries, onion rings, or a chicken Parmigiana sandwich. Ridiculous! Lasagna was good. White pizza was good. Still will not go there again.

Today was a nightmare at the salon. We've gone here before and not had a problem, today not so much. There was 3 nail technicians doing nails and I sat for over 2.5 hrs waiting to get a fill in. Upon walking in they gave no notification that they were running behind so I sat down not realizing that most of the people sitting at the nail stations had not been helped. Once the tech approached me to do my nails she took my polish off then someone walked in wanting a wax done so she stopped helping me to go do the wax. At this point I seriously thought about and should have just walked out but I stayed at which point another nail tech, a man this time came to start the process of doing my nails. I brought my own polish which was a glitter polish which I know was see through with blue and silver flecks of glitter because this was what I wanted. The male nail tech then told me I needed to put a color underneath, I DID NOT want a color underneath but he was not satisfied with that so he pushed until I caved and got a color (blue) that HE chose NOT ME! Had I been able to chose the under color I would've chosen a nude color because I DID NOT want solid blue nails, if I had wanted blue polish I would have chosen blue when I was asked about a color. They're excuse for the wait was that "it's tax time right now so we busy", how about y'all are usually busy so you were just under staffed for a Saturday. I am very unhappy with the service I received today as well as the way my bails turned out. Once I got home I notice a gap between where the acrylic is and where my cuticle is at my nail bed. I can honestly say that I will no longer be doing business here and will no longer tell anyone about this place.
Ordered a St. Louis and it had hardly any meat. Taste was okay but mainly cheese and pizza sause. Atmosphere okay.
Lots of cool stuff here and employees that are eager to help you find what you're looking for, even if you don't know what that is walking into the store.

These guys have done a nice job for a corner coffee shop in the growing part of nashville. They are offering a large selection of homemade bakery items made in house. They also have some of the modern coffee brewing techniques in place.

I have to say that the pastries were a little bit too heavy and overdone for my tastes. I could've done with some simple muffin options instead of the chocolate caramel frosted muffins. I did try the sweet potato donut and it was a bit dense and dry and would have been better without the granular sugar coating.

I don't know what the hypes about, this reminds me of the story of the emperors new clothes. Yes the place is crowded but I think it's more of a place for USF students and teens to hang out at one of the four tables. The ice cream or ice milk is so bland, I thought that is tasted like frozen milk. The toppings were cute but that's it. We had Thai tea and lychee and also the banana chocolate combo. Both were sub par. I'm sure their successful but I've had better ice cream at Jeremiahs, DQ and cold stone which has a similar concept. The show is Hibachi style, lots of chopping but at the end of the show it leaves you empty. I wish that I could give a better review, but..... I gave them 2 stars, one for being able to creating the system and not mixing up orders and another star for creating the hype with great marketing. I give that student an A.

Second time visiting so we knew there could be a bit of a wait to be seated (you're in for an hour wait after 10 and 10:30 am).  Hostess was friendly.  Food is good.

Our biggest issues centered around service and wait time once you are seated.  It took our server 15 minutes to acknowledge us. It is fine if you're busy managing tables, but just say "I'll be with you shortly." Once our food was delivered our server disappeared and never really checked in to see if we needed extra napkins or condiments. We actually had to stop our server while they were attending to other guests to ask for condiments and then had to ask two other folks and after 10 minutes (and cold eggs)...we finally got what we asked for.  We get it. You're busy...so maybe the solution falls with management identifying more help/servers or finding ways to help the servers as they wear a lot of hats including serving as their own bartender.  Our server was not rude, but clearly not able to be attentive and that ruined our experience. 

Food is very good, BUT there are other places that have good food AND good service. We like this place, but something needs to change with the service. Giving 3 stars was generous because the food was good.

I had great times at old Pravda and liked that joint.  This new incarnation has nothing in common with the bar formerly known as Pravda and I don't like it.  Another fancy cocktail bar that ignores the needs of beer drinkers with a very limited selection of overpriced beers.  There is nothing Russian or Slavic about this joint so the name makes absolutely no sense.  I have absolutely no reason to return to this establishment.

Do not come here if you want a salad. It seemed like no one there knew how to actually do the salads. They put way too much cheese and dressing on my salad and we're really slow at making the whole thing. I had to ask multiple times for cheese and dressing (maybe that's why I got a lot).

I have a couple different types of insurance. I remember what it was advantage care care club and now this. In 16 years I have never received a bill until this month greatly disappointed will be taking my business elsewhere.

Ive going to go ahead and be "that" person, but I am not a fan. my rolls were mushy and fell apart and my sushi rice was dry. Maybe it was just a bad day, but I will not be back. And it is verrrrrry expensive!

Fun short trip to view port, battlefield, and city. 
Can be crowded so get in line early.

So the manager was the one who messed up my hair.. a simple comb over with a hard part fade.. fairly common.. before we started I told her not t9 disconnect my ledge cause that's where my hair rests and what does this woman do?? Took the whole damn thing down.. now I'm buzzed on both sides and the long top.. looking like some kinda country ass bumkin with a rebel flag waiving on my truck as u drink bud light driving down the road.... really really not happy with this one.. I don't think I'll ever let that woman touch my head again and honestly I doubt I'll ever go back.. 
What pisses me off the most is the first time I went Becky cut me up and it was proper and clean.... this woman... messed me up on Valentine's day.. and I have a date tonight... Thanks, thanks alot.... #neveragain #trustno1 #wontbeback
went in there and the guy sitting behind the counter was more interested in what he was doing on the computer than talking to me. extremely small selection of toys. and the store itself is small.

My first and last experience at Zoe's kitchen.

It was my first time at Zoe's Kitchen I ordered the steak stack and fresh seasonal fruit as a side. When I received my steak stack a paisley quater sized mold patch was on the bread. Not only did the bread have mold on it but my side was pasta salad and not seasonal fruit because they we out and didnt tell me. I took the steak stack back and told them it was mold. The manager can over and confirmed that the patch was mold on ny bread. The sad part is the samwhich was grilled so in my mind there is no way you could not have seen the mold. They brought out another steak stack with seasonal fruit at the side even though they claimed they ran out. The manager gave me a free entrée. I feel like my whole meal should have been refunded. I thought it was supposed to be fresh Mediterranean not molditerranean. I wouldn't eat there again if my life depended on it.

They completely burnt my burger and still served it to me.  One side was completely charred to a crisp.  There is no way the cook didn't noticed but they still decided to serve it.  Completely unacceptable.

I've been here several times and usually pretty good.
Today not so much. The food took forever and we where Told several bad excuses. When it did come out one Order was missing. A kids chicken fingers took over 20 minutes to correct. We asked for silverware several times and I finally had to run to the bar to get myself. We asked for something as simple as ketchup and again had to get up and get our own. Not say I won't be back but come on guys do better

This place was highly recommended by our hotel - I think he was sending everyone here, but like most of Philly, it was overpriced, and the food was OKAY - not that great.  I don't know... I think we would have fared better heading to the Reading terminal and eating a la carte for cheaper.  Anyway, it's okay.

My mom and I stopped here for dinner back in December 2010.  We never made it in.  There was a line at the door, and everyone in line was murmuring whether or not there was a host/hostess, sign-in sheet, or if it was a "seat yourself" situation, until the hostess finally presented herself after 10 minutes of waiting.  The hostess was an older woman - nothing special about her; she was wearing a plain long-sleeved white shirt and pants that looked like the equivalent of grey yoga/workout pants.  The reason I mention this is because it was like she was judging those waiting in line by their appearance, and helping only those whom she thought deserving of help, while she wasn't exactly your ideal looking hostess.  She seated a party of 3 at the bar, then 2 very obviously drunk girls who got in front of us at an outside table that still had dishes on it from previous customers, took one look at me and my mom, then not-at-all-discreetly asked the couple behind us if she could get them something to drink while they waited.

My mom and I looked at each other, didn't think we were underdressed, and we both thought it was absurd how we just got passed over.  We left immediately and went to Palazzio's, where we were greeted right away, had excellent customer service and fantastic food.

Brio's in Frontenac is pretty good.  Their food's a little pricey, the inside's a bit pretentious (it is Frontenac / "Lah-due" after all), but the patio saves the day.

The restaurant evokes images of a palatial Tuscan villa (an oxymoron if ever there was one), but they've definitely toned down the Italian flavor for the American palate once you survey the menu.  When you enter they give you the option of dining in the wide, airy (unusually noisy) dining room that's partitioned off by chest high dividers creating various walkways, the bar area (it's a round island style bar), or if the weather's nice the patio.  Go for the patio, people, go for it!

The patio's much more laid back, quieter and infinitely more enjoyable than the main dining room, which gets a little more than chaotic when they get really busy.  Once seated a bread basket containing flatbreads and warm Sourdough bread will be delivered to the table before taking your order.  The menu is what you might expect for Americanized-Italian: bruschettas and flatbreads abound, classic chopped and Caesar salads are available (as well as some more exotic ones), and pastas, risottos, "chicken under a brick" and paninis are all readily available.  Heavy emphasis on the pasta combos.  And the portion sizes?  Ahem... generous, to say the least.

I usually opt to split a salad since half portions of anything are strictly verboten.  My first choice was a safe one: the Romano crusted chicken salad, then I graduated to the Tuscan harvest salad, the Tricolor and the strawberry balsamic chicken.  Of those salads, I generally favor the Tuscan one since it has all kinds of goodies packed into it: mushrooms, bacon, almonds, apples and cranberries in addition to the chicken.  The salads are good - but really, it takes a special restaurant to really screw up a salad, doesn't it?  And the prices for everything match the portions pretty well - on the larger side.

Brio's is certainly a good choice (especially if you've got people to impress or pickier eaters), but it's not at the top of my list of restaurants to frequent.

In St. Louis, so I have to try St. Louis style pizza.  The ads all claim best pizza in the city for 25+ years.

The inside was sort of dumpy, whatever.  How did it taste?  Well, it wasn't nasty, but it reminds me of something a kid might make.  Sort of like if you took a bunch of crackers and melted whatever cheese was left in the fridge.  If you like it that's fine.  It just wasn't for me.

First the appointment I made was not for the correct office.  I had specifically told the person I wanted the appointment for the location on Shadeland Avenue and she made it for E Washington St.

Second when I had met with Angie I had specifically told her that I did not have much money and only had so much money to spend on my card.

On my second appointment with her she tried to sell me Hearing aids up close to $9000. Not only that she tried this sells tactic where she read off words but covered her mouth when she was doing it. Anyone with a brain would see this for what it was. Now I don't know about anyone else but when people speak to me they don't cover their mouth when they are speaking to me! Not only that she changed the volume of her voice so I would miss the words she was saying.  

She was trying to prove a point but I am not stupid! When I confronted on this she made up this excuse that people can read lips.  She was trying to prove that if I bought these $9000. hearing aids my hearing would improve by 50%.  She was comparing them to the hearing aids I have vs the expensive ones she was trying to sell me. She told me that with my current hearing aids I was only hearing at 55% and with the new ones I could hear at 80%.  Her math was off as well. That was only a 25% difference with a very faulty test. Not only that I would think the new hearing aids would do a better job than that especially at $9000.

I have suggestion. Stop treating people like they are stupid idiots and don't do this faulty test. As far as I am concerned her credibility went right out the window. I am sure that was a sales tactic that she was taught by them but they need to reconsider what they are doing and how they are trying to defraud their potential customers.  There are a lot of companies out there and I have worked with several and not even once did any of them pull this kind of stupid stunt!

I've eaten at Land of Smile a bunch of times, and have mixed feelings about it. On the one hand, it's really convenient for me and I appreciate having a different option when I'm coming home to UCity from the Creve Coeur area...which I do a lot. I've got tons of choices if I want Chinese or a sandwich, but sometimes you just want something different.

I don't really think their food is the best Thai around, it is good enough to keep me going back. I typically order the Kee-Mao Noodles (minus the green pepper and bamboo shoots). It's a flat wide noodle that with a good soy based sauce. If you like Pad Thai, you should try it. 

I have no idea how busy they get during normal lunch and dinner hours. I typically end up there fairly late grabbing take out. They are almost always empty with just 1 or 2 tables filled which always makes me wonder. I've also never seen anyone Asain eating here, and I think it suggests the food isn't as authentic as it could be.

I did drag my whole family there once and asked the waiter to help us figure out what to order as I was the only one who had ever eaten Thai before. He helped us pick out about 10 entrees to share. Everyone was happy with the end results and I had people who love spicy and who hate spicy. 

The thing that I like the best about Land of Smile is the man who is there every time I go. I think he is an owner, but not positive. I can go in every 6 months and he still remembers what I like to order. He is friendly and never makes me feel guilty for not coming in very often while still treating me like I'm a regular.
Food really wasn't that great --eat before the steamboat ride and go with the cheaper price. The jazz band was great!

I'm a big fan of Moe's and have always had good experiences at the other locations. We were recently shopping at the new outlets and decided to grab some Moe's for lunch. Sadly, I was shocked at how different this place was compared to the other ones I've been to. The place was filthy. The tables were all dirty. There was old food on the floors under the tables too. I had to wipe down my seat before eating. My husband and I both were shocked that none of the employees were even paying attention to this. The place wasn't crowded at all so its not like they were working on orders. I hope they get their act together. In the meantime, I'll be giving my business to the Moorestown location from now on.

So, we used to go to this place a lot when it first opened. I'm sorry to say that this place has really gone down hill and I don't think we'll be back. The place needs a deep cleaning. The staff is always looking unsure of themselves and overworked. It's a shame.
Had this a few months ago. I'm a sushi lover and I know good and bad quality. This is not good quality. Just a visual inspection will tell you this and smell/taste will verify your initial finding.

I stopped by here tonight for a drink with a friend, and although there were people at tables with entrees they had clearly just been served and almost every bar stool was filled, we were told they were closed. Not even, "hi, we're closed", just "we're closed". Very disappointing.

The space was nice, the service was impeccable and most every course was acceptable... but it was all rather forgettable.  Decent Italian-inspired food is rather ubiquitous these days and, besides the onion crepe, nothing really transcended beyond that.  The seafood dishes were particularly uninspired.  The pasta, albeit as delicious as homemade pasta generally is, was not very creative in my opinion.  Of course, I am holding the food to a high standard because of the price point... the correlation in this regard is not sufficient for me to want to return. 

One aside- I did not expect Mr Vetri to personally greet each diner as he walked in and through the dining room to the kitchen that day, but would have hoped for more than what seemed like a quick scan of  how business was doing.

On the short list for most depressing place on Earth along with North Korea and the Eagles' Super Bowl trophy case.  Run down (ripped booths, mismatched chairs) and seemed kinda dirty.  But, if like Rob E. you get that pregnant lady craving for deep dish...Pizza Hut is still pretty good.  And cheap.  Servers were nice enough; I actually feel bad they have to work in such a sad environment.

There's A LOT on the menu, all pretty basic grill food, priced right for a neighborhood joint, but one thing worth mentioning is the Chicken Souvlaki.  A great garlicy tzatziki sauce and freshly grilled chicken with veggies in a very nice pita wrap.  It's a great (and healthy!) lunch or dinner.  Our friends (another couple in the neighborhood) recommended this and we've been hooked ever since.  Steve (the owner) is a nice guy, too.  I wish they offered more Greek food because THAT's what they really do well.  If this place focused on simply varieties of pita sandwiches and a single ethnic theme they would do phenomenal business.  Sometimes too much "choice" is not a good thing.  Would give more stars if there were more items of Greek origin.
Small store packed to the brim with items, the guys at the counter were friendly and very helpful. I have only used their key cutting service, fast and inexpensive.

New renovations = upgrade and with sister-site, Blackbird, next-door it looks like the two could do well in the town known for BYOs.

The pot pie was on special and was one of the dishes I liked best when WSG first opened up shop. The pot pie, now less creamy and more on the sloppy side, also had a smaller portion of puff pastry so I couldn't soak up the sauce. The veggies were fresh but overall it didn't hit the same as the first time I tried this plate. We brought wine but waited for over 40 minutes for the server to pour us a glass.

The snickers bar style dessert is no longer on the menu but on they did make one special for us (!!).

All in all: 3.5 stars

Terrible. A weeknight dinner marred by an overtly rude bartender, subpar food and staff who couldn't keep up with the flow. This place is in our neighborhood but it is not worth a visit. The steak, ordered per the server's suggestion medium rare, was brought out 15 minutes after all other food with apologies about it being overlooked. Indeed, meager in size, it was well done. If you can't cook something as intended, do not bring it out. And certainly do not charge for it. How can a joint that's all about pub food screw up the fries? A crying shame.

1st off I waited for this restaurant to open because of the good reviews the original had. 
First thing I was raised in AZ. and have had Mexican and Spanish food all my life, and I also consider myself a good judge ON the different styles of Mexican food.  Our 1st visit was just plain horrible, I had a chili relleno with no cheese in the chili and my wife had carnitas
 which had no flavor at all, but I chalked this up to new store opening etc.
  Today my wife Scheduled a party of 20 gals for lunch, I say SCHEDULED because they waited 40min just to get their orders taken and then received cold food.
TO MANY GOOD MEXICAN FOOD RESTAURANTS IN THIS AREA TO WASTE YOUR TIME HERE
.

We stayed at Royal Sonesta and ate at Desire several times through the years. Had favorites    Their stuffed oysters, BBQ oysters and my wife and friends are obsessed with their baked potato. Just a baked potato. But they loved it.   We just returned to NOLA and NONE of our favorites on the menu.  Baffling since they still serve oysters. Even fry oysters for PO boys   So tossing them in BBQ sauce not a huge cost or stretch   And baked potatoes are perhaps one of the cheapest things to have and cook.    Hate it when you return to a favorite place and the cheaper faster monster has turned something special into something McToursty    Will seek elsewhere 
Bummer

I hired them to inspect my current house before I purchased it. They didn't catch anything. I had a furnace that leaked water. I ended up having to spend close to $1000 for a chimney liner to fix that problem. The central air was improperly installed. They didn't notice that the front door was too small for the house and  I had many other issues. Do not use!

wow what a disgrace that such an establishment that had the best oysters in town changed the menu up.   the new grilled oysters are horrible.   as well as they got rid of the garlic hot sauce which was a hood look and taste for the food.  i guess with new mgmt comes nwe things but if it aint broke don't  fix it. smfh.  never again.

Slow Hand is in a cool industrial building across from Cummins Station. It's worth a visit if you're in the area, but it's not the best coffee nor the largest selection among coffee shops in Nashville! They do, however, have a variety of food options for both breakfast and lunch. I might stop in for breakfast if I'm in the area as the food menu looked pretty good! However, after my second coffee-only experience, I wasn't that impressed!

Why 2 stars? Let's look at the three S's.

One: smoke. If there is no smoke in the meat, the BBQ needs work. There was no smoke in this meat. There was some flavor, but overall the meat was pretty bland. Might as well have cooked it in the oven.

Two: sauce.  The sauce on the meat and on the side was entirely too sweet. Sweet sauce dilutes smoky flavor to pulled pork. What Edley's needs to offer on the side (or on the sandwich) is a hot sauce. Besides, let's be honest, BBQ sauce was made to enhance bad meat. If the pork is really good, then the sauce is really not needed.

Three: sides. Baked beans are to BBQ as French fries are to a burger. Here, the beans were a bit odd. They almost had a Tabasco foundation to them, and that's a nifty idea, but it didn't seem to work. 

Two stars.

not even happy with this place. here from out of town.  came to see our team play. stupid white girl hostess gave. my table to another party because since we were all black she thought they were with me. REALLY!! so after three loud complaints and LOTS of B.S our game 30 minutes later is on . not happy at all. i will not be back nor will i EVER reccommend:-(

Global Quesadilla. Bright red walls, sassy t-shirts, steady food. I like it here. I've come here with a big group before a few Arch Rival Roller Girls matches and they haven't disappointed yet. For less than ten bucks, you can get an obscenely large quesadilla or an obscenely large plate of nachos. They even throw in a handful of shredded lettuce just in case you need a vegetable side. Service has always been friendly and relatively competent. The concept may slightly outperform the execution, but hey -- other than tortilla and cheese, what more could you ask for?

I have patronized the Paradise since it was converted from a family owned Mexican restaurant in the early 80s. The Paradise has great history. See the mural of Leo Carrillo on the big wall shared with the bar. The ambience is special. The smell of burning oak fills the air around the restaurant. I have eaten dozens of meals there. I liked to take out of towners there because the menu offers something for everyone whether for lunch or dinner and sitting outside in the open air or inside surrounded by the paintings of the local landscape feels so Santa Barbara. But I recently had the salmon dinner. it was not worth the $25 being charged. It reeked of fish smell. Just a bad piece of fish that the Chef, if not the owner, should not have allowed to leave the kitchen. I was very disappointed. It rested on top of mashed potatoes that revealed more potato flakes than the real thing. Paradise, please recommit to quality. I want to support your establishment.
Live less than 3 miles away an they don't deliver. Guess I will drive to penthouse then. Your chain has lost my business forever

Went in a few days ago with co-workers. We've been there before so I was trying to get what I ordered the first time. When asked about nachos selection the waitress informed me that they were the same. My order came out and it was not close to being the same as nachos piled high. When she was told that I didn't want those, she informed me that she told me the difference when I ordered. Thank you to Sammie our waitress who didn't offer to take them back or ask if I wanted to speak to a manager in case she felt like she was going to get in trouble.  I'm pretty easy to please. A flat nacho with cheese & nachos stacked are definitely different. She did offer me lettuce for my flat nachos. They were less than tasty.That day Sammie saved the kitchen from firing a new order, but she also saved your business from a repeat customer. Please re-train your employees on the menu so this mishap won't happen again. Goodbye forever.

I was not impressed . I ordered take out, I arrived and there sat my order on a table not under a heat lamp .. myself being In the industry I would thing one would keep orders warm but mines sat on a table cold  as I took it and paid for it yeah I'm sure I wouldn't be coming back!!!!

The food was pretty good, but the service was terrible.

My girlfriend and I arrived as the evening rush was ending, so we understood that we might not get served right away.  We ordered beers and the blackened alligator appetizer.  She ordered a fried shrimp salad; red beans and rice (comes with sausage) for me.

Our server was pleasant but a little air-headed.  Or maybe she didn't care.  She brought out the appetizer and my girlfriend's entree salad at the same time -- about five minutes passed, we asked for forks, and she eventually brought them by.  So I waited and waited for my food to show up, thinking surely it must be coming soon.  After half an hour we asked where the red beans and rice were, and she said they were on their way.  I finally got my food a full 40 minutes after my date, with only a halfhearted "sorry about that" under our server's breath.  I think she forgot about my food.

This hasn't happened to me before, and I'll bet it's a fluke.  But it's a pretty bad fluke.  Mistakes happen, but we felt downright ignored.  The shrimp salad was just okay, the red beans and rice (and sausage) were very tasty, and the blackened gator was good.  Would have tasted better if they'd come out within 30 minutes of each other.

Ride on the boat was kind of boring. Nothing much to see other than an industrial port. Went for the jazz brunch but didn't hear any jazz.  The band doesn't start playing until the second half of the ride... When the boat has gone as far as its going and turns around to head home. They don't play as you enter... As you're eating... Or as you're leaving. If you sit outside to take in the scenery... You can't hear the band anyway.  The food is ok.  It's cafeteria serving line style.  Will say that the jambalaya I had on board was the best I had all trip.

I was so disappointed in this restaurant! It has certainly lost the family friendly feel! It is definitely no Louie's! The feel now is much more of a bar- and certainly not upscale or nice. The music in the bar is so loud I couldn't even have a loud conversation with my friend- it turned into yelling and even then was hard to hear. The music went from Dolly Parton to heavy metal- back and forth like that all night. The center grove game was on and people were forced to use their phones to watch because they didn't have it on the tvs. Every once in a while the manager or owner not sure which would come and yell the score to people. Strange.  All the tvs were on one channel only.  It was overall a disappointing experience and I won't be back.

Didn't get in from the airport til about midnight. Food was decent, especially for late night. Fried seemed over cooked. Maybe that's a brutish thing? Philly steak sandwich was good but not much cheese.

Enjoyed the food.  Kinda pricey tho.  Loved the buns, the fried pickles were exceptional.  The tea was very good.  The fries ok, dont care for the small stringy fries tho.  Good ambiance, fast, friendly service, clean.

They babysit you at the yogurt, I hate that. I came with my husband and mother who is in her mid sixties. If you would think of people to cause a ruckus two fat people and a geriatric are not it. They continued babysitting after my mother got her cup. The flavors were either too over powering or too bland when compared to other yogurt places.

If the yogurt was better and I time traveled to high school maybe I would understand the babysitting. There are too many other yogurt places you can go to that don't do that.

This review will be a bit jaded.  We were there during a PAC 12 playoff game, Friday night, plenty of Wildcat patrons.  That being said , we had splendid service from JR!  You would have thought we were at the Rtiz Carlton, he was so attentive and informative.  My husband thinks the menu is delicious, my thoughts are that the food is better than average for bar food.  Environment is super clean, especially for a neighborhood bar.  Drink prices reflect that!  Great atmosphere!

All of my friends rave about this place. I called to place an order and was treated so rude by the older woman on the phone. Believe me when you read the reviews of their rudeness!!! I will NEVER shop here again or recommend this establishment to anyone, there are plenty of places in town with better food and friendlier owners and staff!!!

We stopped in here 3 different times.  My husband had a Bloody Mary and really liked it.  On another visit we had a Po Boy.  It was ok, not great, not bad.  The third time we stopped to get a drink and the bartender was one of rudest people I have run into.  There were several people at the bar but only one bartender.  We had been sitting waiting for a while when the bartender asked someone who had just walked in what they wanted.  I said something and the bartender came over, insulted me, and was extremely hostile.  I realize he was busy, and may not have known I was there first but that was no excuse for his behavior.  If you can't be civil then maybe you shouldn't be in a customer service job

Given the recent flooding and fire activity we decided to try a maid service. We landed on Nancy's given the other reviews.

It has been a couple of weeks since the service and we thought enough time had passed for an honest review.

The service was at best average.

We paid/scheduled a deep clean with stove cleaning. The stove cleaning was missed (as in we paid for it but they didn't do it) and several spots were missed as dust bunnies can be spotted behind the bed, dresser and behind the foot locker. Dusting under the beds would have also been missed if my wife didn't check before the team left our home.

To be fair when told about the misses Nancy and team wanted to return to the home to make it better/right but in our view there should not have been these misses to begin with. We declined the offer.

Our advice if you decide to use this service would be to double check behind the team to ensure all spots are clean and double check (or suggest/provide) the cleaning products used to ensure your home has that 'bleach' smell we all know and love.

This place used to be good. I have been going here since I was in high school when it opened. I recently returned after a number of years out of the area. I am very disappointed with the decline in service and cleanliness. If Rich is still the owner, he really needs to get the place back on track.

Service takes forever, the bathrooms are DISGUSTING, huge trash cans are dragged through the cafe during service and coffee, while good, just does not justify going there.
Their famous crab fries were interesting. I wouldn't say they're my new favorite food, but it's definitely worth a try (especially if you're new to the city).
Ordered the pizza to go was ready when promised but seems like they forgot the tomato sauce or something not up to the standards of other BJ's I've been too
$20 a person for dinner. No discounts for children. Not going to spend $40 for two small children to eat.  If going as a special couples night out then its okay but not a great deal

The service here was slow and a giant disappointment! I will never go here again and they even over charged me. Definitely disappointed as there was barely anyone there and the waitress was to incompetent to correctly take my order. The only bright side was nothing DO NOT go here. This place definitely deserves negative stars... please close down.

Please never shop here. Ya the clothes are cute but the quality is shit. Trust me. It is worse than forever 21 and they charge you $100 for a plain white shirt that is going to fall apart in a day. I bought a pair of heels from this LF in Santa Barbara last week for $200 for a prom. I felt so guilty about spending that kind of money but I knew I would get good use out of them so did it. During my night at prom I was literally walking in grass and the strap broke off. Since I had been wearing them the whole night they had a little bit of scruff on the top but regardless a pair of $200 shoes shouldn't just break that easily. When I went to get my money back or at least exchange them the workers were very rude and unprofessional and said they were "too beat up." Ya! Maybe because the strap is ripped off! $200 in the drain. My friends used to shop here and I used to shop here a lot as well but I can assure you that myself and anybody I know will not step foot in this place ever again.

After hearing people swear up and down about this place me and a friend were stoked to head over for some great wings, a few apps and some full stomachs. 

However, as we pulled into the little dives parking lot we were concerned when the ambiance looked more like a garage, rather than a restaurant The decor was very "fighter pilot" style, which being in the service I appreciated and further impressed me.

The wings and apps though seem to have the same "good enough for government  work" sense of taste and presentation. 

Typical place for you and the guys to suck back ALOT of beer and then eat some mediocre wings.  Not recommended for sober dining.

Who doesn't love DD??? When this location opened, I was so excited!! Well, apparently there are and were many people who share my feelings. Almost every time I go, they are slammed.  Good for business but unfortunate for customers. 
Last Sunday, we arrived around 9 and stood in line for a little bit. I'm not sure why seeing that they have so many employees.  
My visit before my last, they had run out of several of the munchkins. I just had them fill my 25 count with whatever they still had left. There was not an apology or we will have a new batch in ____. 
On several other occasions, they have run out of onion bagels.
As far as the parking lot goes.....it can get a little tricky when trying to position yourself for the drive-thru. I'm sure if you have been to this location, you know what I am talking about. 

Yes, I will return because of the location.

I ordered the fish tacos (tilapia) for lunch for $12. The menu didn't indicate whether they were corn tortillas or flour, nor did it indicate how many. I assumed 3, since tha's what most places do. Nope. 2 smallish tacos, flour tortillas, very bland, with guac and salsa. They didn't even include lime wedges. So not only was the service slow (1pm), but the portion size was small, and the taste was, as I said, bland. Would not order again, especially at $6/taco!!!! Not even any rice or beans to go with it.

They deliver! Which is awesome but we ate there tonight and I was not impressed. I have had their tenderloin and smothered chicken and loved them both but tonight I wanted something a bit healthier and went for the Cajun pork chops, steamed veggies and chicken and dumplings. The pork chop was so over cooked I could only take two bites and the steamed veggies while delicous were smothered in butter which defeats the purpose right? The waitstaff was wonderful and perfect with their service but I was so let down by the food. Oh and the salad bar they brag about was small in variety and just 1 bowl of iceberg bagged salad. Moral of the story; if you want something fried and delicious go to dales it's never bad but don't go for more.

I usually find really good restaurants on Yelp, and to be honest I was iffy about this one, but I was tired, hungry, and definitely wanted to visit Fauburgh Marigny, so we gave it a try. 

I do think a two and a half rating is most appropriate, so i will round up because rounding down I feel would be mean, and there are a few things I like about the Praline Connection. They are:

+Location on Frenchmen St. in the Fauburgh Marigny. 
+Old charming upbeat atmosphere. 
+Nice waiters and waitress. 
+Service was decent, unlike most yelpers mentioned, they were not slow at all. 
+Crawfish ettoufee appetizer was small, yet delicious.
+Bread pudding was good

Here is what I didn't love:
-Fried chicken- way overrated. 
-Catfish- bland and barely a taste. 
-Cornbread- hard and tasteless

I would never return.

I went in today on 1/31/13 to retrieve a history of my license information and tickets for $17.00 Prior to this I told the lady that I received notice in the mail today that my license would be suspended on 3/08/13. Well after i gave them the $17.00 I received 3 pieces of paper with the information I already got in the mail. When I told them again that this is the same information i got in the mail today, they became hostile and very unprofessional. I asked for my money back and THEY REFUSED! One lady claiming that "she had to pay for it" and employee? I WILL NEVER RECOMMEND THIS PLACE TO ANYONE, IF I EVER HEAR OF SOMEONE GOING THERE I WILL FOREWARN THEM THAT THEY ARE CROOKS AND WILL ROB YOU WITH A PEN!!

I went to this restaurant as part of a large group and I was impressed with the beautiful patio setting.  The outdoor courtyard was decorated with fountains and had such a serene and romantic atmosphere.  The servers were attentive and provided us with umbrella shade when the sun hit us straight on.

We went straight for the entrees so I can't comment on anything else but what I had.   On that note, I would skip on the food entirely.  I really enjoyed the bread because of the garlic parsely spread that accompanied it but that's about it.  It's pretty sad if the bread turns out to be the best part of the meal.  I also had a sangria, which met the sangria standard.  But my entree proved to be disappointing.  Argentinian food to me means usually means steaks and beef, so I order the chimichurri steak.  When it arrived, it was tough and difficult to cut and swallow.  The chimichurri was so bland it tasted like oil with green leaves in it.  

I was saddened by the meal and it left me wanting more.

Their Mac n cheese sucks because it's not
Mac n cheese and they didn't warn me. It has meat in it and doesn't taste anything like real Mac n cheese. I recommend Annie's if you want anything good

Just wanted to add--someone from the restaurant sent me a message saying he wanted to speak to me about my experience. Great! I'm always happy to see business owners/managers who use Yelp to speak with customers and improve the experience, rather than just claim that it's bringing down their business.

Anyway, he gave a time that he would call, which wasn't convenient for me, but I finagled a way at work to be available for the call at that time. The call never came. It was very disappointing. I would have given the restaurant another chance if he had followed through.

I like this place. Food is good, although slow. Expansive menu. Great beer selection, and the outside patio is a fun place in the summer. Not inexpensive, but not crazy. Overall a fun place for a night out.

Sat at the bar during happy hour for the $1 oysters. The restaurant was not busy at all--probably had 5 small groups dining here at the time. The bartender took her time with everything: bringing a menu, taking our order, making our drink, bringing our food, etc. The dozen raw oysters we ordered took a long time to get to us, as there was only 1 guy shucking oysters during happy hour. The oysters were not fresh compared to Felix's, so I would definitely pay the extra money to go to Felix's instead. On top of that, the crackers they serve with the oysters were stale!

Definitely would recommend paying the extra money for fresher oysters elsewhere.
Go back do the dragons den!! This boston pizza should be revamped. Overcooked food, dirty and discussing.
Tasty little burgers you can eat by the sackful!  What's not to love?

So want to like this place and support it, but the last time I was there, water poured out of a bottle of ketchup.  When we told the waitress, she just put it back with the other bottles.  Never again.
Standard cheap Italian in St. Louis. Nothing special but decent.   Recommended: pizza, carbonara, pasta con broccoli and chicken flamingo

Brunch- you can find better ones in the city that serves better brunch than this one. 

Dinner-it was ok. but not memorable...

TRY:
turkey burger

SERVICE:
Ok.

AMBIANCE:
the best part of the restaurant. cute decor & cozy fire place

The Good: We had such a great time when we visited Olio recently for a lovely ladies dinner. The service during our meal was impeccable. Our server was totally sweet and fun. The cocktails were brilliant. The wine list had begging for more (literally begging - they were out of our choice wine that evening). The food was outstanding and we savored every morsel. 

The Not-so-good: OLIO! I wanted to give you 4 stars but I simply cannot. Blasting Bjork with 1 1/2 hours until closing time just wasn't cool. Does that trick ever really work?

I really wanted this place to be awesome. I eat pho very often. I arrived at 11:30 and they told me that the kitchen wouldn't be ready for another 20 minutes. Okay, I had some coffee at mugshots and went back. I ordered the steak and tendon pho. It took 20 minutes to come out, and it didn't have tendon. I brought it up and they brought me out a plate of tendon. The large is very large, so I asked for a container. They had me wrap it up. The check came and it was for 2 entrees I didn't order. I brought it up and they refunded my card and charged the correct amount. All in all, it took about 1.5 hours to eat a bowl of pho. It should take 30 minutes, tops.

Do not go here if you are a vegetarian! 

Although the ambience was great for Sunday brunch--quiet, no rushed feeling, jazz music in the background--we were upset to learn that we could make no substitutions on the menu, even though we are vegetarians and only one entree did not have meat or shellfish.  The waiter refused to allow us to substitute anything so we had a ham and cheese omelet without the ham and eggs benedict and a crab cake without the crab cake.  Not very customer friendly.

Also the host made a weird remark when we entered, asking us if he could play "Shalom Aleichem" for us.  Combined with eating in a former church, this made me a bit uncomfortable.

I just got the wings, which came with a ranch dip and pickled veggies in a cup (radish, cucumber, red pepper, maybe zuchini).  The wings, for seven dollars, were a fairly decent portion and I appreciated the veggies.  A friend got the spit roasted lamb sandwich and watermelon salad and both looked pretty good.
The guys at the truck showed me the potato chips and to be honest, I would have spent a dollar fifty on it, not three, especially when other fancy lunch trucks would sell their handmade fries for three dollars. I can get chips out of the vending machine for a buck, after all, and the Porch area is office buildings and a university...

When i saw what passes for pizza I knew I should cut my losses, throw it away and find something else. Managed to eat one and one third of a slice before I gave up. This is really bad. Look at the picture. Looks like bad HS cafeteria pizza. Elios frozen pizza from the grocery store is better than this. The fact that I got it to go and it was completely cold 10 minutes later certainly didn't help. This is honestly the worst "pizza" I've ever had. 

;tldr To quote zoidberg "you pizza is bad and you should feel bad"

The hubby and I decided to stop in for a quick bite tonight. It's a Sunday night and the place wasn't busy at all. We were seated fairly quickly. The wait time to order and waiting on our drinks&food was ridiculous! Not to mention the party of 6 that came in after and was approached before us. 

We waited over 35 mins for a glass of wine, over 45 minutes for 6 oysters and a meat plate. The meat plate was depressing looking and super sloppy. The salami was poorly cut and the paper was still attached. 

This place is seriously lacking the staff and definitely needs help. We will most probably not be back...
Loved it until I tried to get a free waffle for downloading the app on a promo off the table. App didn't offer me a promo code and the waitress was ruder then my aunt at thanksgiving

The first reviews which come up when you search Nick's Old Original RB mislead me.  Perhaps these people have a larger sample of experiences to go on but having visited twice I've twice had the occasion to say "wow, this was really bad".
