When the parade was going on there was a lot of people eating outside so I figured it was good. We went during brunch which was good, who doesn't like a good brunch? The server came right over to take our drink order which was good. Then they came back for are order fine. It took close to an hour for us to receive our food. There had to be at least 10 or more staff working. To me they seemed more concerned with the people eating outside then inside. A group of people left because of not being served. Miraculously when I asked where our food was it showed up. The server told us it takes longer to cook because it is brunch? How long does it take to make eggs???? I would not go back it's a shame it has a nice setting to it but service was horrendous.

Ate at Cafe Pontalbo this evening. Blackened gator was just as our server said it would be, great. My wife had the blackened ribs and our only complaint with the ribs is they need to seperate them before they get tableside or invest in better serrated knives for the table. Entrees consisted of Grilled chicken for the wife and cajun combo for myself. All were better than expected. Heather our server was awesome!
If you are looking for linens and crystal on the tables, this is not your place. If you don't mind wood tables, paper napkins and good food for the price, this is a good choice.

I hate to do this.  My coworkers and I ate there this past Thursday.  The service was slow and horrible.  The one thing that topped it off was a burnt hamburger.  I really don't understand I've had very good service every other time I visited this place.

I keep coming back to Pho Hoa over and over again because this place offers good, simple food and fast service. I have never actually sat down and enjoyed a meal at this restaurant because I always order take-out. I have been to this restaurant enough times to not even ask for the menu when I come in, since I already know what I want. And what I want are summer rolls and chicken pho. The broth is fantastic, the meat is tender and not overcooked, rice vermicelli are perfect. Plain, simple and delicious!
On the menu there are many different kinds of pho, rice plates and vermicelli bowls. I have tried prawn, pork and vegetables vermicelli plate, and it left me stone cold. Everything was too dry, and unimpressive, and so I stick to my chicken pho.

I won't nitpick and list all of the missteps or comment with everything that went wrong. I'll just say that we waited over an hour for our entrees and they were not hot when they got to the table. I think they have some kinks to work out and then this place will be amazing. We were compensated appropriately for our lackluster dining experience. In other words, they made it right.

The place is modern, stark, "cool" and noisy, but that's what they want. The food was excellent. I had the Matzo Ball Soup (two fluffy matzo balls) and the Seared Ahi Tuna (not much to it, but good). My wife had grilled Salmon. We both had a drink. We split a desert. My wife had coffee. Everything was excellent and delicious. But here is the catch, the bill came to about $95 including tip. It was good but not that good. The portions were small. No need to worry about taking home a "doggy" bag. I would rate Jones' pricing at $$$ and a half. Definitely New York pricing.

Ordered the $50 sashimi platter.
Soy sauce didn't taste like soy sauce (was it watered down?)
Shaved white radish tasted like it was going bad.
Fish was okay but really, what is sashimi without decent soy sauce?

I'm very disappointed because I've had a better experience here before.
Next time I want sashimi, I'm going to a Japanese restaurant.

I recommend quality control!

Horrible experience with their take out / delivery service. Ordered more than 8 times and not once was the order 100%. 
Similar experience when dining in. Not to mention we got to experience the oh so chipper and happy staff, eager to fix their mistakes and happy to do so! Not. I'm done with Boston pizza I know that they don't need my business but there are so many other better options out there. Haven't been in months and have never looked back :)

Just drop in and grab a hand grenade if you've never had one. Went on a Sunday night and the bartender seemed to be on drugs or something? No greeting or anything. I had to ask if we could order. Definitely just wanted to leave after the unfriendliness.

I like it here, but it doesn't look like the ol' pueblo original and the food is okay, but does not live up to the prices compared to more authentic fare with lower prices elsewhere, ie cheap burritos at Losbetos or Nicos, or better atmosphere at La Parilla Suiza or Macayos.  If you must eat at "the oldest Mexican restaurant in Arizona" go downtown for the original, which I bump up to 4 stars.

I doubt the young lady behind the bar could be any more pretentious. It's a fucking cocktail.  Back in the day we called it hospitality.  Since when has the bartender become more important than the customer?
Sandwich cards for a free sandwich is a bit deceptive since you can only get $5.00 off. Small print should be LARGE print since most people don't read the other stuff.
2 stars for location. Zero stars for food and service. One would think with such a great location there would be a decent meal. NOPE! Don't go here. Don't waste your time or money.

The beef bulgogi taco was wonderful, involving well-marinated, tender strips of beef, shredded cabbage, and onions in a soft taco shell. The flavors melded perfectly and honestly, I could easily put away several of those. The truck has a number of sauces available--I went with gochujang, which is one of my favorite condiments in general.

I also tried a pulled pork slider. This was less successful. The pork was oddly dry and bland.

4 stars for the bulgogi taco, 2 for the slider. The service at this truck was very good--friendly and quick. They did a great job dealing with the crowds at the Mt. Airy Street Fare! I'd come back for the bulgogi taco in a heartbeat.

The cashier told me my 20% off pads or tampons had been automatically applied from my Extra Care Card, and that she could use $5 of Extra Bucks. Neither of these was applied. She did adjust the price of a box of Cheerios for me, because someone forgot to take down the promo tag from the previous week, but I'm a bit upset about the 20% off. The Extra Bucks I can use another time, but I'm not sure I'll be needing to buy more feminine hygiene products before this coupon expires.

their service is fine - nothing special. their prices are also OK. It's really the theft that worries me. I (stupidly) left an ipod-mini in my glove compartment when I sent it in for service. And...surprise! no ipod when I got my car back. ugh.
Rachael was great at the front counter. My Chicago Style Frank was room temperature and there were no napkins in the to-go bag.

I love the food at this place, I frequently get it delivered to my work (which no more than a few minutes away) on days when I forget my lunch or when I'm looking to treat myself. However, the last few times I have not been very impressed. The quesadillas that used to be bursting with fillings are not nearly as full as they used to be, which I understand how prices for food and ingredients can alter from time to time, but it is disappointing when you get used to something being so great for so long then falls short of your expectations several times afterward. 

Back to the delivery comment, The last two times I ordered it took over and hour for my food to arrive, which is also understandable from a food business. But as I was not told there would be such a long wait time when I placed my delivery I would have liked to have been given a heads up, especially since my work is just across the Clayton intersection in another plaza... really not a far distance at all. Now I know to ask how long the wait is, or just to go pick it up myself since it's cheaper that way instead.

Workers are pretty rude. They took over another restaurants location and it shows. They put no effort in to making it look their own. They don't clean the tables and floor. It's extremely cold. They aren't ever really that busy. The prices are pretty high.

HIGHS AND LOWS-had a great roast beef po-boy on FRI-sat-ate with wife-great gumbo,split combo fried oyster,fried shrimp po-boy,1/2 had 4 shrimp,1/2 had 4 oysters,oysters had shell fragments in them.Almost broke a tooth.

Wack-ass cornball waiter tried to tell me they were out of po boys. I protested but he was insistent. So we got jambalaya, mac n cheese, cornbread and collard greens. Cornbread was stale, jambalaya tasted like skanky microwaved leftovers. The mac n cheese was good. Collard greens were bland. When I was leaving, I saw them serving po boys to another table. I called my waiter out on it, and he just said "its never too late."

Disappointed in our visit to El Charro in Oro Valley. Service was mediocre at best. Our waiter was missing in action most of the time, and we had to venture to the bar to refill our own waters from a pitcher we had spotted. In addition, we had to go out of our way to ask someone sweeping the floor to please bring us more salsa. Food was decent, although I would've preferred a Chipotle taco, which would've had more flavor. Not going to return anytime soon!

While the ride for the Natchez was long (2 hours), there really wasn't too much to see. It was a really relaxing ride along the Mississippi River, and I guess checking out the steam room was pretty cool, but beyond that there was not too much.

The commentary was rather lackluster and to sum it up: here are some boats, here are the levies, here is ___. I didn't really felt inspired by the ride but it was nice to sit around on a boat on a lazy afternoon.

If you have a packed schedule, I would skip this. Otherwise, 2 hours for ~$25 is not bad.

I'm stunned at the good reviews for this place. The General Tso Chicken was horrible. It was dry and almost inedibly chewy. It was $8.95 and only came with rice. We also got the sweet and sour pork, so we spent $20 for subpar food. It almost makes me suspect of Yelp because I cannot see how anyone gave this place more than 3 stars!
Still tipsy from the night- fun little spot. Nothing over the top, but relative to the rest it's pretty solid.

This place NEVER HAS BRISKET!!!! We went for the first time to try it out. (We've gone but they never have brisket so we leave) so I decided to try it out. Heck I was starving so why not.  Turkey was not good at all, my son hated the mac n cheese, he hardly touched the turkey. I'm telling you coming from a prior TX resident. Do yourself a favor and don't try this place. What a let down.... will NEVER GO BACK!

Wasn't the best blowout I ever received by any means. Took a very long time and my hair fell flat very wuickly.

That being said, we were late due to traffic and they were nice and accomdated us. Also, with the Yelp deal we bought, the blow out was very inexpensive.

Not sure I'd return for a blow dry but perhaps for a cut or something else if I'm nearby.

this restaurant is an icon to santa barbara, i remember when Angie use to greet us and treat all the customers like family, she was awesome the life of Chase.....the food is still excellent the servers are helpful and efficient, but the new owner has no business etiquette or class, he yells at his servers in front of customers and doesnt mingle or make his customers feel like its a family restaurant. Where did this guy come from? Has he ever worked in a restaurant or run one? i dont think so!!! So sad this iconic restaurant is in the hands of such a terrible owner he is running it to the ground, it saddens me and othes who dine there regularly.
Very disappointing seem to be prepetully temporary closed. The excuse was maintained at first now what excessive heat possibly.

We went to Brio Tuscan Cafe this past Saturday for dinner with some relatives who were in town.  The place was lively and busy.  I had veal  marsala and my wife pasta with vodka sauce.  The veal was very good, the portion a nice size.  It was served with a vegetable medley and a few crispy potatoes.    The veal, according to my wife, was spicy and very good.  Others has mushroom ravioli and chicken with herbed pasta.  All though the food was decent and reasonably priced.  One observation I had was the size of the salad two of the guests had.  It was about a half a head of iceberg lettuce that seemed to be the size of a small mountain.  It looked as though it had a bit of some kind of dressing on it and some other garnish.  Nothing wrong with that, just the size was surprising.  I didn't hear any complaints from anyone and someone remarked that the coffee was very good.  All in all, an enjoyable evening.

Came with with a large group from work. It's a little pricey but you probably expect that. The food was fine. I got a pork chop and it was very good, the appetizers were so-so. Our service was great and the restaurant was very loud even for lunch on a weekday. Full disclosure; this isn't my favorite type of food to begin with so it has that going against it.  No real complaints, just wasn't very memorable.

My family and I took my mom here for her 61st birthday. It was her first time experiencing the habachi grill. Our chef was amazing and the food was delicious, which Is why this review even got 1 star, but our server was the absolute worst. We were a party of 12 and when we were seated the table did not have enough dishes or menus. Our server was very rude when we requested more. My aunt has a mushroom and shrimp food allergy but the server proceeded to give her a soup full of mushroom flakes anyway. Once the chef began preparing our food, we did not see our server again... she didn't stop by to check on our needs and even the chef was looking around for her. All of our cups were empty and in need of a refill, so I had to get up from my seat and search around for our waiter to inform her that we needed refills. She exclaimed that she had other tables to tend to... which is fine but she never returned to check on us. I'm not sure if her customer service skills sucked because she knew gratuity was included regardless if she works or not, but thanks to her we will choose a different habachi restaurant in the future. Oh, and to put the cherry on top, my husband wanted a to-go water, since we had a 1 hour drive home, and of course our server was absent after we paid, so I proceeded to ask another server near the sushi bar, and he and his coworkers laughed right in my face and told me to ask my server... at this moment I was disgusted knowing I gave these people our hard earned money. Never again.

Don't come here for football. No sound, no Sunday ticket. Food prices are way too high.  We asked for one TV volume to be turned up for the game and they wouldn't do it. They call themselves a football bar but refuse to cater to football lovers

I had an awful experience. I will never order from this Pizza Hut again. I ordered online to take advantage of the 50% off deal. As I was driving to pick up my pizza (because they don't deliver to my house 5 miles away) Christine calls me and says they don't have the crust I ordered, Italian sausage or green peppers. I have to order something else. I get there and she has prepared regular breadsticks and not cheese breadsticks like I ordered. She says whatever, she doesn't care, whatever, she's tired, she will make me some cheese breadsticks. Then, the pizza (somehow) falls on the ground. She has to start all over. And to top it all off when I get home and open the pizza box, the pizza looks all mangled. Like it's the one from the floor. But I know I saw her throw it out and I waited 15 mins for the new one. But we can't even eat it. I wish I could upload a picture of it here. Unfreakingbelievable. The breadsticks were not hot (it was a 5 min drive home). I called Christine and she did give me a full refund but we didn't get any pizza tonight. After all that. Hell, I should of just went to little Cesar's, which is right next door, to get a cheaper, faster and better tasting pizza. Corporate needs to look into this establishment ASAP.

Stopped in tonight, and shocked to find what was once my favorite bar in the Quarter, completely changed.  Apparently, it's been sold to new owners and it's awful.  Everything that made this place awesome, down to the rugs, quirky lamps, and Russian decor, has been taken out.  It now looks like your run-of-the-mill bar that could be found in Anywhere, USA.  Terrible idea to sell, and worse idea to change the bar at all.  Absolutely pitiful.  You're better off heading to Molly's on the Market, a few doors down.

Great check in service and nice salon bus but a terrible manicure by a sweet woman. 20$ for a terrible manicure is a lot to pay. Used the nail file like a saw. Not a nice nail shape and did not clean under the nails. 
Please train staff more. She is a nice woman. Nice salon but terrible mani

tried 3 weeks in a row to go @7:30. closed. door says open til 9 but i just snuck in at 7:27 and they were cleaned out. the less than pleasant woman working there said they close at 7:30 every night. and she wasn't even nice about it. won't go back.

There are upsides and downsides to this place. On one hand, they fit me in last minute, the technician was really nice, and they have lots of great deals and specials (got half price because it was my first time - and it was cheap to begin with!)

BUT... (and you knew that was coming, right?) I still don't think I'd go back for two reasons. Firstly, the technician kept tugging on my skin and there were a couple of pulls that she had to keep going back and re-adjusting her grip for. It was really uncomfortable (and I don't just mean the normal discomfort of waxing). Secondly, she was NOT thorough at all. It's kind of like a Monet painting; from far away it looks fine, but up close there are serious problems. There are a lot of hairs that were missed and I can see lots of little broken hairs that I'm sure will be visible in like 3 days. Plus, she did not follow up with tweezers, which almost every single waxer I've ever had has done - that would have fixed some of the hairs she missed. Lastly, the ambiance is very "wham bam, thank you ma'm". It's definitely not like a spa at all. No music, cold lights, they make you get undressed while they're standing right there (which honestly, I don't care about, but it's just something different than every other place I've frequented). 

The bottom line is that this place is good for a cheap wax if you get a coupon or special, but it's not a place that I would frequent.
Food and service is okay. I would say it is a better place if you do not want all that unstable food quality and hustle and bustle in Minado.

If you make appointments you should keep them.  Not sure how you call yourself a cleaning sevice if you never actually clean.  The last 4 apts have been cancelled by you because you had no cleaner avialable.   Then you tell me to book one in June...you must think I am dumb. I know you would cancel again. As Bush once said fool me once, shame on -- shame on you. Fool me -- you can't get fooled again.  Now I cancelled and not rebooking.  Find a more reliable service if you want your house to be cleaned.

CUSTOMER BEWARE! These people are UNPROFESSIONAL! There are maybe two or three stylists that are professional but the others are not. Beside the profanity my stylist was on the phone during most of my service and she didn't listen to what I wanted.

Let's get to my hair. My stylist was a girl with colored hair. I forgot her name. I gave her some bundles that I purchased the night before and asked her to color them blonde with purple tips... the next day I arrived to get it installed. I wasn't a big fan of the way the color turned out. She installed my hair and I HATED it. As I proceeded to tell her what I did NOT like about the install she replied to me saying "I know what I am doing. I went to school and I know how to cut and style" Needless to say my hair was a mess. I went to the bathroom to get a closer look only to hear her on the salon floor talking about me. 

I had to return the next day to have another stylist RE-INSTALL the hair correctly. All in all I will NEVER go back to this place. And if you see a stylist sitting in the front with colorful hair STAY AWAY!!!

Worst Mexican Meal & Service I've Had!!!

As a special 75th birthday treat I took my brother (and sister-in-law) down to Tucson for a night. I had heard great things about the El Charro Café and was pleased that there was one in north Tucson on Oracle Road. We ate there. Big mistake. BIG. MISTAKE.

The restaurant was very large but nearly empty. There were a number of servers joking around waiting for customers, but we were seated right away. I ordered a prickly pear margarita, not seeing that it was an incredible $12. It was served in a regular glass (not a margarita glass), and was very sweet, but I could barely taste any alcohol. It was so not worth the cost.

The meals arrived and my SIL stared at her "taco:" it was a rolled-up TAQUITO that was hard as a rock. We called the server over and complained and he brought a single taco in a soft shell (even though the meal was a two-taco meal). I ordered the two cheese enchiladas, and although they were all right, they were very small. There was a few spoonfuls of rice and beans to accompany it: both tasteless, and there wasn't even melted cheese on the beans: it was some sort of white crumply stuff. My brother ordered the chicken fajitas, and the chicken was so tough he had to use a knife to cut the chicken strips (which wouldn't even cut through the chicken!) and finally gave up on his birthday meal.

We told the server about our displeasure and he said he'd "tell the chef." My sister-in-law wrote a complaint on the payment slip but when he returned with the slip for her to sign, it was without the note: was it discarded? Hmm? She rewrote the complaint, paid the $70 for the terrible meal, and we left. There was no apology nor any attempt to mitigate our complaint about the meal by even a penny's discount on the bill. Customer service? I think not.

It was a terrible way to spend a special dinner meal, and ruined the entire trip experience.

I wish I could give it a negative star, but, alas, I can only rate it a '1' on Yelp.

Sadly, my review is NOT positive! I WAS a member for several years and really enjoyed my membership BUT recently fell into financial hardship due to the loss of employment. Unknown to me is that my daughter's account was a joint account with me because I signed her up before she turned 18. My daughter was worried about our financial crisis and reached out to a man online who convinced her he would help her financially. Long story short it ended up as fraudulent and both accounts were frozen and payments for my bills were sent back. Tried to work with the fraud department and they did NOT care at all! Ended up getting a loan from a different bank for $900 to pay for the fraud and closed all accounts! Chris from the fraud department said that I deserved it...didn't know about the fraud deposit until afterwards..but I deserved it.

I swear to god they have messed up my coffee 5 times in a row it's like a joke to me now lol idk WHY i dont learn my lesson. I have never had one good experience here. The customer service sucks and you can just tell that the employees don't give a crap about their jobs. It's pretty sad that this location is 5 min from my home but i will definetly be going elsewhere now.

Went for our annual Shopping and Christmas Photo Family tradition to be told we had to get a Pass of some kind and come back 3 hours later.  Bass Pro.  Just one persons opinion but I don't like your new process.   There were a lot of other people there just like me.   

Thanks for the memories we will now move on.  Gander Mountain here we come.

This place is great for beers. They have many kinds and the atmosphere is fine. They used to also have good food, but I don't know what has happened.

I used to love the brisket. People raved about it, and the brisket quesadilla was great too. What was once juicy, pulled-pork-like beef is now chunky and weird like dog food. I have to accept the fact that I can't have that good brisket ever again.

The Buffalo Chicken Wrap is inexplicably gone from the menu, as is the artichoke dip. AND the grilled chicken sandwich, which was a staple of my diet. 

Everything is now saltier, sweeter, fries are cooked too long, too much sauce on everything... It was just gross. So sad.

They had great food before, but now it feels like the menu was dabbed at the corners of Guy Fieri's mouth, with items like the "Smashy Melt" and "Nash Vegas" burger with pimento cheese. Ugh. Just be sure to eat before you get here!

We came here on the advice of a coworker who said that this place had good ribs and snoots.  Being that I had never had a snoot before I was eager to give it a go so we made our way out here for dinner one night. 

To our dismay there was a sign in the restaurant that said "No Snoots Today.  Sorry."  Despite the snoot shortage we decided to stay and give it a go.  I got the rib tip meal with a side of mac and cheese and potato salad.  The rib tips were very good but I thought there were too much sauce on it.  I would recommend getting the sauce on the side.  The mac and cheese and potato salad are pretty much your standard fare.  

All in all it was good.  I believe we are going to come back to try the snoots.  But this time we'll call ahead to make sure they are in stock.

I've only been here a few times and I usually order the fish burger with cajun fries. I wouldn't say there is anything particularly special about Gullifty's but it seems to have a cheery but loud atmosphere and attracts a lot of people, which is why it seems busy all the time. The service seems a bit slow in general. The menu has a lot of variety so that's a plus. The food is decent and well-priced, which is hard to find on the main-line sometimes. It's a nice restaurant and place to go to every now and then.
The lunch specials are a well priced consistant portion, and the singapore mei-fun noodles are pretty good as well, a pretty standard local chinese take out spot, they deliver as well which is nice.

I've been to many Cosi shops. This one, like all of the rest, is okay, but a little overpriced considering what you get. The portions are small and should cost no more than $5 or $6. However, they stroke you for more like $9. I've had better sandwiches for $3.

The one cool thing about this location is on a nice day they have lots of outdoor seating. Other than that, it doesn't have much going for it. Save your money and time.

Good price on a Tire Pressure Monitor Sensor. However, I waited 2 and a half hours for them to complete this simple job. 

Edit: Not Surprisingly, upon completion of job, I found out they installed a sensor that is not compatible with my vehicle. They had to order the correct part from the dealer and re-install proper part. 

Please re-think using this shop. Sometimes it's worth paying a bit more to get the job done right, in a reasonable time-frame, the first time. I was sucked in by the price and wish I would have stayed away.

The manager is a nice guy but unfortunately that isn't enough to keep me coming back.

I used to come here a lot, especially when i was younger. I remember coming here and always buying Cuban sandwiches, or buying something to drink with the ruffles chips. After many years i am highly disappointed, some of their food taste old, or has no flavor, such as their cakes. I can't complain about their workers, their workers aren't mean, they do interact with you, and are friendly, even if they have a serious face they always tend and cater to their guests, but the food needs to be stepped up! The only plus side that i see is that they finally opened on Saturday's.

I was excited to find a highly rated Mexican restaurant in Tucson near my hotel.  El Charro has been in business since 1922 so they must be doing something right.  

I sat at the bar and ordered a margarita while I perused the menu.  Carne seca looked interesting.  I've read about it but never tried it.  It's sun dried beef that's then shredded and cooked with onions, peppers and tomatoes; something like jerky reconstituted for tacos.  I have a rule for Mexican restaurants though which is "I will judge you by your chile relleno."  What to do?  No worries.  I found a combo with both and made my request.

Chips and salsa were fine, with a nice little kick.  When my food arrived I was puzzled.  It was the carne seca plate and not the combo I ordered.  Okay, I'll take that, rather than send it back and wait.  My server apologized and comp'd the relleno I had been expecting.  She handled the mixup perfectly well.  

Carne seca was described as a flavor explosion.  I definitely sensed a little bit of a pop.  It was flavorful and good in tacos.

My chile relleno was just okay.  Lots of cheese, not much of the crisp exterior I usually expect.  Red sauce was good.

House margaritas; Pretty light.  No buzz.  No Bueno.  

Overall, pretty good but I was disappointed with the margarita and relleno.

Went there for a simple oil change and quote on my brakes, but before I wanted to confirm that they would be done in my lunch hour... The guy said the bays are open and less than 45 for sure. Over Three hours later.... I learn that the general publics cars get bumped lower in line for these guys friends. Only time they were "really" working on my car was after I kept giving them a hard time about the wait and stood there and watched them. Then saw on the paperwork that the guy wrote I came in only a few mins before they were done. Rather not deal with shady shops. If you are their friends, they do a great job. Otherwise be prepared to wait. Use the Ventura location over this one

Third time's a charm!

Maybe the trick is to go on a Saturday. The 8th and Arch branch was as quiet as a church mouse. 

As luck would have it, the 8th and Arch manager took a shine to my case (without prompting!), apologizing ***profusely*** for the events you most assuredly read below.

Best of all, he got me my dang license in short order. 

Fun fact! Upon retrieving my file we were both amused to learn that one of the earlier clerks had the moxie to invent even more reasons as to why I should not receive a licence.

E.g., the chicken scratch citation of "He only had starter checks." 

OK. Now even if that were true (which it is not), who cares? Is this a legal or policy barrier to furnishing state identification? Or is it more of an expression of personal yearning for the fancy, personalized kind of check? You know, air brushed hearts, Franklin Mint coins, landscapes, We The People caligraphic watermarks, and the like.

Plus. My checks clearly state "Customer since 1995." I.e., not exactly starter material. 

Upshot: 
Licence procured, 300 new PennDOT friends made, and faith in public servants (mostly) restored.

Insider Tip: 
After your visit to the 8th and Arch location, get coffee or lunch around the corner at the fabulous Ray's Cafe and Teahouse on 9th St. between Cherry and Race. (See my review.)

P.S. I still hold that the Columbus Blvd. branch is a Code Red dump.

Wow! This place is dirty and has the worst service ever!   I really wanted to give this place 1 star but the food isn't bad.  

Service -0. no greeting, when the server came up to take my order he just stares at you awkwardly without a word. Hmmm okay, guess I should order now.

When they got us our food they just left it and took off without a word, no checking up on tables with extra napkins, water or tea.  They also forgot our tea we had to remind them.   No courtesy, no smiling, and of course no apology.  

Food 2- I was hungry so it didn't taste too bad but it was not great either.  

Atmosphere -0  dirty and I heard other customers were complaining that they found something in their bowl so the server took back that bowl to the kitchen.  I didn't hear anyone apologizing or offering anything for the customer.  And I found a strand of hair in my bowl as well.  The sad thing was I was almost done with my food. I didn't want to bring it up to the server because it wouldn't make any difference and they probably wouldn't do anything about it based on what I saw.
Went in, sat. No service, hospitality. Previous patron with good experience. Fan of Alan. Tonight was a non-starter. Really bad experience.

My husband and I were looking for a short-term lease for our son and after seeing the "one bedroom junior", we decided to put in an application and rent at this complex.  After we were approved, we asked them to email us a copy of the lease so that we could co-sign.  Well, they wouldn't do it and were quite nasty about it.  We live over 100 miles away and work during the day.  

The day we returned with all the required documents, we had to deal with that lady who sits in the back.  I have never met a nastier person in my life!!!  Long story short, we took our money orders (yes, money orders!!!!  Who deals in those anymore????) and walked out.  

If you pull into the parking lot of this complex, just drive around the property and exit the way you came in.
Food was mediocre, no place too sit. Noisy. Lots a kids Not worth the trip .

OMG DO NOT OPEN AN ACCOUNT HERE!

And here's why. 

Came in to open a joint checking account with my husband and the initial process was horrible. Waited 45 minutes just to meet with someone and then when we finally met with her the whole process took 90-120 minutes until it was done. Then our bank cards never arrived and they had to be re-issued. And then when those came (2 weeks later) they didn't work. We came into the branch 2 more times to work these issues out with them. They froze our account twice saying we needed to bring in proof of address (which I brought in the literal first day we opened it, like wtf guys we can't open account without a utility bill so clearly you saw it on day one). I brought it in a second time and they unfroze the account. But then a few days later my debit card was declined. I was kicked out of online banking. Came in a 4th time and they assured us everything was all fixed after the account got frozen again because they needed proof of address AGAIN, like a I
Utility bill lol! Guess what? It was not. 

Finally we come to close our account and she says the amount in our account that we will get back from what we originally deposited was not correct. They "misplaced" $200 from our savings that they can't "find". They see proof of the deposit. Don't see any withdrawal of the $200 don't see and transfer of it. We literally haven't used the account once! But yet our savings is at $0. And they CANT FIND OUR MONEY!" 

So yeah. Don't bank here. Go to a local credit union. Go to Philadelphia federal credit union. They are GREAT!

I came to Dale's with my momma after an appointment. I'd never been that I can remember (while my dad claims our family has before). We were there for breakfast on a weekday morning. There were a decent amount of people in there - mostly elderly folk enjoying each other's company. 

I really liked the industrial vibe of the place. Apparently this look is new. The reno is working for them if you ask me! Great look! 

As someone who loves breakfast, but is both gluten and dairy intolerant,  I sometimes struggle to find good options. Dale's had plenty that I could try with a little adjustment - like ordering with no cheese. I tried an egg scramble - hash browns with mushrooms, tomatoes, and sausage, topped with an egg. It was supposed to have cheese as well, but I ordered it without.

It took a little longer than we would have liked for our food to come out. I was pretty hungry, so that could have been more of a 'time is passing less slowly than it feels' thing. That's fine! The food came out, and I immediately see cheese melted on my eggs. I made sure to send it back right away, while my mom began to eat her meal. They had to completely remake the order, so it took a bit to get it back the correct way. (I did enjoy the "NO CHEESE!!!" I heard someone yell in exasperation from the kitchen at my seat by the front door.)

When I did get the corrected order, it didn't look as good as that first plate I received. There seemed to be less eggs on it. I think they were just rushing to get it out to me and it showed a bit in the presentation. Not really a huge deal. I care more about the taste! But that wasn't that great, either. I really wanted to love it, but alas... The hash browns were more of a shredded potato layer, and it was a bit limp. It would've been better with just a little bit of crisp. The eggs were good! But when they were gone, that left the potatoes with what looked an awful lot like canned mushrooms and what I honestly believe was one sausage link, sliced super thin and mixed in to the dish. 

Overall, I wasn't impressed by the food. That said, I would give Dale's another shot in the future. My expectations of the experience, though, will be fairly mediocre moving forward. Maybe someday they'll change that for me! If so, I'll update my review. :)
Enjoyed the cruise, the band was good but food is AWFUL!!! I've had better TV dinners.   Dinner cruise is definitely not worth it.  Take the cruise; save your money and buy snacks from the snack bar.

I didn't even enter. we got pre tickets to see tinashe on 07/23 and I guess one on my girls id was expired but a few days. days!!! but I get it 


and i wasn't mad we were turned away. 
i was mad I couldn't get a refund after the guy said I would be reimbursed. I had to reach out through email. and well it been two weeks and no responsed at all.

tomato basil soup, omg delicious.

bbq chicken flatbread is good when I'm eating it but then I feel sick afterwards.

My bf loves the curry chicken but I'm not a big fan of any of Cosi's sandwiches.
So here's the thing...the Famous Toastery offers only one slice of toast with their egg dishes. Really? What's the food cost on a slice of toast (unbuttered, by the way)? Nope, not for me.

This was the first stop for our group's one night in downtown Nashville. First impressions: three young bro-types immediately hit on one of our group's older women, two girls were dancing like no one was watching to the mediocre band, and it took several minutes for our large group to get the attention of one of the 3 chit chatting bartenders.

The band sucked, the energy was bad, so we ditched this place after one drink. MEH.

Staying at boutique hotels in Canada always reminds me of why I love to travel to the US. "boutique" in Canadian speak translates to "no service, steep price, untrained employees, nice fireplace and art in lobby, and an old apartment painted black and grey to pretend to be a high end hotel suite". 
Upon arrival, I pulled in front in my 4 inch heal loubitons and found no doorman. Inhad to pull my suitcase through 2 feet of  ice and snow. I was told there is usually a bellman but since the hotel was not at capacity this fine icy snowy valentine weekend, they choose not offer this service to the rest of us suckers who decided to stay here instead of the hotel Mac (which would have been a comparable price). 
The room was nothing special and very basic.  The walls were paper thin and we had the unfortunate luck of being next to a very loud guy.  It's too gross to go into details but let's just say he gave the entire floor a play by play of his partners actions.   
The breakfast was decent bur upon checkout, we sat in the lobby which is pretty from afar, but upon closer inspection, the coffee tables were scratched and dented and the sofa was the most uncomfortable contraption I've ever sat in. Come on, how much would it coat to replace the plywood tables?  Oh, almost forgot about the elevator, there wad an inside panel missing so the door from the inside wAs just plywood. I thought I entered the staff or freight elevator.  Another example of boutique hotel equals pretty on the outside, lacking on the details.  I've learned my lesson, the only boutique hotel I will ever stay at is the W hotel.

We arrived at 5:55, with a party of six. We were told it would be 35-45 min. I watched a round table sit empty the entire time we waited with a reserved sign on it. Then two four tops became empty and the hostess staff put it together and took it apart 3 separate times until I finally said somerhing, we were seated and were more than 3/4 through our meal before the round table was even sat. We could have eaten and been gone before the so called reservation for the table even arrived. The food was sub par and our server was overwhelmed. I will stick to El Taco Tote. Better food, faster and better service.

Nice Lex... Pack us in  a bunch of sardines!
Came to the Rain Out party... Total disaster. Will remember you after the burn!

not a fan...   Heard so much hype of this place.   ordered the Crabbie Fries which are froozen fries fried and old bay on them...   how does that make them world famous...   no idea.   and we ordered wings which were just a basic franks hot on them...   glad I tried them.   at least I got a picture with Lane Johnson....

If only the last review was true.  Opened acct on April 9, 2015.  As of April 30th, 
STILL no ATM card.  Unfortunately,  have a direct deposit scheduled but thankfully no payroll.  Was yelled at by the Customer Service Mgr who even gave me erroneous info about the whereabouts of my card.  He told me they were " only human"...umm,  think I would prefer an efficient machine.  So much time spent to simply have a working bank system but must mention the atmosphere in the bank is very unprofessional in other ways.  Uncomfortable there, reminds me of Mad Men...too bad there is no Chase in NV.  Now, to find another bank..

Disappointing afghany restaurant in olde city. The Ariana kabob (chicken, beef, and lamb) was served on a large platter that was predominantly rice and a few small pieces of meat. The meat wasn't good. The service was very slow. Can't think of any reason to come back here.

This casino must have the rudest Black Jack dealers in Reno, especially a fat guy (sorry but I did not get his name) from Thailand and another Asian woman, Ming. 

The Thai guy refused to deal the cards for 5 minutes awaiting a new player to join the game who was totally not ready to play.  The player kept telling him to deal the cards without him but he would just let the whole table of 6 sit there and wait.

Ming yelled at me because I scratched the table more than once when I wanted to draw a card.  The supervisor came to advise Ming that there was NO casino policy governing how many times a player might scratch the table (with the cards) when he wanted to draw a card. 

Whew, if those two(2) dealers still work for Western Village, I will never play BJ there again.  

Platinum member# 040091445

We had a great time - we went on the tour and also had a few beers. We tried the flight and then saw an employee drinking a half and half containing the Bucha and Walk the Lime. Beers on the flight were fine but nothing special. The mix was awesome though! 

We would suggest going here to hear some good music, play board games, have a cheese plate and hang out with friends.

The exhaust fan is non-existent.  You will smell like the place for days.
Food is OK, but over-priced.

One of the more affordable options in this terminal. I ordered the salad with bread all for under $10 including the extra 74 cents for butter (which I think is fake). Unfortunately half the advertised toppings are not available which was a huge bummer but the woman working the front was a great sport and willing to remove toppings or cancel the order. I went through with an awkward but sustainable salad with no complaints.

It was our first visit. The service was excellent. We had the mussels appetizer which was very good. My husband ordered the duck breast and I ordered the scallops. I was disappointed with the amount of scallops on the plate. There were 3 scallops on the plate. It was not worth the price of $24.00. I actually left still hungry.

Wow, I don't even know where to start. We decided to eat here in order to get a baked potato, but with five in our party, I guess things were too complicated. We were seated at a table that hadn't been cleaned, first of all. Two of us ordered 8oz sirloins with bleu cheese toppings, one medium, and one medium rare with salads and loaded baked potatoes. One ordered a blackened filet mignon, rare. 

After a while, someone arrived from the kitchen with two filers, black baked potatoes, and the manager right behind her with a blackened sirloin. I told them, very calmly, that I had not ordered the filet, and the one filet ordered should have been a filet, not a sirloin-very calmly and friendly, when the lady from the kitchen threw up her hands and said to the manager, "You're gonna have to deal with this!". We tried to explain, again, what the mixup was when he said the sirloin would need to be cut before he could tell If it was not a filet... Really? The manager of a restaurant that serves steaks doesn't know how to tell the difference between a sirloin and a filet?

Forget the sirloin was far more tender than both filets mistakenly given to us, and the fact the baked potatoes were overlooked and charred. 

I was totally disappointed in the lack of pride, education, and proffessionalism of the staff and management, as well as the cleanliness of the restaurant. 

I hate to give a bad review, but my goodness!  This isn't even close to normal.

This has to be the worst place for customer service around! I keep going back for that damn stuffed crust, so i can't give it up altogether. However, each time i order, it takes 2-3 times the original quoted time to actually arrive. They tell me 30-40 minutes and it's at least 2 hours EVERY TIME. I usually do pickup, but there are times that I can't. I wish someone would whip this place into shape and at least give REALISTIC wait times!

Expect very high end, the staff was unfriendly,pricey drinks,  three shots and  three beers $40, and after waiting in line for an hour and a half they gave our table away told us we had to be there by 10 we were in line at 930, and they gave our table away to clubs in Sacramento. definitely does not cater to locals, we entered the nightclub walked through the whole place was told no drinks on the dancefloor, the only way to get from point A to point B was to walk through  the dance floor we were asked twice no drinks on the dance floor and then we decided to leave. I won't be returning... not the time of my life.

Don't eat breakfast at Cafe Pontalba!

The orange juice was bad. The grits were runny, unseasoned, and flavorless. The omelets were tepid, messy, and boring. The jambalaya was uninspired and underpowered. The coffee was burnt, and served with non-dairy creamer.

I had some great breakfasts at other restaurants in New Orleans! Don't eat breakfast at Cafe Pontalba unless it's your only option.

The serviceman was very nice.  He adjusted two toilets so they would flush efficiently. I also had a new kitchen faucet I wanted to have installed. He received an estimate for the installation from the home office.  The estimated charge was $629.00.  Unbelievable! I sent him on his way......

I am so, so disappointed. I had been jonesing to try Jones for months. I had heard such great things about the famed Starr comfort food restaurant. So this morning, when I finally had a morning off to go to breakfast, I knew where I was headed! Jones got an award in my book, and I'm very sad to say it was an award for "hands down worst service I have EVER received at a restaurant". 

Let me preface this review by saying I completely respect the job servers do. They're constantly on their feet, they get paid low and rely off their tips, and I know that's pretty hard sometimes. And I always give leniency because I know this job is hard. But this service was god awful. 

Was seated immediately upon arrival and say upstairs in a cozy two person booth. So far, so good. That's when it went downhill. Didn't see our waitress for the first 15 minutes. When she finally came over (don't know her name because she never introduced herself), she took our order to start, which was for 2 bloody marys (deliciously customized),  2 coffees, and the monkey bread appetizer. 10 minutes later, beginning to get frustrated, again, she comes over with 2 waters. We were a little confused, but thought maybe it was just protocol to also get waters. But, low and behold, 2 minutes later, she comes back and snatches away our coffee cups and our menus, too quick to even stop her. Um... I needed both of those??!?! 

Next, our monkey bread appetizer and Bloody Marys arrive, and halfway through devouring the delicious bread, we finally flag her down and ask her again for coffee, and if we can possibly order. (Mind you she took our menus so this was a little tough.) She was non apologetic but politely took our orders. She came back with coffee, and spilled the creamer all over my dates shoe. (This is the one thing that's not her fault - accidents do happen.) We helped her mop it up with our napkins, and there was still creamer all over my dates shoe when she ran off with the sopped up napkins, and what do you know, she never came back with more. And she never brought us spoons to stir the coffee, so we were at a loss here. Losses, really.

So our food comes out, so yummy looking, and I don't have silverware (or a napkin). Sat there for 5 minutes, never saw our waitress and honestly knew I never would, so my date stole me a set off another table.

This whole experience was nothing short of a DISASTER.

After getting the main entrees, she never checked on us, it took forever for her to pick up or bill... Just awful awful awful. As we were leaving the couple next to us made a comment as to how awful the service was also.

It's a shame because if the service had been better, this would've been named my favorite Starr restaurant, because the food was so delicious.

The Worst- This was my "make up" cleaning from the person whom they sent the first time who was much more interested in going through my closets vs. cleaning. This time I was told I was getting one of the best and then I got a text 24 hrs prior that it was someone else. I was told that they understood that I wanted to have focus on my floors- I'm sorry a dirty swifter with floor polish is not how you clean floors, after 5 hrs and being 1 hr late- I just asked her to leave didn't even touch the kitchen, vacuum the stairs or do half the things that are on your "standard cleaning list" You also need to advise your clients if the people you are sending have a disability-

I got a bad haircut here.  Just awful.  All I wanted was a simple fade.  My "stylist" almost pulled it off, but didn't bother checking my head over very thoroughly as I later discovered numerous patches that she missed.  Conversation felt forced...the whole experience was lame.  Never again.

Just went there this evening and was pretty disappointed. Had seafood risotto that really was bland and didn't have a lot of flavor. For $20 dollars I would have expected more and also a side salad as this was my entree. Nada. My wife had a caesar salad that was had potato bits dressed as croutons and bacon pieces in it.  Huh? Interesting - no not really nor the $10 dollar price tag. We had mussels as an app as well but nothing to write home about (The Whip Tavern has the best btw). The service was slow and dinner seemed to take forever. I ordered a caesar salad because I was still hungry after the risotto and it took almost 20 mins and finally arrived when we were ready to leave. I don't know about you but for $80 dollars I just would have expected more. FYI - I was in the restaurant business for 15 years so I am a bit more understanding when service is slow but overpriced is overpriced and bland food is something I can eat at home.

After a hot day of walking around the Quarter,  following a great graduation day at Tulane,  we stopped into Cafe Pontalba to rest our feet,  to get out of the sun and to have some drinks.  The waitress asked for our drink order in this half full restaurant.   I informed her my party of 6 was "probably"  just having drinks.   She responded that she would serve us but if the restaurant got busy,  we would have to leave.  Really? I have never been treated this way in any New Orleans restaurant... And I am here every few months visiting my son.   Our drinks  were then very slow in coming.... Sigh.... I will never stop into this convenient location again.

High traffic mall location = high rent = high prices = low value = mediocre food quality.

Need I say more?!

Apps and cocktails were great, but the pasta dish was wayyy too salty.  They remade it and same issue.  Wife had the same dish and it wasn't as bad.   They took mine off the check, but no manager visit or service recovery past the waitress, who was great.  Won't be back though

I love the staff there, but each time, I've gone, the past 3 to eat the food was mediocre at best...the guac isn't good at all, chips n salsa was good, but nothing seems authentic to me.  Hopefully the food will get better, but I'm all about the service and people that work there have been great!

I came to Sears Auto Center in Santa Barbara to buy a new battery and have it installed. My car requires that power be maintained to the computer during the battery change, or else the computer resets. I called Sears and asked if they have the equipment (Memory Settings Saver) and was told that they do. I came and left my car for the battery change, and confirmed multiple times with the advisor that power will be maintained to the computer during the battery change.

When I came to pick up my car, I was surprised to notice that my automatic keys no longer opened my car (both sets of keys). I asked the tech if he kept the power to the computer during the battery change, and he said no because he was not told to do so. I asked the advisor if he told the tech to keep power to computer, and he admitted that he forgot to tell him. This was an annoyance because I had to go to my car's dealership the next day to have the computer reset, but mistakes happen and the advisor was apologetic, respectful, and admitted to forgetting to inform the tech.

However, when I asked to speak to the manager, I was appalled as I had never received such awful service anywhere, ever. When I asked if I still had to pay the $20 service fee even though the service ended up causing me more problems than I came in with, he told me yes with a smirk and walked away from me. I asked him for the name of the advisor that forgot to tell the information, or the name of the tech, and again he rudely told me "no," walked away from me with no explanation and ignored me completely. He told me there is nothing he can do and disappeared into the back room. 

I suggest that if you want to be treated with respect and decency as a customer, you avoid this Sears Auto Center location at all costs. 
I have never written a review for any business before, as it takes a lot for me to feel that service is bad. However, this horrible experience compelled me to make a Yelp account and write this review in order to save other hard-working customers wasted  money and wasted time taken off work to go to their car dealership to correct Sears' mistake. Most of all, I don't want other customers to have to be treated with such disrespect and contempt.

** A competent, respectful option in the area is FireStone Complete Auto Care on State St.

Went to Dante, sit in the chair for 40 minutes today and he did NOTHING.  He didn't care what I wanted, he was so concerned about the back of my head and around my ears.  From other people's thoughts I was thinking I would have a different experience.....thought wrong.  I paid $26 for that
This  place used to be good they changed the menu now it's just crap food, gumbo was like straight from a can not good at all
Usually my sandwich is good but today it was terrible. Ordered number 16. Even if it's crowded I need my sandwich to come out good cheese and peppers fully covering the sandwich as I should.

With all the hype about this place, so I went there and give it a shot.  Once I walked in, there's no one to greet you.  One guy standing at the counter in the back.  Behind him is a hand writing menu on a poster.  $8 for pork, $9 for beef, etc.   No credit card accepted if your bill less than $25.  Once you gave your order and paid.  You get to find yourself a table.  Wow.  So is this self service?  Does it mean I don't have give tips?   Wrong!  I asked the guy where's my water?  He said no free water.  Either you buy bottle water or soda from us.  Wow!
Does he know it's the law to give free water?  Anyway... The lady from the kitchen bought out our dumplings.  Opened the bamboo cover and found six tiny pieces of dumplings.  $8 for six tiny pieces?  Talk about inflation!  There's a mandatory size for dumplings even supermarkets has bigger pieces in the frozen department.
Uncomfortable seat, no music, you can hear everyone's conversations.  One entrance door.  If a new customer comes in, the wind draft will blow everything off your table.  Zero service.  I just left a few bucks on the table and call it a day.
So stop the bitching and tell us how's the dumplings?  My honest answer to you is that you can find this in any dim sum place, and cheaper with service and free water.

On their window they advertise chicken little meal combo for $5... 2 chicken littles, mash potatoes , cookie and a drink... we went in to order and we're told "we don't have that" and it's advertised right on their window... too bad customers are victimized by false advertising!  Oh ! They also don't have lettuce...obviously that was our one and only time! How do they stay in business?

I went with high expectations based on reviews, service was excellent , food not so much. Rice was to sticky I had ordered la Chuleta Kan Kan which is a giant pork chop. Puerto Rico where they make these although they deep-fry them , the meet is moist but the skin is toasty .  Here the hole pork chop was hard over cooked . 

Flavor wise the beans were a bit  "Soso" not very tasty . 

Very clean good presentation , we may have caught them on a bad day , I definitely see there potential. 

If you are having a Birthday and you bring your own cake , don't ask for plates because their is a 10$ service charge !   

Cleanliness I would give them a 10 Hands down . And for that I would give them a second chance in the future .

I walked into this place for the first time today 6/23 at lunch to get a couple of slices.  There was no pizza on display and I asked to see it.  Well, you would have thought I asked for this woman's first born!!  She gave me SUCH an attitude that I walked right out and will never go back.  Don't they realize they're are 10 other places within 5 miles???

I don't even know why I come here anymore. I used to be able to get my glasses and contacts here quickly and without a problem, but it seems as though the new workers they hired are incompetent and don't know what they're doing. I got an eye exam in January - the optometrist is fine but the girl who actually orders my contacts for me has no idea what she's doing. They don't contact you to let you know any updates about your order and they make no effort to make sure you get what you need. She said she would email me with an update about my order but that never happened. I don't think she ever ordered them in the first place. It's April now and I still haven't gotten my contacts that were supposed to be ordered in January. This place is a joke.

This place is a mess. Literally. If you like slop this is it! They just slop your plate/pita & douse it w/ liquid so by the time it gets to the end of the sandwich line it disintegrates in your hands in about one minute. It smells like it might be good if you eat it IMMEDIATELY, but if you go get a drink & walk to your car... it's over. Gone. Also, I don't know how it's possible to wrap a sandwich upside down, but it happened. Maybe they are left-handed handling pitas.

If you loved the movie Caddyshack you hated Caddyshack 2. The same goes for this place.  If you love El Charro on Court St then you'll hate this place. Same name, but a sad rendition in a suburbia strip mall. Even the most inept managerial team is aware of a big football game where you should staff up, but it didn't happen here. While in town for the UofA ASU game we (6)  tried the Court St location but it had a 2 hour wait. So, having to drive back to PHX, we decided to try this location. Mistake. While we were quoted a 1 hr wait we still stayed. While we waited in the front patio area we watched table after table after table get up after dinner and leave. Every one of these tables just sat dirty and empty with no busser to clean and get reseated by everyone waiting for dinner. Once we got seated, we sat with menus for about 10 mins before a couple of measley bowls of chips got placed on the table. Between six people that didn't last long. About another 10 minutes later a waiter walked up and said hi and asked if we were ok. I said no, we've been here 20 mins with no service. He said, oh, I guess I'll take care of you . We ordered drinks which took another 10 mins to receive. Another 10 mins went by before we got our meals ordered. By this time the restaurant had pretty cleared out and it seemed like most of the employees decided they didn't have to work hard anymore. We still didn't have side plates, silverware or napkins. The only time we saw our waiter was to take our orders and drop off the bill. There was no, thank you for your patience, thank you for coming or even have a good night. Nothing. If you're truly looking for the charm, service and food quality that El Charro has to offer, go to the Court St location, no matter how long a wait.

OMG. Just received incorrect order taken by "marble mouth" "fast talking" order taker...called back and manager states they cannot and will not replace order. The worse. They obviously do NOT understand doing the right thing for long standing customers. Boooooo to you Francello's. Will not order from your establishment again.
They say service can make or break an experience. The rudeness of the woman serving food was shocking. Travelers beware pick something else to do...

Rudest people in the area. Plenty of open tables but would not seat me because I was one person.  I complained so they Let me sit there taking person after person. Ignored me waiting for me to just go away but I wouldn't. Finally put me in a room by myself. So rude.

I was pleasantly surprised by the menu.  Very nice selection of food, fair prices and a nice staff.  The location is very bad and you have to pay to park.  The most troublesome issue was the customers.  I expected a lot of kids, but it was full of thugs roaming around.  I noticed that there were 4 deputies working security. 4!!!!!  Please don't bring your small kids here and expect to be safe.  The parking garage had the same issue.

First off it's no longer a Thai restaurant but the sign is still up.  Very deceitful.  We order fish and chips.  It took over 40 minutes to cook.  The cod was BLAND and served with white vinegar.  It had been frozen. The salad was wilted and the fries were frozen steak fries.  Horrible service and outrageous prices  I wouldn't give it a star at all.  Warning do not go there. They need to go out of business.  The worst ever  and i am not exaggerating
Food overpriced for New Orleans breakfast. Unbathed homeless frequent the front steps. Cajun hash browns aren't the norm. Great area and restaurant appears to be nice. Worst breakfast in NO, so far.

I would rate the food as ok. 

The salad bar was respectable but not great. For instance the broccoli salad was no very palatable, nor the seafood pasta. 

Both the minestrone and onion soup obviously from a can, unappetizing and overly salty.  Served luke warm, as was the res of my meal.

BLT sandwich was not bad. Tomatoes weren't the best but they never are unless you grow them yourself. The kids had spaghetti and meatball. Canned tomato sauce was kind of gross and the meatball was sub par. Egg plant parm, like everything else was luke warm and served with the gross canned spaghetti sauce.

My impression is that they rely on pre-prepared foods and a microwave on their dinners

Second time I've been here since it opened.  This place is disappointing, period.  For a brewery/tap house, with the prices their menu demands, whoever is running their kitchens doesn't have a clue.  It is a dilemma to xhoose something from their menu that doesn't sound gross. And a fourteen dollar cheeseburger is absolute robbery.  As for the beer, I didn't like any I drank the first rime, so now I'm drinking iced tea.

To the owners- if you want to see a menu in your price range that doesn't suck, go eat at Brickhouse and steal the person who desined their menu.

Very disappointed.

I was not greeted when I walked in. The customer service was not the best, they got my order wrong, I asked for a certain type of noodle and they gave me the wrong kind, when I asked them to fix it they made me take out the noodles myself and put the new ones in. There was also a small spider on my table as well there was also a number of flys flying around me as I ate. But the food was exceptional.

As soon as I saw the yellow hipster kitten heels and cankles approaching us I should've run full speed anywhere else.  Based on my love of crepes and fabulous experiences at every other creperie I've encountered I thought La Creperie Cafe live up to my basic crepe standards.  

I am fully aware that no one will beat Beau Monde, but what I didn't expect was the raw flour and pancake tasting oddly layered and barely filled mess that was placed in front of me. The food was awful, the service was worse and when I complained about the inedible food I heard a very loud and angry cook yelling about it. Awesome. Stay away, far, far away.

The good: This brisket is so tender it nearly falls apart, delicious base seasoning prior to cooking. The smoked jalapenos are spicy hot and tasty.  The not so good: Online ordering for carryout on their website is odd, they list menu items with no price, details and you can't click on them to select. So they state for you call a central number to order for multiple locations. 10 min later called by location to tell me the dessert I ordered isn't available there.  Was told order was ready, arrived shortly, saw my sack sitting there and quite a while before anyone acknowledged me. Then I had to wait too for her to get a manager to remove the desert they didn't offer.  Was given no choice of BBQ sauce and they have 3 kinds.  Did not care at all for what they gave me, the signature sauce.  Collard greens lacked depth of flavor, mac & cheese was just OK.  It takes away from the quality of the brisket to not enjoy the sauce, ended up eating it plain.  Overall, I'd call it a frustrating dining experience, but there is no doubt they got the brisket seasoning and cooking perfect.

The decor is nice and drinks are good, however, for brunch, there are better places in Philadelphia. I ordered an omelette and it was for sure the "fake" eggs being used...very rubbery and un-appetizing! I wouldn't go back for brunch.
looked like a good place but no one greeted me or offered assistance. Bad service ill go down the street and see what else is available.

Wanted to try this place out because it was something different. The whole family decided to have pizza for dinner and give this place a shot. We arrived around 720pm well before closing hours (Didn't wanna be that guy/family). We were seated, drinks ordered, and pizzas ordered by 730 maybe 745. This was on a Sunday night. Not very busy when we got there. Shortly after small groups would come in and get seated and place their order. We ordered 3 pizzas. After almost an hour of waiting for our order the waitress comes to apologize for the wait and tells us it's gonna be 10 more minutes. At this point we asked for the pizzas to be boxed so we could just take them home. By the time we left it was 830. Honestly the pizza was very plain. The menu description made my mouth salivate more than actually eating the pizza did. Never had a first time experience like we did here before.

The service here is painfully slow, and the food is not very good. After waiting 45 minutes in a empty restaurant, our food came out ice cold and under cooked. Really just grab snacks from somewhere else and go to a different place in Green Hills for a meal.
Doesn't taste very good, especially the pork intestines, it taste raw and a lot of fat. I wouldn't recommend this restaurant. Last time when I go there for dinner, nobody is in the restaurant
Sorry Blake but your service sucks! Went to the roof top bar and couldn't even get waited on. Even asking for a server no one came over. Disappointed

The optometrist was fine. The office staff, as others have pointed out, were not. 

I was paying for contact lenses, my insurance covers $100 of it. I was told that there was a $4 balance so I handed the young lady my credit-card and she told me that they can't take cards for that small an amount. I told her I'd go get cash and she said that's alright, she'll just put a note on my account for when I picked up my contacts a few days later. 

A few days later, I picked up my contacts. The young girl was not there and I mentioned that I owed a few dollars. They told me there was no balance and that my contacts supply had been less than $100.

Shady, shad, shady.

Well I promised someone I would try this place and finally I got around to trying it.  As has been said before the burgers were so so. Nothing to drag a buddy out for that is for sure.  I also HAD to try the coconut prawns.  Well I am sad to report that the coconut prawns were barely prawns and more resembled shrimp...the size was very disappointing.

Maybe this was just an off day...but I was underwhelmed....maybe the building is haunted with the ghosts of lame service.

I doubt that all LF stores are like this, but this one in particular is so incredibly messy. It literally looks like I walked into a 16 year olds closet. Clothes are are everywhere, it's so overwhelming. Nothing is neatly put away and I have to dig for anything cute. No one wants to dig. The staff are friendly, but it's like driving through LA traffic walking into that store. Anxiety.

Service department is lacking, employees don't seem motivated and don't follow through and seem to use the shotgun approach to resolving mechanical issues. I would advise caution and get a second opinion from another shop before proceeding with these guys.  The parts counter inside the store is knowledgeable and they are helpful but I prefer the guys at the O'Reily store at Damonte Ranch by a long shot.

For being a brand new restaurant in the area, this place is full of shenanigans.  Here are some take-away points:

1) This is a sit-down, Mexican style restaurant, but you don't even get free chips and salsa.  I went with a group of 4 and we ordered 3 tacos a piece, but chips and salsa cost an extra $3.95.  When we asked about this, we were told that chips came free with a burrito (not advertised on the menu), but if I don't want a burrito and I'm willing to spend MORE MONEY on 3 tacos, you're telling me I still have to shell out $4?  No thanks.

2) Tacos range from $3.75 - $3.95 each and they're good, but I wouldn't say great.  You can get better tacos for less at places like San Antonio Taco Company.  Plus, they only charge $1.49 for chips there.

3) They advertise a "deal" called the 3 Amigos which is 3 tacos for $11.50.  This is not a deal unless you're ordering 3 of their $3.95 tacos and even that is only saving you 35 cents.  Seriously, just take it off the menu unless you're actually going to save people money.  We even had two people try to convince us our math was wrong and it actually was saving you money even if you bought three of the cheapest tacos.

4) The music.  You're in Music City, USA and you aren't even going to play the real version of very popular songs in your establishment?  We heard covers of Bruce Springsteen, Motley Crue, Ratt, and many others during our lunch that sounded like they were being played off of those "Pure 80s" CDs you see in the bargain bins at K-Mart that no one buys.  Get a blanket license for ASCAP, SESAC, and BMI.  They're not expensive for a restaurant of your size.

In the end, the food was fine, but for the price, I won't be back and can't recommend it.

My friends and I stopped by this place when we were visiting Bourbon Street. We ordered the fried alligator and the Seafood Platter. The alligator was pretty good with the jelly that they provide on the side. However, the place has bad service and is dirty. The first plate I received had a dead fly on it which was promptly replaced by the waitress. The second plate I received had bits and pieces of left over food. The Seafood platter was subpar and did not have any shrimp as the menu clearly stated. For $60 I thought I would have had a much better experience given that I was visiting Louisiana which is known for its seafood. Stay away.

45 minutes to receive two incorrect drinks and waters at a bar with open seats. 
The first time I sat at the bar I excused the experience with the bartender having a bad day. The second time was with the same bartenders about a month later. It took 25 minutes before we were even acknowledged and that was only because the men who sat next to us about 10 minutes after we arrived waved down the bartender. The gentlemen then suggested she take our order first because we had been waiting. Our drinks were made incorrectly. Twice. And it took another 15 minutes and asking twice to receive waters. The bartender that had served me the time before had a habit of rolling her eyes and being abrupt (why I thought she was having a bad day) but her behavior was the same this time. Having both been in the service industry, my friend and I tried to be as polite as possible but we only received attitude and the service never improved.

The service at the Franklin Pike location, however, is always fantastic.

I love Buho's in Exton and was stoked when I saw one opening in Phoenixville. What a letdown. We got there on a Saturday night, but not when it was busy. It was busy by the time we left, because it took forever to get our food. Over an hour from the time we ordered to the time it arrived on our table -- and when it finally showed up, they forgot to make one person's dish, so we had to wait even longer. 

At no point during the amazingly long wait did the waitress think to come over and say she was sorry it was taking so long. A simple "Our kitchen's understaffed tonight" or "We just got swamped" would have done wonders for our attitude. Instead, we had to pull her aside and ask her where our food was, and it was STILL over 10 minutes before it showed up after asking. Not to mention, she rarely entered our room -- much less stopped at our table -- at all. 

When the food finally showed up, it wasn't worth the wait. 

Their response was to offer me a coupon for next time, as if there would be one. 

My wife and I have both worked in restaurants, are very understanding, and are so eager to be patient and let this kind of stuff slip by. But what happened, combined with how poorly it was handled, coupled with how mediocre the food ended up being, made this a miserable, once-in-a-lifetime experience.
Being a Gator fan I was hoping for a little more out of this place. Food was ok... drinks were ok. Atmosphere was eh. Maybe its better on Saturday's during college football games...

The food there may be delicious but I'll never know. We sat there for an hour waiting for our order of an omelette and a burger with tots. 
The noise level at brunch was absolutely insane to the point of hurting our ears.
The waitress stopped by once after an hour and told us she would find out if it was going to be much longer. After another 5 minutes, she didn't come back. We decided to go to the Barrel House across the street instead and left.

I so wanted to love this place. I've never been so disappointed in Mac and cheese. It was so al dente and chewy that I could barely eat it. I'm assuming this was intentional and just how they serve their take on Mac and cheese. Terribly overpriced also. What a letdown.

The most ghetto Admiral's Club I have been to- crowded and they don't even have a bathroom in the club. In their defence- they had to make a makeshift club after the tornado hit STL airport in early 2010, so I understand they can't have it perfect. But if I'd paid for a membership just to come to this club, I'd be pretty bitter. It feels like a repurposed conference room in a faded Holiday Inn- would rather hang out at Missouri Vineyards outside of security of Schlafly's in B terminal. 

Hopefully, they will get the real club up and running again soon.....

I usually go to Petra Express in New Tampa but thought I would try this place since I was in the neighborhood. I thought the food at Petra Express was much better: crispy fries & moist chicken. The fries here were not crispy & the chicken was a little dry. The service at Petra Express is also friendlier & has a better atmosphere. This place was absolutely dead at lunch time. Will not visit here again, I definitely prefer the food, service & ambience at the New Tampa location.

Shut down, revamped, hire new people, and open back up for the dining area. Game area runs smooth with no problems. However, the restaurant area is HORRIBLE! Literally five hostess not doing anything just sitting there looking. We came for my husband's 30th birthday and wasted the whole night.  We arrived at 8 and didn't leave until after midnight. The wait, the food, missing food, and waiting on our food tickets.  I don't recommend this location for food... all the other locations in Texas and Florida was way better! Normally, I don't complain and is very patient but tonight was too horrible.

We love charbroiled oysters and are always looking for good ones in the neighborhood.  I've got to say that these were the worst I have ever had. The server tried...hence two stars.  But the food was barely worth one star.  
   The oysters were way over seasoned (salted) and tasted like they had Velveeta melted on top.  Also they were mostly cold..not grilled at all. We had gumbo and had to send it back twice to be heated.  The soup bowl was actually icy.  Again, even after the second microwave reheat it barely reached room temperature.  We couldn't find any seafood in it either
What a shame to waste such a beautiful view and great location on really bad food.  
   Message to management...you're on the lakefront now and not serving one shot meals to tourists.  You are losing return customers to the much better restaurants right around the corner.

Newer place, went for the first time tonight, Tuesday night, not very crowded. Had enough staff for a Saturday. The host station had eight people greeting us because they were obviously overstaffed. Our waitress over-served us, checking on us every two minutes. We ordered pretty quickly since we were getting so much attention.
