I had the collard greens and they were actually gritty. Like sand between my teeth! And the fried green tomatoes (one of my all-time Southern favorites) were also a waste of 6$ (small slices, mostly end pieces, batter slipping off). The only side I enjoyed quite a bit was the mashed sweet potatoes with marshmallows - delicious. 
Overall, though, it was a pretty negative experience. We won't be returning for a while, unless we hear that the kitchen has stepped it up.

The food deserves 4 stars, the 15% gratuity added to the bill for a party of 2 ruined an otherwise good meal.

I got the red beans & rice, which was very, very good.  My wife got the shrimp platter and she really liked the shrimp, but the fries were soggy.

If not for the gratuity, for which there is NO sign hanging on the wall or "small print" on the menu to indicate it, I would rave about this place.  As it is, I say avoid it because of that extra charge.

My coworker and I were craving a strawberry smoothie and this place was literally 0.02 miles away. We decided, "why not?" because sometimes Orange Julius smoothies can hit the spot.

No.
No.
No.

We were better off making a smoothie with our hands. The smoothie wasn't smooth at all! Seriously? How the hell are you known for shakes and smoothies and I'm literally chewing on the smoothie? Big chucks of ice just ruining each attempt of a sip in your straw.

I'm only giving two stars because I didn't have pho. I heard from a person at work that this place was the bomb, so I decided to do some take out. The lady that was taking the orders was pretty disinterested in my questions which left me a bit confused. After a while pondering the menu, I ordered vegetable fried rice and chicken chow mein. Both items were OK, but nothing to write home about. The vegetable fried rice was more vegetable than rice and the chow mien had large hunks of vegetables in it, yielding little noodles. I'm not a real fan, but maybe it's my tastes.
If you are looking for good food, don't go here.  Mediocre at best.  Salty, expensive and boring.

Went to get fancy donuts and they did not have any fancy donuts available. Only the fresh ones. The restaurant is called Federal Donuts. Donuts should always be available.

P.S. I'm only giving this two stars because they did not have any donuts. The hot fresh donuts with Cinnamon or Strawberry Lavender are good, but you can't just tell customers that you don't have any of the other products available.

First off I have to say the reviews surprise me. I was expecting the best fried chicken I have ever had. The waitress we had was rude and had no personality. I had to keep asking for napkins, water the cornbread.. The bus boy was way more outgoing. I was displeased with the macaroni and cheese, it looked like they made it two days ago and warmed it up and slopped in on the plate. The collard greens were not appetizing. The chicken was pretty good, if I went back I would only get the fried chicken, it was the best part of this place.

I went to That Bar-B-Q Place when it first opened and thought it was decent, so decided to go there for lunch with a friend today to try it again. We met at 11:30 so it was very quiet when I first got there, but it did pick up later.

I ordered and by the time I had paid, my food was ready on the counter. Very fast... maybe too fast? I ordered the brisket sandwich with beans and my friend got the pulled pork sandwich with mashed potatoes. 

The first bite was pretty disappointing. Then bun was stale and tasted a few days old. The brisket was very dry and pretty flavourless. I was able to douse the meat in barbecue sauce, so that kind of salvaged it, but I prefer to have the meat stand alone, not just be masqueraded in a sauce. There was no saving the stale bun though.

I had tried the pit beans the first time I was there and enjoyed the smoky taste and brisket mixed in. This time, I could hardly distinguish them from beans straight out of a can. 

My friend wasn't very fond of his pulled pork sandwich, saying that it was very bland.

Honestly, the only saving grace of this place is the barbecue sauce. Everything else was a big meh. I've had much better barbecue at chain restaurants than this specialty barbecue place.

The building and environment are nice. Lots of little separate areas containing 4-6 tables which helps to control any noise issues. It is nice that they offer some outdoor seating and a stocked bar. But honestly, the food is really lacking flavor and quality is fair to poor. Unfortunately I'd say pass Buho and go elsewhere. A big disappointment.

The place is a bit quirky, but that's ok.  The process of ordering is a bit odd, grab a menu, find a seat and come up to the counter when ready to order.  OK, got that.  The problem is the length of time it takes to get a pizza, nearly an hour.  I've had pizza all over the world and nowhere does it take that long.  But, the pizza is good with a nice crispy crust.
The person working the counter seemed to be the only one working the floor.  There's a lot of shouting and activity, but not a lot getting done.  It appears they could benefit from some actual management and enough people to clean tables and bring the food when it's ready.

I ordered take out from here last night, which apparently was a mistake. I was at a friend's house in NoLibs, which yes, is about 18ish blocks away, but still not that far.

It took close to 2 hours to get our food delivered. When the delivery guy finally arrived, he wouldn't hand me the bag, but kept showing me the receipt. I told him that I'd already paid because I ordered online. He then made me wait while he called his boss. During this process, he set the bag down on a step, only to have it topple over. Seriously, guy, you made us wait way too long, so just give me our food and beat it.

The food itself was whatever. Outside of Chinatown, all Chinese places taste relatively the same.
Bottom line: We wont be back due to the wings not being on the lunch buffet anymore. Myself and several co-workers used to come weekly to get the wings and salad for lunch.

I am a steak lover.  I have tried filet mignon at hundreds of restaurants all over the world.  This was the worst filet I have ever eaten.  The meat itself was actually of good quality.  It was the preparation.

There was a ball of peppercorn coating one end of the filet, like chopped nuts piled high on top of an ice cream cone, and the rest of the filet was grey.  No char marks, just dead grey.  I was a little grossed out.  I finally had to cut off the ball-o-peppercorn, because it was all I could taste.

The cream sauce was soupy, kind of like milk - dipping the steak into it was like pushing a grey hose into a splashing puddle of white.

We sat outside.  Don't, unless you are there for the "good food while watching sports" shtick.  Motorcycles, hot rods, firetrucks, ambulances and police cars, with blaring sirens, raced down Kennedy for the length of our meal.

When I looked at the menu, there was a large kink on the bottom-right 20% where the laminated page folded over on itself.  The menu wasn't thrown out or repaired, it was used.  That same level of quality control seemed evident in the rest of our experience.

I enjoy supporting new places.  Our server was nice, the portions were decent, but the overall quality of the dining experience and the preparation/presentation of the food was not on par with other local restaurants.
The setting is beautiful and the servers are great! Unfortunately, the food is AWFUL! For the price you pay and the Applebee's quality of food it is way overrated.

I was really excited to try this super sweet sauce, especially being a sweet sauce lover.  However, this tasted as if an entire bag of sugar was poured on the pizza.  It was just overwhelming and unsatisfying.  Beyond the sauce, the margarita pizza was under-cooked and by the end, was getting over-saturated with sauce to the point it was falling apart.  The pizza was a thin crust, and another minute in the oven would likely have done it more justice.  That said, I will not be back.

Jones is a bit overpriced for what it is.
Chicken and waffles tasted great, but was very salty. Chicken was nice and crisp. Burger was just okay, nothing crazy. Salads are overpriced though you can customize them. I would not recommend getting calamari on your salad because it's oily, bland, axons just whatever.
Milkshakes are nice and thick though. Decor is quite unique and service is friendly.
Too many great restaurants, especially Steven Starr restaurants, to return to this.

Chill place. My friends enjoy the cornhole.

Here's the reason for my two stars - all over 27 cents. I'm the current Duke: So how about my friends and I spent just over $100 on drinks and tip the chick over $30. We later ask her for Jalapenos and she seriously insists that we pay 0.27 cents for the Jalapenos....REALLY??!!!! We gave her 2 quarters and let her keep the change....  C'mon, really? Am I asking for extra/excessive nugget sauce @ McD?!

Got two things, a Meatball sub, and a cheese pizza.  The sub was OK, good flavor, tasty, soft roll, and was fresh.  Very little sauce or cheese though.  The paper they wrapped it in stuck to the little cheese there was,   The pizza was no better then frozen stuff from the supermarket.  I showed up 20 minuts ofter ordering it on the phone, and had my first slice five minuts after that.  It tasted like it was an hour old.  The crust was crunchy and stale.  For 15 bucks for a plain pie, it should be much better.  The only reason I gave it 2 stars was for the fresh bread, and good meatballs in the sub.  That plus the fresh salad that came with the sub made that part keeping the meal from being a total disaster.

I wouldn't really recommend eating here per se-- your money is always undoubtedly spent better elsewhere --however, Cosi is really not a bad spot to meet someone for coffee at (not to say the coffee is good, but it's alright). Plenty of seats and reasonable decor make it half-decent.

Sat at the bar and it took 20 minutes to be acknowledged by bartender. He took 10 minutes to pour my wine. Asked for a glass of water and he gave it to the lady next to me! There's so many choices in SB, I'll take my business somewhere else! I have gone to Chase at least once a month, but the service was horrible! He wasn't even busy!
No ribs and no biscuits!  I heard the food was good here.  The people who worked her were grouchy and the food was cold.  It was as a little pricey too.

Had a couple of experiences here... the staff was very lazy and unhelpful... I asked for a product under lock and they said the only guy with a key would be back in 20 minutes... maybe.  See ya.  Total Wine has a better selection, better staff and will price match anything Bevmo has.

We stopped in for some oyster because I hadn't had fifty dozen yet in two days. This place isn't as busy as some of the other so it is easy to get in. We sat at the bar and listened to the bartenders complain about the other staff which was awkward. The drinks were poorly prepared because they were too busy talking. We orders the charbroiled oysters and they were ok. Nothing special and the didn't take care in opening them because there were pieces of shell throughout the whole oyster...a couple are ok because it is an oyster but this wasn't ok.

Are reservations necessary? Yes. Are reservations honored? No. This place was busy upon entrance, but no worries I thought, because I reserved a table. Too bad, my girlfriend and I were scuttled to the back, where we waited over twenty minutes for our table, with no apology from the manager in site, Nick Cammarota, who seemed more interested in looking cool than running a smooth restaurant. The food was  decent, prices slightly fairly reasonable. Do not intend on returning though.
Typical franchise  massage today. I left feeling sick. Not sure if it was the coughing on me but if you're a therapist don't get your clients sick too.

Review summary: If you want an expensive selection with amazing staff and a welcoming environment..... Total Wine is less than a mile down the road. 


I have never bought from this store because I have a preferred liquor store right across the street from my house. But through a search on the internet I had been told they had a certain sake that I have been looking everywhere to buy. 
We enter the store, it looks like they just took a concrete room and set up shelves and hung passer signs from the ceiling letting customers know where each type of alcohol is. There are some crude decorations but looks as though they put nearly no time or money into making their place look presentable. And I know this is a chain so I was slightly confused. It looked as if they had just opened. I start searching for sake, which is not a marked section. A man asks me how I'm doing and we talk about where the sake is. He brings it to me and asks me what I'm looking for. I told him that I found it on the Randall's website. He condescendingly said to me "just because it's on a website doesn't mean we will have it in or store"

I said "I understand that... I don't see it. Is the any way to order it?"
Just then, who looks to be the manager came up and interrupted to say "what are you looking for?" I told him and he was like "and you said someone told you that we have it here?" I said "no I found it on the internet" he just stared at me and I said "I found it on the Randall's website. And he interrupted me before I could finish to say in the SAME tone as the man before him "just because it's on the internet doesn't mean it will be in our stores."
I said "okay.. I understand that.. can you order it?" He said "I don't know. I don't know. I'll be back" and comes back after 10 minutes to say "that specific bottle isn't anywhere in Missouri alright?"
I said "ok thank you"
And he walked away.

I'm giving them the benefit of the doubt with a 2 star rating.  In my eyes, it's really a 1.  But the portion of the fish tacos, the beer garden, and the overall potential for the place once it gets out of its nubile demo stage merits an extra star.

My coworkers and I were super extra salty when the Porch's seemingly groundbreaking "Porch 2.0" Twitter announcement was none other than "oh, we're completely wiping out all the rotating food trucks you loved and replacing them with a single food truck containing the same old food day in and day out."  Not to mention an awkward rearrangement of seats and benches(mostly ALL in the hot sun) and fake turf no one sits on.  Great.  

Salty and all, we still decided to give it a gander since, who knows, maybe it would blow us out of the water and become our new favorite go-to lunch spot.  Wrong.  

For one, the menu is super limited.  Not saying food trucks should ever have more than like 8-10 items, but the fact of the matter is that when the prior food trucks were there and I didn't like something that Spot Burger had, I could hop over to Taco Mondo or Pitruco or Kai Chi or Foolish Waffles or.....the list is endless.  OPTIONS is what lured the crowds in. 

Now we're stuck with a basic bitch menu: wings, porchetta, watermelon salad, homemade BBQ chips, fish tacos, burger, veggie burger, BBQ chicken wrap, and some other crap not worth mentioning and also not reasonably priced for the quality.  At Mucho Bueno, for example, I would easily drop $12-$13 on lunch without regretting it since I knew I was getting good food.  And even in those food trucks, they would vary up their menu weekly so you were always surprised.

The fresh tomato/watermelon/feta/mint salad is very delicious and healthy- but also $7 for essentially just a shit ton of large watermelon chunks.  The BBQ chips are a hit or miss- some days they're fresh and the dipping sauce is tasty and other days they're stale and from the bottom of the barrel.  The porchetta is NOT a sandwich, but rather, a heaping pile of fatty pork meat-and both people I knew who had it threw away half because of how inedible the fat became.  The fish tacos are fairly tasty, large, and the portion is worth the $7 but the fish is awkward and square shaped and the tortillas get soggy real quick. 

Maybe I'll try the burger next or possibly scope it out for cocktails at happy hour, but this is one food truck that should have been left out of the rotation.

Ok bubble tea but only limited variety. I have had a few bad bubbles in the past.  What is most disappointing is the frozen yogurt.  All super sweet varieties. Even ice cream isn't usually this sweet.  Expect the common flavors of cake batter, red velvet cake and cookies and cream.  This place is way better for kids than adults.  There are too few frozen yogurt places in town that have the nice clean flavors that should differentiate yogurt from ice cream.  They have an ok mix of toppings.

This cafe looks upscale but is actually not. The food is ok but pricey. I ordered key lime pie for dessert and I noticed a fly had landed in the cream probably after it arrived on my table. I pointed this out to the waitress who didn't seem keen on fixing it, but who did take it away promising another. The second portion was considerably smaller than the first so I'm assuming all they did was cut the first down and send it back - hardly first class service. 

I would have given this three stars but for the sneaky trick of including 15% tip in the bill and the credit card receipt still having the option to add a tip. I suppose you could add more for good service, but I expect it catches many unwary people who effectively tip twice.

Their pizza and Stromboli's are horrible. Pizza was soggy and bot crisp like it should be. The Stromboli's are small and not of very good quality. Party of 4 tonight and the service was horrible! We won't be back and DO NOT Recommend this place to anyone. Poor quality of food.

Stopped in for lunch and was very disappointed. Actually, 2 stars may be overly generous but it is almost Christmas. There is zero ambiance, in fact, except for fresh paint and some pictures on the wall, I don't believe they made any improvements from the previous establishment. As for the fare, pretty damn boring. The home made potato chips were good but cold. The mushroom soup was very good but a bit salty.  Meatball sandwich was soggy and without flavor and the beet salad was so-so.

They have potential if they improved their food ingredients a bit more. I ordered the Chicken and Waffles as well as a burger. The chicken lacked ANY flavor whatsoever, but the waffles were nice, warm, and sweet. I could tell that the syrup and whip cream were a little low quality, but It satisfied me because I was hungry. 

The burger quality was very poor. I asked for medium-rare, and got a dull greyish burger. Didn't even finish it. 

Like I said, they are new and they can turn this around by choosing better ingredients for their dishes.
Used to be absolutely amazing! The margaritas and the food were spot on delicious. Now the food taste old and the margaritas just aren't as good! I used to be a regular here, not anymore.

Overrated. Great decor, lovely staff..but the food was mediocre at best. I had the London broil, which was not great, and the twice-baked potato left a lot to be desired.
Should have tried the Mac and cheese..
Margaritas, however, were delicious!

My husband and I went to Jones last night on a recommendation from a friend. The times I'd been by there before they had a line out the door, so I was eager to try it to say the least. The decor is modern and chic, and it seemed like a great bet for date night out. 

The service was good, everyone was friendly and really attentive. Of course we were there at a time that wasn't too busy. Nothing to complain about on that front. 

The food on the other hand was a *total* disappointment. In fact, I'm not convinced anything was actually cooked there. We ordered the chicken and ginger dumplings, Tuna Steak Tacos and the Meatloaf to share. The dumplings were fine, but tasted like something I get out of the freezer case at Trader Joes. The filling to the tacos was pretty good actually, but they were served on the hard taco shells that I swear were the Old El Paso brand you can buy at the grocery store. I asked for a side of salsa--also not fresh or homemade. I asked my husband what the glaze on the meatloaf was and he described it as "brown gravy and ketchup, mixed together". Yuck! In short, there is nothing remotely memorable that gave me any indication they are doing anything in the kitchen that doesn't involve a can opener or deep frying pre-cooked packaged food. What a waste of chic place with good atmosphere. I won't go back, and would suggest you don't waste your money here. 

And I was really surprised by the low quality, given all the good reviews I've read here and for as popular this place seems. You're not going to find anything offensive here, and if you're serving picky eaters you'll probably be safe. But I won't electively go back to Jones. Save your money.

Great location, my friend ordered bbq oysters and they were from a jar covered in Kraft BBQ sauce....really? My gumbo was passable with some hot sauce. My other friends catfish was good. Given the competition in the area they could easily raise their game a bit.

Regular chain food but do I really want these guys making my burger with their bare hands.  I made them re-do it becasue I don't know what they are touching before they slap thier sweaty  hands on my lettuce and tomatoes.  Good food, expensive, over ten with a shake and fries, and watch the cooks.

I was excited to try a sushi place near my house. I hate to say it, but my experience with Ninki was mediocre at best and above all disappointing: service and food. I placed a carry out order, to apparently the hostesses' chagrin, as the two of them seemed less than interested in taking my order or properly taking patrons to their tables. In addition to being treated like I broke up a private party, I was scoffed at when I asked for low sodium soy sauce. I wasn't aware that request warranted that response. To add insult to injury, the sushi wasn't even that good, and was sub par to some I have, in the past, had from the grocery store. Being a very recent transplant from NYC, It was my first time having sushi in Nashville, and last time having it at Ninki. I look forward to trying other places with better service and food.

Everything is very outdated. Arcades are outdated and very few to select from. Half of the vending machines and arcade games were out of order. Food was horrible. (Don't have automatic bumpers. Very surprised!) Not a great experience compared to other family fun centers/bowling we have been too.

First let me say that I spend between 30 to 40 nights a year in Hilton properties. I have seen Hampton Inns all over the country.  This hotel tried to charge almost $170 for an old beat up room.  They are going through a renovation starting with the reception and breakfast area.   This area looks nice but absolutely going for style over function.  Not enough seating for individual travelers.  Nothing worse then seeing a bunch of business men sharing a sofa trying to eat eggs.  
Parking area is restricted due to construction.   When I mentioned the unusually high price of the beat up room to the staff person they said that the hotel just raised prices. Im thinking that the hotel is trying to offset the cost of the renovation.  I would think hotels should not over charge during renovations due the inconvenience to the guest.   To be clear when I made the reservation I knew what the charge was going to be up front.  I did not expect an old room in a hotel that is being renovated.  I am sure it will be worth the price after all of the upgrades.   In addition the manager was professional listened to my grievance and lowered the price to a more reasonable fee commensurate to the room condition.   Unfortunately I felt like the powers that be are trying to take advantage of the guests.

Maybe this place needs male servers, because the girls arent cutting it. They seem uptight, barely hold a smile, and doesn't check on you as they should, And when I get a server that doesn't do any of that, its hard to be a returning customer. And it would be nice to see some minority's working there besides the staff in the kitchen or bus boys. I don't know, I've gave this place 2 chances.. Maybe it's just not for me.

Extremely disappointed in today's visit here! The drive thru is supposed to be the faster alternative to parking and walking in. The lady in front of me had asked for a bacon, egg, and cheese on a scooped out bagel. The girl on the receiving end rudely asked, "what?!" and the customer proceeded to tell her what she wanted. After explaining what a scooped out bagel is probably 3 more times, someone else started responding to the customer. Instead of asking her to come around to the window so they could face to face converse and maybe the customer could even draw a scooped out bagel to help reduce this confusion, they continued this for about 6 more minutes. Now it's my turn to order, I ordered two iced coffees and a water cup. The guy was nice and helpful. Awesome. I get to the window and received my drinks (they even remembered the water- yay!. I check my receipt and it says two munchkins. So I asked why it did and the girl immediately says we have to charge for water...well that's weird because I thought water was free everyone? Either way, you should explain this to me upon ordering instead of surprising me with this. When they first opened many of my receipts had random donuts and munchkins added to them. this Dunkin' Donuts is very inconsistent in my opinion. If you're going to charge me for water, you better charge me every time, otherwise I think it's suspicious. But maybe, that's just me. I live close by and generally enjoy DD, but I've never had so many issues at any other location before.
I've been here a few times in the past and the experience was always good. Today it was the same. Only thing was they tacked on 20% tip on a table of 5?!  Wtf. Never again!!!

I thought the sushi here was mediocre, and they would not accomodate any specific requests (rice paper instead of sea weed).  The service was inconsistent, or non-existent.  I have tried this place twice, and I won't be back.
This Starbucks is very inconsistent between shifts! The early morning barista gets it right every time. Weekends and afternoons are always a chance to what the flavor profile will be

The building interior is fishtonian. It took over 5 months until the desolate building became a faux hipster lair building. I think when the construction crew began work, the bakers also began work on their brownies. That is the only explanation for the stale, athletes foot consistency of the brownie I bought. The cupcake (about a month fresher than the brownie) packed an eight year old running a soft serve machine amount of icing on top. The icing was so flavorful that if Emril Lagasse recreated the recipe he'd include a "kaboom" amount of vanilla flavoring. As for the cupcake under the brick of cream cheese icing, I've eaten eucharist wafers that were tastier. All in all, the building creates an atmosphere that makes you wonder why a baked goods store needs that much space and the outrageous price let you know how it's paid for. 
TL;DR both treats were stale, save your money and go to a bake sale

"Meh, I've experienced better" - is right...

I have been going to Jones since it first opened... Over the years, the food has become sooooo mediocre.  We took some of my husband's cousins last Tuesday night.   Service was fine and the drinks are still good.

We ordered:

couple local beers - yum :)

mac n cheese - Some of us at the table LOVE mac n cheese in our daily lives.  This stuff was gooey in all the wrong ways and yucky.  I love strong cheese but this just wasn't good!

fried calamari - This was like your local take-out restaurant level.  meh.

meat loaf - I actually didnt eat any but folks seemed to like this the best that night.

fried chicken basket - It was pretty good but nothing worth any "oohs" or "ahhs".

potato pancakes - It was kind of greasy... it was okay.

chicken pot pie - My husband said my home made version that is low-fat/healthy is much better.  That is NEVER a compliment for the restaurant...

conclusion: $150 later for 6 people which isnt that bad, I dont think I will go back unless someone makes me.

We arrived about 4:30 PM on a Sunday night.  As there were very few people, we were seated quickly.  We waited and waited.  Finally a server came to ask if anyone had waited on us yet.  We asked for a couple of beers ... and waited and waited.  Finally, I asked another waiter to please ask our waitress to bring our drinks.  (We never were served with water.).  We finally got our food, but there was no cutlery.  When it became apparent that the server was not coming back, I got up to get our own cutlery.  By that time, my shrimp was cold.  I noticed that our server was often busy bussing her tables, even if it meant that her customers had to wait to order or pay the bill.  When she fInally arrived with our bill, I asked her for the cornbread that was supposed to come with my platter.  If she had checked on us sooner, I could have asked for the cornbread to go *with* my meal instead of just to go.  I gave two stars as my husband thought his shrimp po'boy was good, but my food was just okay and the service was TERRIBLE despite the fact that there were several staff members with not enough to do.  This used to be such a popular restaurant.  It has certainly gone downhill.

Went within a month of opening and was disappointed. People have been praising 4 Rivers as the best BBQ joint in Tampa; I beg to differ.

----- Positives -----

- Hype: I was so excited about the new 4 Rivers location opening in South Tampa I actually went a day before the grand opening by mistake. Manager explained they weren't yet open and gave me a coupon for a free sandwich.

- No server, no tip: I, for one, am glad I don't have to pay 15-20% tip for having my water refilled.

- Delicious BBQ smell: Entire South Tampa smells like BBQ now. 


----- Not so positives -----

- Cold food: I know I'm not the first person to notice/complain the food is here COLD. The Burnt Ends I had was lukewarm at best. Fried okra was lukewarm/cold as well.

- Parking: A major FAIL by owner/developers. Seeing how busy the Carrollwood location is every day, they should have expected the same high volume at the South Tampa location and built a big parking lot to accommodate it. Nearest parking is about a mile away on De Leon St by a Jewish synagogue. 

- Parking 2: Half the parking spots have a sign saying "compact car only," yet biga$$ gas-guzzling SUVs take up all the parking. Either they can't read or they have no shame.

Atmosphere is really nice. Cool wooden seats and outdoor area. Nice wall art and lighting. Food was just OK. Wasn't crazy about the pulled chicken at all. Mac n cheese was good, homestyle baked kind. Didn't like the corn skillet or collards. Kinda bummed was looking forward to a better meal.

While walking through the shady corridors of the La Encantada Shopping Center, I was beckoned inside by a sample carrying shop lass.  Her samples were delightful, and her knowledge of tea types and tea pots was unquestionable.  She told me to hold onto my cup, because there were more samples through out the store.  She was right, and each sample was delicious.  I was hooked, I had made up my mind that I would leave the store with some tea.  

I was in tea heaven, Teavana apparently, and I did not want to leave without some of my own.  While browsing the shelves of tea and tea paraphernalia, the helpful staff often asked if I need assistance, or if I understood how to use the tea pots in front of me.  This is where my delightful experience, started going downhill.  The staff quickly went from helpful to pushy.  I was asked if I needed help a total of seven times by four different employees.  The store is actually quite small, so I'm not sure how they wouldn't be aware of that.  

Not only did they seem overly eager to tell me what a tea light was for, but they were experts at avoiding answering the questions I actually did ask.  For example, when I finally decided to purchase a tea I was interested in, I couldn't get a straight answer as to how much it cost.  Instead, the salesperson insisted on pour the tea into a tin, weighing the teas, and talking about the benefits of the tea.  In the end, the price of their tea isn't what drove me away, but the pushy sales strategy.  

I wanted to like it, it was love at first sight, but in the end I'll take my business to the wonderful Seven Cups.

This place has gone down hill as far as service goes. The last few times I have gone for lunch I'm faced with people that don't listen, don't smile, don't even look at you and sure enough - 3 times in a row - messed up my order. Buck-up Quiznos.

Beautiful spa, polite front desk and neat handling give the spa the two stars but my first time experience at the Rescue Spa has sadly left me dissatisfied and sourly offended.

I booked a pedicure appointment for me and my husband as a Valentine's Day gift. Hubby badly wanted a pampering for his feet but was too hesitant to go to a spa by himself. I warned the staff about my fungal toe before hand and they said it was fine.

Hubby's experience was right on. Gina Riccioli who did my pedi was good at her job but made me feel very ignored and discriminated. I don't need someone to sweet talk to me but if she doesn't utter a word with me and does not look me in the eye throughout my appointment, but doesn't stop yapping to the very next client, yes I feel discriminated.

There was no follow up after my pedi, she would check on the rest of the clients if they needed anything, if their nails were dry, but for me, I got nothing. For $140 price that I paid for two pedicures and for looking forward to a relaxing satisfying happy Vday treat, my experience has only been one big disappointment! Never again will I go to this spa.

I have been here a few times and the mac and cheese is good, all be it a smidge thick but otherwise good with a drink. Today I came for the lobster mac bowl. I had seen a picture on facebook and it looked amazing. I get there, order, and as I am beig handed my first bowl I realize it is half the size of the normal bowl. Turns out there are two kinds of mac and cheese with lobster the baller mac, which is what I actully saw on FB and lobster mac which is apparently half the size(which you can tell because they use two different color bowls) and twice the price and simply offers lobster on mac and cheese with some bread crumbs. I asked if I could just pay the difference for the baller bowl and have them use the mac I hadn't even touched yet(still had the lid on it and the second order hadn't been completed) and the lady working had an attitude said she couldnt do anything "because its already made" we came specifically for the baller bowl and we were disappointed to say the least. The lobster mac is not bad but underwhelming for nearly 13 dollars especially given that you get half the amount.

A weekend stay that started with a room not ready until later in the afternoon. This alone was okay but there seemed to be consistent confusion and attitude at the front desk. It almost seemed that we were bothering them by checking in - strange. Okay, the room, while decent and at first glance looked nice, it was certainly nothing special. The furniture was way tired and while the bed was comfortable but we actually did not enjoy being in the room, which means that it just did not feel right. We were also blown away with really awful, actually grossed out by the breakfast buffet. This was made worse, if possible, by severe server attitude and total lack of service or caring. Marriott needs to get someone involved to fix it.

So... This place is always packed. People love this place, but there is nothing special about it. It's regular food that I make better at home. I had the BBQ oysters that were 8.95 and it only came with 4! That's over $2 per oyster! It was in BBQ sauce and came with bread to dip in the sauce. Who made up this dish? I don't want to dip bread in BBQ sauce. My friends had catfish and alligator sausage which were really good, but again not to die for. I won't be back.

Pretty unimpressive. Our server was nice but we waited 10 minutes before anyone came to our table and the restaurant wasn't even full on Saturday night. We were excited to try this place after seeing it was recommended in parents magazine. Disappointing. We ordered the fried chicken with greens and luke warm macaroni and cheese. My husband ordered the crawfish étouffée. The rice was overlooked and watery and the dish was very one dimensional with a heavy crawfish flavor. Definitely skip this place and eat elsewhere. It did not meet our expectations for being flavorful New Orleans soul food.

Second location, Taqueria El Pastorcito is just not for me

I have mixed experiences at the De La Vina location that certainly carry over here on Holister.

The space is exactly what I want taqueria to look like. Not dirty but not fancy either. Parking is easy in the lot out front or in the back where you'll also find the sketchy bathroom (dirty and smells like a truckstop). There's a TV hung high on the wall but nothing was on.
Service was ok. They just opened and the woman behind the counter was struggling to get through the iPad based POS. I expect this will improve over time. The food came out quickly and as ordered.

*Enchilada with chicken and red sauce $11.35, like the service, was ok. There's nothing special and the sauce was boring. I asked which was hotter, the reg or green and was totld the red. My guess over a Tomatillo Salsa Verde but I would expect there to be some heat with a good smokiness. Neither. There was allot of chicken so they've got that going for them. Which is nice.
*Alambres with al pastor $10.50, as far as I've heard, what Taqueria El Pastorcito is known for, was good. On par with offerings from other taquerias in the area but not the best.

A good salsa can make you
*Avocado has a good burn, more funny than whipped creamy and too much lime
*Red is hot with a hint of smoke
*Orange is thick, hot goodness

Back to the bathroom for a moment. Don't use it. The facilities are shared with other tenets in the strip center and it did not appear to be cleaned in some times. I did not wash my hands as I was definitely the cleanest ting in there. After seeing the bathroom, I was grossed out even to hold the key which is attached to a spoon. Who the hell would want to walk off with the key only to come back later and utilize the facilities? Yuck.

Bathroom aside, the restaurant was clean, the food fair but not amazing and the parking easy. I'm on the fence with the De La Vina location and this one does not make it's marks.

Not as good as we expected.  My fiance and I went to celebrate an anniversary.  We had such high expectations due to all the reveiws we read on yelp, but left very disappointed.  It was a hot summer weekend, and this tiny restaurant was packed with people.  The first thing we noticed was how uncomfortably warm it was, and I am rarely warm.  Also, the noise level was the loudest I have experienced and we have been to plenty of other intimate dining experiences to compare.  It was not a pleasant noise level, I could barely hear my fiance, and we pretty much stopped talking, because it was very frustrating, Understandably, that is not quite the restaurants fault but, the temperature is. Please upgrade your air conditioning system.  On to the food, My fiance ordered the scallop with pork belly appetizer, which was great. I ordered the crab and shrimp salad which was pretty good, but flavors got boring after awhile. Then for our entrees he had the King Salmon, which he said was nothing special.  I had the duck breast with foie gras, lets just say I honestly feel as though the forgot to season my plate.  The foie was delicious, but the duck breast, which is a staple menu choice for me, was extremely bland.  We did not bother staying for dessert, because we were so uncomfortably warm, we could not wait to get outside.  There are so many restaurants to experience if one does not stand out we will not go back.  I think it is safe to say we will not be back here.

The last two times I have ordered from Papas the pizza has been really substandard.  I will be looking elsewhere for my pizza from now on.  A real shame - Papas used to be the absolute best.  No more.

Me and my girl came here on 40c wing night Hoping to eat cheap. An order comes with 10 wings. We got an order for each of us and waters to drink. We only ate 10 wings between us and spent 19$.... The math and the price changes for taking home what you didn't finish and what you dont eat and leave is a clusterfu*k. I was so angry and confused. The service did NOTHING to clearify. I paid the 20 out of frustration.

This place is just not me. I wanted to go bc we would drive by and it's always packed. It could be for u then.
I ordered smoked mahi mahi w coleslaw potato salad and fresh veggies. I didnt touch the lettuce tomato and onions. There was no dressing w it.
My husband had a cheeseburger and ate my potato salad.
Fish is just quite smoky for me. I won't complain bc that's the name of the restaurant. Just not my thing. I did think of my dad who I'm sure would have loved it

Was here today for lunch and to watch some NFL games.  Great atmosphere.   Very neat menu.  I was really looking forward to trying some food.  However.  It seemed there were some kitchen issues today.  Every dish our great waitress ( Dee) brought out to a table was incorrect.  Lots of customers around us complaining.  So when our food arrived  and it was unsatisfactory I didn't have the heart to complain to her.  I ordered the Brie Castle sandwich.  It was missing both the Brie and the onions.  It had one tiny slice of apple and the toasted sourdough bread was so salty I could not eat it.   My husband enjoyed his philly cheese steak but they brought him the wrong sandwich first.  
All this being said I will come back again and give it one more try.   Our waitress was great, and the drinks were very good.

Went here with my dinner group last week. I should note that I am the one with the Southern roots, who knew what a 'meat and three' is, and recommended it. I was not impressed.
First off, I do not understand why we're standing in line to order, and being served on institutional-style metal trays. It adds nothing to the charm of the place, and it is certainly not 'southern' in any sense. 
The food was uniformly mediocre. I had ribs, which were kind of dry and VERY salty, mashed potatoes, which were reminiscent of what they serve at KFC, summer squash with a sunflower seed pesto--the highlight of the meal--and cornbread. I am so tired of places serving cold, obviously hours-old cornbread. If Red Lobster can get fresh hot cheese biscuits on your plate every five or ten minutes, why can't other places serve hot fresh cornbread? It is really only good fresh and hot; it is nothing but a disappointment otherwise. 
Two of my friends had catfish, which looked pretty much like nuggets, served (for some reason) on a piece of cold white bread. My other friend had the special Korean ribs, which he shared out to the rest of us, apparently not that impressed with them. They all had greens, which they said were 'alright.' 
Fortunately, the place is not expensive, so we didn't waste a lot of money. But I absolutely fail to understand the hype surrounding this hugely mediocre eatery. Can't see myself going back.

Got a gift card for this place from Costco a long time ago when I lived in Clearwater.  When I was visiting my ex-boyfriend in Dunedin last week, we decided to make it our night out before I left.  What a disappointment.  Too bad, it had such promise.  Oh yeah, the crab cakes were pretty good.  Everything else.. uh .nothing too impressive.  Probably wouldn't go back, especially for the price.  Glad I used up that old gift card.  The service was generally nice.  

.k
Good food but TERRIBLE service. Don't let them seat you in the back room unless you're prepared to wait over an hour for your food.

Where's my salad? Where's my soup?  Where's my server? I know where the rest of the staff is-standing in front of the G-d Damned front door sucking down cigarettes. Classy. This place needs a GM that can run a restaurant instead of a flock of half-wits that can't. 

I've been to Japan, too dude and this ain't it.

Not really sure what the fuss is all about?? 

We were in SB for an appointment and my friend had a burger craving. Searched yelp for burger places in the area and this had good reviews. 

Ok so we walked into what was apparently the bar area. He suggested we go upstairs to the dining room. We did and we stood a few minutes before anyone acknowledged us. 

The Hispanic gentleman working there came and seated us & seemed to be the only one actually working.  He asked one of the  young guy waiters to bring us menus which he did after a few minutes. 

Burgers were just ok. Not bad by any means but nothing to write home about. 

Service was just not that great. Had to ask for refills when our cups were bone dry. In a busy city like SB you need to at least have great customer service and this was not that great. 

Next time were in SB we will check out somewhere else. Won't go back here.

Today I went there and get something to go. I got one mango red tea with bubble, banana bubble tea and peach green tea with bubble. For food, I order a homestyle Fries chicken.
First, the mango red tea with bubble, the bubble in it feel like doesn't cook well, texture is soooo hard, not really like it. Before I work in a place were selling bubble tea, for my experience I know that the bubble were not well cook. And the drink is not really good too, because I can't taste any mango only the red tea, and the red tea taste bitter and astringent.
And the banana bubble tea, the taste sooooo powder.
However I think the peach green tea was fine.
For the chicken I think the taste was good but one thing worse is too dry, if it more juicy will be good.

But I know that this store is new, I hope it will be better later. Will go next time.

Checkers are rude and not helpful
Prices are wrong when you get to the register
When I questioned the prices they were rude and begrudged my inquiry
checkers are rude and not helpful
Not the best selection or quality Fruit and Veggie organics 
No helpful people to find what you may be look for.
Checkers are rude and not helpful  
Oddily the Customers were nice!  That is why I gave it 2 stars.
They must have horrible Management style for the employees to be like that 
But, the price problem is rampant at every Safeway I have been to.

I tried to have my first ever Eggs Benedict and BOY was I disappointed when I touched my fork to the soft head of the egg and experienced no ooze but instead a hard, crumbly yolk. BUMMER. Also, I was given only one half of a hard muffin but would have enjoyed the other half. Sue me and my carb loving heart!

My impression was that they didn't know how to accommodate the 6 of us and our different brunch orders while keeping my poached egg poachy (runny?). This wasn't an isolated experience, my friend ordered the same meal and same disappointingly hard yolk. Another friend had her spongy omelette arrive after we were done eating.

So I am still technically an Eggs Benedict virgin. Look out Bennys, here I come!

We ate dinner here tonight, myself and two girlfriends. This place came highly recommended. I made a reservation and we were seated. The place was bustling and pretty loud. We had to ask the waitress to repeat herself several times due to the noise level. We got the duck fries as an appetizer and they were tasty. Perfect crispy fries tossed with flavorful shreds of duck and topped with a creamy white cheese.  The entrees were a different story! I got the Veal Milanese for $24. What I got was two seriously overcooked veal cutlets on a bed of spring mix and drizzled with balsamic vinegar. The cutlets were overly breaded and almost burnt. I couldn't even taste the veal. My friend wanted me to send the dish back and called the waitress over. The waitress argued that the veal wasn't burnt, it was just the balsamic vinegar that made the breading so dark. A blatant lie. I don't send food back, so I did finish the dish, but it was a severely overpriced mistake on my part.  My friend got the wild boar meatloaf platter and it was awful  The meatloaf had a strange and tangy flavor,. I am familiar with wild boar, but this was not good.  She wasn't pleased with it. Our other dining partner got the tacos of the day, Mahi-Mahi.  She enjoyed them and said they were fresh and tasty.
I am a a Northeast girl and like to support local business, but was severely let down by the Blue Duck. It was busy, but the food certainly did not live up to the hype.  Service was inattentive at best. They couldn't wait to rush us out after we were finished the meal.  
Don't know if I'd give them another shot.

The room is cleanish, except that the sheets were clearly not changed - why do some hotels do that? Gross.

However, every single bit of foot traffic, door closing or car passing outside the room is heard well enough to wake me up. The "free wi-fi" has kicked me out and put me in quarantine about every 3 mins ALL NIGHT for accessing such malicious pages as Facebook and Yahoo Mail. The front desk, rather than fixing it, very rudely told me it's not their fault and redirected me to spend endless time on the phone with their IT dept, troubleshooting the issue. How the heck that falls on my shoulders is beyond me but calling IT was not a desired feature in my vacation package. 

Also, they were unable to check me in at check-in time and had no accommodations for my pup outside my room. I was told to sit in my car in the burning heat with my dog for almost 2 hours - "ma'am, we can't bother housekeeping until at least 3. At that time we can ask them if they're getting to your room". Three IS check-in. So they waited until I was supposed to be welcome to even see if there was room for me. There wasn't. At that time Housekeeping began to get to my room. What a ridiculous rule! So can I have a late check out to make up for the delay? Of course not. I am expected to leave promptly regardless of their deficit. 

There are plenty of other pet-friendly hotels in this area, and I bet most of them are far more people-friendly than this one. I will not be returning.

The yazoo porter on tap was good? That's about all I can say. 1) who adds a ton of nutmeg to mac&cheese? How can you ruin macq& cheese?? 2) I got the chicken platter---undercooked and bloody at the bone. The cooked parts had no flavor.will NOT be goin back. Nashville has many great rib joints. This ain't one!
.

Been going to gators for more than 10 years but in the last two years the food quality has gone down the tubes. The location is brilliant down by the water and the staff are friendly but alas the tools they have to work with are second rate. I'll keep checking in the coming years to see do things improve and report back October 2014.

well this is one of the better ones, although the one on dorsett rd is the best pasta house I've had. the salad can be really good just depending on the day you go, rolls are good and service is as well.

I've been coming here almost all my life. The restaurant past 3 years have gone downhill. The food was mediocre and service was poor. They are understaffed and the servers ignore your request and could care less. I won't be coming here unless they get new management.
Dinners came out in a staggered order.  Meatloaf was over cooked.  Strawberries on desert cut were cut on the same board as an onion.  Nice atmosphere for a beer but avoid the food.

Ordered delivery and it arrived on time. Ordered a NY strip that was over cooked, chicken Alfredo with extra sauce that we didn't receive, and a grilled (instead of breaded that we ordered) tenderloin without any toppings on the sandwich. Our total was $46 and most definitely wasn't worth it. We will try Dales one more time to give it a fair chance. If it turns out to be more of the same, we will stick with our usual places instead. 

Overall... not impressed and not worth the money.

We went on a Sunday at lunch time. We stood in line to order for about 1hour. Food was good, but a bit cold once when we got it. It took about 30min to get our food. We are from Texas, so we have high expections for BBQ. I would have rated it a 3/4, but unfortunately there was a tiny baby roach crawling across our table after we finished our food. I wish I could say we'd  be back, but most likely not.

We went on Sunday the 20th around 7pm.  I work in costumer service as well which is why the service there was so disappointing.  I know what is to be expected and we were never rude to our server. However, we were very upset when my friend got over charged and when we went back to ask why he gave us some bs excuses and made it seem like it was our fault. Awful experience.

I went here yesterday (a Saturday--my first mistake) around 4 pm for the first time.  I had never been to a D&B's before, so I was excited to check it out.  I don't have anything against kids, but I thought this place was for adults and was shocked at how many kids were there.  I'd say more then 50% of the customers were kids (probably under 10).  A lot of them weren't even playing the games they were sitting at or standing by, they were just there making it look like the machine was taken when it really wasn't.  We didn't want to eat there, we just wanted to get a drink and then start playing some games.  We stood at one end of the bar for at least 5 minutes without an acknowledgment from the bartender who was counting his drawer before deciding to divide and conquer. I moved to the other end while my husband stayed put.  After waiting at least 5 minutes at that end of the bar a manager finally came over to get my drink order but I let him get the girl's order who was waiting before me. Let me just say that, as I waited, I got a good idea of what was going on behind the bar. We had one bartender who seemed to be endlessly counting money, one barback who looked like relatively busy cleaning up, one bartender who looked crazy busy making drinks, and another barback who literally made circles behind the bar all while eating drink garnishes and doing nothing except looking extremely pilled out. 
After the manager got the girl's drink order who was next to me he just disappeared and I stood there for 10 (yes 10) more minutes waiting for a drink.  Finally I got pissed off and was ready to leave when my husband decided to wait for the drinks while I walked around.  It was 30 minutes before he got our drinks. That's ridiculous, I don't care how busy you are.  If he hadn't already put money on the game cards, we would've left. 
In my waiting period I went to the bathroom. Now, I'm not a princess when it comes to bathroom situations but there was no toilet paper, a wad of chewing gum stuck to the side of the stall, and paper towels exploding out of the trash can. My feet were also constantly sticking to the floor in the restaurant area. Overall, not adding to my already unpleasant opinion of this place.

Finally we played some games (the ones not perpetually occupied by 7 year olds) and I tried my best to spend the money on my card as quickly as possible so we could leave. It was "ok." I don't think I care to return but it was a decent way to occupy myself for a few hours.  I had more hopes for this place, though, and think they need to hire more staff and keep the place clean.

Drinks weren't great. My calamari came with fried broccoli and squash probably as fillers. Nothing special. Boyfriend found plastic on his burger with the Mac and cheese.  Service was slow, food and drink took forever and they weren't busy at all. Never coming back. Manager did comp the plastic wrap burger.

I'll preface this review by saying that I've had great experiences here for manicures and pedicures, which is why I'm giving 2 stars instead of 1. My review is based solely on my prenatal massage experience yesterday.

As a pregnant woman in my third trimester, I'm uncomfortable and in pain most of the time. My back and hips have given me the most trouble and I was given a generous gift certificate to have a prenatal massage here to help alleviate some of my discomfort. I was assured when I booked the massage that the man who would be doing it was very professional and is supposed to be the best. I beg to differ on both accounts. 

When he first took me back to the room, he explained to me that I would start off on my back. I explained to him that I'm not supposed to be on my back because it compresses some major blood vessels and I personally have a history of almost passing out because of this (it's also commonly accepted in the medical community that you're not supposed to be flat on your back in your 3rd trimester). The masseuse seemed to take offense to this, becoming very defensive and informing me that he's been doing this for over 20 years and has massaged approximately 18,000 women and he's never heard of this. He then informed me that I can't trust everything that the doctors tell me because they're just trying to scare me. I should have left at that point because clearly he and I were off to a bad start; however, I was still hopeful that, despite our differences, he might be able to help. Wrong, but I'll get to that. 

After reluctantly agreeing to prop me up a bit with a folded pillow, he went to work on my neck and shoulders. He was very rough, using a lot of pressure. As I grimaced through the experience, I told him that it was hurting, to which he replied, "well that's why you're here." I'm not generally a huge fan of massages but I really don't like them when they are painful and the masseuse doesn't ease up after being told that he's hurting a client. He then moved on to my arms and I felt like he was very rough, to the point where I almost snatched back my hands because they were hurting. My legs were next; however, this was less painful.  I should note that he told me that his wife informed him that he should "beat her up" during the prenatal massage that he gave her. If he gave her the same treatment, that's exactly what happened.

He then had me roll onto my side to (finally) work on my back using both stones and his hands. This was also a very painful process and it felt like he was causing bruising to my spine, rib cage, and all of the muscles in my back. I'm no expert, but I don't believe that imparting more pain is the way to relieve existing pain. When I left, I was in more pain than when I first entered the salon. Super.  

Today, my entire body feels like it is bruised. My back is actually worse, because in addition to the existing low back muscle pain, I feel like I have bruising in the area, too, as with the rest of my back, neck, and arms. In fact, when my husband hugged me this morning, I actually cried out because it was painful. I also must say that I don't necessarily trust that he avoided the pressure points that are supposed to be avoided during a prenatal massage. 

In all, I found the experience to be painful (and potentially dangerous) and the masseuse to be arrogant. I would never recommend anyone for a prenatal massage here.
Would not go back.  Overpriced.  Too many tourists.  Food really not that well executed.  Servers are good looking tho....

Bummer because we wanted to like it. Absolute worse margarita we've ever had in a restaurant.  It was not fresh and clearly was made with some pretty cheap mixer.  The cheese sauce appetizer was dripping like it was watered down.  The shrimp dish was pretty good and the kid's burrito seemed okay.  The spinach enc made you wish you were somewhere else.  The service was good and friendly, so it would be great if they worked on their food and drinks.

Was disappointed they didn't have the chicken chipotle pizza anymore from which I saw on the menu. And order came with thin crust and was never asked which type of crust I wanted. Taste is fair the spread and quality  of ingredients is good. Good for the price when you get two for one. Would like to explore other items on the menu and would say they should be more generous with their cheeses .
The drive thru has gotten increasingly slower. I think steak n shake's Drive thru is faster

The food is better than average and they do offer good pricing for lunch specials.  Problem is with the service.  The daytime waitress seemed disinterested in being there and was slow with refills, condiments, etc.  It wasn't like the place was busy, she just had zero personally.  Won't be back.

Note: My husband wrote this review which I posted under my Yelp account.
Note: we don't drink alcohol therefore we have nothing to say about the bar.

It's a nice little place but some work on the interior wouldn't hurt.

We arrived at 6pm on Saturday night and the place filled up just after we arrived. It became obvious the staff was beginning to struggle taking care of so many customers at once and they began to get testy and curt. 

We had to stop the server from rushing away after taking our drink order so we could place an order for the cheese dip appetizer. Beware the Cheese Dip appetizer; when it came to the table it was really cheese soup. When we asked for a different serving of it the waitress said it would all be like that; so we sent it back and had it removed from the tab.

The chips and salsa. The Salsa was fine for my wife as she likes it spicy. I don't but ate some anyway. The chips were cold, thick and not really fresh.

My wife ordered the Taco salad. She liked the tortilla salad bowl. The lettuce was shredded and not leaf but she said she liked it. The other items in the salad were one slice of Tomato and one small piece of Chicken which she had to shred. She put some of the salsa we already had on the salad for a little flavoring. I gave her my slice of Tomato and some sour cream from my plate No shredded cheese on the salad. Her rating of the salad: 3 out of 5 stars.

I ordered two Chimichanga's: One was Chicken and the other Beef. Neither had much sauce inside, such as red Chile sauce or enchilada sauce, therefore they were very dry. The outsides, which most recipes instruct "should be deep-fried to a crisp golden perfection" were instead white and soggy. Possibly they were soggy by being covered with cheese dip (aka cheese soup) or perhaps by being re-heated in a microwave and then covered by the cheese dip. The re-fried beans were passable, if a little flavorless. The obligatory portion of sour cream was placed on my shredded lettuce, which mixed in making most of it impossible to separate out and put on my Chimi's. The rating of my plate: 2 of 5 stars. The plate itself was hot, which keeps the food hot, so "points" for that.

Our decision: nothing to draw us back to San Jose in Webster.
Food took to long to arrive, and the food didn't really have much flavor, the plates were hotter then the actual meal, which makes me think that they microwave everything. I wouldn't come back.

Disappointing service and food. I went asking for a chili cheese hotdog and was told they didn't have any chili at the time, which was okay. I thought I'd just get a plain one instead. When I got my food about 20 minutes later, the hot dog was cold and extremely greasy and just gross. Not impressed.
Literally just received a bowl of breadcrumbs. Still looking for the mac and cheese in the bowl. Maybe I'll find it eventually... pretty unsatisfying.

OK, so I know you aren't expecting haute cuisine at place that looks so bland and has no atmosphere at all. This is a place for a quick lunch, nothing more. So none should be surprised to hear the food is just so-so. The wonton soup is the worst I've ever had, with wontons clearly made several days earlier, and too-salty broth. The noodles were just rubbery and filled with something reddish and nasty looking. And even though soup was the only thing I was ordering on 2 occasions, I still had to wait 10 minutes for them to ladle it out. So don't expect much from this place.

We have come to Beawon for many years. The food is very good but the service has definitely taken a turn for the worst. They had several change in management over the many years that we have come. Each change was positive overall. However this last change, the overall service efficiency has dropped by 50%. You dont feel too welcome when you walk in. They seat you and give you water or tea. Then it takes awhile to get served the side dishes. The busier they get, the more hectic it seems. Then the kicker is waiting for your bill. We had to wait for awhile just to get the bill and even longer to get my CC back. Their previous manager ran a very tight ship and servers were bouncing all over you. Current manager needs to wake up OR they will start losing patronage.

Workers were nice um didn't have chocolate chips and hash browns with cheese is government cheese (gross) and they forgot our syrup and many other things and were hard to tag down but food was cooked in 5 minutes which is nice and waffles are very yum even without toppings.

Not really a fan of this type of restaurants to begin with, as it's never what you think.  Went there a couple of weeks ago on a Friday night (late review).  The chef doesn't know me and will not know me any better by me simply telling the waiter that I don't care for clams or any other little facts that I share- so a meal is not created or prepared for me specifically!  The chef has made a few dishes, which you receive,  based on what you do and don't like on the general menu that they present to you.  The point of such a restaurant is too eagerly await the next course.  I did not find this to be the case- and in fact, each course was overly emphasized with heavy butters or sauces, that not only do you not get the true taste of what you are eating, but by the end of dinner, you need an alka seltzer to counter all the rich food- I did not find any of the courses to be more than just ok.  On top of this, the inside of the restaurant is very small and you are sitting very close to your neighbor and it lacks ambiance of any sort.  To top it off, there was a bug roaming around our table to I finally trapped it under my water glass.  When I told the waiter, he removed my glass and used his finger to squash and remove it- again-he used his fingers to kill and remove the bug off our white table cloth- not cool.  There was 4 of us and after dinner, a couple of drinks, and tip, our bill was over $1,000! Philadelphia has some tremendous restaurants and this was definitely not one of them.  And need I say, way over-priced.  The only reason that I gave this restaurant any stars at all was that the wait staff was very attentive.

Yesterday me and my partner went this place to try their food, and we do the Togo order. Being an Asian I did not feel the Asian way of hospitality, the one who took my order sounds not like happy what her job is. Food are alright not that great. I hate giving a bad review but this is my experience.

First time eating from here, I ordered through ubereats. All I ordered was 8 wings , Mac & cheese , & a sauce for my wings. My total including uber fee and tax was $21.84. The food got to me in less than 35 minutes which I really appreciate however I only received 7 wings.. On top of that it had no fork in the bag for my Mac & cheese and no napkins ! This place is a little to pricey to be acting so cheap, I will not be back.

Publix is notorious for having great customer service.  This one, not so much.  The employees in general are rude.  I've received less than stellar service from the bakery, the deli, the pharmacy, and the cashiers.  I'll stick to shopping at Publix in Nashville because they are great.  If I'm in Smyrna, maybe I'll try Kroger instead.

This place looks terrific, but what a major disappointment. The beer is good, but the food is below average and the service is absolutely dreadful. We went there from work with a group of 6 people and they were relatively busy which I was happy about it, because they are fairly new and you want them to succeed.

But even though they had available tables, it took about 5-6 minutes to get seated....then another 10 minutes before someone took our drink orders...then another 5-6 minutes before someone took our food orders. Now if you have an hour-plus for Lunch, this is off to a bad start. But then the real problems started...we had one of those waitresses who doesn't write down the orders....because she presumably remembers everything....WRONG

Well about 20 minutes more goes by and we are now there for about 40 minutes with no sign of food. So one of us asks the waitress how much longer it will be...and she says she will check. Then we hear nothing from her for another 10 minutes. When we flag her down again she explains that they have been busier than they expected and she's sure it will be soon...then she asks what we ordered! To which I replied "don't you know?" She then proceeds to confirm all of our orders, writing it down this time.

In another 20 minutes she brings our food...which was so-so at best. I had a simple hamburger which you would expect to be hard to mess up, but it was extremely well done and the meat quality was so-so. So overall we were there for nearly TWO hours for a simple Lunch...and it wasn't very good. No plans on returning...though the Beers were good.

5 stars for the actual store, 2 or honestly 1 for the sandwich. I ordered a delicious sounding spinach wrap and then was given, and didn't check which is my fault, an utterly soggy and skimpy sauerkraut sandwich. The bread was completely wet and soggy and there was barely anything on it. For the price of the sandwich, I'm better off going back to NYC where I live and going to any deli. Rollin Oats is a lovely health food store though. It's pricey but is very nice. Would skip the cafe though and go anywhere else in St. Pete. Service is so, so slow as well.

Was invited for a practice opening, was not pleased being placed at a large table in the middle of the restaurant with strangers. My wife and I had sit across from each other which was impossible to talk to each other without yelling since it was so loud. The food was decent but nothing to encourage others to go to. Our waitress even though she was nice still kept on asking if we wanted our plates cleared out before we were done and we spent less than a hour there. We ate at the joining restaurant Ford's Garage a day earlier and had a MUCH better experience food, service and atmosphere.

Ok, so my review may have been accurate at the time but I think I should update it.. I had to be my father's advocate. Its my job.. No biggie, that's what happens in stressful times. 
You have to be their voice.. These poor staff are overworked. There are many nurses who really do care, they're just overwhelmed. I feel that I as a person had to relate to them too.. My father unlike many of the residents of manor care is pretty coherent, he can express his wants and needs. There are many, many folks who don't have that ability.
 I'm a pretty passive person I feel like my review may have been a little passive aggressive. So here's the skinny... 
            CONS
The staff is overworked. Call lights will go unanswered for an extended amount of time.
Some of the staff would rather not be there, and probably shouldn't be, but they are. But you will have that anywhere you go. 
If you need something for a loved one, demand it! Ask ask ask ask ask ask ask ask again and again.. There are plenty of people walking from point A-B... But it's like nobody is available.. Busy, busy. You almost feel bad for asking to throw one more thing on their plate.. ( like asking for a glass of ice)
Social workers are.. Busy.. Too busy to do their jobs. So, you will have to do it, you will have to do all the leg work depending upon your situation. Ours was very difficult and our "social worker" was almost useless... 
FOOD IS HORRIBLE on most days. 
           PROS
PT TEAM IS AMAZING!!!!!!
HUGE shout out to the wonderful physical therapists at this establishment! 
Matt & Michelle.. You guys were outstanding. My dad was so encouraged working with you. 
You guys have no idea the positive impact you have made on my father in such a short amount of time. I don't know if you will ever see this, but I thank you. One day he'll come back, walking. I really appreciate the care Michelle has shown for my father, pushing him just enough. Thank you so much! 
Holly the nursing director is amazing, thank you for your kindness. 
BONNIE! The woman who wears many hats!! Thank You for all your help with transport! You are amazing. Seriously. I'm forgetting so many loving people, so many people deserve recognition.. And yelp isn't the place for it.. But hey, I appreciate the ones who did what they could. With all that being said.. Love your loved ones.

I wanted to give this place a try because of the good reviews but was disappointed. I found the food to be way overpriced and very average. I tried the chicken salad and the vegetarian grilled cheese sandwich. While the grilled sandwich was good, the chicken salad was average. Pricey for what you get though.

What has happened to this place? I ordered my usual, the Kiss of Eel and Spicy Crazy rolls. They were both warm. Eww. It gets worse. The fish was anything but fresh. I think they tried to cover that with about a cup of oyster sauce on each roll! And the roe was bleeding pink. 
Service was great! Two stars for my server. That is all.

It took us a long time to try Zagol, and we were really excited for our dinner there; unfortunately, we left really disappointed.

The main reason for our displeasure was the really, really terrible service.  From the brusque greeting we got upon entering the restaurant, to the twenty minutes it took to cash out the check before we could leave, we spent our entire time in Zagol feeling totally unwelcome.  When we were seated, there were only a few other people there, but several groups came in after us and many of them were getting their food orders taken before we had even seen the waitress.  It got so bad that when she went to help the table next to us, they spoke up and told her, "That other table has been here a lot longer than us."  Thanks, other people!  If you hadn't been so kind, we might still be sitting there!  Ordering our drinks turned into a long list of what they were out of that night.  Even so, we should have each ordered multiple drinks because once we got our food, she disappeared again, leaving us to eat a pile of spicy food WITH NO WATER REFILLS!  To highlight all of this, the dining room was too cold.  Most of the people in the restaurant left their coats on at the table.  Bad form, Zagol.  I know heating that big space must be pricey, but that's one of the costs of doing business.  Pump up the thermostat a bit.

If it hadn't been for the service, we probably would have really enjoyed our meal.  We tried the Key Wat, Beg Tibs, and Mesir Wat.  Everything we ate was delicious, and the flavors quite different from anything else available in town.  We enjoyed eating with the Injera, although about halfway through the meal it became a bit too much, and I just wanted to finish off the remainder of the food with a fork.  We would have asked for utensils at that point if we had someone to ask.  Daughter was particularly fond of eating with her hands: a warning to other parents, though--if your kid isn't into spicy food, they probably will be very unhappy here.

Overall, we probably won't be back, which is unfortunate, since Zagol has a tasty offering, and Reno is really lacking for diversity in ethnic foods.  Unfortunately, they are a bit too costly to bother with that poor of an experience again.

It's either a hit or a miss at this salon honestly. 
My first time here wasn't the best, I didn't have an appointment so I waited almost 2 hours. The stylists are very comfortable with one another because they were having multiple conversations with each other(over the music) And that makes the work take longer than usual , especially when your in a rush, && when the sew in's are only $50.. You expect to just get in & get out. 
That brings me to recently. I scheduled my appointment For 9am, I know that's the time they're posted to open, but I didn't get into the actual salon chair until about 9:50(give or take a few minutes) .. After my braids for my sew in were done, the stylist doing my hair switched me to another stylist so she could do another customers hair, I'm assuming she had another customer for the timeframe because she was LATE( I had an issue with that) THEN the stylist I was switched to did not seem like she knew what she was doing(or had some struggles with the threading) 
So like I said HIT OR MISS. ‍

Will do in a pinch, which is how I got here in the first place.  I got a slice of  spinach mushroom.  Nice thin crust, well heated, good cheese but much too much garlic and salt.  Also, they just dropped the spinach on top and put it in the oven to heat, so the spinach got completely dried out and I had to pull it off before eating the slice.

Ok...  So I've eaten at Vetri probably 8-10 times so I'm very familiar with it.  Usually crush the grand tasting with wine flight on the weekend.  This place used to be one of the best spots in the city, but since Marc Vetri has left to go full Steven Starr money grubber opening up crappy spot after crappy spot (Except Amis that is banging) the place has just dropped off.  It's not like the food is bad, but it's the biggest rip-off in the city.  I've probably only paid for a bill here once or twice, which is the only reason I have gone so many times.  If it was on my dime forget it.  I remember one meal which friends were paying for, we had a goat course which was just dry and horrible, and we had the spinach gnocchi which I've had there 6 times and they were tasteless and mushy.  I'm sorry, if you're charging north of $300 per person, then everything better be spectacular every time - no it better be LEGENDARY every time.  It's just not the same as it used to be. Vetri used to hand paint the menus and plan all these ridiculous menu items, now it's basically standard stuff with the occasional new item.  Wine flight is usually unremarkable as well.  Save your money and go to Spraga for the chef's tasting counter, or Vernick (crushes all Vetri restaurants) or La Croix for 4-courses for $75.  Way better ways to spend your money than think you're the coolest kid on the block going to this wack overpriced pretentious donkey paradise.

Food is as bland as the ambiance. We were asked how spicy we wanted our vegetarian dishes.  We asked about 1-10 scale and were told that "8" would be very spicy.  We couldn't taste any heat whatsoever.  If you like spicy food, ask for a 15 on their 1-10 scale and you might get lucky.  We won't be going back, especially with the TVs playing sports in the corners. Is it an Indian restaurant or a makeshift sports bar?

Yes, at first I rated Marquita very highly. However, with a closer look I found many areas which were not cleaned at all. Corners  in each room still had dust on the floors and baseboards.
