Floors were cleaned (they're hardwood) but piles of wet dirt/dust from the floors remained in the corners and behind furniture which created a putrid smell which I had to track down. That's how I found the dirt. I really don't want her back. This was my 2nd booking with Homejoy. The first cleaner never showed at all and after a poorly cleaned apartment, I'm rethinking my using Homejoy again at all.

This is my second visit for a lotus and i have to say they have both been horrible. I had leftover waxes everywhere that she did not clean off and left patches unwaxed here and there (unbelievable! I thought the lotus was supposed be "entirely bare"). My friend is loyal to this place so before I decided on this second visit I wanted to give it a try again..sadly to say, I will not be returning. If I had noticed before I left, I would have definitely have said something to the receptionist! Extremely disappointed.

I'm relatively new to Nashville and haven't found a good eyebrow place yet. I read the reviews for this place and decided to risk it because I was already at Green Hills mall. The end result was okay considering the price ($11 versus $20+ everywhere else in Nashville). However, there were areas where the lady missed several long hairs. I debated asking her to go over these areas but decided I'll just do it myself and not risk having my brows too thin. When it came time to pay, I have a little more than 15% since I thought that was fair for a sub par job and the lady started questioning me about my tip amount. The whole thing was very uncomfortable. 

Just know what you're getting into- you probably will have to go back and tweeze some hairs and stand your ground when it comes to tipping. But for the price, it may be an okay last resort (maybe...).

Have you ever been to a brewpub with concrete floors, plain but tasteful wooden chairs and tables, a bar for tasting and maybe some shelves along the wall for t-shirts and such?  

Huh? What, all of them fit this description?!?  Hmmm, I guess you're right, now that I think about it. 

An overwhelming feeling of boring deja vu is not a great way to kick off a visit to a place that's supposed to be fun, but we forged ahead anyway.  We were just stopping in for a couple of beers after dinner.  The bartender was super friendly and quick with a taste, which alone is a big plus at a brewpub.   Unfortunately, though,  we tried three beers; one was not too good (lager), one was pretty bad (a mint/chocolate/burnt toast abomination), the third was very good (dunkel).  

There's not much to make us want to come back, except it's the closest brewpup to home.   If you're out to go visit a lot of brewpubs, you can check this one out but I can't recommend putting it high on your list.

Inquired Friday afternoon Aug.18 about a Dodge Challenger. Chatted online and was asked what time could I come in Sat. morning. I told them about 10:30. Then got a call from Tony Zombrowski about 7:00 P.M. and was told not to come in at 10:30 because car would not be prepped for delivery till 1:00 P.M. Sat. Got an appraisal from Friendly Kia on my trade. Got to dealer about 1:30 and was told car had been sold. Best part is the car was still up on service rack when I arrived. If I went earlier like I should have, I would have been able to buy the car. Soo-how many times has this happened?? I was told that the online/ used/ new car departments don't communicate very well. WHAT A LOUSY EXCUSE. This is not the first time for this game they play. Went to buy a Optima awhile back and guess what!! Almost same game the cars we considered were already sold. We were talked into a newer model and spent more money! Are you catching my DRIFT?? I am fuming at this so-called FRIENDLY Dealership. Forget about customer service. They got me.

I love Ethiopian restaurants, and have been to several.  I was so excited to see that Reno has one now....

We went for my birthday; I we were just about the only people in the chilly, cavernous restaurant.

Service was prompt.  We ordered two large combo meals, expecting something special.  Unfortunately, they were out of collards, so we ended up with green beans (frozen???)  The other dishes were okay, but small.  We also ordered Ethiopian tea (remember, it was chilly in there.)

The waitress brought us more bread when we asked, but never did refill our teacups.  Maybe it's $2.00 per glass?

I enjoyed our dinner, but the total was way out of our price range.  We haven't been back.

This was my first visit to the restaurant.

I ordered the glazed salmon entree served over warm greens with a beurre blanc for dinner, and it was pretty terrible. I should have probably stuck with the pizza, but I was having a craving for fish. The salmon had a very fishy taste, which implies to me that it was not fresh. Luckily I had enough salt and pepper on hand to make it somewhat edible.

My server was excellent and very attentive.

I was a bit confused by the interior decor. Three flat-screen TVs were showing sports but it didn't have a sports bar feel at all.

If I were to go back, it would be because of the server, and I would try the pizza and stay away from the entree selections.

THIS IS FOR BAR ONLY!!

OK, I like this place.  Please see my prior review.  That said--my wife and I stopped in for a drink (without kids!  Woot!) before dinner at another restaurant.  I ordered the PERFECT MARGARITA--which I have had and liked before.

The menu CLEARLY tells you what is in this margarita--and it is NOT TRIPLE SEC, but PATRON CITRONGE.  My confusion came from the fact that I was sitting at the bar, chatting with the bartender (who was very pleasant and nice) as he made our drinks--and I very clearly saw him pour a cheap well triple sec (maybe even Dekuyper?!?!).

This is quite disappointing --as if you pay $9 for a margarita I do not expect there to be the cheapest quality ingredients.

They have a nice showroom and decent (not amazing) selection.  Unfortunately, the customer service is just awful.  The staff acts like they don't want to bother with you, and even if you can persuade one to talk to you they'll wander off in mid-sentence to make a phone call or talk to someone else.  Then, if you order something, it's not in when they say it will be and, even after they call you to tell you it's in, they waste half an hour first telling you that it's not really in (and looking at you like you're trying to put something over on them) until they actually look for (and find) it.   I like to patronize independent businesses, but this one is a challenge.

Stopped by for lunch after a flea market visit.   Was never told about the specials which included fish and chips which I definitely would have ordered had I known about it.  Only found out about the specials from over hearing a server at a near by table after our order was already out in.  Ordered the $14 seared Tuna app which was a very small portion of toughest most chewy tuna I've ever had.  

Beers arrived promptly though.

If you're a fancy cocktail drinker this place is a four or possibly five star place.  If you're a beer drinker then this place is a bit silly as it is just another fancy schmancy cocktail bar that is just way too cool to carry Abita Amber and other well loved beers.  They do have beer here but only expensive ones and some I even like but if I want an Abita Amber I want an Abita Amber!  

The bar is pretty cool with lots of fresh herbs lined up along the bar that they use in the drinks and if I was going to drink a fancy cocktail this would be a pretty cool place to do it.  The bar is a bit narrow and small and there is a larger lounge type area with cushy chairs and couches.  There were VERY few people here for a Friday during happy hour time when I was here but it seems they don't have happy hour pricing so maybe that's why.  I liked the ambiance of this place alright and if there were actually people here and if they had Abita Amber I might even frequent it but otherwise I probably won't be back here unless I get some really unusual craving for a fancy cocktail.

On Labor Day morning most of the French Quarter restaurants that were open were full with lines out the door. To prevent a wait, we ended up at Cafe Pontalba which is on Jackson Square, tourist central. 

Service was just sleepy and nonchalant; maybe the server had a long night out. The food was super bland. The crawfish omelet and grits looked as sad as it tasted. It needed a lot of hot sauce to try and Cajun spice it up. Maybe they were trying to hit mid-American taste sensibilities. Except for being seated at a table by open French doors, nothing was memorable. The coffee was ok. 

It was a disappointing end to an otherwise fantastic weekend vacation.

Some of the people were really great there. I liked working w/Josh & Eileen. My car was brought here by force due to an accident on the Turnpike, and them being contracted w/the PATPC. So I had no choice but to go there or drop tons of money for a tow I never asked for. Thanks. After my car was determined to be a loss, they gave my insurance company a hassle and refused to release the car to them unless they kept my personal belongings including my license plate. Even though I authorized the release to my insurance company. 

I was able to retrieve my personal belongings and tags after a phone call through my insurance company. But the entire time, it felt shady and slimy. There was absolutely no reason to keep my personal belongings. And the main person who kept trying to "Sell" me on Robs Collision seemed very shady and disinterested in what I had to say. Only waiting for their turn to speak each time. I am still not certain what they were able to do for me, but overall, if you have to go to Robs, they may be fine. But being forced to go there and then having the experience of having to retrieve my personal belongings after the fact even though I told them not keep them and release them with my car, left a bad taste in my mouth. Thanks Josh & Eileen. You two were really great and made the experience better.

I was really excited about visiting this restaurant. As others have said, the service was very slow. I ordered the beef cubes, only to be told five minutes later they don't have any. Not the end of the world. I ordered the Pho. The sideplate came out with only one stallk of basil leaves. Again, not the end of the world and I'm sure if I had asked for more it would have been provided. The broth itself was exquisite. However,it was a little light on the meat. That is unacceptable! It was almost impossible to get my waitresses attention. There's also a serious language issue. And as others have mentioned, my water glass remained unfilled throughout the course of my visit. I'll wait a month and give it another try.

Chocolate was disgusting and pineapple was OK. It's cash only, not cheap, and disorganized, so I'm really upset NPR convinced us to make this detour http://www.npr.org/2016/07/31/488150522/johns-water-ice-a-philadelphia-institution for their nostalgia.

This is the worst hotel I have ever stayed in. 
Don't get me wrong, the common areas are beautiful- the restaurant and bar are sexy and great people watching HOWEVER the rooms are terrible!! They are cold and clinical. Drab grey and puce green, very sterile. Cold cork floors, no rugs, it feels like you are staying in a mental institution or orphanage. Go for drinks, not to stay. Very disappointing.

***giving an extra star for Diamond at the front desk who was very sweet and accommodating.

I had been looking forward to checking this restaurant out, but have to say the food was overly-priced and heavy on the salt. This would have been excusable, but the bartender was outwardly rude...maybe from all the shots he was doing behind the bar. Not going to return.

Came here on my last night in New Orleans because it had a nice view of Jackson Square and it was convenient.  The hurricanes taste like fruit punch - I don't think that they had any alcohol in them.  I ordered the grilled chicken, and it was NOT grilled - it was pan fried.  Very unappetizing.  Don't recommend coming here as there are MUCH better places to eat in the area - sadly disappointing.
Bread was stale at the ends. Bathrooms were dirty. Bathrooms alsi didn't have a changing table in the men's Nore the womans. Glad I'm just passing through town.

I love Ethiopian food and was excited to check this place out.  We walked in at 7:15 on a Saturday night and were the only customers, but still had to wait a few minutes to be seated.  Once seated, the service was prompt and the right level of attentiveness.  I ordered my staple, the Doro Wat with extra injera, and my companion ordered the Gored Gored.  Both dishes were heavy on the spice and low on flavor--exactly the opposite of what I've experienced elsewhere.  The portions were also pretty small--I easily could have finished all mine, plus the extra injera (which has never happened to me before at an Ethiopian restaurant), had the food been so underwhelming.  I felt the price was a bit high for the what we got.  I probably won't be back.

Thai Cuisine & Cafe was empty on the Friday night when we ate here, and for good reason. The waitress didn't seem to know the menu, and the food was really run of the mill. The red curry was runny, and the drunken-noodle had an off flavor. To top it all off, the rice was dry and the prices high for low-quality food.

Service here was terrible.  Barely got our water refilled, and the sushi took over 40 minutes to arrive (and we only got 3 rolls).

This location also did not have a brown rice option.

The only perk to this place is the convenience of it when doing shopping around the Elmwood area.

The bar/restaurant has a really nice and hip vibe to it.  I only wish the food and service equaled the decor.  The food was mediocre at best and the service was terrible.  The restaurant was not busy, but still the food took extremely long .  I heard and saw another customer complaining about the same issues.  The restaurant needs to step up their game, as the hotel is not within walking distance to any other eating establishment .

OK, some locals swear by this place.... they love it,  are addicted to it, and come here more than once a week.... those are the people I like to call... dumb.

What you should be doing: coming here with your friends once or twice to have a tiny drink and some fun on the machines just so that you say you did it. 

What you shouldnt do:  Come here all the time and slowly let your sole and happiness die with every push of a button. 

Those guys aren't wearing trucker hats to be ironic... they actually like them. Chain smoking? You don't know the meaning until you've been here.  

As for the coffee house.. all I have to say is Chinese Food.... and never never never get it.  The lemon chicken, I shit you not, is chicken with lemon meringue pie filler drizzled over it.

Come on folks... you can do better than this. Raise your standards just an inch.

Oh, I gave two stars for the cocktail waitresses who put up with all the knuckle-draggers.

Lunch was pretty sub-standard. 
Ordered a Bento C Sushi and Teriyaki Chicken. Also a Bento D Beef, Chicken and Tempura. The Bentos come with a side salad. Dressing was very salty and just tasted of soy sauce. Tempura was oily and the batter was chewy. Teriyaki chicken looked like it was made ahead of time and just reheated. The batter was dried out so it was very crunch and hard to chew. What little chicken there was inside though was moist. Sushi was just ok. Tuna smelled fishy and was warm. Salmon was fresh.

I had heard great reviews but was sadly disappointed. The service was lacking and our water was never refilled through our entire dinner. The quality of the food for the price could have been better as well.

I had no idea that they moved.  The store looks nice.  A sales clerk helpfully assisted us in finding the books we were looking for.  

And then we got to checkout.  Friday evening around nine.  The store was moderately busy.  One person working at checkout, and he was helping a woman with a return or exchange or something.  Another salesperson was at check-out when we first walked up, but she walked away.  We waited ten minutes.  People began to form in line behind us.  No one came up to assist the poor man alone at the check-out, and he didn't call anyone for assistance.  We considered leaving.  

Finally he paged for help.  No one arrived.  He paged again.  Then he told us we could check out at the cafe or in children's.  REALLY?  The sales clerk in the children's section couldn't have told us that when we were back there?  We trrudged over to the cafe, and the girl working there was not happy to ring up our books, but she did it.

It's terrible to judge a place on one experience which may have been a total anomaly, but we both said we would not return.

I went in today, asked for a gel polish change and to fix 2 acrylic nails. I had the holo powder on my nails, and they said they'd match it. The one nail is fine, the other doesn't match the others, as I have the stiletto nails. Then we get to color, guess what? They don't match! Then she tried arguing with me that they did. And was saying she didn't want to rip me off- honestly, it felt like more of a rip off that she didn't want to fix it. But she eventually did. The polish matches, but my left pointer finger is bubbly as hell. This cost $27. The decor was nice, though.

The past 3 times we gave been here the service has been poor. Waiting at least 10 minutes before anyone comes to the table and even longer to get drink orders back from bar.  Burger and fries were cold when they cane out to table. Burger was so salty we couldn't hardly eat it. Server was nit attentive at all. She only came back one time to check on drinks. Had to ask for our ticket. This use to be our favorite place, not anymore or at least until things improve.

This place has really gone downhill from what it was. The orders are frequently wrong for pickup. Cheese is missing. Meat is burnt. They don't give you the drink for your order unless you ask, even though it's on your order and they are asking you to pay for it. Sweet tea is often empty in one of the containers. 

Most of these issues are pickup issues, not dine in issues. Also the service is much better on weekdays vs weekends.

Ordered delivery Saturday and was pleasantly surprised at how fast the food came and how friendly the delivery driver was. Unfortunately -- that's where the good stuff ends. My mom had shrimp scampi that tasted like someone literally threw shrimp on an entire box of spaghetti noodles and poured lemon juice on it. The jumbo shrimp were also more like medium shrimp with jumbo sized breading.  Really disappointed, especially given the reviews.

I just gave Beawon my second and last attempt.  The first time I ate there I had one of their lunch specials with bulgogi and some banchon.  There was some fried rice that came along side and it was depressingly mushy.  I don't know if they were off or what, but mushy fried rice is a travesty.  Seriously, if this white girl can master fried rice, I would expect anyone could.  Apparently not the chef at Beawon.

So along comes today. I had a craving and as Beawon is closest to the job, they won.  Alas, I ordered the bibimbap.  As I was not in the restaurant I asked for just plain ol' bibimbap.  I was asked if I wanted the bibimbap cold, and I said, "No, bibimbap, without being heated on the stone, but warm please."  I picked up and was given cold veggies, a cold fried egg (with way too much unset white for my taste) and a block of cool rice.  I might have been able to forgive all that, after all this is why God invented the microwave...but it also didn't taste that great.  The veggies were very salty.  There was maybe one tablespoon of meat, and bah, just not the best bibimbap. Not even close.

Disappointing.... If you're going to Beawon, it sounds like you should eat in, and from the sounds of it, you should order some variety of barbecue.  I fear the mushy rice and the over salted and yet still more or less flavorless bibimbap.

Not a great night at Sake Café.  My companions both had sushi rolls for their dinner.  They both thought the sushi was very dry, almost as if it had been pre-made for quick service.  The shrimp tempura roll was also filled with a large amount of lettuce, which I have never seen before and wasn't very welcome.  I had the chicken katsu entrée.  The chicken was also on the dry side, as was the rice that accompanied it.  The dipping sauce reminded me of Bullseye BBQ sauce.  Overall, a very uninspired meal, definitely not a priority to return.

I ordered an acai bowl and the two girls who were working wouldn't stop talking to each other and I was waiting for 10 minutes. Then, when the girl started making my bowl three girls walked in and she decided to help them instead of continuing to make mine. She talked to them for 5 minutes and told them all about the smoothies and what is good, completely disregarding the fact that I had ordered a long time ago and just wanted her to finish my damn bowl. The service here is so bad. I wish I didn't come and I'm definitely not coming back no matter how good the bowls are.

Went there last night for the Trombone Shorty concert.  It was the opening show of the theater's new season.

Pros:
-Theater is beautiful
-The show was great

Cons:
-They won't let you bring drinks to your seats even though they have a bar right outside the doors and serve drinks right before the show starts.
-Downstairs bathrooms are small.  They say larger bathrooms were upstairs but I didn't walk all that way to check them out.
-The legroom in the seats is small.  If you need to get out during the show, everybody has to stand up while you try to walk past and not step on anybody's feet.  It was an ordeal and everybody gets irritated.
-They do not allow you to stand up and dance during the show.  They have ushers they literally run at a full sprint and tell you to sit down.  Eventually the band told everybody to stand up and we did and the ushers couldn't do anything at that point.  I felt like I was in the movie Footlose where dancing was banned.
-You are not allowed to take pictures OF ANY KIND.  That includes cell phones.  They have a Nazi female Photo Usher who will run run up on you and get in your face if they see you hold up your cell phone to take a picture.

I think most of the patrons are season seat holders and are likely retired persons.  Most of them likely dont take cell phone pictures.  In this day and age the theater personnel need to get with it and allow photos.  Everybody under 50 years old takes cell phone pictures at concerts to create memories of the experience.

This theater needs to rethink some of its old fashioned rules.  The show was great but the overzealous ushers left a bad taste in my mouth.

Perhaps we had high expectations for this new Thai restaurant, as it had 4.5 star rating, but we really didn't enjoy the food. We left hungry and $30 lighter, but we like to say that we gave it a shot. While the service was great and staff friendly, their Pad Thai with tofu was pretty weak. Even after a thorough dose of crushed red pepper flakes, the flavor was pretty non-existent. I get that traditionally Pad Thai can be a concoction of whichever ingredients you have on hand, but the dish had no flavor and the tofu was pretty much uncooked. The wife ordered some kind of curry dish. The flavor was good, but it was very, very watered down. I prefer my curry to be more of a sauce with a bit of consistency. Their curry dish had the consistency of Thai Iced tea. We may give it another shot in the future, but most likely as a last resort.

The fact that this place didn't die 15 or 20 years ago is a testament to: A. The lack of dining choices in the Bryn Mawr/Rosemont area B. The number of intoxicated 20 somethings who don't care about the food C. Main Liners are creatures of habit who are too lazy to drive five more minute for a better meal. Image a TGIFridays, but with worse food. In the bar, they even have self serve taps at the tables, which is handy because the service was also pretty bad. I get dragged here for group events once or twice a year. That is too much.

It was okay. Nice to be able to see parts of NOLA that I wouldn't have otherwise seen. However, we spent more time in the parking lot due to poor planning on the company's part than during the cemetery stop. Service FAIL.

This review is long overdue. I stayed at the eagle inn as part of my honeymoon. I stayed two nights. Once in the San Clemente room. This was a nice big suite and it came with its own private garage space and a large spa tub. It was spacious however it felt a little outdated. 

The negative review is because the other night I stayed in one of their regular rooms and it was not a good experience. The room size was small, the TV outdated, the carpet looked old, and the bed was not comfortable. They do offer complimentary breakfast with your stay but I'm probably not coming back.

Very nice atmosphere and the staff is very friendly.. But the food was just OK. We got guacamole and queso with our chips.. Both bland and not enough flavor. The entrees were good portions (even lunch size) but again, lacked in flavor. My friends and I all got different dishes but they all looked and tasted the same. I don't think I'd go back again soon.

Overrated. Everything on the menu is fried. They are known for the crab fries, which is nothing more than french fries with old bay on them. The portions are average. The BF ordered an extra marinara sauce for the mozzarella sticks when we got the bill there was a $1.00 charge  when questioned about it the  waiter said if I could take off the $1.00 I would. (the  waiter didn't tell us there was an additional charge nor was it posted on the menu) No problem the $1.00 gets deducted  from your tip.

The friendliness of the staff was exemplary, so I'm sorry for the low review. The crepes were delicious, but I really have had better and for a fraction of the cost. Though the quality was high, I did not expect to pay almost $30 for a crepe and a cup of coffee. To clarify, I did ask to make one of the omelette options into a crepe. I was so thankful for the flexibility of the restaurant to accommodate this request and they did warn me that there would be an additional charge, but I really wish that they had told me upfront that it would be $5 extra. I think that is a bit too much, considering it is more than a third of the cost of the omelette.

I came with a group of nine and we had a good time, but others in my group experienced the "surcharge surprise" at the end as well. Perhaps we should have asked upfront for more detailed costs, but I think the add-ons were overpriced regardless. I want to repeat that the staff was perfectly attentive and kind to us and there were no mishaps with serving our large group. This is just a case of the food not quite matching the price we paid. For the bill total at the end, you would have thought we had a fancy brunch of "best ever" crepes, but they were just OK in a mediocre setting.

The place is decorated nicely and always very clean and the workers have been nothing short of nice. However, I usually get a gel manicure done and the past 2 times I went, my nails peeled off within the first 24 hours. I am very unhappy with the quality of the nail polish!

nice location and comfortable small room. for the $350/night i would expect more i n amenities than some beat up beach cruisers. no hottub, no wine in the afternon, and  skimpy breakfast of lox and bagels or yoghurt. The service was good but not worth the price

This store is called SAVE Mart... but I have no idea why they call it that. These prices are higher than Safeway in Tahoe City. It's clean and well lit and is at a convenient location, but holy smokes, bring MONEY

We returned to Jones for a second time because the first visit (two months ago) was delicious! I was really looking forward to it. The menu changed slightly so what I wanted was no longer available, but that was okay with me. I started off with the matzo ball soup. It was so scalding hot I couldn't even eat it. While waiting for it to cool, I shared some Caesar salad with my husband. The lettuce was overly chopped and slightly bitter.  There was hardly any dressing on it and had little taste. I went back to my soup and it was still absurdly hot. I asked for ice and put some in. When I got to taste it, it was a huge letdown. The matzo balls were complete mush, no flavor and it had a vegetable broth instead of chicken (which is typically the norm.) For dinner, I had the Maryland crab cake that came with shoe string fries and coleslaw. It was delicious but I was disappointed there was only one. Fries were good and cole slaw was fantastic but I was again disappointed in the serving size. I got about 3 forkfuls of slaw and that's all. Dessert was another let down. I ordered the peanut butter cup brownie sundae. The brownie was a bitter dark chocolate, and the peanut butter cups had an awfully fake taste. The ice cream was practically non-existent and it was mostly whipped cream covering the entire dish.  My husband has the bacon cheeseburger for dinner. He loved it and it was enormous. He had vanilla ice cream for dessert that was no lie, the size of a golf ball
I also ordered the special drink- pink grapefruit margarita, that had zero pink grapefruit taste. It tasted like a regular margarita. I had two because it was still a good margarita but trust me, there wasn't even a hint of pink grapefruit in it. Our entire meal cost us $97. I do not think we will be back.  It was a very disappointing second experience. Service was okay and the place is very noisy.
Food was way too expensive. Wait staff was rude and Didn't know the menu. Cool vibe, but missed opportunity.

I usually love this place, but I had a really disappointing experience on Saturday, which is too bad, because we were trying to show it off to an out-of-town friend. Maybe the trick is to order drinks, because this time we just went for s'mores and coffees. I ordered a decaf. Our waiter explained that it would come in a French Press, and asked whether that was okay. Of course! But he shortly arrived with a coffee in a paper cup -- I think he might've gone to the wedding reception down the hall for it. It felt like, "Here's your hat, what's your hurry?" The coffee was unsurprisingly stale. Anyway, he eventually brought out our thoughtlessly prepared and presented s'mores (there were three of us, but only two skewers, and the chocolate was melting on the plate). As he was clearing our plates--or was it when he gave us our bill?--he asked me whether I wanted cream or sugar with my coffee. The coffee he had brought me twenty or thirty minutes prior!!! I was speechless.

I used to feel Starbucks kept up with the times. Everyone's feeling the pinch of the economy right now, so why raise prices. However, with the new year came new prices. I'm not happy that a house coffee is a 1.99 for a venti drip.  I was content with a  1.75, but come on that's still pricey compared to McDonald's for a hot cup of joe. I used to give that extra quarter as a tip, but now I'm keeping my penny. The personality of the barista's only go so far.

I've been to Plat 99 a few times before and today brought a friend from out of town.  As always, the list of drinks was interesting and pricing was a bit high, but service was good.  While there, my friend's hand was cut on a piece of glass that was wedged in the seat.  The staff checked in about the incident but am not sure we would return due to this incident.

I went to the Sicilian Pasta kitchen on Jasper ave after they opened and never went back until recently because the food was pretty forgettable.

This is a typical casual dining pasta place (I wouldn't say Italian) like the olive garden. They bring you fresh bread while you are waiting for your food and there are many meat, vegetarian and seafood pasta options to chose from. 

I had the gnocchi which was average but my friend had ravioli with a cream sauce and snow peas which was yummy. The service was a little slow but friendly. I might come back here again but prefer the smaller more authentic Italian restaurants in Edmonton.

Welcome to Florida! Rolled into the area latish on a Saturday evening following an epic 29 hour drive from MN. We took the recommendation for a place nearby where we could get a cocktail and a bite to eat. 

Service was poor. Having wandered aimlessly looking for a place to sit, we caught the attention of our apparent server who promptly looked disgusted at the prospect of another table?  She did eventually help us, but we felt rushed and clearly like a burden. 2 out of 4 of us enjoyed the food. 

Despite the convenient location and proximity, we have not made plans to repeat during a week stay in the area.

I ordered an unsweet ice tea and it was weaker than water. I ordered the regular guacamole and it came with a strange texture and lacked any flavor. I ordered the Speedy Gonzalez and started with the cheese enchilada which was drowning in red mole sauce. The sauce had an off taste. The refried beans lacked any significant flavor. And last but not least was the taco. It was in a fried flour tortilla shell and the ground beef had no other special flavor. I was truly disappointed. I left a note on my ticket. I hope they work on improving their flavor palate.

The wine list consists of bottles that could be bought at the store for $6 and priced here at $55+. The wine selection is really horrible. The food wasn't bad but way way way too salty. It's kinda straddling between a nice restaurant and a diner and I don't think it really nailed it.

There is a menu online. That menu is not accurate. They no longer have any soul food items, except for the Sunday after-church brunch thing. They were out of 1/3 of the African items on the menu as well. 

If you aren't actually from this country, I wouldn't recommend eating at this restaurant as there are NO DESCRIPTIONS on the menu. I was sitting there with Google trying to figure out what the foods were. It's just a word (not all in English), and a price. Nothing about preparation or what's in the dish at all. 

The server did a nice job, he tried to answer our questions but admitted he wasn't Nigerian so he didn't know what everything on the menu was. He was friendly and polite and refilled our drinks. 

I ordered the lunch special ($10.99). It was a massive amount of reddish rice, a small pile of fried plantains, and I chose tilapia as my protein. I received the back half of the fish, tail, bones, and all, probably 3-4 oz. of meat, covered in a tasty red sauce. The fish was tender and the sauce was flavorful. However, $11 for a tiny piece of fish with a huge platter of bland rice??? I also ordered a meat pie, which was kind of like a beef stew empanada...very tasty, and at $1.49 it was priced right. I do not like plantains but I tasted a corner of one of these-it was crisp and fresh. I think if I DID like them, I would've enjoyed these. 

My companion ordered the lunch special with beef. It was the same pile of reddish rice, with slightly spicy and very pungent beans (instead of plantains), and 2 tiny pieces of beef under red sauce. The  beef was inedible. Seriously, it was gross. It had an odd flavor, it was dry, it was overcooked. It was so tough it couldn't be cut with a knife and crunched when you chewed it. He said "Here...try some...if you can cut a piece off." He took 2 bites, I took 1, and the rest was left on the plate. Again...$10.99 for that. 

Extremely disappointing experience and I will not be back, unless it is to pick up some meat pies for carry out. If you want African food in Indy I would recommend any of the other restaurants over this one. I think this is your only choice for Nigerian/Nairaland food though.

They need to invest in a cleaning service.  Bathrooms were absolutely disgusting, like I was afraid to touch anything nasty.

Food was ok, however we were really grossed out by the bathrooms.

Would never return.

Personally the ice cream here wasn't worth the wait. The selection of mixes seemed tantalizing as you wait in line to order. The shop is pretty simple, stripped down and bare with a nice area to view the rhythmic chopping of the ice cream. The wait was a little too long for this to be worth while, about an hour and a half. The heat and mass of people in the shop did not make the experience any better, some ladies started to get worked up with me when they misunderstood me talking to my friends. Standing and watching the ice cream being made and rolled is pretty cool unless you're literally watching 10s of ice creams being made that aren't yours. It's an interesting concept, but they really need to expedite it some how, they had a whole rolling station that was not being utilized even with enough workers to man it. Also only having one girl topping the ice creams created a real and tangible bottle neck in service. I'm glad I came with friends because we didn't get the same ice creams and I could taste a bunch of different kinds. The ice cream is harder and icier than I'd like and had week flavor. The toppings don't really do much to add flavor but they look great for the obligatory Instagram photo. I don't think I'd want to waste another 2 hours to get a mediocre ice cream.

Lunch special for $5.29 is big enough to feed a family of three.

Not sure what was cajun, mostly Asian foods & flavors. Fried rice tasted a lot like Rice-A-Roni. 

Added a credit card surcharge even though there were no signs indicating the fee.

The food was pricey, the atmosphere was too much (It was very crowded and loud), and they seemed to be shortstaffed, because the host was also delivery boy and a waiter at the same time? Food was pretty good, even though taste was overshadowed by everything else.
